•LIMS retrieves the query tag with the specified name.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Report does not exist".

•LIMS generates the report. LIMS passes the sample name as a formula field named "SAMPLE_AUDIT_RPT.SAMPLE_NUMBER".  If the value of viewReportFlag is false, the report will be generated in the background.  If the value of printFlag is true, the report will be sent to the printer.

The source audit trail table for this function is the table named SAMPLE_AUDIT_LOG.

The audit reporting tables for this function are the tables named SAMPLE_AUDIT_OLD, SAMPLE_AUDIT_CURRENT, and SAMPLE_AUDIT_RPT.

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example populates the audit reporting tables with all audit trail records for the sample with sample number 16471 without generating a report.

Status = AuditSampleReport(16471, "", "", false, "", "", "")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

AuditTableReport    

Purpose:

The AuditTableReport function generates an audit report for a record in a Table Manager table.

Syntax:

status = AuditTableReport(key, tableName, eventTypesArray, fieldsArray, appendRecords, reportQueryTagName, viewReportFlag, printFlag)

key – the primary key value of the record for which the audit report is to be generated.

tableName – the name of the table for which the audit report is to be generated

eventTypesArray – an array of the audit types to be included in the audit report.  If no array or an empty array is specified, all audit types will be included.

fieldsArray – an array of the database fields to be included in the audit report.  If no array or an empty array is specified, all database fields will be included.  Elements of this array must be specified in the form tablename.fieldname.  Elements that do not contain a period are ignored.

appendRecords – the value true if records should be appended to any existing records already present in the audit reporting tables; the value false if any existing records for the specified record should be deleted before generating new audit report records (optional; the default is false).  The value true is valid only when no eventTypesArray and no fieldsArray are specified.

reportQueryTagName – the name of the query tag to be used for generating the report (optional; the default is blank, i.e., no report will be generated)

viewReportFlag – the value true if the report should be shown in a window (optional; the default is false)

printFlag – the value true if the report should be sent to the printer (optional; the default is false)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the record for which the audit report is to be generated.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Table record does not exist".

•If the value of appendRecords is true, and if either or both of the eventsTypesArray and the fieldsArray have at least one element, LIMS will trigger the error "Can only append records when all records are requested".

•LIMS retrieves all records from the audit trail for the specified record.  If none are found, LIMS will stop and return the value true.

•If the value of appendRecords is false, LIMS will delete all records from the audit reporting tables for the specified record.

•If the value of appendRecords is true, and if the audit reporting tables contain partial records (i.e., records created from a prior call to this function with at least one element for eventTypesArray or fieldsArray), LIMS will delete all records from the audit reporting tables for the specified record.

•LIMS adds the retrieved audit trail records to the audit reporting tables.  If the value of appendRecords is true, LIMS will only add those records whose audit timestamp is greater than the maximum audit timestamp already present in the audit reporting tables for the specified record.  If an eventTypesArray is specified, LIMS will only add those records whose audit types matches one of the elements of that array.  If a fieldsArray is specified, LIMS will only add those records whose field name matches one of the elements in that array.

•If any insert fails, LIMS will roll back the database inserts and trigger the error "Error writing to the TABLE_AUDIT_RPT table"  Otherwise, LIMS will commit the inserts to the database.

•If no value is specified for reportQueryTagName, LIMS will stop and return the value true.

•LIMS retrieves the query tag with the specified name.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Report does not exist".

•LIMS generates the report. LIMS passes the key and table name as formula fields named "TABLE_AUDIT_RPT.PARENT_KEY" and "TABLE_AUDIT_RPT.PARENT_TABLE".  If the value of viewReportFlag is false, the report will be generated in the background.  If the value of printFlag is true, the report will be sent to the printer.

The source audit trail table for this function is the table named TABLE_AUDIT_LOG.

The audit reporting tables for this function are the tables named TABLE_AUDIT_OLD, TABLE_AUDIT_CURRENT, and TABLE_AUDIT_RPT.

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example populates the audit reporting tables with all audit trail records for the analysis named "LOD" without generating a report.

Status = AuditTableReport("LOD", "ANALYSIS", "", "", false, "", "", "")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

 

28.3.4    Automation Script Functions

ActiveFolderSamples   

Purpose:

This function returns an array of samples numbers based on the active folder within LIMS.

Syntax:

Status = ActiveFolderSamples("sampleArrayName")

sampleArrayName – text parameter that defines the name of the array that LIMS will populate with the sample number.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS evaluates the sampleArrayName.  If no array name was specified, the LIMS sets the LastError variable to: "Error, no sample array name specified."

•LIMS verifies that the function was called within the context of an automation script event.  If not, LIMS sets the LastError variable to: "This function can only be called from automation script events."

•LIMS verifies that the active LIMS window is a folder.  If not, LIMS sets the LastError to: "Active window is not a folder manager."

•LIMS retrieves the sample number of the samples in the folder and populates the specified array.

NOTE:  This function must be used in context of an automation script on a folder.


Example:

 Status = ActiveFolderSamples("mySampleAray")

 

Returns:

If successful, this function returns the status = true and populates the specified array.  If unsuccessful, this function returns the status = false.

 

AddEventHandler    

Purpose:

The AddEventHandler function assigns a subroutine to handle a specific event.

Syntax:

status = AddEventHandler(eventName, subroutineName)

Comments:

This function must be executed in an automation script environment.  It is used in the Configure Automation Script Dialog.

Example:

‘ Class name – Batch manager

eventName = "aboutToCreateBatch"

subroutineName = "LB-SETBATZ"

status = AddEventHandler(eventName, subroutineName)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

AddMenu    

Purpose:

The AddMenu function adds a menu and its associated menu items to a window.

NOTE:  This function should be used in the opened event since the menus are not updated by most other events.


Syntax:

status = AddMenu(menuTitle, menuItem, subName, bitmap)

menuTitle is the menu name. 

menuItem is an item in the menu. 

subroutineName is the subroutine called when menuItem is selected. 

Bitmap is the icon representing menuItem and is displayed on the toolbar

Comments:

This function must be executed in an automation script environment..

Example:

The following example will add a menu called File with a menu item Print.  When the Print menu item is selected the PRINT_SUB subroutine will be run.menu:

Title = "&File"

menuItem = "Print"

subroutineName = "PRINT_SUB"

bitmap = "printButton" 

status = AddMenu(menuTitle, menuItem, subroutineName, bitmap)

Returns:

status =  true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

CurrentFolderObjects   

Purpose:

The CurrentFolderObjects function returns an array containing key values for the objects in the currently selected folder. 

Syntax:

status = CurrentFolderObjects("arrayName")

arrayName – the name of the one dimensional array containing the key values for all objects in the folder (the key value depends on the folder object class e.g. SAMPLE folder returns sample numbers, etc.)

Comments:

If the folder contains samples, sample numbers are returned.  If the folder  contains tests, test numbers are returned.  If the folder contains results, result numbers are returned.

 Example:

status = CurrentFolderObjects("arrayName")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

DisableMenu    

Purpose:

The DisableMenu function disables a menu item in a menu.

Syntax:

status = DisableMenu(menuTitle, menuItem)

menuTitle is the menu name.

menuItem is an item in the menu.

Comments:

NOTE:  This function must be executed in an automation script environment. The menu item is  grayed out when disabled.


Example:

menuTitle = "Help"

menuItem = "Contents…"

status = DisableMenu(menuTitle, menuItem)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

DisableWindow    

Purpose:

The DisableWindow function disables (grays out) a pane in a window.

Syntax:

status = DisableWindow(paneName)

paneName – name of the Window to be disabled (case sensitive).

Comments:

paneName is case sensitive.

This function must be executed in an automation script environment.

Example:

‘ Sample login template

status = DisableWindow("CUSTOMER")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

Purpose:

The EnableMenu function enables a menu item in a menu.

Syntax:

status = EnableMenu(menuTitle, menuItem)

menuTitle is the menu name. 

menuItem is an item in the menu

Comments:

This function must be executed in an automation script environment. The menu item changes from grayed out to active.

Example:

menuTitle = "Help"

menuItem = "Contents…"

status = EnableMenu(menuTitle, menuItem)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

EnableWindow    

Purpose:

The EnableWindow function enables a disabled (grayed out) pane in a window.

Syntax:

status = EnableWindow(paneName)

paneName – a pane in a Window to be enabled (case sensitive).

Comments:

This function must be executed in an automation script environment.

Example:

‘ Sample login template

status = EnableWindow("CUSTOMER")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

HideWindow    

Purpose:

The HideWindow function hides a pane in a window.

Syntax:

status = HideWindow(paneName)

paneName - a pane in a Window that is to be hidden (case sensitive).

Comments:

NOTE:  This function must be executed in an automation script environment. 


Example:

The following example will hide the PROJECT field (such as in a Sample Login Template)

‘ Sample login template

status = HideWindow("PROJECT")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

PerformAnyWindowMethod    

Purpose:

The PerformAnyWindowMethod function executes window commands on a window that may not be the window that is in focus..

 

Syntax:

status = PerformAnyWindowMethod(windowName, paneName, methodName, argument1, argument2)

WindowName – a text parameter defining the name of the window to be manipulated.

TIP:  The window name for some windows change when objects are opened. For example, when a project is open in Project Manager the window name changes from ‘Project Manager’ to the name of the project template with the name of the project.  This title is stored in the system variable windowlabel.

paneName – a text parameter defining the name of the window pane to be manipulated.

methodName – a text parameter defining the method to be invoked.

argument1 – optional first parameter used by the invoked method during its execution.

argument2 – optional the second parameter used by the invoked method during its execution.

 

CAUTION: The methodName and arguments should be provided by a LabWare developer.  These methods are subject to change so care should be taken when using this function.

 

This function may not work in the web client.

 

Comments:

This function must be executed in an automation script environment.  Unlike  PerformWindowMethod, this function does not return any value.

 

Example:

The following example will perform the Close function for the Sample Window after the subroutine process has completed.

status = PerformAnyWindowMethod(, "close", , ,"T") 

 

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise

 

 

PerformWindowMenu    

Purpose:

The PerformWindowMenu function can trigger a parameterless LIMS menu item.

NOTE:  This function wiil work with either the NLS key or NLS value for a menu name.


Syntax:

status = PerformWindowMenu(menuName)

menuName – a string entry that defines the name of the menu item to trigger. The window containing this menu must be in focus when this function is executed.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

LIMS triggers the specified menu option.

If the LIMS performs the action successfully without additional prompts, the status is returned as true.

If the LIMS cannot identify the menu item, or if the triggered process included prompt dialog, then the function returns false.

NOTE:  This function must be executed in an automation script environment.  This function may save changes to the database based on the menu item specified

 

CAUTION:  If a result does not have a value enterd for the Formatted Entry, this LIMS Basic function will not trigger the Formatted Calculation for that result


Example:

The following example triggers the menu option: Save when the automation event is triggered.

menuName = "Save"
status = PerformWindowMenu(menuName)

NOTE:  The menu specified must be enabled and viewable on the menu lists.  If the menu option triggers a prompt, then the system prompts the user.  This function does not allow for the passing of parameters.

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered or if the action is not completed due to additional user prompts.

 

PerformWindowMethod    

Purpose:

The PerformWindowMethod function executes windows commands.

Syntax:

status = PerformWindowMethod(paneName, methodName, argument1, argument2, postFlag)

paneName – a text parameter defining the name of the window pane to be manipulated.  This window must be in focus when this function is executed.

methodName – a text parameter defining the method to be invoked.

argument1 – optional first parameter used by the invoked method during its execution.

argument2 – optional the second parameter used by the invoked method during its execution.

postFlag – optional Boolean that determines when the method is to be invoked.  If set to true, the method is not invoked until the end of the current subroutine processes.  If set to false, the method is invoked immediately.  If this value is not specified, the LIMS defaults this value to false.

CAUTION: The methodName and arguments should be provided by a LabWare developer.  These methods are subject to change so care should be taken when using this function.

 

This function may not work in the web client

Comments:

This function should be executed when the relevant window is in focus.

NOTE:  If the methodName returns any value, the value is put in context in a variable called windowMethodReturnValue.


Example:

The following example will perform the Close function for the active window after the subroutine process has completed.

status = PerformWindowMethod(, "close", , ,"T") 

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise

 

RemoveFolderManagerTab    

Purpose:

The function: RemoveFolderManagerTab is intended to be used within an automation script on a CrystalFolderManager class.  The function removes the specified tab from the user interface.

Syntax:

Status = RemoveFolderManagerTab(tab)

tab – string value that is the displayed tab name.

Comments:

Valid values for tab are: Edit, Log Sample, Edit Tests, Enter Results

•LIMS evaluates the tab parameter.  If there is not tab specified, LIMS returns the last error: No tab specified..

•LIMS verified that the Crystal Folder Manager window is open.  If it is not, the LIMS returns the last error: No folder manager window is open.

•LIMS evaluates the specified tab.  If the entry is not one of the defined values, the LIMS returns the last error: Invalid tab specified.

•If the Crystal Folder Manager is not displaying the specified tab, LIMS returns the last error: Specified tab does not exist.

•LIMS removes the specified tab from view.

This function does not commit changes to the database.

Example:

Status = RemoveFolderManagerTab(Log Sample)

Returns: 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

Purpose:

The RemoveMenu function removes a menu item from a menu.

Syntax:

status = RemoveMenu(menuTitle, menuItem)

menuTitle is the menu name.

menuItem is an item in the menu.

Comments:

This function must be executed in an automation script environment.

Example:

menuTitle = "Help"

menuItem = "Contents…"

status = RemoveMenu(menuTitle, menuItem)

Returns:

RemoveProjectManagerTab    

Purpose:

The function: RemoveProjectManagerTab is intended to be used within an automation script on a ProjectManager class.  The function removes the specified tab from the user interface.

Syntax:

Status = RemoveProjectManagerTab(tab)

tab – string value that is the displayed tab name.

Comments:

Valid values for tab are: Sample, Project Results, Items

•LIMS evaluates the tab parameter.  If there is not tab specified, LIMS returns the last error: No tab specified..

•LIMS verified that the Project Manager window is open.  If it is not, the LIMS returns the last error: No project manager winow is open.

•LIMS evaluates the specified tab.  If the entry is not one of the defined values, the LIMS returns the last error: Invalid tab specified.

•If the Project Manager is not displaying the specified tab, LIMS returns the last error: Specified tab does not exist.

•LIMS removes the specified tab from view.

This function does not commit changes to the database.

Example:

Status = RemoveProjectManagerTab(Sample)

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

SendKeys    

Purpose:

The SendKeys function sends keystrokes to an application or pane.

Syntax:

status = SendKeys(paneName, keyString, delayMilliseconds)

paneName - a pane in a Window and is case sensitive.

keyString – a string containing characters corresponding to keystrokes

delayMilliseconds is the number of  milliseconds between keystrokes.

Example:

' Bring up the Windows Start menu
keys = "{Ctrl+}{Esc}{Ctrl-}"
status = SendKeys(, keys, 50)

' Select Run from the Windows Start menu
keys = "R"
status = SendKeys(, keys, 50)

' Start Notepad
keys = "Notepad{Enter}"
status = SendKeys(, keys, 100)

'Write a message
keys = "LIMS sucks!{Backspace}{Backspace}{Backspace}{Backspace}
{Backspace}{Backspace}is Great!"
status = SendKeys(, keys, 200)
status = Wait(2)

' Close Notepad and disregard changes
keys = "{Alt+}{F4}{Alt-}{N}"
status = SendKeys(, keys, 50)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

ShowWindow    

Purpose:

The ShowWindow function unhides a pane in a window.

Syntax:

status = ShowWindow(paneName)

paneName - a pane in a Window and is case sensitive.

Comments:

This function must be executed in an automation script environment.

Example:

The following example will unhide the PROJECT field (such as in a Sample Login Template)

‘ Sample login template

status = ShowWindow("PROJECT")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

AddQCToBatch    

Purpose:

The AddQCToBatch function adds a QC sample to a batch. 

Syntax:

status = AddQCToBatch(batchName, qcSample, position, shiftFlag, resultReps, referencePosition)

qcSample must be defined in the QC Samples table.

position is the batch position where the QC sample is to be added.

resultReps is an array of replicate numbers for RESULT batches (optional).

referencePosition is the position in the batch of the reference sample for the QC (used for dependent QCs).

shiftFlag is a Boolean flag indicating if existing objects can be shifted.  If true, the objects in the batch are shifted forward and the new QC is added.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the batch information for the specified batchName.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch does not exist".

•LIMS checks the Closed field for the batch.  If true, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch does not exist".

•LIMS checks the position.  If the position is < 1, LIMS will trigger the error "The specified position does not exist".

•LIMS checks the maximum position for the batch.  If the position is > maximum position, LIMS will trigger the error "The specified position is past the end of the batch: position".

•LIMS checks the batch position corresponding to the position argument.  If the position is occupied and the shiftFlag is false, LIMS will trigger the error "There is an object at the specified position".

•If the position is occupied and the shiftFlag is true, but the occupied position is at the end of the batch then LIMS will trigger the error "The specified position is past the end of the batch".   If the position is not the maximum allowed position but the shift would result in the last batch position to be moved beyond the maximum allowed position, LIMS will trigger the error "The batch is full: batchName ". 

•If the qcSample argument is does not match a QC Samples record, LIMS will trigger the error "QC Sample does not exist".

•If the qcSample cannot be created, LIMS will trigger the error "Could not create QC" else the QC sample will be added to the batch.

NOTE:  LIMS checks the sampleNumber.  If the sample number is not found, This  function does not do a database commit.


Example:

The following example adds the a BLANK QC sample to the batch named "MY-BATCH-1".

status = AddQCToBatch("MY-BATCH-1", "BLANK", 5, "T")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

AddResultsToBatch    

Purpose:

The AddResultsToBatch function adds results to the specified RESULT batch at a specified position.

Syntax:

numberAdded = AddResultsToBatch(batchName,resultNumsArray, startingPosition)

batchName – a string defining the batch to which results are to be added

resultNumsArray – a one dimensional array of result numbers that are to be added

startingPosition – integer defining the batch position to start adding results. 

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the batch information for the specified batchName.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch does not exist".

•LIMS checks the Closed field for the batch.  If true, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch already closed".

•LIMS checks the type of the batch.  If not a RESULT batch, LIMS will trigger the error "'Batch is not a result batch".

•LIMS checks the startingPosition.  If the position is < 1, LIMS will trigger the error "The specified position does not exist".

•LIMS checks the resultNumsArray.  If it is an array and the result numbers are not nil then the results will be added to the batch if the Add If Batched flag is true for the batch or the Result.Batch field is 0 for the result.

•If the Assign to Objects field in the Batch Tests Template table is checked, a result may be assigned to only one batch.

NOTE: This  function does not do a database commit.


Example:

resultNumsArray[1] = 124

resultNumsArray[2] = 125

numberAdded = AddResultsToBatch("MY-BATCH-1", resultNumsArray, 7)

Returns: 

The function returns the number of results added.  It returns the value 0 if an error is triggered.

 

AddResultToBatch    

Purpose:

The AddResultToBatch function adds a result to the specified RESULT batch

Syntax:

status = AddResultToBatch(batchName, sampleNumber, resultNumber, position, shiftFlag)

batchName – the name of the batch to which the result is to be added

sampleNumber – the sample number corresponding to the result number to be added

resultNumber – the result number to be added

position is the batch position where the result is to be added.

shiftFlag is a Boolean flag indicating if existing objects can be shifted.  If true, the objects in the batch are shifted forward and the new object is added.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the batch information for the specified batchName.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch does not exist".

•LIMS checks the Closed field for the batch.  If true, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch already closed".

•LIMS checks the type of the batch.  If not a RESULT batch, LIMS will trigger the error "'Batch is not a result batch".

•LIMS checks the position.  If the position is < 1, LIMS will trigger the error "The specified position does not exist".

•LIMS checks the sampleNumber.  If the sample number is not found, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample does not exist".

•LIMS checks the resultNumber.  If the result number is not found, LIMS will trigger the error "Result does not exist".

•LIMS checks to see if the result is already in the batch.  If found in the batch, LIMS will trigger the error "Result already in the batch".

•LIMS checks to see if the result.batch.  If > 0 and the Add If Batched flag is false for the current batch , LIMS will trigger the error "Result already in another batch".

•LIMS checks the maximum position for the batch.  If the position is > maximum, LIMS will trigger the error "The specified position is past the end of the batch".

•LIMS checks for a result the batch position.  If already occupied and the shiftFlag is false, LIMS will trigger the error "There is an object at the specified position". 

•If the position is occupied and the shiftFlag is true, but the occupied position is at the end of the batch then LIMS will trigger the error "The specified position is past the end of the batch".   If the position is not the maximum allowed position but the shift would result in the last batch position to be moved beyond the maximum allowed position, LIMS will trigger the error "The batch is full: batchName ".

•If the result still cannot be added to the batch, LIMS will trigger the error "Could not add result to the batch".  Otherwise, the result will be added to the batch

•If the Assign to Objects field in the Batch Tests Template table is true, a result may be assigned to only one batch.  

NOTE:This  function does not do a database commit.


Example:

The following example adds result number 405 to position 5 in the batch named "MY-BATCH-1".

status = AddResultToBatch("MY-BATCH-1", 165, 405, 5, "T")

Returns: 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

AddSampleToBatch    

Purpose:

The AddSampleToBatch function adds a sample to a batch.

Syntax:

status = AddSampleToBatch(batchName, sampleNumber, position, shiftFlag)

BatchName – a string that defines the target batch.

SampleNumber – the sample number of the sample to be added to the batch.  Sample status must be Unreceived, Incomplete, In Progress, or Complete.

Position – an integer defining the position where the sample is to be added.

Shiftflag – Boolean.  If position is occupied and shiftFlag is true, the objects in the batch are shifted forward and the new object is added. The default value is true.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•LIMS evaluates batchName parameter.  If LIMS cannot find a corresponding batch,  LIMS will return the last error "Batch does not exist".

•LIMS checks the Closed field for the batch.  If true, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch already closed".

•LIMS evaluates the position.  If the position is invalid, LIMS returns the last error: The specified position does not exist:  followed by the input value.

•LIMS evaluates the sample number input.  If the sample number is invalid, LIMS returns the last error: The sample does not exist: followed by the input value.

•LIMS checks the batch for the input sample.  If the sample is already assigned to the batch, the LIMS returns the last error: Sample already in the batch: followed by the input value.

•LIMS checks the sample to determine if it has been assigned to a batch.  If the sample has, LIMS returns the last error:  Sample already in another batch: followed by the input value.

•LIMS compares the specific position to the max position for the batch.  If the position is higher than the mamimum position, the LIMS returns the last error: The specified position is past the end of the batch: followed by the input position value.

•If the position in the batch is already occupied, LIMS returns the last error: There is an object at the specified position: followed by the input position value.

•LIMS verifies that there is room in the batch.  If there is no room in the batch for the sample, the LIMS returns the last error: The batch is full: followed by the input batchName value.

•LIMS posts the sample to the batch and updates the sample and batch.

NOTE: This  function does not do a database commit.


Example:

status = AddSampleToBatch("AM-BATCH-1", 45, 6, "T")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

AddTestsToBatch    

Purpose:

The AddTestsToBatch function adds several tests to a batch.

Syntax:

numberAdded = AddTestsToBatch(batchName, testNumsArray, startingPosition)

batchName – a string defining the target batch

testNumsArray – one dimensional array of test numbers to be added to the batch.

StartingPosition – an integer defining the starting batch position.

Comments:

If the Assign to Objects field in the Batch Tests Template table is checked, a test may be assigned to only one batch. 

NOTE: This  function does not do a database commit.


Example:

 

testNumberArray[1] = 88

testNumbreArray[2] = 89

numberAdded = AddTestsToBatch("PM-BATCH-2", testNumberArray, 4)

Returns:

numberAdded = 2

 

AddTestToBatch    

Purpose:

The AddTestToBatch function adds a test to a batch.

Syntax:

status = AddTestToBatch(batchName, sampleNumber, testNumber, position, shiftFlag)

batchName – a string defining the target batch.

sampleNumber – integer defining the sample number of the test to be added.

testNumber – integer defining the test number of the test to be added.

Position – integer defining the position in the batch to add the test.

Shiftflag – Boolean.  If position is occupied and shiftFlag is true, the objects in the batch are shifted forward and the new object is added. The default value is true.

Comments:

If the Assign to Objects field in the Batch Tests Template table is true, a test may be assigned to only one batch. 

NOTE: This  function does not do a database commit.


Example:

status = AddTestToBatch("PM-BATCH-2", 25, 40, 3, "T")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

AuthorizeBatchResult    

Purpose:

The AuthorizeBatchResult function authorizes the specified batch result.

Syntax:

status = AuthorizeBatchResult(batchName,resultNumber,commitFlag)

batchName – the name of the batch that contains the result to be authorized

resultNumber – the result number of the result to be authorized

commitFlag – true if the batch should be saved to the database (the default is false)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the batch whose name is equal to the value of batchName.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch does not exist".

•If the batch is closed, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch is closed".

•LIMS retrieves the batch result whose result number is equal to the value of resultNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the result "Batch result does not exist".

•LIMS determines if the batch result can be authorized by the current user.  If present, the CONTINUE_AUTHORIZE status rule is processed.  If the result cannot be authorized, LIMS will trigger an error using the status rule message as the error message.

•LIMS authorizes the batch result.

If the value of commitFlag is true, LIMS will save the entire batch to the database using "Authorize" as the audit type.

NOTE: This function saves results to the database only if explicitly requested.


Example:

The following example authorizes result 4821 in batch "BATCH1" and saves the changes to the database.

status = AuthorizeBatchResult("BATCH1",4821,true)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

BatchAction    

Purpose:

The BatchAction function executes a batch action. 

Syntax:

status = BatchAction(batchName, actionName, actionText, startTime)

batchName – string defining the target batch

actionName – string defining the target action

actionText – string displayed by LIMS when action is triggered.

startTime – dateTime variable defining when the action is to take place.  If not specific, the current dateTime is defaulted.

Comments:

actionName is defined in the Batch Action Definition dialog in the Batch Tests Template table.

NOTE: A database commit is part of this function.


Example:

startTime = DateTime()

status = BatchAction("LOD-980416-2", "WEIGH", "Weigh the samples", startTime)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

BatchCalculate     

Purpose:

The BatchCalculate function (re)calculates the calculated batch results of the specified batch

Syntax:

status = BatchCalculate(batchName)

batchName – the name of the batch whose results are to be (re)calculated

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the batch whose name is equal to the value of batchName.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid batch name".

•If LIMS is already calculating a batch, the value true will be returned without any additional processing.

•If the batch is closed, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch is closed".

•LIMS (re)calculates the calculated batch results.  A progress dialog is displayed during this process.

•Any open grid result entry windows are refreshed.

NOTE:This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example (re)calculates the calculated results in the batch named "BATCH1".

status = BatchCalculate("BATCH1")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

BatchObjects    

Purpose:

The BatchObjects function returns an array containing the object ids of the objects in a batch.

Syntax:

status = BatchObjects(batchName, "idArrayName") 

batchName – string defining the target batch.

idArrayName – name of the array that contains the object ids.

Comments:

The output array contains test numbers for objects in a test batch and sample numbers for objects in a sample batch.  In addition, the array returns the object's position in the batch.  Empty batch positions return nil.

NOTE:This function does not support queries against Result batches


Example:

status = BatchObjects("LOD-980415-1", "idArray")

Returns:

idArray[1] = nil

idArray[2] = 54

idArray[3] = 55

 

BatchObjectType    

Purpose:

The BatchObjectType function returns the object type (e.g. SAMPLE, SPIKE, DUPE) of an object in a batch.

Syntax:

type = BatchObjectType(batchName, objectId)

batchName – string defining the target batch.

objectID – integer defining the Sample Number or Test Number depending on the type of batch.

Comments:

objectId is sample number for sample batches, test number for test batches, result number for result batches.

NOTE:This function does not support queries against Result batches


Example:

type = BatchObjectType("LOD-980415-1", 1)

Returns: 

type = "SAMPLE"

 

BatchPosition    

Purpose:

The BatchPosition function returns the position of an object in a batch. 

Syntax:

position = BatchPosition(batchName, objectId)

batchName – string defining the target batch.

objectID – integer defining the Sample Number or Test Number depending on the type of batch.

Comments:

objectId is sample number for sample batches and test number for test batches.

NOTE:This function does not support queries against Result batches


Example:

position = BatchPosition("LOD-980415-1", 74)

Returns: 

position = 2

 

CloseBatch    

Purpose:

The CloseBatch function closes a batch.

Syntax:

status = CloseBatch(batchName)

batchName – string defining the target batch.

Comments:

This function does not perform a database commit.

Example:

status = CloseBatch("LOD-980415-1")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

CreateBatch    

Purpose:

The CreateBatch function creates a batch.

Syntax:

batchName = CreateBatch(templateName, fieldsArray, valuesArray, linkKey, objectIdsArray, objectType)

templateName – string defining the Batch Test Template to use when creating batch.

fieldsArray – optional one dimensional array of fields to be populated through LIMS Basic.

valuesArray – optional one dimensional array of values to be posted to the corresponding fields.

linkKey – the string defining the Link Key for the batch.

objectIdsArray – one dimensional array of integers defining the object to be posted to he batch.  These may be Sample Number, Tests Number, or Results Number depending on the batch type.

objectType – optional text parameter defining the type of object specified in the objectIDsArray-  Valid values are SAMPLE, TEST, RESULT depending on the type of batch created.  If the parameter is not specified, the system defaults to SAMPLE for Sample Batches, TEST for Test Batches, and RESULT for Result Batches.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following actions:

•LIMS verifies that the specified template exists.  If the template does not exist, LIMS populates the LastError variable with: "Batch template does not exist: " followed by the specified template.

•LIMS attempts to create the batch using the fields and values arrays specified.  If this operation fails, LIMS populates the LastError variable with: "Batch could not be created."

•LIMS sets the audit reason is defined in the LIMS Basic.

•LIMS commits the batch record.

•LIMS determines the objectType if not specified as defined above.

•LIMS retrieves the specified objects and inserts them into the batch.

•LIMS commits the objects to the batch created in step 4 above.

NOTE: A database commit is part of this function.
Mandatory entries are not enforced.  Template formulae are executed.


Example:

fieldsArray[1] = "DESCRIPTION"

valuesArray[1] = "My first batch of the day."

linkKey = "CET_ANALYSIS"

testNumberArray[1] = 248

testNumberArray[2] = 249

objectType = "TEST"

batchName = CreateBatch("TEST-BATCH", fieldsArray, valuesArray, linkKey, testNumberArray)

Returns:

If successful, returns the batch name in the form: batchName = "LOD-991231-1".  Otherwise, returns the batchName = "ERROR"

 

 

DefaultBatchName    

Purpose:

The DefaultBatchName function returns the name of the batch that is currently open in the Batch Manager window or that is currently being imported by the importer.

Syntax:

batchName = DefaultBatchName()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

If a batch is currently open in Batch Manager or if a batch is currently being imported, LIMS will return its name.

Otherwise, LIMS will return an empty string.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example returns the default batch name.

batchName = DefaultBatchName()

Returns:

The function returns the default batch name if one is defined.  It returns an empty string otherwise.

 

GetBatchSampleResult    

Purpose:

The GetBatchSampleResult function returns a sample result from a batch.

Syntax:

status = GetBatchSampleResult(batchName, sampleNumber, analysisName, resultName, resultfield, "resultArrayName")

batchName – Name of the target batch.

sampleNumber – Sample number of the target sample.

analysisName – Name of the Analysis associated with the target test.

resultName – Name of the result to be retrieved.

resultField – Name of the result field to retrieve.

resultArrayName – One dimensional array of values that match the selection criteria.

Comments:

 

Example:

batchName = "CET-27"

sampleNumbr = 206

analysisName = "MOISTURE"

resultName = "% Moisture"

field = "ENTRY"

resultArrayname = "resultArray"

status  = GetBatchSampleResult batchName, sampleNumber, analysisName, resultName, resultField, "resultArrayName")

Returns:

resultArray[1] = 20

 

GoToBatchPosition     

Purpose:

The GoToBatchPosition function sets the active batch position.

This function must be run within the context of an Automation Script event.

Syntax:

status = GoToBatchPosition(position)

position – integer defining the batch position

Comments:

This function must be executed in an automation script environment.

Example:

string = GoToBatchPosition(3)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise

 

MoveBatchObject    

Purpose:

The MoveBatchObject function moves an object from one position with in a batch to another position within a batch.

Syntax:

status = MoveBatchObject(batchName, startPosition, endPosition)

batchName – string defining the target batch.

startPosition – integer defining an occupied batch position. 

endPosition – integer defining an empty batch position.

Comments:

Objects are moved, not shifted.  Objects can be moved backward and forward within the batch.  Objects between startPosition and endPosition do not participate in the move.

NOTE: This function does not perform a database commit.


Example:

batchName = "AM-BATCH-9"

startPosition = 2

endPosition = 9

status = MoveBatchObject(batchName, startPosition, endPosition)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

ReactivateBatchResult    

Purpose:

The ReactivateBatchResult function reactivates a batch result.

Syntax:

status = ReactivateBatchResult(batchName,resultNumber,commitFlag)

batchName – the name of the batch whose result is to be reactivated

resultNumber – the result number to be reactivated

commitFlag – the value true if the reactivation should be saved in the database.  The default value is false.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the specified batch does not exist, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch does not exist".

•If the specified batch is closed, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch is closed".

•If the specified result does not exist, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch result does not exist".

•If the specified result cannot be reactivated based on the CONTINUE_REACTIVATE status rule, LIMS will trigger an error with the status rule message as error message.

•LIMS reactivates the batch result.

 

NOTE: When a batch result is reactivated, the batch result will be flagged as modified.

Example:

The following example reactivates result 749 of the batch named "BATCH1" and saves the change in the database.

status = ReactivateBatchResult("BATCH1",749,true)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

RejectBatchResult    

Purpose:

The RejectBatchResult function rejects the specified batch result.

Syntax:

status = RejectBatchResult(batchName,resultNumber,commitFlag)

batchName – the name of the batch that contains the result to be rejected

resultNumber – the result number of the result to be rejected

commitFlag – true if the batch should be saved to the database (the default is false)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the batch whose name is equal to the value of batchName.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch does not exist".

•If the batch is closed, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch is closed".

•LIMS retrieves the batch result whose result number is equal to the value of resultNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the result "Batch result does not exist".

•LIMS determines if the batch result can be rejected by the current user.  If present, the CONTINUE_REJECT status rule is processed.  If the result cannot be rejected, LIMS will trigger an error using the status rule message as the error message.

•LIMS rejects the batch result.

If the value of commitFlag is true, LIMS will save the entire batch to the database using "Reject" as the audit type.

NOTE:  This function saves results to the database only if explicitly requested.


Example:

The following example rejects result 4821 in batch "BATCH1" and saves the changes to the database.

status = RejectBatchResult("BATCH1",4821,true)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

RemoveResultFromBatch    

Purpose:

The RemoveResultFromBatch function removes a result from the specified RESULT batch

Syntax:

status = RemoveResultFromBatch(batchName, sampleNumber, resultNumber)

batchName – the name of the batch from which the result is to be removed

sampleNumber – the sample number corresponding to the result number to be removed

resultNumber – the result number to be removed

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the batch information for the specified batchName.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch does not exist".

•LIMS checks the Closed field for the batch.  If true, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch already closed".

•LIMS checks the sampleNumber.  If the sample number is not found, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample does not exist".

•LIMS checks the resultNumber.  If the result number is not found, LIMS will trigger the error "Result does not exist".

•LIMS checks to see if the result is in the batch.  If not found in the batch, LIMS will trigger the error "Result is not in the batch".

•Otherwise the result will be removed from the batch.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example remove result number 405 from the batch named "MY-BATCH-1".

status = RemoveResultFromBatch("MY-BATCH-1", 165, 405)

Returns: 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

RemoveSampleFromBatch    

Purpose:

The RemoveSampleFromBatch function removes a sample from a SAMPLE batch.

Syntax:

status = RemoveSampleFromBatch(batchName, sampleNumber)

batchName – the name of the batch from which the sample is to be removed

sampleNumber – the sample number to be removed

Comments:

Sample status does not matter.

NOTE: This function does not perform a database commit.


Example:

status = RemoveSampleFromBatch("AM-Batch-98", 83)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

RemoveTestFromBatch    

Purpose:

The RemoveTestFromBatch function removes a test from a sample in a TEST batch.

Syntax:

status = RemoveTestFromBatch(batchName, sampleNumber, testNumber)

batchName – the name of the batch from which the test is to be removed

sampleNumber – the sample number corresponding to the test number to be removed

testNumber – the test number to be removed

Comments:

Test status does not matter.

NOTE: This function does not perform a database commit.


Example:

status = RemoveTestFromBatch("AM-Batch-98", 83, 112)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

SaveBatch     

Purpose:

The SaveBatch function saves pending changes of a batch.

Syntax:

status = SaveBatch(batchName, auditReason)

batchName – the name of the batch whose pending changes are to be saved

auditReason – the reason for the pending changes (optional)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If LIMS is unable to find the specified batch, it will trigger the error "Batch does not exist".

•If an audit type has not yet been associated with the pending changes, LIMS will apply the audit type "UpdateBatch".

•If an audit reason is specified, LIMS will apply it to the pending changes.

•LIMS saves the changes to the database.  If this fails for any reason, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch commit failed".

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example saves batch "BATCH001" with reason "typo".

status = SaveBatch("BATCH001", "typo")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

SetOrCreateBatchResult    

Purpose:

The SetOrCreateBatchResult function creates a batch result or updates an existing result with the ability to control the entry value, unit, limits, and reportable flag.  

Syntax:

status = SetOrCreateBatchResult(batchtName, resultNameOrNumber, value, units, type, minLimit, maxLimit, reportable, newReplicate)

batchName – the name of the batch for which a result is to be updated

resultNameOrNumber – either the result name or the result number of the batch result record to be created or updated.

value – value to be posted to the entry field of the result.

units –value to be posted to the units field of the result.

type – value specifying the type of result.

minLimit – value to be posted to the minimum field of the result.

maxLimit – value to be posted to the maximum field of the result.

reportable – Boolean to be posted to the reportable field of the result. 

newReplicate – Boolean to determine if the system should update an existing result if located based on the result name or add a new replicate of the result.  The default value is True.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evaluates the newReplicate parameter.  If no value is specified, LIMS sets the value to True. 

•LIMS interprets the type. 

•If a type value is provided, LIMS uses the first character of the input for the type.

•If the type is not specified and the minLimit and MaxLimit are not specified, the type defaults to T (text).

•If the type is not specified and the minLimit or MaxLimit is specified, the type defaults to N (numeric).

•LIMS verifies the batchName is valid.  If the batch is not found, LIMS returns the error: "Batch does not exist:" followed by the input value.

•If the batch is already closed, LIMS will trigger an error and return: "Batch already closed:" followed by the specified batch name.

•LIMS will perform one of the following steps for the retrieved batch.

•If the specified resultNameOrNumber can be interpreted as an integer, LIMS will retrieve the batch result whose result number matches this value.

•Otherwise, LIMS will retrieve all batch results whose result name match the specified value.

•LIMS creates a new result if no result was retrieved.  If a result was retrived, the LIMS updates the result or adds a new replicate based on the setting of the newReplicate parameter.

•The LIMS updates the audit trail and the result fields as specified by the inputs. 

•If an audit reason is set for the batch result table, that reason is posted to the audit trail.

•If there is no audit reason on the batch result table, the audit reason set on the batch table is posted to the audit trail.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.

 

This function does not enforce entry types when updating the entry field.  For example, it is possible to enter text into a numeric result or a non-list value into a list result type.

Example:

The following example creates a batch result (or updates an existing LB Result if located).

batchName = "14"
resultNameOrNumber = "LB Result"
value = 2.5
units = ""
type = "N"
minLimit = 7
maxLimit =  10
reportable = "T"
newReplicate = "F"

status = SetOrCreateBatchResult(batchName, resultNameOrNumber, value, units, type, minLimit, maxLimit, reportable. newReplicate)

Returns:

The function returns true if it completes successfully.  Otherwise, it returns false with the appropriate Last Error.

 

ShiftBatchObject  

Purpose:

The ShiftBatchObject function shifts the objects in a batch from one position to another. 

Syntax:

status = ShiftBatchObject(batchName, startPosition, endPosition)

batchName – string defining the target batch.

startPosition – the position of the first object to shift and cannot be empty. 

endPosition – the position to which the object at startPosition is shifted to. 

Comments:

Objects between startPosition and endPosition are shifed as well. 

This function does not do a database commit.

Example:

status = ShiftBatchObject("AM-BATCH-78", 3, 6, "T")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

UpdateBatchFld

Purpose:

The UpdateBatchFld function updates a field in a batch.

Syntax:

status = UpdateBatchFld(batchName,field,value,commitFlag)

batchName – the name of the batch to be updated

field – the name of the field to be updated

value – the value to be assigned to the specified field

commitFlag - Boolean that determines if the information is posted to cache awaiting the next save or committed immediately.  The default value is false.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•If the system is unable to find a batch with the specified batchName, it will trigger the error "Batch does not exist".

•If the batch table does not include a field with the specified field name, the system will trigger the error "Invalid field for table".

•If the specified field is a link field (foreign key), the system will validate the specified value against the target table of the link.  If this fails, the system will trigger the error "Invalid value for batch record".

•If the specified field is a date field and if the specified value cannot be converted to a date, the system will use a null value instead.

•If the specified field is a Boolean field and if the specified value is missing or does not represent the value true, the system will use the value false instead.

•If the specified field is an interval field and if the specified value is missing or blank, the system will use the value zero instead.

•The system assigns the specified value to the specified field.  If an audit reason is specified for the batch table, the system will assign it to the transaction.  The system also assigns the audit type "UpdateBatch".

Example:

 

The following example updates the note field of batch "BATCH12" to "Special batch".

status = UpdateBatchFld("BATCH12", "NOTE", "Special batch", “T”)

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

 

UpdateBatchResultFld    

Purpose:

The UpdateBatchResultFld function updates the value of a field in one or more batch result records.

Syntax:

status = UpdateBatchResultFld( batchName, resultNameOrNumber, field, value, commitFlag)

batchName – the name of the batch for which a result is to be updated

resultNameOrNumber – either the result name or the result number of the batch result record(s) to be updated.

field – the name of the batch result field to be updated

value – the value to be stored in the specified field

commitFlag – Boolean setting defining if the function should immediately post changes to the database or if the changes should be cached until a save is triggered.  This value defaults to false if unspecified.  Values starting with "T" or "Y" are interpreted as true.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the batch whose name is equal to the specified batch name.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch does not exist".

•If the batch is already closed, LIMS will trigger the error "Batch already closed".

•LIMS will perform one of the following steps for the retrieved batch.

•If the specified resultNameOrNumber can be interpreted as an integer, LIMS will retrieve the batch result whose result number matches this value.

•Otherwise, LIMS will retrieve all batch results whose result name match the specified value.

•LIMS performs the following steps for each retrieved result record.

•If the batch result table does not have a field whose name is equal to the specified field name, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid field for table".

•If the specified field is a link field, and if the specified value does not appear in the linked table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid value for result record".

•LIMS converts the specified value to the appropriate data type for the specified field and updates the batch result.

•If an audit reason is specified for the batch result table, LIMS will apply it to the pending database transaction.  Otherwise, if an audit reason is specified for the batch table, LIMS will apply that reason instead.

•If the value true is specified for the commit flag, LIMS will save the changes to the database and write a record to the audit trail with audit type "UpdateBatch".  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Error committing sample record".

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database only if the commit flag is true.


Example:

The following example sets the description of each batch result with batch name "BATCH1" and result name "AVGCONC" to "Average Concentration" and then saves the changes to the database.

status = UpdateBatchResultFld( "BATCH1" , "AVGCONC" , "DESCRIPTION" , "Average Concentration", "T")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

 

28.3.6    Comparison Functions
 

Function

Equals

IsEmpty

IsEquivalent

IsFraction

IsNull

IsNumeric

Not

NotEmpty

 

Equals    

Purpose:

The Equals function returns a Boolean indicating whether two values are equal.

Syntax:

status = Equals(firstVal, secondVal)

Comments:

firstval and secondVal can be string, numeric, or dates.

Example:

boolean = Equals(2.2, 1.4)

Returns:

status = true if the values are equal, false otherwise.

 

IsEmpty    

Purpose:

The IsEmpty function returns a Boolean indicating whether a string is empty. IsEmpty() returns true for non-printable characters

Syntax:

status = IsEmpty(string)

Comments:

The IsEmpty function returns true if string is EMPTY, "", string contains non-printable characters, a string of blanks, or  nil.

Example:

Var1 = ""

status = IsEmpty(Var1) 

Returns:

status = true if the string is empty, false otherwise.

 

IsEquivalent

Purpose:

The IsEquivalent function compares two numerical values and determines if they are equivalent, to within a user-defined relative delta. Refer to Knowledge Track KT00757 for more details.

Syntax:

status = IsEquivalent(number1,number2,epsilon)

number1– the first number to be compared

number2– the second number to be compared

epsilon – the allowed difference between the numbers, below which the numbers are considered equivalent.  If this parameter is not specified, then the value of FLT_EPSILON is used (this is a Windows defined constant defined as 1.192092896e-07F).  If one of the numbers is exactly 0 or the numbers have different signs then epsilon is treated as an absolute difference.  Otherwise, epsilon is the maximum relative difference between the numbers.  The relative difference equals epsilon times (the larger of the two numbers).

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•The system compares the numbers. There are 2 special cases: a)  one of the numbers is exactly 0 or b) the numbers have different signs.  If either of these is true then epsilon is treated as an absolute difference.  Otherwise, epsilon is the maximum relative difference between the numbers.  The relative difference equals epsilon times the larger of the two numbers.

Example:

 

 

The following example

firstVal = 30.01 - 30.00

secondVal = 0.01

boolean = Equals(firstVal, secondVal)  'Returns False

status = IsEquivalent(firstVal,secondVal,0.0000001)  'Returns True

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if the numbers are not considered equivalent or if an error is triggered.

 

IsFraction   

Purpose:

This function evaluates if a given number is a fraction and returns whether the evaluation is True or False.

Syntax:

status = IsFraction(number)

number – the number to be evaluated as a fraction or not.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS verifies if the entered number is actually numeric and if the number includes a division sign ("/").

•If the above condition is satisfied, then LIMS performs the division; if the result is an integer (a whole number), then LIMS returns the status False, else LIMS returns the status True, indicating that the entered number is a fraction.

Example:

 i) The following example evaluates the input number ¼.

status = IsFraction(1/4) ‘returns:True.

ii) The following example evaluates the input number 4/2.

status = IsFraction(4/2) ‘returns:False.

Returns:

If the number is a fraction, this function returns the status true, otherwise false.

 

 

IsNull    

Purpose:

The IsNull function returns a Boolean indication whether a value is the null value.

Syntax:

status = IsNull(value)

Comments:

The IsNull function returns true if value is nil or "".

Example:

status = IsNull("abc")

Returns:

status = true if the string is null, false otherwise.

 

IsNumeric    

Purpose:

The IsNumeric function returns a Boolean indicating whether a string can be     converted to a numeric data type.

Syntax:

status = IsNumeric(string)

Comments:

 

Example:

status = IsNumeric("123")

Returns:

status = true if the string can be converted, false otherwise.

 

Not    

Purpose:

The Not function inverts a Boolean variable.

Syntax:

notBool = Not(boolean)

Comments:

 

Example:

ans = Not(false)

Returns:

ans = true

 

NotEmpty    

Purpose:

The NotEmpty function returns a Boolean indicating whether a string is not empty.

Syntax:

status = NotEmpty(string)

Comments:

This function is useful in template formulae and testing variables that may contain database information.  This is the opposite functionality from the IsEmpty function.

Example:

Var1 = “Stuff”

status = NotEmpty(Var1)

Returns:

status = true if the string is not empty, false otherwise.

 

28.3.7    Curl Functions
 

Function

CurlEasyCleanup

CurlEasyEscape

CurlEasyGetInfo
