Trace

UnloadDebuggingSet

 

Breakpoint    

Purpose:

The Breakpoint function halts execution and opens the debugger window.

Syntax:

Breakpoint(aReason)

aReason – a message to be displayed when the breakpoint is encountered

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

LIMS halts execution, opens the debugger window, and displays the message "LIMS Basic BREAKPOINT command encountered" followed by the value of aReason.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example encloses a LIMS Basic statement in a pair of breakpoints.

Breakpoint("before")
status = CalculateResult(1104)
Breakpoint("after")

Returns:

The function returns the value true.

 

CodeSearch    

Purpose:

The CodeSearch function performs a search of the LIMS Basic code throughout the LIMS for the defined patterns and writes the search results to a specified path and file.

NOTE: In the Windows client, the file will be saved with the LogFileName in the client directory.  In the web, the file will not automatically be saved; a File Download dialog will appear with the default value as the LogFileName value (if browser is configured to download automatically the dialog will not be displayed).


Syntax:

status = CodeSearch(SearchPatternArray, LogFileName, ReportNoMatchFlag, tablesToExclude)

SearchPatternArray – a one dimensional array containing the string values that should located within the LIMS Basic throughout the system.

LogFileName – the file name and path for the log file generated by the CodeSearch function.

ReportNoMatchFlag – Boolean defining the functions behavior for reporting when a record is located that does not have a match to any of the search strings.  This value defaults to true is null.  Entries starting with "T" or "Y" are interpreted as true.

tablesToExclude – a one dimensional array containing the table names that should be excluded from the code search.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS performs the code search using the defined parameters.

•LIMS posts results to the specified file.

•If the ReportNoMatchFlag is set to true, the log file displays the name of all records checked and indicates if a match was or was not found.

•If the ReportNoMatchFlag is set to false, the log file displays the name of all the records with matches.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code searches the database for the specified strings except for the analysis_rules table and the sample table.

searchPatternArray[1] = "AssignTestToSamples"
searchPatternArray[2] = "AssignTest"
LogFileName = "C:\DEMO\bject_Files\Search"
ReportNoMatchFlag = "F"
tablesToExclude[1] = "SAMPLE"
tablesToExclude[2] = "analysis_rules"

status = CodeSearch(SearchPatternArray, LogFileName, ReportNoMatchFlag, tablesToExclude)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

CreateLIMSLog    

Purpose:

The CreateLIMSLog function is used to create a new LIMS Log record.

Syntax:

logNumber = CreateLIMSLog(message, table, key, category, reportLastError, display, isError, includeTrace, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

message – the string value to be stored in the Message field.

table – optional argument that can be used to specify the name of the database table that the error was triggered in.

key – optional argument that can be used to specify the key identifier value for the record that the error was triggered in.

category – optional argument that can be used to specify the LIMS Log Category to be stored in the Category field.  Note:  The system LIMS Log Category values (SYS_*) should not be specified.

reportLastError – optional argument that determines whether the value of the lastError variable will be stored in the Error Text field and included in the message displayed in the Information Dialog, or not.  If the Boolean flag set to True, it will be stored and included in the displayed message.  If not specified, the default value True applies.

display – optional argument that determines whether an Information Dialog with the message (and lastError) will be displayed, or not.  If the Boolean flag is set to True, the dialog will be displayed.  If not specified, the default value False applies.  Note:  It should be set to False for routines running in the background.

isError – optional argument that can be used to flag the record as an error, or not.  The Boolean flag can be set to True or False.  If not specified, the default value True applies.

includeTrace – an optional argument that determines whether system context information will be stored in the Trace Text field.  If the Boolean flag is set to True, the data (truncated at the max of 100,000 bytes) will be stored. If not specified, the default value False applies.

fieldsArray – optional two-dimensional array of non-system LIMS Log table fields to be updated with values in the valuesArray.

valuesArray – optional two-dimensional array that holds the values to be stored in the fields in the fieldsArray.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

1.  Evaluates the display argument.  If not specified, the default value True is applied.

2.  Evaluates the isError argument.  If not specified, the default value True is applied.

3.  Evaluates the category argument.  If an invalid LIMS Log Category is specified, the lastError variable is set to “Invalid category specified:  <category>”.

4.  Creates a new LIMS Log record.

5.  If a valid LIMS Log Category is specified, the LIMS_LOG.GROUP_NAME field is set to the group name of the category record.

6.  Evaluates message.  If the specified value includes a NLS key, message is set to the NLS value.  Performs a context translate action on message after the NLS translation.

7.  Evaluates the includeTrace argument. If set to True, the Trace Text field is populated with trace log data (truncated at the max of 100,000 bytes).

8.  Compares the size of the fieldsArray and valuesArray.  If not equal, lastError is set to “Field and value array size are not the same”.

9.  Validates the fields in the fieldsArray.  If an invalid field is encountered, lastError is set to “Invalid field for table: <table>”.

10. Validates the values in the valuesArray.  If an invalid value is encountered, lastError is set to “Invalid value for record: <value> for field <field>”.

11. Updates the field in the fieldsArray with the values in the valuesArray.

12. Performs a database commit and places the LIMS Log record in context.

If the display argument is set to True, an Information Dialog will be opened to display the message.  If reportLastError is True, the lastError to be stored in the Error Text field will be appended to the message displayed in the dialog.

Example:

The following example will insert a new record in the LIMS Log table. 

message = “an error message value specified in the LB routine”

table = “USER_PROGRAM”

key = “EXAMPLE”

category = “LIMS_BASIC”

reportLastError = “T”

display = “T”

isError = “T”

includeTrace = “T”

fieldsArray[1,1] = “STEPS_TO_REPRODUCE”

valuesArray[1,1] = “Run the Macro:  Example”

logNumber = CreateLIMSLog(message, table, key, category, reportLastError, display, isError, includeTrace, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

Returns:

 

The following example shows the use of NLS and context substitution functionality to set the value for the message argument.  A custom NLS entry like “LIMSLogCustomerCopyFailed "Failed to copy customer record {sourceCustomer} to new customer record {newCustomer}" can be used to generate the value for the message argument when a record cannot be copied.

message = “{NLS:LIMSLogCustomerCopyFailed}”

table = “USER_PROGRAM”

key = “CUST_COPY”

category = “LIMS_BASIC”

reportLastError = “T”

display = “T”

isError = “T”

includeTrace = “T”

fieldsArray[1,1] = “STEPS_TO_REPRODUCE”

valuesArray[1,1] = “Run the Macro:  Customer Copy”

logNumber = CreateLIMSLog(message, table, key, category, reportLastError, display, isError, includeTrace, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

 

CreateSchema    

Purpose:

The CreateSchema function

Syntax:

fileName = CreateSchema()

Example:

The following example creates a file labware-lims.xsd in the xsd folder of the LIMS working directory. It returns the file path.

fileName = CreateSchema()

Returns:

The function returns the file name and path when successful.

 

CreateSupportLog    

Purpose:

The CreateSupportLog function allows a support.log file to be created.

Syntax:

status = CreateSupportLog(comment)

comment – the comment value to be displayed in the support log

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•A support.log file is created in the LW working directory.

Example:

 

 

The following example creates a support.log file with the comment "Example  Support Log".

status = CreateSupportLog("Example Support Log")

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

DebuggingSet    

Purpose:

Retrieves the name of the currently loaded debugging set.

Syntax:

name = DebuggingSet()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS checks if a debugging set is loaded.  If no set is loaded LIMS returns EMPTY

•LIMS checks if a debugging set is loaded and has a name associated with it from the ‘reference’ variable assigned from the LoadDebuggingSet() function.  If no reference name is assigned, LIMS returns EMPTY.

•If the debugging set is loaded with the reference name assigned, LIMS returns the reference name.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

name = DebuggingSet()

 

IF (ISEMPTY(name)) THEN

    Msgbox("Debugging Set does not have a reference name or there is no set loaded")

ELSE

    Msgbox("Debugging Set Name: " & name)

ENDIF

Returns:

The function returns the reference value of the currently loaded debugging set or EMPTY if no set is loaded or the reference value is not defined.

 

DebugMessage   

Purpose:

The DebugMessage function writes a message to the debug.log file.

 

debug  mode is active.

Syntax:

status = DebugMessage(message)

Comments:

 

Example:

status = DebugMessage("Testing new function.")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

DebugOff    

Purpose:

The DebugOff function turns off the writing of messages to the debug.log file.

Syntax:

status = DebugOff()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

LIMS turns off the writing of messages to the debug.log file.

Example:

The following example turns off the writing of messages to the debug.log file.

status = DebugOff()

Returns:

The function returns the value true.

 

DebugOn    

Purpose:

The DebugOn function turns on the writing of messages to the debug.log file.

Syntax:

status = DebugOn()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

LIMS turns on the writing of messages to the debug.log file.

Example:

The following example turns on the writing of messages to the debug.log file.

status = DebugOn()

Returns:

The function returns the value true.

 

GetDiagnosticsMode

Purpose:

The GetDiagnosticsMode function identifies if the LIMS Basic compiler is in or out of Diagnostics mode.

Syntax:

status = GetDiagnosticsMode()

Returns:

The function returns true if the LIMS Basic compiler is in Diagnostics mode. If it is not in Diagnostics mode, it will answer false.

 

HasDebuggingPoints    

Purpose:

Checks if there are any Debugging Points currently defined in memory for the current LIMS session

Syntax:

status = hasDebuggingPoints()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS checks if there are any currently loaded debugging points in the current LabWare session.  If there are no points loaded, LIMS returns False.  If there are debugging points loaded, LIMS returns True.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following statement checks if there are loaded debugging points and displays a message box

status = hasDebuggingPoints()

 

IF (status = true) THEN

    msgbox("Debugging Points are currently loaded in this session.")

ELSEIF (status = false) THEN

    Msgbox("There are no debugging points loaded in this session")

ENDIF

Returns:

The function returns the value true if there are loaded debugging points and false if there are no debugging points currently loaded.

 

LIMSLogClose

Purpose:

The LIMSLogClose() function is used to set the status of a LIMS Log record to closed.

Syntax:

status = LIMSLogClose(sysIdArray, closeComment)

sysIdArray – an array (one or two-dimensional) that holds the Log Number values of the LIMS Log records to be closed.

closeComment – the comment to be reflected in the LIMS_LOG.CLOSED_COMMENT field for each of the LIMS Log records included in the sysIdArray.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

1.  Evaluates the sysIdArray argument.  If is not an array, the lastError variable is set to “The first argument must be an array.”.

2.  Validates the data in the sysIdArray.  If the data value is not an integer, lastError is set to “Ids must be integers”.  If a LIMS Log record cannot be found for the specified data value, lastError is set to “Record does not exist: <data value>”

3.  Updates the LIMS Log records.  The Status field is set to CLOSED.  The Closed By field is set to the user name of the logged in user.  The Closed On field is set to the local data time.  The Closed On UTC field is set to the UTC date time.  The Closed Comment field is set to the closeComment value.

Note:  The function does not include a database commit.

Example:

The following example will clear a LIMS Log records with the Log Numbers 2351 and 2385.  The Comment field will reflect “LIMS Admin corrected the LabWare Client Names”.

sysIdArray[1,1] = 2351

sysIdArray[2,1] = 2385

closeComment = “Resolved by specifying correct LabWare Client Names”

 status = LIMSLogClose(sysIdArray, closeComment)

Returns:

The status variable is set to True if the function is performed successfully, else it is set to False.

 

 

LoadDebuggingSet    

Purpose:

Loads a debugging set from a string which can be retrieved from a file or field in the database.

Syntax:

status = LoadDebuggingSet(aString, append, reference)

aString – an XML string defining the debugging points to load.

append – Optional true or false.  If true, the new debugging points from the debugString will be added to any currently loaded debugging points.  If false, currently loaded debugging points will be unloaded and only the new points will be loaded. 

reference – Optional reference name of the loaded debugging set.  The DebuggingSet() function will retrieve the reference name.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS checks the debugString value.  If it is not a valid XML string for debugging points LIMS will trigger an error "Unexpected debug set format."

•LIMS checks if the value for append start with a T or F.  If no values is assigned or append does not start with T or F, the LIMS assigns the default value of False.

•LIMS checks if a value is assigned for reference and associates the reference value to the debugging set.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following statement gets the file contents of a debugging set file as a string and loads the debugging set from the file contents, adding the new debugging points to any already loaded.

append = "T"

reference = "DebugFile1"

treatAsText = "T"

debugFileName = "C:\LabWare8\DebugFiles\DebugDemoFile1.dbg"

 

debugString = FileContents(debugFileName, treatAsText)

 

status = LoadDebuggingSet(debugString, append, reference)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

RebuildNLS    

Purpose:

The RebuildNLS function rebuilds the National Language Support cache.

Syntax:

status = RebuildNLS()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

If a previous cache file exists, LIMS will delete it.

LIMS rebuilds the National Language Support cache.

The default name of the cache file is "NLS.obj".  If the current user has a language prefix, that prefix will be used for reading NLS files and writing the NLS cache.

Example:

The following example rebuilds the National Language Support cache.

status = RebuildNLS()

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

SetDiagnosticsMode

Purpose:

The SetDiagnosticsMode function puts the LIMS Basic compiler in Diagnostics mode.

Syntax:

status = SetDiagnosticsMode(aBoolean)

aBoolean – a Boolean value which puts the compiler in Diagnostics Mode if true.

Comments:

Diagnostics mode forces all compiled LIMS basic to including debugging information such as line numbers. If there is a LIMS basic exception, then the line number information will be included along with the routine name and any exception message in the basic.log file. Without diagnostics mode, only the line number of the last line of code along with the routine and exception information is provided.

Example:

 

 

The following example puts the compiler in Diagnostics mode.

status = SetDiagnosticsMode("T")

Returns:

 

The function returns true if successful. If it is not successful, it will answer false and lastError will be set with an appropriate error message.

 

Trace    

Purpose:

The Trace function generates a trace to assist in debugging of LIMS Basic Code.  The trace.log file can be updated with the state of global variables, context and local variables, arrays and selected table records each time the Trace function is called.

Syntax:

Trace(label, valuesArray, alwaysDoFlag)

label – The name of the trace point displayed in the trace.log file as Trace Point: label

valuesArray – Optional. An array of the variable names to log to the trace.log file.  If provided, this will limit the variables reported in the trace.log file to only the variables listed in array.

alwaysDoFlag – Optional true or false, by default is false. If true, will always log to the trace.log file even if the Debugging Option “Write hardcoded trace() call data to trace.log” is false.  If false, will only write to the trace.log file if the Debugging Option setting is true.  

Comments:

This function may be called multiple times within the same LIMS Basic code.  A different label should be given for each position in the code where the function is called.  

Example:

x = 1

y = 2

Trace("first", , “F”)

z = 3

TracePointsArray[1] = “x”

TracePointsArray[2] = “z”

Trace("After z", TracePointsArray, “F”)

Status = SelectSample(5)

Trace("After SelectSample")

Returns:

       

For the examples shown previously, the following entries are produced in the trace.log:

*********************

Trace Point: first

Variables

     x   1

     y   2

     z   EMPTY

Arrays

Selects

**********************

Trace Point: After z

Variables

     x   1

     z   3

Arrays

Selects

**********************

Trace Point: After SampleSelect

Variables

     x   1

     y   2

     z   3

Arrays

Selects

         Table:  SAMPLE

    Record:  1

SAMPLE_NUMBER  ->  5

TEXT_ID  ->  LW-02/10/99-5

STATUS  ->  A
