inputarrayF[1] = 1

inputarrayF[2] = 15

inputarrayF[3] = 1

inputarrayF[4] = 1

inputarrayF[5] = 1

    For i = 1 to 3

        For j = 1 to 5

            inputarray[j,i] = inputarrayF[j]

        next j

    Next i

status = SetGridTextColors(inputArray)

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

SetGridValues  

Purpose:

The SetGridValues function sets the default values in a user grid.

Syntax:

status = SetGridValues(inputArray)

inputArray – 2-D array of values specifying the values to put in the User grid cells.

Comments:

This function does NOT save changes to the database.

Example:

The following example updates values in first 3 columns of the first 6 rows:

For i = 1 to 3

        inputarray[1,i] = "ENTRY" & str(i)

        inputarray[2,i] = "Value A" & str(i)

        inputarray[3,i] = "Value B" & str(i)

        inputarray[4,i] = "Value C" & str(i)

        inputarray[5,i] = "Value D" & str(i)

        inputarray[6,i] = "Value E" & str(i)

Next

status = SetGridValues(inputArray)

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

28.3.19  HTML Note
Function

AppendToHTMLNote

DisplayHTMLNote

EditHTMLNote

 

AppendToHTMLNote    

Purpose:

The AppendToHTMLNote function creates a new HTML Note based on the specified note number.

Syntax:

newNoteNumber = AppendToHTMLNote(noteNumber, appendText)

noteNumber – an integer value that identifies the note to which text should be appended

appendText – the text to be appended to the note.  The text can include HTML markup but no header or body tags.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the note from the database.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid note specified".

•If the value of appendText is blank or omitted, LIMS will trigger the error "No text specified to append".

•LIMS creates a new note record and copied the note type and reportable flag from the old note to the new note.

•LIMS appends the specified text to the contents of the old note and stores the result in the new note.

•LIMS saves the new note to the database.

•LIMS returns the note number of the new note.

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example appends the text "This is important" to the note with note number 521.

appendText = "<p><u>This</u> is </p><p><strong>important </strong></p>"

newNoteNumber = AppendToHTMLNote(521, "Reviewed on April 29, 2005")

Returns:

The function returns the note number of the new note if it completes successfully.  It returns the value "ERROR" if an error is triggered.

 

DisplayHTMLNote   

Purpose:

This function displays the HTLM editor for the specified note in a read only mode.

Syntax:

Status = DisplayHTMLNote(noteNumber)

noteNumber – numeric input defining the HTLM note to be displayed.

Comments:

When this function is triggered LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS verifies that the specified noteNumber is valid.  If not, LIMS populates the lastError with: "Invalid not specified: " followed by the input.

•LIMS displays the specified HTML note.

Example:

Status = DisplayHTMLNote(60)



Returns:

If successful, the status = true; if not, false.

 

EditHTMLNote   

Purpose:

This function updates  or creates HTML Notes interactively or silently.

Syntax:

newNoteNumber = EditHtmlNote(noteNumber, label, noteType, reportable,templateNote, interactiveFlag, noteHtml)

noteNumber – numeric parameter defining the existing note to open.

label – Title to be displayed on the HTML editor.

noteType – Text parameter defining the HTML note type.

reportable – Boolean setting the reportable field on the HTML note.  There is no default value for this parameter.  Values starting with T or Y will be treated as true, all else will be treated as false.  Null will generate a database error.

templateNote – numeric parameter defining the existing note to use as a template.

interactiveFlag – Boolean parameter that determines if the HTML Notes editor is displayed to the user or if it is a silent process.  To display the editor, set to true.  To act silently, set to false.  The default value is false.

noteHtml – Text to automatically insert into the HTML Note when created or displayed.  Note that existing text is removed.

 

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS evaluates the reportable Boolean to determine if it is true or false.

•LIMS evaluates the interactive Boolean to determine if it is true or false.  Defaults to false.

•If a noteNumber is specified, LIMS loads the note.  If unsuccessful, LIMS populates the lastError with: "Invalid note specified:" followed by the input.

•If a noteNumber and a TemplateNote is specified, LIMS populates the lastError with: "Cannot specify a note template for an existing note."

•If a TemplateNote is specified, LIMS loads the note.  If unsuccessful, LIMS populates the lastError with: "Invalid note specified:" followed by the input.

•LIMS sets the following values on the HTML Note:
     Reportable
     Note Type
     HTML Content

•If interactive is set to true, LIMS displays the HTML Notes editor.

Example:

 noteNumber = "68"

label = "M0391 Example"

noteType = "TYPE_C"

reportable = "y"

templateNote = ""

interactiveFlag = "T"

noteHtml = "more text"

newNoteNumber = EditHtmlNote(noteNumber, label, noteType, reportable,templateNote, interactiveFlag, noteHtml)

Returns:

If successful, LIMS returns the number of the create HTML Note.  If unsuccessful, LIMS returns the newNoteNumber = ERROR.  If the HTML Notes editor is canceled, LIMS returns the newNoteNumber = 0.

 

28.3.20  Importing Functions
 

Function

BulkInsert

ExportObject

ImportFile

ImportObject

ImportParserPollDir

 

BulkInsert    

Purpose:

The BulkInsert function inserts the records defined by the fieldsArray and valuesArray into the table named tableName.

Syntax:

status = BulkInsert(tableName, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

tableName – the name of the table into which rcords are to be inserted

fieldsArray – a one-dimensional array that contains the names of the fields to be assigned

valuesArray – a two-dimensional array that contains the values to be assigned to each field in each new record.  The first dimension identifies the record (row) and the second dimension identies the field.  For example, the value stored in valuesArray[2,3] will be assigned to the second record and to the field whose name is stored in fieldsArray[3].

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the fields array is not a one-dimensional array, LIMS will trigger the error "Fields array must be one dimensional".

•If the values array is not a two-dimensional array, LIMS will trigger the error "Values array must be two dimensional".

•For each unique value of the first dimension of the values array, LIMS will insert a new record into the database and assign it the specified values.

•If any insert fails, LIMS will trigger an error.  The error message that was returned by the database server is written to the database log file.

•LIMS commits all records after all the inserts have been completed.

NOTE:  This function provides an efficient mechanism to insert multiple records directly into the database.  LIMS performs no data validations and LIMS does not insert any records into the audit trail.  Fields normally populated by the LIMS such as GROUP_NAME, CHANGED_ON, CHANGED_BY and REMOVED are not inserted automatically so they should be included in the Fields and Values arrays.

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example adds two records to the customer table.

t = "CUSTOMER"
f[1] = "NAME"
f[2] = "DESCRIPTION"
v[1,1] = "ACME"
v[1,2] = "Acme Corporation"
v[2,1] = "LABWARE"
v[2,2] = "LabWare, Inc."
status = BulkInsert(t,f,v)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

ExportObject    

Purpose:

The ExportObject function exports an object to a disk file.

Syntax:

status = ExportObject(tableName, keyFieldValue, version, filePathName , keepActive)

tableName – the name of the table that contains the object to be exported

keyFieldValue – the primary key value of the object to be exported

version – the version number of the object to be exported, if applicable.

filePathName – the name, including path, of the file to which the object is to be exported

NOTE:The filePathName argument must have a .zip file extension.

keepActive – A Boolean indicating if the Active flag should be preserved for the object to be exported, if applicable. If this argument is set to True then if the current object has Active = true the exported object will have the Active flag = true.  Otherwise the Active flag is set to false.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the specified table is not a TableManager table, LIMS will generate the error "Specified table does not exist".

•LIMS retrieves the record for the specified primary key value and version number.  If no version is specified, but the table is versioned, LIMS will use the latest version.  If the retrieval fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified record does not exist".

•LIMS exports the object to the specified file.  If no file is specified, LIMS will generate a file name based on the primary key of the object with extension ".obj".  If this fails, LIMS will generate the error "Object dump failed".

NOTE:This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example exports the latest version of the analysis named "LOD" to the file named "ANALYSIS_LOD.zip" in the current directory.

fileName = "ANALYSIS_LOD".zip"

filePathName = objdirectory & fileName

status = ExportObject("ANALYSIS", "LOD", "", filePathName,"T")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

ImportFile    

Purpose:

The ImportFile function pulls a record currently outside LIMS into the database.

Syntax:

status = ImportFile(filePathName, auditReason)

filePathName – a string defining the file name with path.

auditReason – an optional audit reason to be applied to the import.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•The system retrieves specified file.  If the specified file is not found, the system returns the error: "Specified file does not exist:" followed by the input value.

•The system parses the files.  If an error is detected during parsing, the system returns the last error:  "Parser error:" followed by the parsing error detected.

•The system saves the parsed data to the database.  If the system cannot save the save the information, the system returns the last error: "Error saving parsed data."

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following code imports the file: targetfile.csv into the database.

filePathName = "c:\DEMO\data_files\targetfile.csv"
auditReason = "Importing file"
status = ImportFile(filePathName, auditReason)

Returns:

The function returns true if successful. If it is not successful, it will answer false and lastError will be set with an appropriate error message.

 

ImportObject    

Purpose:

The ImportObject function imports an object from a file into the database.

Syntax:

status = ImportObject(tableName, filePathName, overwriteFlag)

tableName – the name of the table into which the object should be imported

filePathName – the name, including path, of the file to be imported

overwriteFlag – the value true if any existing record should be overwritten.  (The default value is false.)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the specified file does not exist, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified file does not exist".

•LIMS loads  the file into memory.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Object load failed".

•If the value of overwriteFlag is false, and if the record already exists, LIMS will trigger the error "Record already exists".

•If the value of overwriteFlag is true, and if the record already exists, and if the record is not versioned, LIMS will delete the existing record from the database using audit type "TableUpdate".

•If the record is versioned, LIMS will apply the next version number if the record already exists, or the version 1 otherwise.  (This means that the record may not be restored with its original version number.)

•If the table name in the record does not match the tableName argument, LIMS will trigger the error "Object table name does not match specified table name".

•LIMS erases any cached static data from memory.

•LIMS saves the imported object in the database using audit type "TableUpdate".  If this fails, LIMS triggers the error "Error updating record".

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example loads an analysis from the file named "c:\mine.obj" without overwriting any existing records.

status = ImportObject("ANALYSIS", "c:\mine.obj", false)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

ImportParserPollDir    

Purpose:

The ImportParserPollDir function returns the name of the directory where the import parser looks for files to be processed.

Syntax:

directoryName = ImportParserPollDir()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS returns the name of the File Import Directory as configured in the Client Configuration Manager dialog.  This is the dialog that is accessed via the menu item Configure / System / Client.

•If no directory is specified, LIMS will return the current directory.

Example:

The following example returns the name of the import directory.

directoryName = ImportParserPollDir()

Returns:

The function returns the name of the import directory.

 

 

28.3.21  Instrument Functions
 

Function

Calibrate

CheckInstruments

InstrumentApprovals

InstrumentRemoveApproval

PerformMaintenance

PerformService

PutInstrumentOnLine

TakeInstrumentOffLine

UpdateInstrumentFld

 

Calibrate    

Purpose:

The Calibrate function sets the Calibration Date of an instrument and optionally updates its parameters.   The function records an entry in the instrument log and optionally sets custom field values there.  This function is typically used in an event trigger when a calibration sample is authorized.

Syntax:

status = Calibrate(instName, comment, paramArray, fields, values)

instName – the name of the instrument to be updated

comment – an optional comment to be stored in the instrument log.

paramArray – an array that contains the parameter values to be stored in the instrument record (optional; see comments below)

fields – an array that contains the fields in the instrument log table to be updated

values – an array that contains the values for the specified fields

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the instrument from the database.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Instrument does not exist".

•LIMS sets the last calibration date of the instrument equal to the current date and time.

•If a parameter array is specified, LIMS will store the parameter values as follows.

•The value of the first array element is stored as the first instrument parameter value, i.e., in the column named parameter_1_value.

•Each subsequent value in the array is stored in each subsequent parameter value column.

•If an audit reason was specified for the instrument table, LIMS will apply it to the pending transaction.

•LIMS saves the changes in the database and uses "InstrumentEvent" as the audit type.

•LIMS creates a new record in the instrument log table for the updated instrument.

•LIMS set the event date equal to the last calibration date (as defined above).

•LIMS set the event equal to "CAL".

•If a comment is specified, LIMS will store it in the instrument log record.

•LIMS sets the name of the operator in the log record equal to the username of the current user.

•If a fields array is specified, LIMS will process each element as follows.

•If the field does not exist in the instrument log table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid field for table".

•If the field is defined as a link field, and if the corresponding value does not exist in the linked table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid value for record".

•If the field is a date field, and if the specified value cannot be interpreted as a date, LIMS will assign the value NULL instead.

•If the field is a boolean field, LIMS will assign the value true if the specified value begins with the letter "T".  Otherwise, it will assign the value false.

•If the field is defined as an interval field, and if the specified value is blank, LIMS will assign the value zero instead.

•LIMS saves the changes in the database.

NOTE:  The paramArray indices must be numbered in consecutive order, starting with the number 1.  The instrument table in the base database is configured with two parameter columns.  This implies that only the following three scenarios are valid: a) the paramArray argument is not specified or has no arguments;  b) paramArray[1] is the only element of the array;  c) paramArray[1] and paramArray[2] are the only two elements of the array.

NOTE:  The indices of the fields and values array must be numbered in consecutive order, starting with the number 1.  The two arrays must have the same number of elements.  If these conditions are not met, LIMS will trigger the error "index is outside of collection bounds."

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.

NOTE:  The LabWare base system does not include any fields in the INSTRUMENT_LOG table for use with the fields and values arguments but these arguments can be used with user added fields.


Example:

The following example updates the calibration date of instrument GC_1.

instrumentName = "GC_1"
logComment = "Everything looks good"
parameterArray[1] = 2.4

status = Calibrate(instrumentName, logComment, parameterArray)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

CheckInstruments    

Purpose:

The CheckInstruments function checks if any instruments need calibration or preventative maintenance.

Syntax:

status = CheckInstruments()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS checks all instruments to see if they need calibration.  If any instrument needs calibration, LIMS will set its field named NEEDS_CALIBRATION to true and save this change in the database using audit type "InstrumentEvent".  If the instrument has a parent, LIMS will make the same change to the parent instrument.

•LIMS checks all instruments to see if they need preventative maintenance.  If any instrument needs such maintenance, LIMS will set its field named NEEDS_PM to true and save this change in the database using audit type "InstrumentEvent" .  If the instrument has a parent, LIMS will make the same change to the parent instrument.

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example checks if any instruments need calibration or preventative maintenance.

status = CheckInstruments()

Returns:

The function returns the value true.

 

InstrumentApproval    

Purpose:

The InstrumentApproval function approves a given user for a given instrument and optionally updates one or more fields in the analysis approval record.

Syntax:

status = InstrumentApproval(userName, instrument, approver, approvalDate, approvalInterval, logComment, fields, values)

userName – the user name of the user to be approved

instrument – the name of the instrument for which the user is to be approved

approver – a boolean flag that indicates whether the user is authorized to approve others for the same instrument.  If this value is blank, LIMS will assume the value false.

approvalDate – the date on which the approval was granted

approvalInterval – the number of days for which the approval remains valid.  This value is ignored in the case of a recertification.

logComment – in the case of recertification, a comment to be included in the log.  This value is ignored in the case of an initial certification.

fields – an array that contains the names of the fields in the INSTRUMENT_APPROVAL table to be updated

values – an array that contains the values to be assigned to each of the fields

NOTE:  the fields and values arguments are optional.  However, if you specify one, you must specify both and the two arrays must be the same size.  The behavior of LIMS is unspecified if these conditions are not met and is likely to result in a runtime error.


Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the user record for the specified user name.  If this record does not exist, LIMS will trigger the error "The user record does not exist, or is removed".

•LIMS retrieves the instrument record for the specified instrument name.  If this record does not exist, LIMS will trigger the error "The instrument does not exist".

•LIMS retrieves the instrument approval record for the specified user and instrument.  If this record does not exist, LIMS will perform the following steps.

•LIMS creates a new instrument approval record for the specified user and instrument.

•LIMS sets the last certification date of the new record equal to the specified approval date.

•LIMS sets the approval interval of the new record equal to the specified interval.

•LIMS sets the approver flag equal to the specified boolean value (false if left blank).

•If the fields and values arrays were specified, LIMS will perform the following steps for each field/value pair.

•If the specified field name does not exist in the instrument approval table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid field for table".

•If the specified value does not have the correct data type or, in the case of a list or link field, the value does not appear in the corresponding list or table, LIMS will skip this field/value pair without triggering an error.

•LIMS updates the specified field in the instrument approval record with the specified value.

•LIMS saves the instrument approval record to the database, and writes a record to the audit trail with audit type "Certified".

•If the instrument record does exist, LIMS will perform the following steps instead.

•LIMS creates a new user instrument log record.

•LIMS sets the event date of the new log record equal to the specified approval date.

•LIMS sets the event name of the new log record equal to "Recertified".

•LIMS sets the comment of the new log record equal to the specified comment.

•LIMS sets the last certification date of the instrument approval record equal to the specified approval date.

•If the fields and values arrays were specified, LIMS will perform the following steps for each field/value pair.

•If the specified field name does not exist in the instrument approval table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid field for table".

•If the specified value does not have the correct data type or, in the case of a list or link field, the value does not appear in the corresponding list or table, LIMS will skip this field/value pair without triggering an error.

•LIMS updates the specified field in the instrument approval record with the specified value.

•LIMS saves the instrument approval record to the database, and writes a record to the audit trail with audit type "Recertified".

•LIMS saves the new log record to the database, and writes a record to the audit trail with audit type "Recertified".

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example approves user JSMITH for the balance BAL3 for 90 days.

u = "JSMITH"
a = "BAL3"
d = Date()
status = InstrumentApproval(u, a, "F", d, 90, "")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

InstrumentRemoveApproval    

Purpose:

The InstrumentRemoveApproval function modifies the targeted user account by removing approval for the specified instrument.

Syntax:

tatus = InstrumentRemoveApproval(userName, instrument, logComment)

userName – name of the user account to be updated.

instrument – name of the instrument to be removed from the user account’s approvals.

logComment – string posted to the log for the event.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that the specified user name is valid.  If the user name is not valid, the LIMS returns the last error: "The user record does not exist, or is removed."

•LIMS verifies that the instrument name is valid.  If the instrument is not valid, LIMS returns the error: "The instrument does not exist:" followed by the input value.

•LIMS verified that the specified user has approval for the specified instrument.  If the user does not have approval, LIMS returns the following error:
"Approval for instrument does not exist:" followed by the input value.

•If successful, the intrument approval will be removed for the specified user.

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following code updates the user account by removing approval for the specified instrument.

userName = " DEMO_B"
instrument = " DEMO_A"
logComment = "Documentation"
status = InstrumentRemoveApproval(userName, insturment, logComment)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

PerformMaintenance    

Purpose:

The PerformMaintenance function sets the PM Date of an instrument.   The function records an entry in the instrument log and optionally sets custom field values there.

Syntax:

status = PerformMaintenance(instName, comment, fields, values)

instName – the name of the instrument to be updated

comment – an optional comment to be stored in the instrument log.

fields – an array that contains the fields in the instrument log table to be updated

values – an array that contains the values for the specified fields

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the instrument from the database.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Instrument does not exist".

•LIMS sets the last PM date of the instrument equal to the current date and time.

•If an audit reason was specified for the instrument table, LIMS will apply it to the pending transaction.

•LIMS saves the changes in the database and uses "InstrumentEvent" as the audit type.

•LIMS creates a new record in the instrument log table for the updated instrument.

•LIMS set the event date equal to the last calibration date (as defined above).

•LIMS set the event equal to "PM".

•If a comment is specified, LIMS will store it in the instrument log record.

•LIMS sets the name of the operator in the log record equal to the username of the current user.

•If a fields array is specified, LIMS will process each element as follows.

•If the field does not exist in the instrument log table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid field for table".

•If the field is defined as a link field, and if the corresponding value does not exist in the linked table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid value for record".

•If the field is a date field, and if the specified value cannot be interpreted as a date, LIMS will assign the value NULL instead.

•If the field is a boolean field, LIMS will assign the value true if the specified value begins with the letter "T".  Otherwise, it will assign the value false.

•If the field is defined as an interval field, and if the specified value is blank, LIMS will assign the value zero instead.

•LIMS saves the changes in the database.

NOTE:  The indices of the fields and values array must be numbered in consecutive order, starting with the number 1.  The two arrays must have the same number of elements.  If these conditions are not met, LIMS will trigger the error "index is outside of collection bounds."

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example updates the PM date of instrument GC_1.

instrumentName = "GC_1"
logComment = "No parts replaced"

status = PerformMaintenance(instrumentName, logComment)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

PerformService    

Purpose:

The PerformService function sets the Service Date of an instrument.   The function records an entry in the instrument log and optionally sets custom field values there.

Syntax:

status = PerformService(instName, comment, fields, values)

instName – the name of the instrument to be updated

comment – an optional comment to be stored in the instrument log.

fields – an array that contains the fields in the instrument log table to be updated

values – an array that contains the values for the specified fields

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the instrument from the database.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error: "Instrument does not exist".

•LIMS saves the changes in the database and uses "InstrumentEvent" as the audit type.

•LIMS creates a new record in the instrument log table for the updated instrument.

•LIMS set the event equal to "SERV".

•If a comment is specified, LIMS will store it in the instrument log record.

•LIMS sets the name of the operator in the log record equal to the username of the current user.

•If a fields array is specified, LIMS will process each element as follows.

•If the field does not exist in the instrument log table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid field for table".

•If the field is defined as a link field, and if the corresponding value does not exist in the linked table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid value for record".

•If the field is a date field, and if the specified value cannot be interpreted as a date, LIMS will assign the value NULL instead.

•If the field is a Boolean field, LIMS will assign the value true if the specified value begins with the letter "T".  Otherwise, it will assign the value false.

•If the field is defined as an interval field, and if the specified value is blank, LIMS will assign the value zero instead.

•If an audit reason is specified for the instrument log table, LIMS will apply it to the current update.

LIMS saves the changes in the database and uses "InstrumentEvent" as the audit type.

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example updates the PM date of instrument GC_1.  This configuration also updates the customer fields: X_SERV_PROV and X_SERV_TYPE.

instrumentName = "GC_1"
logComment = "No parts replaced"
Fields[1] = "X_SERV_PROV"
Fields[2] = "X_SERV_TYPE"
Values[1] = "Instruments United"
Values[2] = "Value replacement"

Status = PerformService(instrumentName, logComment, fields, values)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

PutInstrumentOnLine    

Purpose:

The PutInstrumentOnLine function puts an instrument online and adds an entry to its log.

Syntax:

status = PutInstrumentOnLine(instName, comment, fields, values)

instName – the name of the instrument to be put online

comment – a comment to be recorded in the instrument log (optional)

fields – an array that contains the names of the fields in the instrument log to be updated

values – an array that contains the values to be assigned to each of the fields

NOTE: The fields and values arguments are optional.  However, if you specify one, you must specify both and the two arrays must be the same size.  The behavior of LIMS is unspecified if these conditions are not met and is likely to result in a runtime error.


Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the instrument for the specified name.  If no instrument is found, LIMS will trigger the error "Instrument does not exist".

•LIMS puts the instrument on line and immediately saves this change to the database.  A record is written to the audit trail with audit type "InstrumentEvent".  If saving the record fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Error committing instrument record".

•LIMS creates a new instrument log record for the specified instrument and updates it as follows.

•LIMS sets the event date equal to the current date and time.

•LIMS sets the event name to "ON".

•If a comment is specified, LIMS updates the comment field in the log record with the specified comment.

•If the fields and values arrays were specified, LIMS will perform the following steps for each field/value pair.

•If the specified field name does not exist in the instrument log table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid field for table".

•If the field is a link field, and if the specified value is not present in the corresponding link table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid value for record".

•LIMS updates the specified field in the instrument log record with the specified value.

•LIMS saves the instrument log record to the database.

NOTE:  This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example puts balance "BAL1" online.

status = PutInstrumentOnLine("BAL1")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

TakeInstrumentOffLine    

Purpose:

The TakeInstrumentOffLine function takes an instrument offline and adds an entry to its log.

Syntax:

status = TakeInstrumentOffLine(instName, comment, fields, values)

instName – the name of the instrument to be taken offline

comment – a comment to be recorded in the instrument log (optional)

fields – an array that contains the names of the fields in the instrument log to be updated

values – an array that contains the values to be assigned to each of the fields

NOTE: The fields and values arguments are optional.  However, if you specify one, you must specify both and the two arrays must be the same size.  The behavior of LIMS is unspecified if these conditions are not met and is likely to result in a runtime error.


Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the instrument for the specified name.  If no instrument is found, LIMS will trigger the error "Instrument does not exist".

•LIMS takes the instrument off line and immediately saves this change to the database.  A record is written to the audit trail with audit type "InstrumentEvent".  If saving the record fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Error committing instrument record".

•LIMS creates a new instrument log record for the specified instrument and updates it as follows.

•LIMS sets the event date equal to the current date and time.

•LIMS sets the event name to "OFF".

•If a comment is specified, LIMS updates the comment field in the log record with the specified comment.

•If the fields and values arrays were specified, LIMS will perform the following steps for each field/value pair.

•If the specified field name does not exist in the instrument log table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid field for table".

•If the field is a link field, and if the specified value is not present in the corresponding link table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid value for record".

•LIMS updates the specified field in the instrument log record with the specified value.

•LIMS saves the instrument log record to the database.

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example takes balance "BAL2" offline.

status = TakeInstrumentOffLine("BAL2")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

UpdateInstrumentFld    

Purpose:

The UpdateInstrumentFld function updates a field of an instrument.

Syntax:

status = UpdateInstrumentFld(instrumentName,field,value)

instrumentName – the name of the instrument to be updated

field – the name of the field to be updated

value – the value to be assigned to the specified field

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If LIMS is unable to find an instrument with the specified instrumentName, it will trigger the error "Instrument does not exist".

•If the instrument table does not include a field with the specified field name, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid field for table".

•If the specified field is a link field (foreign key), LIMS will validate the specified value against the target table of the link.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid value for instrument record".

•If the specified field is a date field and if the specified value cannot be converted to a date, LIMS will use a null value instead.

•If the specified field is a boolean field and if the specified value is missing or does not represent the value true, LIMS will use the value false instead.

•If the specified field is an interval field and if the specified value is missing or blank, LIMS will use the value zero instead.

•LIMS assigns the specified value to the specified field.  If an audit reason is specified for the instrument table, LIMS will assign it to the pending transaction.  LIMS also assigns the audit type "UpdateTable" to the pending transaction.

 

NOTE:This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example sets the room location of instrument "BALANCE01" to "A12".

status = UpdateInstrumentFld("BALANCE01", "ROOM", "A12")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

28.3.22  Inventory Functions
 

Function

CreateInventory

ReduceInventory

RemoveInventory

UpdateInventory

 

CreateInventory    

Purpose:

The CreateInventory function creates an item in Inventory Manager.

Syntax:

itemNumber = CreateInventory(fields, values, auditReason)

fields - must contain fields from the INVENTORY_ITEM table.

values - are the values to be assigned to the fields. If the Group Name is not specified, the item will default to DEFAULT group. 

auditReason is the reason for the action. If the auditor is on (audit all changes), then auditReason is written to the audit log.)

Comments:

Mandatory entries are not enforced.

NOTE: If the Stock field is included in the FieldsArray and the Stock field in the INVENTORY_ITEM table is linked to the STOCK Table then the value supplied for the Stock field must be a valid entry in the STOCK table.  If the Stock field is not linked then any value can be used for the Stock field.

NOTE: A database commit is part of this function.


Example:

fieldsArray[1] = "STOCK"

fieldsArray[2] = "LOCATION"

fieldsArray[3] = "QUANTITY"

valuesArray[1] = "Formulation A"

valuesArray[2] = "B256-A912"

valuesArray[3] = 120

auditReason = "New item delivered."

itemNumber = CreateInventory(fieldsValues, valuesArray, auditReason)

Returns: 

itemNumber = 70

 

ReduceInventory    

Purpose:

The ReduceInventory function reduces the quantity of an inventory item.

Syntax:

status = ReduceInventory(itemNumber, amount, units, auditReason)

itemNumber – the item number of the inventory item whose quantity is to be reduced

amount – the amount by which the quantity is to be reduced

units – the unit of measure in which the amount is expressed

auditReason – the audit reason to be saved in the audit trail (optional)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no inventory item exists with the specified itemNumber, LIMS will trigger the error "Inventory item does not exist".

•If no record exists in the UNITS table for the specified units value, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid units for inventory record".

•If the value of the units argument is different from the unit of measure stored in the inventory record, LIMS will convert the specified amount to that unit of measure.

•If the specified amount is negative, or if the specified amount is greater than the amount currently in inventory, LIMS will trigger the error "Error… Cannot make requested inventory adjustments".

•LIMS adjusts the quantity in inventory.

•If an audit reason is specified, LIMS will apply it to the inventory adjustment.

•LIMS saves the changes to the database using the audit type "UpdateInventory".  If this fails for any reason, LIMS will trigger the error "Error committing inventory record".

 

NOTE:  This function saves changes to the database.

NOTE:  To avoid unit conversion (or if the amount has no unit of measure), specify the value "NONE" for units.


Example:

The following example reduces the quantity of item 53 by 2 grams without specifying an audit reason.

status = ReduceInventory(53, 2, "G", "")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

RemoveInventory    

Purpose:

The RemoveInventory function deletes an inventory item in Inventory Manager.

Syntax:

status = RemoveInventory(itemNumber, auditReason)

itemNumber is the number generated by the CreateInventory function.

auditReason is the reason for the action. If the auditor is on (audit all changes), then the value is written to the audit log.

Comments:

 NOTE:  A database commit is part of this function


Example:

auditReason = "Stock expired."

status = RemoveInventory (19, auditReason)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

UpdateInventory    

Purpose:

The UpdateInventory function updates a field in an inventory item.

Syntax:

status = UpdateInventory(itemNumber, fieldsArray, valuesArray, auditReason, commitFlag)

itemNumber – integer defining the inventory number.

fieldsArray – one dimensional array containing fields to be populated.

valuesArray – one dimensional array containing values posted to the corresponding fields.

auditReason – string posted to the audit trails documenting the action.

commitFlag – Boolean determining if the value is committed to the database.  "T", true, commits the value, "F", false, retains the value in case.  The default value is "F".

Comments:

CAUTION:  This function should be used with caution as any field in the INVENTORY_ITEM table may be updated. 

 

NOTE: This function does not perform a database commit


Example:

 

fieldsArray[1] = "LOCATION"

fieldsArray[2] = "QUANTITY"

valuesArray[1] = "C2_T30_H90"

valuesArray[2] = 100

auditReason = "Pulled from stock for special testing."

status = UpdateInventory (5, fieldsArray, valuesArray, auditReason)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

 

28.3.23  Investigation Functions
 

Function

AddInvestigationItem

AddObjectToInvestigation

CloseInvestigation

CreateInvestigation

DefaultInvestigationName

RemoveObjectFromInvestigation

RestoreInvestigation

UpdateInvestigationFld

UpdateInvestigationItemFld

 

AddInvestigationItem    

Purpose:

This function adds an entry to an item table on a specified investigation.

Syntax:

Status = AddInvestigationItem(InvestigationName, ItemTable, FieldsArray, ValuesArray)

InvestigationName – string defining the target investigation.

ItemTable – string defining the target item table.

FieldsArray –one dimensional array of field names on the target item table.

ValuesArray –one dimensional array of values to be posted to the corresponding fields.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•LIMS evaluates the input investigationName.  If the investigation is not located, LIMS returns the last error: "Specified investigation does not exist:"

•LIMS verifies that the investigation is not closed.  If closed, the LIMS returns the last error:  "Investigation closed:"

•LIMS verifies that the fields array is the same size as the values array.  If not, the LIMS returns the last error: "Field and value array size do not match."

•LIMS evaluates the input itemTable.  If the item table is not associated with the investigation, LIMS returns the error: "Specified item table is not valid for investigation".

•LIMS evaluates the fields.  If a specified field is not on the item table, the LIMS returns the last error: "nvalid field for table: " followed by the table name.

•If the LIMS is unable to post a value to a field due to input constraints, the LIMS returns the last error: "Invalid value for record:" <field name> then "for field:" <value>.

•LIMS updates the item table in cache.

NOTE: this function does not perform a commit.


Example:

 

fieldsArray[1] = "SEQ_NUM"

fieldsArray[2] = "DATE"

fieldsArray[3] = "MEMO"

valuesArray[1] = 14

valuesArray[2] = date()

valuesArray[3] = "Impurity was identified as compound 123, see NB J765DJ"

status = AddInvestigationItem("DEMO-0123", "INVEST_ATTACH", fieldsArray, valuesArray)

Returns:

Function returns status = true if successful and false if an error is triggered.

 

AddObjectToInvestigation    

Purpose:

The AddObjectToInvestigation function adds the specified object to the specified investigation, with an optional remark.

Syntax:

status = AddObjectToInvestigation(investigationName, objectClass, objectId, remark)

investigationName – the name of the investigation to which an object is to be added

objectClass – the name of the table that contains the record to be added

objectId – the primary key of the record to be added

remark – a text value to be associated with the added object

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the investigation with investigation name equal to the value of investigationName.  If it does not exist, LIMS will trigger the error "The specified investigation does not exist".

•If the investigation is closed, LIMS will trigger the error "The investigation is closed".

•LIMS adds a new reference object to the investigation.  It sets the values of object_class, object_id, and remark to the specified values of objectClass, objectId, and remark, respectively.

•LIMS sets the audit type equal to "UpdateInvestigation" for the pending database transaction of the investigation.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

status = AddObjectToInvestigation("INV12", "SAMPLE", 4523, "Test sample")

Adds the sample with sample number 4523 to investigation "INV12" with the remark "Test sample".

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

CloseInvestigation    

Purpose:

The CloseInvestigation function closes the specified investigation.

Syntax:

status = CloseInvestigation(investigationName)

investigationName – the name of the investigation to be closed

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the specified investigation.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified investigation does not exist".

•If the investigation is already closed, LIMS will trigger the error "The investigation is already closed".

•LIMS closes the investigation.

•LIMS assigns the audit type "CloseInvestigation" to the investigation.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example closes the investigation named "INVEST1".

status = CloseInvestigation("INVEST1")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

CreateInvestigation    

Purpose:

The CreateInvestigation function creates a new investigation

Syntax:

investigationName = CreateInvestigation(Template, fields, values)

Template – the name of the template from which the investigation is to be created

fields – an array that contains the names of the fields to be set

values – an array that contains the values to be set

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no template exists with the specified name, LIMS will generate the error "Specified template does not exist".

•LIMS creates a new investigation based on the specified template and assigns the values in the values array to the fields in the fields array.

•LIMS generates a name for the investigation based on the ID configuration specified in the template.  If the generated name is longer than the width of the corresponding field, the name will be truncated.

•LIMS assigns the audit type "CreateInvestigation" to the investigation and adds the investigation to its context.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example creates an investigation from the template named "INVTPL1" and sets the value of "CUSTOMER" equal to "ACME".

myFields[1] = "CUSTOMER"
myValues[1] = "ACME"
investigationName = CreateInvestigation("INVTPL1", myFields, myValues)

Returns:

The function returns the name of the investigation if it completes successfully.  It returns the value "ERROR" if an error is triggered.

 

DefaultInvestigationName    

Purpose:

The DefaultInvestigationName function returns the name of the investigation that is currently open in the Investigation Manager window.

Syntax:

investigationName = DefaultInvestigationName()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If an investigation is currently open in Investigation Manager, LIMS will return its name.

•Otherwise, LIMS will return an empty string.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database


Example:

The following example returns the default investigation name.

investigationName = DefaultInvestigationName()

Returns:

The function returns the default investigation name of one is defined.  It returns an empty string otherwise.

 

RemoveObjectFromInvestigation    

Purpose:

The RemoveObjectFromInvestigation function removes an object from an investigation.

Syntax:

status = RemoveObjectFromInvestigation(investigationName, objectClass, objectId)

investigationName – the name of the investigation from which an object should be removed

objectClass – the type of object to be removed from the investigation

objectId – the primary key value of the object to be removed from the investigation

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If LIMS is unable to locate an investigation with the specified investigation name, it will trigger the error "The specified investigation does not exist".

•If the investigation is closed, LIMS will trigger the error "The investigation is closed".

•If the specified object is not associated with the investigation, LIMS will trigger the error "The specified object does not exist".

•LIMS marks the association between the object and the investigation for deletion.  (The object itself is not marked for deletion, only its association with the investigation.)

•LIMS associates the audit type "UpdateInvestigation" with the pending deletion.

NOTE:  This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example removes sample 2324 from the investigation named "INVEST1".

status = RemoveObjectFromInvestigation("INVEST1", "SAMPLE", 2324)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

RestoreInvestigation    

Purpose:

The RestoreInvestigation function reopens a previously closed investigation.

Syntax:

status = RestoreInvestigation(investigationName)

investigationName – the name of the investigation to be reopened

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

If LIMS is unable to find the specified investigation, it will trigger the error "The specified investigation does not exist".

If the investigation is not currently marked as closed, LIMS will trigger the error "The investigation cannot be restored because is not closed".

LIMS reopens the investigation.

LIMS associates the audit type "RestoreInvestigation" with the pending changes to the investigation.

  NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example reopens the investigation named "INVEST1".

status = RestoreInvestigation("INVEST1")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

UpdateInvestigationFld    

Purpose:

The UpdateInvestigationFld function updates a field of an investigation.

Syntax:

status = UpdateInvestigationFld(investigation,field,value,commitFlag)

investigation – the name of the investigation to be updated

field – the name of the field to be updated

value – the value to be assigned to the specified field

commitFlag – the value true is the change should be saved in the database (optional; the default value is false)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If LIMS is unable to find an investigation with the specified investigation name, it will trigger the error "Investigation does not exist".

•If the investigation table does not include a field with the specified field name, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid field for table".

•If the specified field is a link field (foreign key), LIMS will validate the specified value against the target table of the link.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid value for investigation record".

•If the specified field is a date field and if the specified value cannot be converted to a date, LIMS will use a null value instead.

•If the specified field is a boolean field and if the specified value is missing or does not represent the value true, LIMS will use the value false instead.

•If the specified field is an interval field and if the specified value is missing or blank, LIMS will use the value zero instead.

•LIMS assigns the specified value to the specified field.  If an audit reason is specified for the investigation table, LIMS will assign it to the pending transaction.

•If the value of commitFlag is true, LIMS saves the change to the database using the audit type "UpdateInvestigation".  If this fails for any reason, LIMS will trigger the error "Error committing investigation".

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database if explicitly requested to do so.


Example:

The following example sets the note of investigation "INVEST1" equal to "Urgent" and saves the change to the database.

status = UpdateInvestigationFld("INVEST1", "NOTES", "Urgent", true)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

UpdateInvestigationItemFld    

Purpose:

The UpdateInvestigationItemFld function updates a field of an investigation item.

Syntax:

status = UpdateInvestigationItemFld(investigationName, itemTable, seqNum, field, value, commitFlag)

investigationName – the name of the investigation to be updated

itemTable – string defining the item table to be updated.

seqNum – integer defining the item table entry to be updated.

field – the name of the field to be updated

value – the value to be assigned to the specified field

commitFlag – the value true is the change should be saved in the database (optional; the default value is false)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If LIMS is unable to find an investigation with the specified investigation name, it will trigger the error "Investigation does not exist".

•LIMS verifies the investigation is not closed.  If closed, the LIMS returns the last error: "Investigation closed".

•LIMS checks the specified item table to entries for the investigation.  If none are found, LIMS returns the last error: Specified item table has no entries for investigation."

•LIMS evaluates the SeqNum.  If no entry matches the value, LIMS returns the last error:  "Specified item is not valid for the investigation"

•LIMS verifies that the specified fields are on the item table.  If not, the LIMS returns the last error: "Invalid field for table:"

•LIMS evaluates the values and field types.  If LIMS is unable to post a value to the specified field, LIMS returns the last error: "Invalid value for item record:" + <value> + " for field:" + <field>.

 NOTE: This function saves changes to the database if explicitly requested to do so.


Example:

The following example sets the note of investigation "INVEST1" equal to "Urgent" and saves the change to the database.

status = UpdateInvestigationFld("INVEST1", "NOTES", "Urgent", true)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

Comments:

Example:

The following example returns the name of the background server.

workstation = BackgroundWorkstationName()

Returns:

The function returns the name of the background server.

Syntax:

Comments:

Example:

name = ClientName()

Returns:

The GetClientConfigurationSetting function returns settings from the client configuration.

key – the name of the client setting to be returned

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•The LIMS checks the key specified.  If the key is not found, the LIMS returns the last error: "Key not found - ' , key."

•The LIMS retrieves the value.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


 Example:

The following example returns the value of the checkactions setting from the client configuration:

key = "checkactions"

value = GetClientConfigurationSetting(key)

Returns:

The function returns the value if it completes successfully.  It returns the value ERROR if an error is triggered.

Comments:

NOTE:This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

Returns:

Purpose:

The GetDefaultPrinter function returns the name of the default printer.

printerName – The name of the default printer retrieved by the function

Comments:

 

Example:

printerName = GetDefaultPrinter()

Returns:

GetLabelPrinterConfig    

Purpose:

The GetLabelPrinterConfig function retrieves the label printer configuration information.

Syntax:

status = GetLabelPrinterConfig("labelPrinterInfoArray")

labelPrinterInfoArray – the name of the LIMS Basic array in which the configuration should be written

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

LIMS sets the first element of the labelPrinterInfoArray equal to current label printer type.

LIMS sets the second element of the labelPrinterInfoArray equal to the current label printer port.

NOTE: The input argument is a string value that should contain the name of a LIMS Basic array, i.e., not the array itself.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example obtains the label printer configuration using an array named "myArray".

status = GetLabelPrinterConfig("myArray")

myType = myArray[1]

myPort = myArray[2]

Returns:

The function returns the value true if successful.  The value of false is returned if an error is triggered.

 

GetLabelPrinterConfigFromPath    

Purpose:

The GetLablePrinterConfigFromPath function retrieves the label printer type and label printer port from the client configuration.

Syntax:

status = GetLabelPrinterConfigFromPath(pathToConfig, "labelPrinterInfoArray")

pathToConfig – Directory path name for the Config.sys file.

labelPrinterInfoArray – one dimensional array containing the label printer type in the first position and the label printer port in the second position.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•The LIMS verifies that the defined path.  If the Config.sys file cannot be found or read, the LIMS returns the last error:
"Unable to read file:" followed by the specified pathToConfig value and "Config.sys.".

•LIMS reads the Config.sys file and returns an array with the value specified for the labelPrinterInfoArray.  The first value is the label printer type.  The second value is the label printer port.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code returns the label printer type and the label print port that is saved to the Config.sys file in the DEMO directory.

pathToConfig = "c:\ DEMO\"
labelPrinterInfoArray = "LPIA"
status = GetLabelPrinterConfigFromPath(pathToConfig, labelPrinterInfoArray )

Returns:

The function returns the status True if the completes successfully.  The function returns a status of False if an error is triggered. 

 

GetModuleVersion   

Purpose:

This function return the .sll dev number of the specified module.

Syntax:

moduleVer = GetModuleVersion(moduleNumber)

moduleNumber – text parameter specifying the module to be reported. 

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS verifies that the format of the moduleNumber is five characters and the first letter is M or P.  If not, then LIMS populates the lastError with:  "Invalid module specified: " followed by the input.

•LIMS verifies that the module is bound.  If not, LIMS populates the lastError with: "The specified module is not bound:" followed by the input.

•LIMS returns the dev number of the module.

Example:

moduleNumber = "M0391"

moduleVer = GetModuleVersion(moduleNumber)

Return:  moduleVer = 24

Returns:

If successful, moduleVer is set to the dev number of the module.  If not, the moduleVer is set to ERROR.

 

GetReportsDir    

Purpose:

The GetReportsDir function returns the path to the report files directory.  This is the path that is set using the Configure Reports item on the main menu.

Syntax:

pathName = GetReportsDir()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

LIMS returns the current value of the report files directory.

NOTE: This function does not save any changes to the database.


Example:

The following example displays the report files directory in a message box.

pathName = GetReportsDir()
Msgbox(pathName)

Returns:

The function returns a text value.

 

GetSessionID    

Purpose:

The GetSessionID function returns the value of the web Session ID. 

Syntax:

sessionId = GetSessionId()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

LIMS returns the current value of the web Session ID.

NOTE: This function does not save any changes to the database.


Example:

The following example displays the report files directory in a message box.

sessionId = GetSessionId()
Msgbox(sessionId)

Returns:

The function returns a text value prefixed with the LIMS instance name followed by a string of hexadecimal characters e.g.  LIMS1_513868561344299cda6a479f2d338585.

Returns:

Comments:

•LIMS sets the label printer port equal to the port specified in the Client Configuration Manager dialog.

•LIMS sets the label printer type equal to the type specified in the Client Configuration Manager dialog.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

status = ResetLabelPrinterConfig()

Purpose:

The ServerDir function. returns the directory in which the LIMS executable file resides.

Syntax:

pathName = ServerDir()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If a directory name was specified on the command line, LIMS will return it.

•Otherwise, LIMS will return the current default directory.

Example:

The following example returns the server directory path.

pathName = ServerDir()

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

SetDefaultPrinter

Purpose:

The SetDefaultPrinter function sets the name of the default printer.

Syntax:

status = SetDefaultPrinter(printerName)

printerName – the name of the printer to be used.  Must be a valid printer for the Windows client.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•The system checks the printerName parameter.  If it is empty, the system will trigger the error: “No printer name”. 

•If the printerName parameter is not a valid printer for the Windows client the function will return false. Otherwise the system sets the default printer to the specified printer.

Example:

The following example sets the default to the PDF Camp printer:

printername = "PDFcamp Printer"

status = SetDefaultPrinter(printerName)

Returns:

 

The function returns true if successful. If it is not successful, it will answer false and lastError will be set with an appropriate error message.

 

SetExternalLinkDirectory

Purpose:

This function will reset the named External Link directory to the value specified.

Syntax:

status = SetExternalLinkDirectory(directoryId, newPath)

directoryId – The name in the CONFIG_SYS table of the directory value to update.  Valid values are: DYNAMIC_DIR, REPORTS_DIR, STATIC_DIR, WORKFLOWS_DIR, or WORKSHEET_DIR

newPath – The new path name for the directory

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•The system checks the newPath.  If not valid, the system will trigger the error: “The new directory path does not exist.”

•The system checks the directoryId.  If not valid, the system will trigger the error: “Invalid directory id. Valid options: DYNAMIC_DIR, REPORTS_DIR, STATIC_DIR, WORKFLOWS_DIR, WORKSHEET_DIR”.

•The system will set the External Link directory to the specified path.

Example:

 

 

directoryId = "REPORTS_DIR"

newPath = "C:\LabWare-8\Reports"

status = SetExternalLinkDirectory(directoryId, newPath)

Returns:

 

The function returns true if successful, false otherwise.

 

SetLabelPrinterConfig    

Purpose:

The SetLabelPrinterConfig function sets the configuration of the label printer.

Syntax:

status = SetLabelPrinterConfig(port, printerType)

port – the port to which the printer is attached

printerType – the name of the printer to which labels should be printed

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If both arguments are blank or missing, LIMS will trigger the error "Must specify a printer port or type".

•If a value is specified for port, and if the value is not one of COM1 through COM8 or the name of a printer as defined in Windows, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid printer port".

•If a valid value is specified for port, LIMS sets the label printer port equal to that value.

•If a value is specified for printerType, and the value is not one of the supported label printer types (or the NLS translation of the value "ConfigurationManagerNone"), LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid printer type".

•If a valid value is specified for printerType, LIMS sets the label printer type equal to that value.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example sets the port to "COM1" and the type to "Intermec3400".

status = SetLabelPrinterConfig("COM1", "Intermec3400")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

SetReportsDir    

Purpose:

The SetReportsDir function sets the report files directory.  This is the path that is set using the Configure Reports item on the main menu.

Syntax:

status = SetReportsDir(pathName)

pathName – the directory path of the desired report files directory.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no path is specified, LIMS will trigger the error "No path name specified".

•If the specified path does not point to an existing directory, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid reports directory specified".

•LIMS sets the value of the report files directory equal to the specified path.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example sets the reports files directory to c:\reports.

status = SetReportsDir("c:\reports")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

VisualWorkflowDir

Purpose:

This function will gets the value the VisualWorkflowDir.

Syntax:

pathname = VisualWorkflowDir()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•The system checks if the code is executed from a Windows or a web client.  If the client is web then the pathname is set to the folder: common/visualworkflows/  in the web server folder.  Otherwise, the system will get the VisualWorkflowDir directory and return the path name for the directory.

Example:

 

pathName = VisualWorkflowDir()

Returns:

 

The function gets the value the VisualWorkflowDir that is being used by the client.

 

WhereUsed    

Purpose:

The WhereUsed function searches the LIMS database for occurrences of a linked field value.

Syntax:

status = WhereUsed(tableName, keyFieldValue, fileName, arrayName, maxRecordsToCheck)

tableName – the name of the table that contains the value whose references are to be found

keyFieldValue – the key field value whose references are to be found

fileName – the name of the file in which the references are to be writted.  This argument is optional.  If no file name is specified, no file will be written.

arrayName – the name of the LIMS Basic array in which the references are to be stored.  This argument is optional.  If no array name is specified, no LIMS Basic array will be created.

maxRecordsToCheck – the maximum number of matching records to be retrieved from each table that is linked to the specified table.  If no value is specified, the value 1000 will be used.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no table name is specified, LIMS will trigger the error "No table name specified".

•If no key field value is specified, LIMS will trigger the error "No key field value specified".

•If the specified table does not exist in the table master table, LIMS will trigger the error "The specified table does not exist".

•LIMS retrieves the record with the specified key field value from the specified table.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified record does not exist".

•If a file name is specified, LIMS will create a new file with that name.

•LIMS searches all tables for which the specified table is a linked table, with the following exceptions.

•LIMS does not search the result table for occurrences of a given analysis name.

•LIMS does not search any table whose name ends with the characters "_LOG".

•For each table searched, LIMS performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves all records in which the specified key field value is used, up to the maximum number of records specified.

•If a file name is specified, LIMS will append all references to that file.

•If an array name is specified, LIMS will create a new LIMS Basic array with that name and store all references in that array.  The array is a two-dimensional array in which the second dimension has the following elements.

•The first element is the name of the linked table.

•The second element is the name of the linked field.

•The third element is the name of the first key field in the linked table.

•The fourth element is the value of the first key field in the linked table.

•If the table has multiple key fields, items c and d repeat for each additional key field.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example searches the database for all records in which milligrams is used as the unit of measure.  The results are provided in an array named "MyLinks" and are not written to any file.  Up to the default number (1,000) references are retrieved from each linked table.

status = WhereUsed("UNITS", "MG", "", "MyLinks")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

WorkstationType

Purpose:

The WorkstationType() function returns the Workstation Type applicable to the LabWare client instance.

Syntax:

status = WorkstationType()

Comments:

If the SERVER.SYS and CLIENT.SYS files are present in the LabWare start in directory, the function will return the value DEFAULT because these files take precedence over the WORKSTATION.SYS file.

Example:

The following example will return the Workstation Type applicable to the Labware client instance

Status = WorkstationType()

Returns:

The Workstation Type specified in the WORKSTATION.SYS file in the LabWare start in directory.

 

 

 

28.3.25  LIMS Functions
LIMS functions are used to perform LIMS operations (such as logging a sample) using LIMS Basic commands.  Many of these commands involve making changes to the data in the database.  These changes are saved when a commit is made to the database (a commit can be thought of as another term for any action that saves information to the database). 

However, most functions will not commit the changes immediately.  This is intentional since in many cases the changes should not be saved until the final action is ready to commit.  For example, a result calculation in a test may have code that will change values in the database but if the result entry process is halted by pressing the Quit button, these changes should not be made to the database.  The changes will be committed if the Done button is pressed.  Similarly test event triggers take effect when the OK button is pressed in the Test Editor and so on.  A User Program typically includes a commit at the end of the code being run because there is no external action performed at the end which will commit, like when pressing the Done button in result entry.  The changes generated in a subroutine will be committed whenever the code that is calling the subroutine is committed.  Consequently, if the subroutine code is tested by using the Test button in a formula dialog, no changes will be committed to the database when the subroutine is finished because it is not run as part of another action.

Some functions do commit changes right away.  When a function is called that does perform a commit to the database, then all changes that have been made up to that point in the code will be committed.   The user should be aware that these changes cannot be undone by any subsequent abort command.  In the case where changes must be committed, a commit can be forced by using a function at the end of the code, such as UpdateTableFlds (which does include a commit), to update the value in an unused field in the database.  When the update is performed all changes will be committed to the database.  However, it should rarely be necessary to force a commit.
