Abort    

Purpose:

The Abort function terminates a LIMS Basic program.

Syntax:

Status = Abort()

Comments:

If the Abort function is encountered and the LIMS Basic code is in the foreground, the code will stop and no pending commits will be made to the database.  If the code is run in a background process (such as sample login) it doesn't stop the background process or the code that is running but it does stop pending commits.

CAUTION: Use of the Abort function may cause loss of context of LIMS Basic variables and database objects in memory.  Consequently, use of the Abort function is generally not recommended except when used at the end of code execution, since context and commit behavior is unpredictable following the function call.

Example:

Status = Abort()

Returns:

Status = True if code is stopped, = false if code is not stopped

 

AddAdHocReportedResult

Purpose:

The AddAdHocReportedResult function adds an ad hoc reported result for the specified result.

Syntax:

status = AddAdHocReportedResult(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber, resultConversion, resultName, reportedName, units, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

sampleNumber - the sample number of the result

testNumber– the test number of the result

resultNumber– the result number of the result

resultConversion– the Result Conversion record associated with the new ad hoc reported result

resultName– the result name that is being converted (optional, if not specified the Result Name from the Result Conversion record is used)

reportedName– the reported name for the new reported result (optional, if not specified, the Result Name from the Result Conversion record is used)

units– the units for the new reported result (optional, if not specified the Units from the Result Conversion record are used)

fieldsArray– a one dimensional array of the fields in the REPORTED_RESULT table that will be added for the new entry (optional)

valuesArray– a one dimensional array of the values for the fields that will be added for the new entry (optional)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

1.  The system verifies the sampleNumber is valid.  If the sample is not found, the following error is returned: "Invalid sample specified: sampleNumber".

2.  The system verifies the testNumber is valid.  If the test is not found, the following error is returned: "Invalid test specified: testNumber".

3.  The system verifies the resultNumber is valid.  If the test is not found, the following error is returned: "Invalid result specified: resultNumber".

4.  The system compares the fieldsArray to the valuesArray.  If they are not the same size, the following error is returned: "Fields and values arrays are not the same size ".

5.  The system evaluates the resultConversion.  If not found, the following error is returned: "Invalid result conversion specified."

6.  The system adds the reported result.

NOTE:  This function does NOT save changes to the database.


Example:

sampleNumber = 247

testNumber = 360

resultNumber = 453

resultConversion = "LEN_CM_M"

fieldsArray[1] = "CONVERSION_TYPE"

valuesArray[1] = "SI"

status = AddAdHocReportedResult(sampleNumber,testNumber , resultNumber, resultConversion, , , ,fieldsArray, valuesArray)

Save()

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

AddFlag    

Purpose:

The AddFlag function adds a Flag to an object.

Syntax:

status = AddFlag (objectType, objectId, templateName, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

objectType – the type of the object can be either Sample or Order.

objectId – the identification of the object (viz. Sample Number)

templateName – the name of the Flag Template

fieldsArray – the fields (if any) included in the Flag Template.

valuesArray – the values (if any) assigned to the fields in the Flag Template.

Comments:

A database commit is not part of this function.

Example:

The following example adds a flag to a "SAMPLE" with Sample Number 199

objectType = "SAMPLE"

objectId = 199

templateName = "FLAG_TEMP1"

fieldsArray[1,1] = "FLAG_COMMENT"

valuesArray[1,1] = "High Priority"

status = AddFlag(objectType, objectId, templateName, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

Returns:

 

status = true if the function is successful, false otherwise.

 

AddFlags

Purpose:

The AddFlags function adds a Flag to multiple objects.

Syntax:

status = AddFlags(objectType, objectIdArray, templateName, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

objectType – the type of the object, can be either Sample or Order.

objectIdArray – a one dimensional array containing the identification of the objects to be flagged.

templateName – the name of the Flag Template

fieldsArray – the fields (if any) included in the Flag Template.

valuesArray – the values (if any) to be assigned to the fields in the Flag Template.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•The system checks objectType.  If not specified, the system will trigger the error “Object type must be specified”.

•The system checks each object ID.  If not specified, the system will trigger the error "Object ID must be specified".  If invalid, the system will trigger the error “The specified object does not exist”.

•The system checks templateName.  If not specified, the system will trigger the error “Template name must be specified”.

•The system checks that fieldsArray and valuesArray are the same size. If not, the system will trigger the error “The fields and values array must be the same size'”.

•The system adds a flag to the specified objects.  If this fails, the system will trigger the error “Error creating flag”.

NOTE:  A database commit is not part of this function.


Example:

 

 

The following example adds a flag to a "SAMPLE" with Sample Number 199

objectType = "SAMPLE"

objectIdArray[1] = 199

objectIdArray[2] = 206

objectIdArray[3] = 274

templateName = "FLAG_TEMP1"

fieldsArray[1,1] = "FLAG_COMMENT"

valuesArray[1,1] = "High Priority"

status = AddFlags(objectType, objectIdArray, templateName, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

 

Returns:

 

status = true if the function is successful, false otherwise.

 

AddReportedReferenceRangeNamesToSample

Purpose:

The AddReportedReferenceRangeNamesToSample function adds the specified reference ranges to the sample and adds the corresponding reported results.  The sample must have Reference Range already applied to the sample.

Syntax:

status = AddReportedReferenceRangeNamesToSample(sampleNumber, reportedReferenceRangeNamesArray)

sampleNumber - the sample number that is to be updated

reportedReferenceRangeNamesArray – a one dimensional array of the fields in the Ref Range Names table that will be added 

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

1.  The system verifies the sampleNumber is valid.  If the sample is not found, the following error is returned: "The specified sample is not valid: sampleNumber ".

2.  The system verifies the reportedReferenceRangeNamesArray is valid.  If the array is empty, the following error is returned: "No reference range names were specified ".

3.  The system checks the reference ranges assigned to the sampleNumber.  If none are found, the following error is returned: "The sample does not have a reference range assigned ".

4.  The system checks the reference ranges in the reportedReferenceRangeNamesArray.  If a value is already assigned to the sample, the following error is returned: " The sample already has the reference name specified: rname" (where rname is the entry from the reportedReferenceRangeNamesArray).

5.  The system adds the reported result.

NOTE:  This function does NOT save changes to the database.


Example:

sampleNumber = 246

reportedReferenceRangeNamesArray[1] = "CUST_X"

reportedReferenceRangeNamesArray[2] = "GLUC_1"

status = AddReportedReferenceRangeNamesToSample(sampleNumber, reportedReferenceRangeNamesArray)

save()

 

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

AdHocCharge    

Purpose:

The AdHocCharge function adds an ad hoc charge to the charges table.

Syntax:

chargeEntryNum = AdHocCharge(chargeCode, project, chargeType, chargeAmount)

chargeCode – the charge code for the ad hoc charge

project – the project to be charged

chargeType – the charge type for the ad hoc charge

chargeAmount – the amount to be charged

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the charge code is blank or missing, LIMS will trigger the error "No charge code specified".

•LIMS creates a new charge entry for the specified charge code, project, charge type, and charge amount.

•LIMS assigns as primary key the next value from the increment for table "CHARGES" and field "CHARGE_ENTRY".

•LIMS saves the entry to the database using audit type "TableUpdate".

•If an error occurs, LIMS will trigger the error "Error making charge".

 NOTE: This function saves changes to the database


Example:

The following example creates an ad hoc charge of $8.75 for project "MY_PROJ" with code "TAXABLE" and type "SUPPLIES".

chargeEntryNum = AdHocCharge("TAXABLE", "MY_PROJ", "SUPPLIES", 8.75)

Returns:

The function returns the assigned charge entry number if it completes successfully.  It returns the value zero if an error is triggered.

 

Alert    

Purpose:

The Alert function sends an alert to a LIMS user.

Syntax:

status = Alert(createdBy, userName, dueDateTime, message, responseRequired, readReceipt, acknowledgeReceipt)

createdBy - is the LIMS ID of the user sending the alert.

userName - is the LIMS ID of the user receiving the alert.

dueDateTime - is the date and time for the alert to be delivered.

message - is the text message to be included in the alert.

responseRequired -  Boolean

readReceipt – Boolean

acknowledgeReceipt - Boolean.

Comments:

 NOTE: A database commit is part of this function.


 Example:

createdBy =  "SUSAN"

userName = "WAYNE"

dueDateTime = DateTime()

message = "Here is the report for Project T00102"

responseRequired = TRUE

readReceipt = TRUE

acknowledgeReceipt = TRUE

status = Alert(createdBy, userName, dueDateTime, message,responseRequired, readReceipt, acknowledgeReceipt)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

AppendToContactNote  

Purpose:

The AppendToContactNote function appends text to an existing note.

Syntax:

status = AppendToContactNote(contactNumber, noteText)

contactNumber – the contact number associated with the note

noteText – The text to be appended to note

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS checks the size of the noteText argument.  If null, LIMS will trigger the error "No note text specified".

•LIMS checks the contact related the contactNumber argument.  If the contact is not found, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified contact is not valid: contactNumber".

•LIMS appends the specified text to the note.

This function does NOT save changes to the database.

Example:

 

 

contactNumber = 48

noteText = "This is the text for my note!"

status = AppendToContactNote(contactNumber, noteText)

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

ApproveObject    

Purpose:

The ApproveObject function

Syntax:

status = ApproveObject(tableName, keyFieldValue, primaryFlag, approvalComment)

tableName – string defining the table of the target object.

KeyFieldValue – string defining the key value for the target object.

primaryFlag –  Boolean; T, true, sets the approval as the primary approver; F, false, sets the approver as an alternate approver.  This is a mandatory field, there is no default value.  Text starting with t or y are interpreted as T.

approvalComment – string posted

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•LIMS verifies that a tableName has been specified.  If not, LIMS returns the last error: "No table specified."

•LIMS verifies that a keyFieldValue has been specified.  If not, LIMS returns the last error: "No key field value specified."

•LIMS verifies that the primaryFlag has been specified.  If not, LIMS return the last error: "No primary flag specified."

•LIMS retrieves the specified object.  If this fails, LIMS returns the last error: "Invalid object specified."

•LIMS approves the record.

NOTE: This function does perform a commit to the database.


 Example:

The following example approves the product: DEMO_PROD as an alternate approver.

status = ApproveObject("PRODUCT", "DEMO_PROD", "F", "Demonstration of LIMS Basic."

Returns:

status = true if the function is successful, otherwise false.

 

ApproveObjectStep

Purpose:

The ApproveObjectStep function will approve the current approval step for a specified object.  The logged in user is prompted to approve the record and enter an approval reason.

Syntax:

status = ApproveObjectStep(tableName, keyFieldValue, version, approvalComment)

tableName – The name of the table

keyFieldValue – The key field value for the record

version – If applicable, this is an integer corresponding to the version of the record. This parameter is optional. If not specified it defaults to the highest version, if the record has versioning.

approvalComment –  If specified, this string  parameter contains the text that will be defaulted into the Approval dialog. This parameter is optional.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•The system checks the tableName.  If not specified, the system will trigger the error “No table specified”.

•The system checks the keyFieldValue.  If not specified, the system will trigger the error “No key field value specified”.

•The system retrieves the object information for the specified table and key.  If this fails, the system will trigger the error “Invalid object specified”.

•The function first attempts to approve as primary, if that fails, attempts as alternate.

Example:

 

 

tableName = "PRODUCT"

keyFieldValue = "ACME"

version = 1

approvalComment = "First version"

status = ApproveObjectStep(tableName, keyFieldValue, version, approvalComment)

Returns:

 

The function returns true if successful. If it is not successful, it will answer false and lastError will be set with an appropriate error message.

 

AssignSampleSpecListEntry  

Purpose:

The AssignSampleSpecListEntry function adds a specification list to the specified sample.

Syntax:

status = AssignSampleSpecListEntry(sample, specList, entryCodesArray, updateAliquots)

sample – the sample number to which the spec list will be applied.

specList - the specification list to be applied.

entryCodesArray – a one dimensional array containing the SEQ_NUM corresponding to the Spec List Entries to be assigned (if not specified, no entries for the Spec List are assigned to the sample).

updateAliquots – a Boolean flag indicating if aliquots of the sample are to be updated.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

7.  The system checks the sample.  If this fails, the system will trigger the error “Sample does not exist: sample”.

8.  The system checks the specList.  If it is specified but it is invalid, the system will trigger the error “Invalid SpecList name: specList”. If not specified, the system checks the spec list on the sample.  If no spec list is found, the system will trigger the error “'No SpecList name specified for the sample”.   If the sample does have a Spec List, the system checks that the spec list is valid.  If this fails, the system will trigger the error “Invalid SpecList”.

9.  The system assigns the spec list to the sample then updates the sample and it aliquots, if specified. If this fails, the system will trigger the error “Error committing sample record”.

This function does save changes to the database.

Example:

 

 

Sample = 999

specList = "SPL_123"

entryCodesArray[1] = 25

updateAliquots = "T"

status = AssignSampleSpecListEntry(sample, specList, entryCodesArray, updateAliquots)

 

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

AuthorizeAllResultsForTest

Purpose:

Authorizes all the results for a specified test.

Syntax:

status = AuthorizeAllResultsForTest(sampleId,testNumber)

sampleId – The sample number

testNumber – The Test number to authorize results on.  Must be a valid test number for the specified sample.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•The system retrieves the specified sample.  If this fails, the system will trigger the error "Sample does not exist: sampleId ".

•The system retrieves the specified test.  If this fails, the system will trigger the error "Test does not exist: testNumber ".

•The system determines if the result can be authorized by the current user.  If present, the CONTINUE_AUTHORIZE status rule is processed.  If the result cannot be authorized, the system will trigger an error using the status rule message as the error message.

•The system authorizes the results on test.

•the system sets the audit type at the sample level to "Authorize".

NOTE:  A database commit is not part of this function.


Example:

 

sampleId = 65

testNumber = 120

status = AuthorizeAllResultsForTest(sampleId,testNumber)

Returns:

 

status = true if the function is successful, false otherwise.

 

BackgroundSubroutine    

Purpose:

The BackgroundSubroutine function requests that a subroutine be executed on a specified client computer.

Syntax:

status = BackgroundSubroutine(subName, variableNames, variableValues, clientName)

subName – the name of the subroutine to be executed

variableNames – an array of variable names to be placed in context for the subroutine

variableValues – an array of values to be assigned to the corresponding variable names

clientName – the name of the client computer on which the subroutine is to be executed

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no subroutine name is specified, LIMS will trigger the error "No subroutine name specified".

•If no client name is specified, LIMS will trigger the error "No client name specified".

•If the variableNames array and the variableValues array do not have same number of elements, LIMS will trigger the error "Variable names and values must be the same size".

•LIMS creates a new server action for the specified subroutine and client and saves it in the SERVER_ACTION table.

 NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.

 

LIMS does not validate the client name.  If an invalid client name is specified, the subroutine will not execute.

In order for the subroutine to execute, the specified client computer must be configured as a background server.

LIMS does not validate the subroutine name.  If an invalid subroutine name is specified, the client computer will trigger the error "Invalid subroutine" when it attempts execution of the server action.

Arrays can be added to the context of the subroutine as follows.  Specify the name of the array as a single variable name in the variableNames array.  Specify the list of values as a comma-delimited string enclosed in brackets.

Example:

The following example requests that the subroutine named "PRINTLIST" be executed on the client named "PC104".  The requested context variables are the number 2 and an array with elements "red", "green", and "blue".

myNames[1] = "numcopies"
myNames[2] = "colors"
myValues[1] = 2
myValues[2] = "[red,green,blue]"
status = BackgroundSubroutine("PRINTLIST", myNames, myValues, "PC104")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

Syntax:

Beep()

Comments:

 

Example:

Beep()

Returns:

 

 

CalculateReportedResults

Purpose:

The CalculateReportedResults function calculates the reported results for the specified result.

Syntax:

status = CalculateReportedResults(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber)

sampleNumber - the sample number of the result that is to be updated

testNumber– the test number of the result that is to be updated (optional)

resultNumber– the result number of the result that is to be updated (optional)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

1.  The system verifies the sampleNumber is valid.  If the sample is not found, the following error is returned: "Sample does not exist: sampleNumber ".

2.  The system checks the status of sampleNumber.  If the sample status is authorized, rejected or canceled, the following error is returned: " Sample is reviewed or canceled: sampleNumber ".

3.  The system verifies the testNumber is valid.  If the test is not found, the following error is returned: "Test does not exist: testNumber ".

4.  The system verifies the resultNumber is valid.  If the test is not found, the following error is returned: "Result does not exist: resultNumber ".

5.  The system calculates the reported result(s).

NOTE:  This function does NOT save changes to the database.


Example:

sampleNumber = 233

testNumber = 346

resultNumber = 423

status = CalculateReportedResults(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber)

save()

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

 

CalculateReviewedReportedResults

Purpose:

The CalculateReviewedReportedResults function updates the entries in the REPORTED_RESULT table for the specified sample.

Syntax:

status = CalculateReviewedReportedResults(sampleNumber, reactivateFlag, suppressTriggersFlag, auditReason)

sampleNumber - the sample number of the result that is to be updated. This parameter is required.

reactivateFlag – a Boolean flag that determines if the sample is to be reactivated. If not specified, the default value is False.

suppressTriggersFlag – a Boolean that will suppress event triggers. If not specified, the default value is False.  This flag is only applicable if an external sll is loaded and has no impact on behavior without this sll.

auditReason – the audit reason to be applied to the audit transaction associated with the calculation.  This parameter is required.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

1.  The system checks the size of the auditReason.  If not specified, the following error is returned: "An audit reason must be specified ".

2.  The system verifies the sampleNumber is valid.  If the sample is not found, the following error is returned: "Sample does not exist: sampleNumber ".

3.  The system checks the reviewed tests on sampleNumber.  If there are no reviewed tests, the following error is returned: "Sample does not have any reviewed tests: sampleNumber ".

4.  The system reactivates the sample and tests then posts the reported result calculation.

NOTE:  This function does save changes to the database.


Example:

sampleNumber = 240

reactivateFlag = "T"

suppressTriggersFlag = "T"

auditReason = "Entry Error"

status = CalculateReviewedReportedResults(sampleNumber, reactivateFlag, suppressTriggersFlag, auditReason)

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

CanApproveObject

Purpose:

Checks if the current logged in user can approve a specified record.

Syntax:

status = CanApproveObject(tableName, keyFieldValue, primaryFlag)

tableName – The name of the table

keyFieldValue – The key field value for the record

primaryFlag – Boolean.  If true, the function will return true if the current user can approve as the primary approver.  If false, does not check if must be primary approver.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•The system checks the tableName.  If not specified, the system will trigger the error “No table specified”.

•The system checks the keyFieldValue.  If not specified, the system will trigger the error “No key field value specified”.

•The system checks the primaryFlag.  If not specified, the system will trigger the error “No primary flag specified”.

•The system retrieves the object information for the specified table and key.  If this fails, the system will trigger the error “Invalid object specified”.

•The system checks the approval records. If no record found, the system will trigger the error “There is no approval pending”.

Example:

 

 

tableName = "SAMPLE"

keyFieldValue = 68

primaryFlag = "T"

status = CanApproveObject(tableName, keyFieldValue, primaryFlag)

Returns:

 

Returns true if the current logged in user can approve the object as primary approver (based on primaryFlag), false if approval is not allowed or an error is triggered. 

 

ClearInterpreterObjects    

Purpose:

The ClearInterpreterObjects function clears the LIMS Basic interpreter’s collection of database objects that are currently in context.

CAUTION:  This function can have a negative impact on code execution if used improperly and should be used with caution. LabWare recommends that you review the design of your LIMS implementation with a LabWare LIMS configuration consultant before you use this function in a production system.


Syntax:

status = ClearInterpreterObjects(tableName)  'Use with caution

tableName – Name of the target table to be cleared.  (Optional, null clears all tables)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

LIMS clears all pointers to database objects from the LIMS Basic interpreter for the designated table.

 NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.  Pending changes made to database objects that were retrieved through LIMS Basic will be lost.  Pending changes made to database objects that were retrieved through the LIMS user interface will still be pending and may possibly be committed to the database through the LIMS user interface even though the corresponding database objects are no longer accessible through LIMS Basic.


Example:

The following example clears the interpreter objects.

status = ClearInterpreterObjects()

Returns:

The function returns the value true.

ClipboardGet

Syntax:

string = ClipboardGet()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the content from the Windows clipboard as a string.  If the content cannot be rendered as a string, an empty string is retrieved instead.

•LIMS returns the retrieved content.

This function does not save changes to the database.

Example:

The following example displays the contents of the clipboard in a message box.

string = ClipboardGet()

Returns:

  NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.

Returns:

CopyTableRecord     

Purpose:

The CopyTableRecord function copies a record in a Table Manager table

Syntax:

status = CopyTableRecord(table, keyFieldArray, keyValueArray, newName, auditReason)

table – the name of the Table Manager table in which a record is to be copied

keyFieldArray – an array of field names whose values uniquely identify a single record

keyValueArray – an array of field values that uniquely identify a single record

newName – the name (primary key value) to be assigned to the new record

auditReason – the audit reason to be assigned to the copied record

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the specified table is not a Table Manager table, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified table is not valid".

•If the keyFieldArray has no elements or is not specified, LIMS will trigger the error "At least one key field must be specified".

•If the keyFieldArray and the keyValueArray do not have the same size, LIMS will trigger the error "Key fields and values must be the same size".

•If the table is versioned, and if no version number is specified, LIMS uses the latest version currently in use for the specified primary key value and adds it to the list of field value criteria. (See second note below.)

•LIMS retrieves the record for the specified criteria.  If no record is found, LIMS will trigger the error "No record found with specified key values".  If more than one record is found, LIMS will trigger the error "More than one record was found using the specified key information".

•If the value of newName is equal to the primary key value of the record being copied, and if the copied record is versioned, LIMS will copy the record as the next version.

•Otherwise (i.e., if the value of newName is different or the table is not versioned) LIMS will trigger the error "Record already exists with new name" if the table already contains a record with the value of newName.  If no such record exists, LIMS will copy the record.  If it is a versioned record, LIMS will assign it the version number 1.

•If no audit reason is specified, LIMS will assign the NLS tranlation of the key "AuditorNoReasonSpecified".  If this key is not present, LIMS will assign the value "No reason specified".  LIMS sets the audit type of the new record to "TableUpdate", and adds the record to its context.

 NOTE: If the specified table is not a versioned table, the keyFieldArray may contain field names other than the primary key field of the table, as long as the collection of field values uniquely identify a single record.

NOTE: If the specified table is a versioned table, the keyFieldArray must contain the name of the primary key field only, and optionally the version field.  If no version field is specified, LIMS will use the latest version.  In order to copy the specified record to the next version of the same primary key, the value of newName must be equal to the primary key value being queried.  Otherwise, the value of newName must not yet exist in the table.  In other words, it is not possible to copy one record as a new version of a different record.  The behavior of the function is unspecified if the keyFieldArray contains fields other than the primary key field and the version field.

NOTE:This function does not save changes to the database


Example:

The following example copies the analysis that has name "LOD" and version 2.  If the highest version is currently 5, the new record will be saved as version 6.  The audit reason is unspecified.

myKeys[1] = "NAME"
myKeys[2] = "VERSION"
myValues[1] = "LOD"
myValues[2] = 2
status = CopyTableRecord("ANALYSIS", myKeys, myValues, "LOD", "")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

CreateContact   

Purpose:

The CreateContact function creates a new contact entry.

Syntax:

contactNumber = CreateContact(fieldsArray, valuesArray)

fieldsArray – an array containing field names

valuesArray – an array containing values corresponding to the fields specified in the fieldsArray

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS checks the sizes of the fieldsArray and valuesArray.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Field and value array size are not the same".

•LIMS checks the fields the fieldsArray. If an invalid field is found, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid field for table:".

•LIMS checks the records associated with the valuesArray. If an invalid record is found, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid value for record:".

•.LIMS creates a new contact and returns the contact number.

  NOTE: This function does save changes to the database.


Example:

fieldsArray[1] = "PATIENT"

fieldsArray[2] = "PRACTITIONER"

fieldsArray[3] = "CONTACT_NAME"

fieldsArray[4] = "REASON"

valuesArray[1] = "20-101-31-0402"

valuesArray[2] = "PAINE"

valuesArray[3] = "JBROWN"

valuesArray[4] = "INCOMP"

contactNumber = CreateContact(fieldsArray, valuesArray)

Returns: 

The function returns the contact number if it completes successfully.  It returns the value ERROR if an error is triggered.

 

CreateMessage    

Purpose:

The CreateMessage function creates a new LIMS Message.

Syntax:

status = CreateMessage(allUsers, userName, userGroup, lab, role, highPriority, objectClass, objectID, displayForMinutes, subject, messageText)

allUsers – (mandatory) defines whether the created  message is to be sent to all users. Valid values are "T" or "F".

userName – defines the user name to which the message is to be sent.

userGroup – defines the user group to which the message is to be sent.

lab -  defines the lab to which the message is to be sent.

role – defines the role to which the message is to be sent.

highPriority – defines the priority for the message. Valid values are "T" or "F". The default value is "F".

objectClass – defines the LIMS object name.

objectID – defines the record for the LIMS object.

displayForMinutes – defines in minutes the time the message will be displayed. The default value is 24 hrs.

subject – defines the message subject.

messageText – defines the Message Text.

Comments:

If allUsers is set to "F" then  at least one of userName , userGroup, lab, role must be entered. If this value is not entered the following error message is displayed :"Must send the message to someone"

The seconds part of the DISPLAY_UNTIL field in the table LIMS_MESSAGE is set by this function.

NOTE: A database commit is part of this function.

Example:

The following example creates a LIMS Message which will be displayed to all users for 6 hours from the current date-time.

status = CreateMessage("T", , , , , , , , 240, "AD_HOC", "This is a Test")

Returns:

 

If successful returns a message "LIMS Messages  nn", where nn is the message Sequence number.

auditReason – The audit reason to be applied to the insert in the PERSON table

Comments:

 NOTE: A database commit is part of this function.

Example:

The following example creates an entry for "John Smith" in the Person table.

fieldsArray[1] = "FIRST_NAME"

fieldsArray[2] = "LAST_NAME"

valuesArray[1] = "John"

valuesArray[2] = "Smith"

personNumber = createPerson(fieldsArray, valuesArray, "Creating entry using LIMS Basic")

Returns:

 

If successful returns the PERSON_NUMBER for the new entry.

CurrentProcessName    

Purpose:

The CurrentProcessName function returns the name of the currently executing process.  LIMS has one foreground process and zero or more background processes.  The foreground process is usually named "USER I/F".  Note that the IsBackgroundProcess function should be used to determine whether the current process is a background process.

Syntax:

processName = CurrentProcessName()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS returns the name of the currently executing process.

 NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example sets the variable processName equal to the name of the currently executing process.

processName = CurrentProcessName()

Returns:

The function returns a string.

DisableDataBaseEvents   

Purpose:

This function disables the Database Events configured within LIMS during the current session.

Syntax:

status = DisableDataBaseEvents()

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS suppresses all Database Events for the current session.

Example:

 status = DisableDataBaseEvents()

Returns:

If successful, the function returns status = true.  If not, the function returns status = false.

 

EditFlag    

Purpose:

The EditFlag function edits and existing Flag on an object.

Syntax:

status = EditFlag(entryCode, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

EntryCode -  an Integer corresponding to the Entry Code of the entry to be modified from the FLAGS table

fieldsArray – the fields (if any) included in the Flag Template.

valuesArray – the values (if any) assigned to the fields in the Flag Template.

Comments:

NOTE:  A database commit is not part of this function.

Example:

The following example edits an exiting flag.

EntryCode =  100
fieldsArray[1,1] = "Flag Comment"
valuesArray[1,1] = "High Priority"

status = EditFlag(entryCode, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

Returns:

status = true if the function is successful, false otherwise.

 

EnableDataBaseEvents   

Purpose:

This function re-enables the Database Events configured within LIMS during the current session.

Syntax:

status = EnableDataBaseEvents()

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS re-enbles all Database Events for the current session.

Example:

 status = EnableDataBaseEvents()

Returns:

If successful, the function returns status = true.  If not, the function returns status = false.

 

EraseCache    

Purpose:

The EraseCache function erases the cache of the specified table or all tables.  Static tables are cached in order to improve performance.  When a cache is erased, LIMS will reload the table from the database the next time the table is accessed.

Syntax:

status = eraseCache(tableName)

tableName – the name of the table whose cache is to be erased

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the value of tableName is equal to "ALL", LIMS will erase the cache for all tables.

•Otherwise, LIMS will erase the cache of the specified table.

Example:

The following example erases the cache of the units table.

status = eraseCache("UNITS")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

Execute    

Purpose:

The Execute function executes a Windows command line from LIMS Basic.

Syntax:

status = Execute("commandLine", showFlag)

Comments:

showFlag = 0 will execute the command in the background, showFlag = 1 will execute the command in the foreground.

 NOTE:  Depending on the application, it may be necessary to include the path to the application.


Example:

status = Execute("pbrush c:\lw-lims\lw-lims.bmp", 1)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

ExecuteQueryTag    

Purpose:

The ExecuteQueryTag function executes a query tag and returns the results in an array. 

Syntax:

status = ExecuteQueryTag(qtName, "returnArrayName")

Comments:

The user may be prompted for input by the query tag.

TIP: If using this function to execute a prompt query tag like a date prompt, and the user hits the Cancel button, the function will return false.

If using this function to execute a query tag that is linked to a Subroutine Access Routine, the subroutine should return True or False in the return statement so that this value will be a status returned by the ExecuteQueryTag() function.

If using this function to execute a folder query tag, the returnArrayName is a two dimensional array. The following columns are returned in the array:

Column 1. Sample, Test or Result Numbers. Depending on the class of object. This element itself is also an array.

Column 2. The SQL generated by the query tag

Column 3. The class of the object (SAMPLE, TEST or RESULT)


Example:

status = ExecuteQueryTag("BATCH_STEP_LIST", "returnArray")

Returns:

returnArray[1,1] = ‘SECOND’

returnArray[2,1] = ‘FIRST’

 

GetConstant    

Purpose:

The GetConstant function is used to fetch the value of the LIMS Constant from the Database or the memory.  If there is a User Constant defined for the currently logged user or the User Name argument is specified then this function will retrieve the value from the User Constants table.

Syntax:

const = GetConstant(constantName, userName)

constantName – the name of the LIMS Constant.

userName– the name of the user for which the constant value is to be retrieved.

 NOTE:  Even if the username argument is not specified if there exists a User Constant for the currently logged user the GetConstant function will retrieve the value from the User Constant table not the value from the LIMS Constant table.

If no User Constant record exists for the specified user then the value from the LIMS Constant table will be returned

Comments:

If SetConstant is used to set a new value to a LIMS Constant with the Static flag set to true, when using GetConstant, the old value of the LIMS Constant will be retrieved unless the user logs out and back in again or the ReloadConstants function is used.

NOTE: If the LIMS constant that is being accessed is Static the system pulls the value from memory else the value will be pulled from the database.

NOTE: If the value of the constant is numeric and has more that 11 sig figs, only the first 11 will be accessed.  For example, If the value in the Constant Value field is 1234567890.1234567890, the GetConstant will only return 1234567890.1

Example:

The following example accesses a LIMS constant "TEST".

constantName = "TEST"

const = GetConstant(constantName)

Returns:

If successful returns the constant value else returns ERROR.

 

GetDescriptionForLinkedField   

Purpose:

The GetDescriptionForLinkedField function returns the description of the record linked to a field in the table.

Syntax:

desc = GetDescriptionForLinkedField(tableName, fieldName, fieldValue)

tableName – The name of the table containing the field specified in the fieldname argument

fieldname- The name of the field with the link to a table or list

fieldValue-  The value in the field (specified in the fieldname argument) that is to be checked against the linked record. 

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that the tableName is specified.  If the tableName is not specified, the LIMS returns the last error: "No table name specified."

•LIMS verifies that the fieldname is specified.  If the fieldname is not specified, the LIMS returns the last error: "No field name specified."

•The LIMS checks the fieldValue argument. If the linked field is linked to a list and the fieldValue argument is not valid, LIMS returns the error: "Invalid list reference: fieldValue".  If the linked field is linked to another table and the fieldValue argument is not valid, LIMS returns the error: "Invalid link reference: fieldValue".

•If a linked record is found the Description for that record is returned by this function..

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


 Example:

 'Example of field linked to a list

tableName = "SAMPLE"

fieldName = "SPEC_TYPE"

fieldValue = "NONE"

desc = GetDescriptionForLinkedField(tableName, fieldName, fieldValue)

 

 'Example of field linked to another table

tableName = "SAMPLE"

fieldName = "SAMPLING_POINT"

fieldValue = "NONE"

desc2 = GetDescriptionForLinkedField(tableName, fieldName, fieldValue)

Returns:

The function returns the description if located. It returns ERROR if an error is triggered.

 

GetGlobalVariable    

Purpose:

The GetGlobalVariable function returns the value of a global variable.

Syntax:

value = GetGlobalVariable(globalName)

Comments:

Global variables are created and set using the SetGlobalVariable function. 

Example:

 

globalName = "GlobalPW"

status  = SetGlobalVariable(globalName, "password")

value = GetGlobalVariable(globalName)  

Returns:

value = "password"

 

GetIncrement    

Purpose:

The GetIncrement function returns the next value of an entry in the Increments table.  This function may be used for generating unique numbers that can be used as primary key values.  Multiple unique numbers can be generated with one call by requesting more than one unique number to be allocated.

Syntax:

increment = GetIncrement(incrementName, numberToAllocate, useIncrementsCache)

incrementName – the name of the entry to be incremented

numberToAllocate – the integer amount by which the entry is to be incremented

useIncrementsCache – Boolean setting that determines if the system uses the cached increments value.  This setting defaults to True if not specified.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evalues the numberToAllocate input.  If the number is not an integer LIMS triggers an error and the Last Error is set to: "Number to allocate argument is not a valid integer" followed by the input.

•LIMS evaluate the numberToAllocate input.  If the number is not positive, LIMS triggers an error and the Last Error is set to: "Number to allocate argument is not a positive integer" followed by the input.

•LIMS retrieves the increment record that has the specified increment name and that has type "LIMS Basic".  If no such record exists, LIMS will insert a new record.

•If no value for numberToAllocate is specified, LIMS will increment the entry by the value 1.  Otherwise, LIMS will increment the entry by the value of numberToAllocate.  

•LIMS returns the previous value of the increment plus 1.

 NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.

NOTE: This function exists in the base V5 software without the numberToAllocate argument.


Example:

Suppose the increment named "EXAMPLE" does not yet exist.  The following lines of code will return the values 1, 6, 7, and 10 respectively.

increment1 = GetIncrement("EXAMPLE", 5)
increment2 = GetIncrement("EXAMPLE")
increment3 = GetIncrement("EXAMPLE", 3)
increment4 = GetIncrement("EXAMPLE", 1)

Returns:

The function returns an integer value. The numbers that have been allocated comprise a sequential range of integer numbers that begins with the returned value.  For example, if the function returns the value 12, and the request was to allocate three numbers, the allocated numbers will be 12, 13, and 14.

 

GetMemoryIncrement    

Purpose:

The GetMemoryIncrement function returns the next value of an increment in memory.

Syntax:

increment = GetMemoryIncrement(incrementName)

incrementName is user defined string identifying increment for subsequent use.

Comments:

The first call to GetMemoryIncrement with a new increment name returns a value = 1.  Each subsequent execution of GetMemoryIncrement with the same increment name during the current LIMS session will increase the returned value by 1.  This command is very similar to the GetIncrement command except that no records are written to the database.  The memory increment is cleared with the ClearMemoryIncrement function.

Example:

x = GetMemoryIncrement(myIncrement)   

y = GetMemoryIncremetn(myIncrement)   

Returns:

x = 1

y = 2

 

GetResultLimitDetails

Purpose:

This function retrieves Result, Specification and Test Limits for the specified result.  Test Limits correspond to the entries in the Minimum and Maximum fields in the RESULT table.  These limits usually come from the Analysis or Test List. Result Limits (also known as Ad Hoc spec limits) correspond to the entries in the Min Limit, Max Limit, Text Limit, Lo Control and Hi Control fields in the RESULT table.  The remaining Specification Limits come from the Product associated with the sample.

Syntax:

status = GetResultLimitDetails(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber, "detailsArrayName")

sampleNumber – sample number corresponding to the result to be checked.

testNumber – test number corresponding to the result to be checked.

resultNumber – result number corresponding to the result to be checked.

detailsArrayName – the name of the return array which will contain the detected limit values. If Test limits are present, they will be displayed in Row 1 of the array.  If Result Limits or Specification Limits are present, they are contained in the rows after the Spec header row. 

The array has 3 columns with the following structure:

Limit Type

Column 1

Column 2

Column 3

Test limits

“Test Limits”

RESULT.MINIMUM

RESULT.MAXIMUM

(Spec header)

“Spec Limits”

Product

version

Result Limits

(empty)

ResultSpec

(empty)

Specification Limits

Grade

ProductSpec

units

 

where Product is a string based on the Sample.Product field value.  If no product is defined this will be blank.  If no product is defined, version will be “Version 0”, otherwise it will be the version for the product from the Sample.Product_version field.

ResultSpec is a string that is built using the values in the RESULT.MIN_LIMIT, RESULT.MAX_LIMIT and RESULT.TEXT_LIMIT fields and is formatted as follows:

MIN_LIMIT and MAX_LIMIT have values

RESULT.MIN_LIMIT <= Result <= RESULT.MAX_LIMIT

Only MAX_LIMIT has value

Result <= RESULT.MAX_LIMIT

Only MIN_LIMIT has value

Result >= RESULT.MIN_LIMIT

 

TEXT_LIMIT

Same format as displayed for Text specs e.g.

Result is Like White

Refer to the section on Text specs for more information

 

If there are entries in the LO_CONTROL, and HI_CONTROL fields, there will be a separate row for the control limits.  This row is formatted as follows (where value is the value from the field):

LO_CONTROL and HI_CONTROL have values

LO_CONTROL= valuelo,HI_CONTROL  = valueHi

Only HI_CONTROL has value

HI_CONTROL  = value

Only LO_CONTROL has value

LO_CONTROL = value

 

 

Each Product Specification limit is entered as a separate row, where Grade is a string containing the grade associated with the Product Specification, ProductSpec is a string that is built using the limits in the Product Specification and units is a string containing the units defined in the Product Specification.  If no units are defined then a – is entered.

Since Product Specification can contain more than one type of limits there may be more than one row associated with a single specification.  Limits are returned for values in the MIN_VALUE, MAX_VALUE, LO_CONTROL_1 HI_CONTROL_1, LO_CONTROL_2, and HI_CONTROL_2 fields in the PRODUCT_SPEC  table, if they have values. The ProductSpec string is formatted as follows:

MIN_VALUE and MAX_VALUE have values

PRODUCT_SPEC .MIN_VALUE <= Result <= PRODUCT_SPEC.Max_VALUE

Only MAX_VALUE has value

Result <= PRODUCT_SPEC.MAX_VALUE

Only MIN_VALUE has value

Result >= PRODUCT_SPEC.MIN_VALUE

 

Only TEXT_VALUE has value

Standard format as displayed for Text specs e.g.

Result is Like White

Refer to the section on Text specs for more information

For control limits, the ProductSpec string is formatted differently.  The following shows the format for these limits, where value is the value from the Product Spec table (The table below uses the LO_CONTROL_1 and HI_CONTROL_1 fields, however, the format is the same for all control limits):

LO_CONTROL_1 and HI_CONTROL_1 have values

LO_CONTROL_1= valuelo,HI_CONTROL_1  = valueHi

Only HI_CONTROL_1 has value

HI_CONTROL_1  = value

Only LO_CONTROL_1 has value

LO_CONTROL_1 = value

 

 

 

 

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•The system retrieves the specified sample. If unsuccessful, the system returns the last error: “Specified sample does not exist: sampleNumber”.

•The system retrieves the specified test. If unsuccessful, the system returns the last error: “Specified test does not exist: testNumber”.

•The system retrieves the specified result. If unsuccessful, the system returns the last error: “Specified result does not exist: resultNumber”.

•The system checks detailsArrayName.  If not specified, the system returns the error:  “No array name specified”.

•The system evaluates the result limits and returns the limits in the detailsArrayName.

Example:

 

 

sampleNumber = 80

testNumber = 162

resultNumber = 382

status = GetResultLimitDetails(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber, "detailsArrayName")

 

In this example, the return array looks like the following:

Row 1

Test Limits , 15 , 100

Row 2

Spec Limits , ACME , Version 1

Row 3

, 20.0 <= Result <= 50.0 ,

Row 4

G-2 , Result > 22 , g

Row 5

G-3 , Result >= 23 , g


Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered

 

 

GetTableIncrement    

Purpose:

The GetTableIncrement function returns the next increment for the defined table and key.  If the table and key do not exist on the Increments table, the LIMS creates the increment and returns 1.

Syntax:

increment = GetTableIncrement(table, key)

table – defines the table variable within SmallTalk and recorded in the field INCREMENTS.TYPE.

key –defines the key variable within SmallTalk and recorded in the field INCREMENTS.NAME.

NOTE: For User Added Tables the table name should be TABLE and the key should be the name of the user added table.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that a valid table has been specified.  If an invalid table has not been input, LIMS returns the last error: table must be specified.

•LIMS verifies that a valid key has been specified.  If an invalid key has not been input, LIMS returns the last error: key must be specified.

•Function returns the next increment from the INCREMENTS table for the table and key specified.

 NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following code provides the increment for SAM_A Aliquots.

table = "SAM_A"
key = "Aliquots"
increment = GetTableIncrement(table, key)

Returns:

The function returns the next increment from the INCREMENTS table if it completes successfully. 

 

GUID    

Purpose:

The GUID function returns a new Global Unique Identifier (GUID) using the Microsoft Windows Object Linking and Embedding (OLE) API function named CoCreateGuid.

Syntax:

guidString = GUID()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS asks the operating system for a new GUID and returns the created identifier as a string.

Example:

The following example returns a new identifier.

guidString = GUID()

The following is an example of a GUID value.

{68F112A0-62F1-46F0-A59E-5E25D25E21B1}

Returns:

The function returns a string value.

 

HasChanges    

Purpose:

The HasChanges function checks if the specified record has changes that have not yet been saved in the database.  The function is intended is for use with the objects in LIMS Basic context only.  If used with Automation Scripts it may not recognize changes made in field objects because an interface does not always keep the object up to date with the current state of every field on the screen.

Syntax:

status = HasChanges(tableName, keyFieldsArray, keyValuesArray, checkChildrenFlag)

tableName – the name of the table that contains the record to be checked

keyFieldsArray – an array that contains the fields that uniquely identify the record

keyValuesArray – an array that contains the values of the specified key fields

checkChildrenFlag – the value true if LIMS should not only check the record itself but also all of its dependent records.  The default value is false.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the keyFieldsArray and keyValuesArray are not the same size, LIMS will trigger the error "Key field names and values arrays must be the same size".

•If LIMS has no records in context at all for the specified table, LIMS will trigger the error "There are no records in context for the specified table".

•If the specified record is not in context, LIMS will trigger the error "There is no record in context matching the specified key criteria".

•If the value of checkChildren is true, LIMS will check the record and all of its children for changes.  Otherwise, LIMS will check the record itself only.

•If changes are found, LIMS will return the value true.  Otherwise, it will return the value false.

NOTE:This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example returns true if the analysis named "LOD" has changes.  The components of the analysis are not checked.

myFields[1] = "NAME"
myValues[1] = "LOD"
status = HasChanges("ANALYSIS", myFields, myValues, false)

Returns:

The function returns the value true or false if it completes successfully.  It returns the value "ERROR" if an error is triggered.

 

InputsToCalcsOnATest

Purpose:

This function checks whether a test is an input to another test calculation.

NOTE:  This function does not check if the test is an input to a Batch Calculation.


Syntax:

status = InputsToCalcsOnATest(sampleNumber, testNumber, "inputTestArrayName")

sampleNumber – the sample number of the test to be checked

testNumber – the test number of the test to be checked

inputTestArrayName – the name of the return array which will contain the test numbers (and their status) which are dependent on the test that is checked

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•The system retrieves the specified sample. If unsuccessful, the system returns the last error: “Specified sample does not exist: sampleNumber”.

•The system retrieves the specified test. If unsuccessful, the system returns the last error: “Specified test does not exist: testNumber”.

•The system processes the test and populates the return array.

Example:

 

 

The follow example checks the test number 164 to see if it is used as the input to calculations on another test.

sampleNumber = 81

testNumber = 164

status = InputsToCalcsOnATest(sampleNumber, testNumber, "inputTestArrayName")

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if tests are found.  It returns the value false if no tests are found or if an error is triggered

 

InsertTable     

Purpose:

The InsertTable function inserts a record into the database.

Syntax:

status = InsertTable(table,keyFields,keyValues,fields,values,auditReason, auditFlag, commitFlag)

table – (Required) the name of the table into which a record is to be inserted.

keyFields – (Required) an array in which the first element is the name of the primary key field of the record to be created.

keyValues – (Required) an array in which the first element is the primary key value of the new record.

fields – (Optional) an array of the field names to be assigned.

values – (Optional) an array of the corresponding field values to be assigned.

auditReason – (Optional) the audit reason to be saved in the audit trail when the record is inserted into the database.

auditFlag – (Optional) a Boolean flag that determines if the insert will be audited.  If false, audit entries are not created. If not specified the default value is true.

commitFlag – (Optional) Boolean that determines if the record is committed immediately or is posted to context awaiting the next save.  The default value is true.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the fields array and values array are not the same size, the system will trigger the error "Number of fields does not equal number of field values.".

•If the specified table has not been defined in the table master table, the system will trigger the error "Table is not defined".

•If a record already exists for the primary key value, the system will trigger the error "Record already exists for the specified keys".

•If the fields array does not include the GROUP_NAME field, the system will assign the value "DEFAULT" to GROUP_NAME.

•If the table is versioned, the system will assign the value 1 to the VERSION, if not specified, unless the new record is a child record.  In that case, the version is taken from the parent.

•If any of the elements in the fields array represent a field name that does not exist in the table, the system will trigger the error "Invalid field for table".

•If any of the elements in the fields array represent a link field (foreign key), and if the corresponding element in the values array represent a linked record that does not exist, the system will trigger the error "Invalid value for record".

•If any of the elements in the fields array represent a date field, and if the corresponding element in the values array cannot be converted to a date, the system will assign a null value instead.

•If any of the elements in the fields array represent an interval field, and if the corresponding element in the values array cannot be converted to an interval, the system will not assign that value.

•If any of the elements in the fields array represent an external link field and the Static data directory defined in the corresponding element in the values array does not exist, LabWare will trigger the following error: " Error copying static data to server".

•The system saves the record in the database using the specified audit reason and audit type "TableUpdate".  If no audit reason is specified, the system will use the translated value of the NLS key "AuditorNoReasonSpecified".  If this key does not exist, the system will use the value "No reason specified" as the reason.

 NOTE: This function saves changes to the database based on the setting of the commitFlag.


Example:

The following example inserts a customer named "ACME" with state "DE" and zip "19806".
keyfld[1] = "NAME"
keyval[1] = "ACME"
fldsArray[1] = "STATE"
fldsArray[2] = "ZIP"
valsArray[1] = "DE"
valsArray[2] = "19806"
status = InsertTable("CUSTOMER",keyfld,keyval,fldsArray, valsArray, "New Customer", "T","T")

Returns:

The function returns true if successful. If it is not successful, it will answer false and lastError will be set with an appropriate error message.

 

IsBackgroundProcess    

Purpose:

The IsBackgroundProcess function returns the value True if the current process is executing as a background process.

Syntax:

status = IsBackgroundProcess()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

If the current process is a user interface (foreground) process, LIMS will return the value false.

Otherwise, LIMS will return the value true.

Example:

The following example returns true if the current process is a background process.

status = IsBackgroundProcess()

Returns:

The function returns the value True if the current process is a background process, and the value False otherwise.

 

IsVariableDefined

Purpose:

This function checks if the specified variable is defined in the code prior to the IsVariableDefined function call.  Global variables are not considered variables in the context of this function.

Syntax:

status = IsVariableDefined("varName")

varName – the name of the variable to be checked

Comments:

The system checks the variable name varName.  If the variable has been defined using a DECLARE statement or corresponds to an array name, the function will return true. If the variable has been initialized to a value (other than the default value corresponding to the variable name) prior to the IsVariableDefined function, the function will return true.

Example:

 

expectFalse = IsVariableDefined("myVar")

myVar = 123

expectTrue = IsVariableDefined("myVar")

 

Returns: 

The function returns the value true if the variable is defined.  Otherwise, it returns the value false.

 

ListEntryDescription    

Purpose:

The ListEntryDescription function returns the description of the specified entry on the specified list.

Syntax:

value = ListEntryDescription(listName,entryName)

listName – a string parameter that specifies the name of the list the entry resides.

entryName – a string parameter that specified the entry to retrieve the description from.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•The LIMS verifies that the listName is specified.  If the listName is not specified, the LIMS returns the last error: "List name must be specified."

•The LIMS verifies that the entryName is specified.  If the entryName is not specified, LIMS returns the error: "Entry name must be specified."

•When a listName and entryName is specified, the LIMS retrieves the description.

•If the list and/or the entry name is not found the LIMS returns a null value.

•If the list and entry name are found, the LIMS returns the description.

 NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code returns the description of the entry: P on the list: AP_STATUS.

listName = "AP_STATUS"
entryName = "P"
value = ListEntryDescription(listName,entryName)

Returns:

The function returns the description if located.

 

ListEntryDescriptionsArray   

Purpose:

The ListEntryDescriptionsArray function returns an array containing the descriptions of the entries specified in the input array for the specified list.

Syntax:

status = ListEntryDescriptionsArray(listName, entryNamesArray, "entryDescriptions")

listName – a string parameter that specifies the name of the list the entry resides.

entryNamesArray – a one dimensional array containing the names of the entries

entryDescriptions – the name of a one dimensional array containing the names of the specified entries

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•The LIMS verifies that the listName is specified.  If the listName is not specified, the LIMS returns the last error: "List name must be specified."

•The LIMS checks the entries in the entryNamesArray.  If there is a null entry in the entryNamesArray, LIMS returns the error: "Entry names must be specified."

•The LIMS checks the size of the entryDescriptions argument. If the entryDescriptions argument is not specified, LIMS returns the error: "Output array name must be specified."

•The LIMS verifies that the listName is valid.  If the listName is not a valid list, the LIMS returns the last error: "Invalid list specified: listName."

•The LIMS checks the entries in the entryNamesArray.  If there is an invalid entry in the entryNamesArray, LIMS returns the error: "Invalid list entry specified:" followed by the name of the invalid entry.

•When a listName and entryNamesArray are valid, the LIMS retrieves the descriptions.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


 Example:

The following code returns the description of the entries: INTERVAL and LIST in the list: ADHOC_TYPE.

listName = "ADHOC_TYPE"
entryNamesArray[1] = "INTERVAL"
entryNamesArray[2] = "LIST"
DescriptionsArrayName = "entryDescriptions"
status = ListEntryDescriptionsArray(listName, entryNamesArray, DescriptionsArrayName)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

ListEntryDescriptionsCSVString   

Purpose:

The ListEntryDescriptionsCSVString function returns the description of the specified entries on the specified list.

Syntax:

csvString = ListEntryDescriptionsCSVString(listName, entryNamesCSVString, delimiter)

listName – a string parameter that specifies the name of the list the entry resides.

entryNamesCSVString – a string parameter that specified the entries in the specified list to retrieve the descriptions from the list entries as a CSV string.  Entries are separated by the delimiter.

delimiter – a string parameter contacting the delimiter used for separating list entries for both the input and output CSV strings.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•The LIMS verifies that the listName is specified.  If the listName is not specified, the LIMS returns the last error: "List name must be specified."

•The LIMS checks the entryNamesCSVString.  If the entryNamesCSVString is null, LIMS returns the error: "Entry names must be specified."

•The LIMS verifies that the listName is valid.  If the listName is not a valid list, the LIMS returns the last error: "Invalid list specified: listName."

•The LIMS checks the entries in the entryNamesCSVString.  If there is an invalid entry in the entryNamesCSVString, LIMS returns the error: "Invalid list entry specified:" followed by the name of the invalid entry.

•When a listName and entryNamesCSVString are valid, the LIMS retrieves the descriptions.

 NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code returns the description of the entries: INTERVAL and LIST in the list: ADHOC_TYPE.

listName = "ADHOC_TYPE"
entryNamesCSVString = "INTERVAL,LIST"
delimiter = ","
csvString = ListEntryDescriptionsCSVString(listName, entryNamesCSVString, delimiter)

Returns:

The function returns the descriptions if located. It returns ERROR if an error is triggered.

 

LogErrors    

Purpose:

This function sets the error logging flag within SmallTalk.

Syntax:

Status = LogErrors(logFlag)

logFlag – Boolean; T, true sets the LogFlag in smallTalk to true; F, false, sets the LogFlag in smallTalk to false.  The default value is false.

Comments:

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

status = LogErrors(logFlag)

Returns:

status = true if the function is successful, otherwise false.

 

LogOut    

Purpose:

This function logs out the current user.

Syntax:

Status = LogOut()

Comments:

 NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

status = LogOut()

Returns:

status = true if the function is successful, otherwise false.

 

NLS    

Purpose:

The NLS function returns the translation for a National Language Support key value.

Syntax:

string = NLS(NLSKey, defaultValue)

NLSKey – the key value to be translated (NOTE: This is Case-Sensitive)

defaultValue – the text to be returned if the NLSKey is not found (optional).

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no translation exists for the specified NLSKey, If no default value has been defined, the LIMS will trigger the error "No key found".

•If no translation exists for the specified NLSKey, and a default value has been defined, the LIMS will return the default value.

•LIMS translates the key and returns its translated value.

NOTE:  The key values and their translations are cached in a file named nls.obj which can be built from files named nls*.txt.  If the NLS function triggers an error and the key does exist in one of the nls*.txt files, it may be possible that the nls.obj cache is out of date and needs to be rebuilt.  This can be achieved by simply deleting (or renaming) the nls.obj file before starting LIMS or using the RebuildNLS function.


Example:

The following example returns the NLS translation for the key "ExplorerTableManager").

string = NLS("ExplorerTableManager")

in this case the return string will contain "Explorer Table Manager"

Returns:

The function returns the translated value of the NLS key.

 

PostSubroutine  

Purpose:

The PostSubroutine calls a subroutine and creates a new LIMS Basic context, containing the variables listed in the variable names array.  The execution of the subroutine is delayed until the current executing code is finished.

NOTE:  If there is a Wait function in the code after the subroutine function, the Wait call stops the current process and allows any items on the pending que to process, including posted subroutines..


Syntax:

status = PostSubroutine(subName, subErrorHandler, variableNamesArray)

subName – string defining the subroutine to be triggered.

subErrorHandler – string defining the subroutine to be triggered if an error occurs.  This parameter is optional.

variableNamesArray – one dimensional array of strings defining variables to pass on to the called subroutine.

Comments:

If there is an error executing the subroutine, the subroutineErrorHandler will be exceuted.

The PostSubroutine function behaves different than the Subroutine function with the post flag = T.  The post flag on the Subroutine call maintains the existing context so the objects are not freed from memory.  This can be a problem in a situation where a large collection of objects are being looped over since memory can build up to the point where LIMS crashes.  The PostSubroutine call creates a new context, so a loop can free objects, etc without affecting the posted routine.

 Example:

The following example calls the LB_SUB2 subroutine passing the variables var1 and var3 into the subroutine but not var2.

var1 = "TEST"

var2 = 9

var3 = 3

anArray[1] = "var1"

anArray[2] = " var3"

status = PostSubroutine("LB_SUB2", "LB_LOGERR2", anArray) 

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

QuickCodesSelect

Purpose:

Creates a Quick Codes selection dialog from an array of values for searching for an selecting values.

See the section Quick Codes Selection Dialog    in Chapter 4 for more information on this dialog.

Syntax:

number = QuickCodesSelect(inputArray, queryString, titleString, "returnArrayName", maxRecords, translateConcatenationOperator, defaultValuesArray, fullSearchFlag)

inputArray – a two-dimensional array with the key value and description value of the items to select. If inputArray is not present, querySting must be present.  If both inputArray and querySting are present, the inputArray is used.

queryString – a SQL query string to select the records to display in the quick codes dialog. If queryString is not present, inputArray must be present.

titleString – Title to display on the Quick Codes selection dialog title bar

returnArrayName – the name of the one-dimensional return array which will contain the selected values from the Assigned pane.

maxRecords – An integer indicating the max number of records to be returned by the queryString argument ( only applicable if queryString is specified)

translateConcatinatorOperator –Boolean.  If True, the system determines the Concatinator Operator for the current database and replaces the ‘+’ within the SQL statements.  If False, the system executes the SQL statement as specified.  Default value is True.

defaultValuesArray -  Array of values which will be in the Assigned pane by default, on open of the Quick Codes Dialog.

fullSearchFlag – (optional) Boolean.  True will select the “Full Search” on open of the Quick Codes Selection Dialog.  False, the “Full Search” is not checked. Default is false

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•Checks for an inputArray or queryString.  If neither are found, the system will trigger the error: “Only Select and Execute queries are allowed”.

•Checks the inputArray.  If it is more then 2-dimensional, the system will trigger the error: “Input array cannot be more than 2 dimensional”.

•Checks if the queryString is a valid query.  If not valid, the system will trigger the error “Only Select and Execute queries are allowed”.

•If Translate is true, replaces all + with the translateConcatinatorOperator. 

•If the result of the queryString fails to return records, the system will trigger the error: “The query failed: queryString”.

•Opens the Quick Codes dialog with the selected records and sets the title, full Search flag, and the default values in the Assigned pane.

Example:

 

 

inputArray2D[1,1] = "A"

inputArray2D[1,2] = "Option A"

inputArray2D[2,1] = "B"

inputArray2D[2,2] = "Option B"

inputArray2D[3,1] = "C"

inputArray2D[3,2] = "Option C"

titleString = "Select your options then click OK"

defaultValuesArray[1,1] = "C"

fullSearchFlag = "T"

number = QuickCodesSelect(inputArray2D,, titleString, "returnArrayName", , "F", defaultValuesArray, fullSearchFlag)

 



Returns:

 

Returns the number selected.  If none are selected, returns 0. If not successful, it returns ERROR.

 

RecordInSync

Purpose:

Checks that the specified record is in sync. The record must be in context for the function to work if the table does not have the Changed On field.  If the table has the Changed On field, the ChangedOnDate parameter can be used to check if the record is in sync against the database record value.

Syntax:

status = RecordInSync(table,keyValue,changedOnDate)   

table – Table name for the record

keyValue – Key Value of the record to check

changedOnDate  - (optional) – Not required if the record is in context.  Can be used if the record is not in context to compare the database Changed On Date/Time to the specified ChangedOnDate value.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•Checks if the table includes the Changed_On field

•Checks if the table includes a Version field

Example:

 

table = "SAMPLE"

keyValue = 82

status = RecordInSync(table,keyValue,)

Returns:

 

Returns false if the database Changed on value not equal to the Changed on value in context otherwise returns true.

 

ReloadConstants    

Purpose:

The ReloadConstants function is used to reload the value of the all Static LIMS Constants from the database into memory.

Syntax:

status = ReloadConstants()

Comments:

This function can be called at the beginning of a subroutine to make sure all static constants are up to date.

Example:

status = ReloadConstants()

Returns:

If successful returns True else returns False.

 

ReloadScheduler    

Purpose:

The ReloadScheduler function reloads the scheduled entries for the specified client workstation..

Syntax:

status = ReloadScheduler(schedulerName)

schedulerName – the name of the scheduler (i.e., client workstation) whose scheduled entries are to be reloaded.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no scheduler name is specified, LIMS will trigger the error "No scheduler name specified".

•LIMS creates a new entry in the SERVER_ACTION table for the specified scheduler name.  The entry tells the scheduler to reload its scheduled entries.  It will be executed by the scheduler the next time the scheduler checks the SERVER_ACTION table.

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.

 

NOTE:  This function does not verify that the specified scheduler name is valid since there is no way to check for valid client names.


Example:

The following example reloads the entries for the client workstation named "SCHED1"

status = ReloadScheduler("SCHED1")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

RemoveSampleSpecListEntry  

Purpose:

The RemoveSampleSpecListEntry function removes the specified specification list entries from the specified sample.

Syntax:

status = RemoveSampleSpecListEntry(sample, entryNumbersArray)

sample – the sample number to which the spec list will be applied.
