entryNumbersArray – a one dimensional array specifying the entry numbers corresponding to the SAMPLE_SPEC_LIST table entries to be removed.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

1.  The system checks the sample.  If the sample does not exist, the system will trigger the error “Sample does not exist: sample”.

2.  The system checks the spec list on the sample.  If no spec list is found, the system will trigger the error “'No SpecList name specified for the sample”.   If the sample does have a Spec List, the system checks that the spec list is valid.  If this fails, the system will trigger the error “Invalid SpecList”.

3.  The system checks the entryNumbersArray.  If the array is not specified, the system will trigger the error “No entry codes specified”.  

4.  The system removes the spec list from the sample then updates the sample. If this fails, the system will trigger the error “Error committing sample record”.  If there is an entry number in the entryNumbersArray that belongs to a different sample the function will return true.

This function does save changes to the database.

Example:

 

 

Sample = 23456

entryNumbersArray[1] = 525

entryNumbersArray[2] = 5265

status = RemoveSampleSpecListEntry(sample, entryNumbersArray)

 

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

RemoveTableRecord    

Purpose:

The RemoveTableRecord function removes a record from a LIMS table based on the Business Rule setting. 

NOTE: This function is intended for use with Static table records (i.e. tables that are edited through Table Manager).


Syntax:

status = RemoveTableRecord(table, keyFields, keyValues, auditReason, incrementVersion, commit)

table – the name of the table from which a record is to be removed

keyFields – an array of field names that define the record to be removed.  The field name that contains the primary key value of the highest level parent record must be the first element in the array.

keyValues – an array of field values for the corresponding key fields

auditReason – the reason to be associated with the transaction (optional)

incrementVersion – Boolean flag defining if the version should be incremented.  This values defaults to False if undefined.  If value starts with  "T" and "Y" then the value is interpreted as True.

Commit – Boolean flag defining if the action should be committed to the database immediately or not.  This values defaults to False if undefined.  If value starts with  "T" and "Y" then the value is interpreted as True.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no table exists with the specified table name, LIMS will trigger the error:
"Table is not defined".

•LIMS retrieves the highest level parent record using the first element on the keyFields and keyValues arrays. 
(For example, if the record to be deleted is a component, LIMS will retrieve the analysis record.)
 If the number of records found is zero or more than one, LIMS will trigger the error: "More than one parent record found for the specified keys".

•LIMS retrieves the record to be deleted using the keyFields and keyValues arrays.  If the specified table is versioned, LIMS will retrieve the current version and ignores any version number specified in the keyValues array.

•If no records are found, LIMS will trigger the error:
"No record found for the specified keys".

•If more than one record is found, LIMS will trigger the error
"More than one record found for the specified keys".

•LIMS marks the record for deletion.

•LIMS assigns the specified audit reason to the pending deletion.  

•If no audit reason is specified, LIMS will use the translated value of the NLS key "AuditorNoReasonSpecified".

•If this key does not exist, LIMS will use the value "No reason specified" as the reason.

•LIMS assigns the audit type "TableUpdate" to the pending deletion.

NOTE:  This function immediately save changes to the database if the commit flag is set to true.  Otherwise, the function posts changes to cache until a save is triggered. 

Example:

The following example removes the component named "WT_BEFORE" of the analysis named "LOD"

myKeys[1] = "ANALYSIS"
myKeys[2] = "NAME"
myVals[1] = "LOD"
myVals[2] = "WT_BEFORE"
status = RemoveTableRecord("COMPONENT",myKeys,myVals, "")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

ResetSelectsForTable    

Purpose:

This function resets the selects in cache for the specified table..

Syntax:

status = ResetSelectsForTable(tableName)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that a table name has been provided.  If not, LIMS returns the last error: No table name specified.

•LIMS resets the selects.

Example:

The following example saves all pending changes.

Status = ResetSelectsForTable("SAMPLE")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if successful, otherwise false.

 

Save    

Purpose:

The Save function saves all pending changes for all objects in context.

Syntax:

status = Save()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS saves each object that has pending changes.  If no audit type was specified for any audit, LIMS will use the audit type "CalculationUpdate".

This function saves changes to the database.

Example:

The following example saves all pending changes.

status = Save()

Returns:

The function returns the value true.

 

SelectAliquot    

Purpose:

The SelectAliquot function selects a sample and places it in context.  If there is more than one aliquot, the first aliquot found is put in context.

Syntax:

status = SelectAliquot(sampleNumber,fields,values,checkAliquots,checkSample)

sampleNumber – Sample number of the source sample to be searched for aliquots.

fields – One dimensional array that defines the fields to be used as search parameters.

values – One dimensional array that defines the field values to be uses as search criteria.

checkAliquots – Boolean that determines if the system checks the aliquots of the specified sample for further aliquots that  match the search criteria.  This value defaults to False.

checkSample – Boolean that determines if the system checks the aliquots of the specified sample for matches to the search criteria.  This value defaults to False.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that the sampleNumber is an integer.  If the sampleNumber is not an integer, LIMS returns the last error: "Sample number not numeric: " followed by the input value.

•LIMS verifies that the sampleNumber is valid.  If the sampleNumber is not valid, LIMS returns the last error: "Sample does not exist: " followed by the input value.

•LIMS searchs for aliquots that match the fields and values array.

•If the CheckSample is true, the system checks the specified sample for aliquots that match the search criteria.

•If the CheckAliquots is true, the system checks the aliquots of the specified sample for aliquots that match the search criteria.

•LIMS verifies that matching aliquots have been located.  If no matching aliquot is located, the LIMS returns the last error:  "No matching aliquot found for sample: " followed by the input sampleNumber

 NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code places the samples in context that are aliquots of the Sample 357 and have a description: Information Only.

sampleNumber = 357
fields[1] = "DESCRIPTION"
values[1] = "Information Only"
CheckAliquots = "F"
CheckSample = "T"

status = SelectAliquot(sampleNumber,fields,values,checkAliquots,checkSample)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

SelectRecord    

Purpose:

The SelectRecord function selects a record and places it in context with any items from a specified item table.

Syntax:

status = SelectRecord(tableName,keyValue,version,itemTableName)

tableName – the name of the table the record is to be selected from.

keyValue – the value uniquely identifying the record within the targeted table.

version – integer specifying the version of the record to be retrieved.

itemTableName – the name of the associated item table.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that a table name has been specified.  If no table name has been specified, LIMS returns the error: "Table name must be specified."

•LIMS verifies that a key value has been specified.  If no key value has been specified, LIMS returns the error: "Key value must be specified."

•if the key field is a integer, LIMS verifies that the specified key value is an integer.  If the key value is not an integer, LIMS returns the error: "Key value must be integer:" followed by the input value.

•LIMS verifies that an integer is specified for the version if a version is required.  If the version is not an integer, the LIMS returns the last error: "Version must be integer:" followed by the key value.

•LIMS verifies that the item table is valid.  If the item table is not valid, the LIMS returns the last error: "Cannot use this function to select records from item table:" followed by the specified item table.

•LIMS places the specified record and associated items into context.

 NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code retrieved the specified project and all entries on the packaging item table and place them into context.

tableName = "PROJECT"
keyValue = 1
itemTableName = "PACKAGING"
status = SelectRecord(tableName,keyValue, ,itemTableName)

The following code retrieves the specified analysis and version and places it into context.

tableName = "ANALYSIS"
keyValue = " DEMO_A"
version = 2
status = SelectRecord(tableName,keyValue,version,)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

SelectSample    

Purpose:

The SelectSample function selects a sample and places it in context.

Syntax:

status = SelectSample(sampleNumber, sampleOnly, setDefaultSampleFlag)

sampleNumber – Sample number of the sample to be placed into context.

sampleOnly – Boolean that determines if the system places the tests and results in context as well as the sample.  This value defaults to False.

setDefaultSampleFlag – Boolean that determines if the sample is to be set as the LIMS default sample. This value defaults to False.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evaluates the setDefaultSampleFlag Boolean.  If the parameter is not specified, LIMS sets the value to False.

•LIMS evaluates the sampleOnly Boolean.  If the parameter is not specified, LIMS sets the value to False.

•LIMS places the specified sample into context.

•If the sampleOnly is true, the system places only the sample into context.

•If the sampleOnly is false, the system places the sample, and its tests and results into context.

•LIMS verifies that a sample was located.  If a sample was not located, the LIMS returns the last error: "Sample does not exist: " followed by the input sampleNumber.

•If no error then LIMS sets the sampleNumber as the default sample number.

 CAUTION: This function is not recommended for use in analysis calculations.

 

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code places the Sample 357 and all its tests and results into context.

sampleNumber = 357
sampleOnly = "F"

status = SelectSample(sampleNumber,sampleOnly)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

SelectTest    

Purpose:

The SelectTest function selects a test based on the field criteria provided in the function.  The sample and its tests are put in context and the test identified by the search  is placed first in context.

Syntax:

status = SelectTest(sampleNumber, fields, values, checkAliquots)

sampleNumber – Sample number of the sample to be searched.

fields – One dimensional array that defines the fields to be used as search parameters for the test.

values – One dimensional array that defines the field values to be uses as search criteria for the test.

checkAliquots – Boolean that determines if the system checks the aliquots of the specified sample for tests that match the search criteria.  This value defaults to False.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evaluates the checkAliquots parameter.  If it is not specified, the system defines the parameter as False.

•LIMS verifies that the sampleNumber is an integer.  If the sampleNumber is not an integer, LIMS returns the last error: "Sample number not numeric: " followed by the input value.

•LIMS verifies that the sampleNumber is valid.  If the sampleNumber is not valid, LIMS returns the last error: Sample does not exist: " followed by the input value.

•LIMS verifies that the fields array is valid.  If the array is empty or the input is not an array, the LIMS returns the last error: "Invalid or empty fields array".

•LIMS verifies that the values array is valid.  If the array is empty or the input is not an array, the LIMS returns the last error: "Invalid or empty values array".

•LIMS verifies that the fields array is the same size as the values array.  If they are not, the LIMS returns the last error: "Fields array and values array are different sizes".

•LIMS searchs for test that match the fields and values array.

•If the CheckAliquots is true, the system checks the aliquots of the specified sample for tests that match the search criteria.

•LIMS verifies that matching tests have been located.  If no matching test is located, the LIMS returns the last error:  "No matching test found for sample: " followed by the input sampleNumber

 NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code places the sample and its tests in context and puts the ICP test first in the TEST records in context.

sampleNumber = 357
fields[1] = "ANALYSIS"
values[1] = "ICP"
CheckAliquots = "F"

status = SelectTest(sampleNumber,fields,values,checkAliquots)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

SemaphoreGet   

Purpose:

This function returns a handle that can be used by other semaphore functions to operate on a semaphore.

Syntax:

handle = SemaphoreGet(name)

name – an arbitrary name that will be used to identify the semaphore in the SEMAPHORE table.

Comments:

When this function is invoked, LIMS performs the following activities:

•LIMS verifies that the name is not empty.  If it is, LIMS returns the LastError: "No name specified".

•LIMS creates a handle for the name specified.

The semaphore name can be a new name or an existing name in the SEMAPHORE table. Once at least one SemaphoreWait() operation is invoked on the semaphore, an entry will be created in the SEMAPHORE table if it does not already exist.

Example:

The following example returns a handle for the semaphore named "SEMBATCH1".

 

name = "SEMBATCH1"

handle = SemaphoreGet(name)

Returns:

 

handle = a handle that can be passed to other semaphore functions.

 

 

SemaphoreHasWaits  

Purpose:

Tests whether the semaphore has any wait operations.

Syntax:

status = SemaphoreHasWaits(handle)

handle – a valid handle returned by the SemaphoreGet function.

Comments:

When this function is invoked, LIMS performs the following activities:

•LIMS verifies that the handle is valid.  If it is not, LIMS returns the LastError: "Invalid handle specified".

•LIMS tests whether the semaphore has any wait operations on it.

Example:

The following example will test for wait operations on the semaphore named "SEMBATCH1".

semName = "SEMBATCH1"

handle = SemaphoreGet(semName)

 

status = SemaphoreHasWaits(handle)

Returns:

 

If the function returns TRUE, the semaphore is locked and the process will have to wait if SemaphoreWait() is called.

If the function returns FALSE, the semaphore is not locked by another process.

 

SemaphoreRelease  

Purpose:

Discard the handle so that SemaphoreGet() must be called again to perform another operation on the semaphore.

Syntax:

status = SemaphoreRelease(handle)

handle – a valid handle returned by the SemaphoreGet function.

Comments:

When this function is invoked, LIMS performs the following activities:

•LIMS verifies that the handle is valid.  If it is not, LIMS returns the LastError: "Invalid handle specified".

•LIMS releases the semaphore

 

NOTE: You should always call SemaphoreSignal() to increment (unlock) a semaphore that you have locked before calling SemaphoreRelease(). Do not rely on the SemaphoreRelease() to do that.

Example:

The following example will release a semaphore

status = SemaphoreRelease(handle)

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

 

SemaphoreSignal  

Purpose:

Increments (unlocks) a semaphore that has been decremented by the current process.

Syntax:

status = SemaphoreSignal(handle)

handle – a valid handle returned by the SemaphoreGet function.

Comments:

When this function is invoked, LIMS performs the following activities:

•LIMS verifies that the handle is valid.  If it is not, LIMS returns the LastError: "Invalid handle specified".

•LIMS verifies that this process has the semaphore raised (locked). If it does not, LIMS returns the LastError: "The semaphore was not holding".

•LIMS increments (unlocks) the semaphore.

 

NOTE: It is not possible to increment (unlock) a semaphore that has been decremented (locked) by another process using this call.

 

NOTE: It is not possible to increment (unlock) a semaphore that has been decremented (locked) by another process using this call

Example:

The following example will signal a semaphore.

status = SemaphoreSignal(handle)

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

 

SemaphoreWait  

Purpose:

Decrements (locks) a semaphore.

Syntax:

status = SemaphoreWait(handle, timeOut, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

handle – a valid handle returned by the SemaphoreGet function.

timeOut - The maximum amount of time in seconds to wait for the semaphore to become available. If the timeout expires without locking the semaphore, then the function will return with status = FALSE.

fieldsArray - (optional): an array of field names to set in the SEMAPHORE_WAIT table.

valuesArray - (optional): an array of field values to be updated.

Comments:

When this function is invoked, LIMS performs the following activities:

•LIMS verifies that the handle is valid.  If it is not, LIMS returns the LastError: "Invalid handle specified".

•LIMS verifies that the fields array is the same size as the values array.  If not, the LIMS returns the last error: "Fields and values arrays must be the same size".

•LIMS verifies that the timeout specified is valid. If not, the LIMS returns the last error "Invalid timeOut specified: " followed by the timeout specified.

•LIMS attempts to decrement (lock) the semaphore. If another process has already decremented the semaphore, the function call will wait until the timeOut has expired. When more than one process is waiting for the semaphore, the longest waiting process gets the semaphore.

Example:

The following example will decrement (lock) the semaphore named "SEMBATCH1". It will wait for 60 seconds to obtain the lock.

semName = "SEMBATCH1"

handle = SemaphoreGet(semName)

 

status = SemaphoreWait(handle, 60)

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it decrements the semaphore successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered or the call times out.

 

SetConstant    

Purpose:

The SetConstant function is used to assign a value to the LIMS Constant.

Syntax:

status = SetConstant(constantName, constantValue , userName)

constantName – the name of the LIMS Constant.

constantValue – the value of the LIMS Constant.

username – the name of the user (optional). If included, this function will updated the User Constant that is associated with the specified LIM Constant and the specified user.

NOTE:  If the name of the constant does not exist it will be created in the LIMS_CONSTANTS table.  Constants created this way will have the Data Type field defaulted to Text and Static field defaulted to F.

Comments:

The function will check the following:

•If the constant is defined as a Boolean Data Type, the constant value must start with the letter T or F (case doesn’t matter) otherwise the function returns "false"

•If the constant is defined as a Date or DateTime data Type, the constant value must be in the date format YYYY-MM-DD.

•If the constant is defined as an Integer Data Type, the constant value must be an integer.

•If the constant is defined as a Numeric Data Type, the constant value must a valid number (which can be an integer or floating point). 

•If the constant value is null, the only Data Type that will accept this value is the Text Data Type otherwise the function returns "false".

•If a User name has been specified, the user name is checked.  If the user name is not valid function returns "false". If valid then the value for the User Constant record for the specified LIMS Constant and user will be updated.  The LIMS Constant record remains unchanged.

•If no user name is specified the LIMS Constant record is updated with the new value.

NOTE: A database commit is a part of this function.

NOTE:  This updated value is only in use if Static=False, otherwise it is not in use until the user logs out then logs in again or the ReloadConstants is used.

Example:

The following example assigns a value to the LIMS constant "TEST".

constantName = "TEST"

status = SetConstant(constantName, "Hi !")

Returns:

If successful returns True else returns False.

 

SetGlobalVariable    

Purpose:

The SetGlobalVariable function sets the value of a global variable.  This variable is available system wide to all LIMS Basic formulae until the LIMS application is closed.

Syntax:

status  = SetGlobalVariable(globalName, value)

Comments:

If the global variable does not exist, it is created.  The variable is maintained on the client PC until the LIMS application is closed, even across different databases.  See the GetGlobalVariable function for reading the contents of the variable.

Example:

status = SetGlobalVariable("myVar", "stuff")

value = GetGlobalVariable("myVar")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

SetNLS    

Purpose:

The SetNLS function changes the translation of the specified key to the specified value.

Syntax:

status = SetNLS(nlsKey, nlsValue)

nlsKey – the key whose value is to be modified

nlsValue – the value to be applied

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no NLS key is specified, LIMS will trigger the error "Missing NLS key".

•If no NLS value is specified, LIMS will trigger the error "Missing NLS value".

•LIMS sets the translated value of the specified key equal to the specified value.

NOTE:  This specified value will remain in effect until it is modified or until the user closes the LIMS application entirely, whichever happens first.  If the user logs out and then logs back in as the same or a different user, the specified value will remain in effect.


Example:

The following example sets the value of "ANALYSIS.NAME" to "Method Name", which will cause LIMS to display the field label "Method Name" whenever it prompts for Analysis Name.

status = SetNLS("ANALYSIS.NAME", "Method Name")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

SetNotebookRef    

Purpose:

The SetNotebookRef function sets the current user’s notebook reference.

Syntax:

status = SetNoteBookRef(notebook, page, comment)

notebook – string defining the notebook to be referenced.

page – string defining the page number to be referenced.

comment – string defining the comment to included with the reference.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•LIMS verifies that the notebook parameter is specified.  If not, LIMS returns the last error: Error: no notebook specified.

•LIMS verifies that the supplied notebook is in the system and is not closed.  If the string is absent or the reference is closed, the LIMS return the last error: Invalid notebook.

•LIMS sets the supplied reference data.

Example:

status = SetNoteBookRef("NB0187XY", "14A", "Research Z continued")

Returns:

status = true if function is successful, otherwise false.

 

SetVariable     

Purpose:

The SetVariable function sets the value of the specified variable.

Syntax:

status =  SetVariable(variableName, variableValue)

variableName – string designated as the name of the variable.  This value must be alphanumeric.

variableValue – value of the variable.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that a variable name is specified.  If a variable name is not specified, LIMS returns the error: "No variable name specified."

•LIMS verifies that the variable name is alphanumeric.  If the variable name specified is not alphanumeric, LIMS returns the error:
"Variable name is not alphanumeric:" followed by the input value.

•LIMS sets the variable to the specified value.

NOTE: This function  does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code sets the variable: VN123 to the value: ABC.

variableName = "VN123"
variableValue = "ABC"status =  SetVariable(variableName, variableValue)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

Shutdown    

Purpose:

The Shutdown function shuts down the current instance of the LIMS client by executing the "Log Out" main menu item followed by the "Exit" main menu item.

Syntax:

Shutdown()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS performs the "Log Out" main menu item.

•LIMS performs the "Exit" main menu item.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database. 


Example:

The following example shuts down the current LIMS client.

Shutdown()

Returns:

The function has no return value.  If the function fails, control will be returned to the currently executing LIMS Basic script.

 

ShutdownHard    

Purpose:

The ShutdownHard function immediately terminates the LIMS executable.

Syntax:

ShutdownHard()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

LIMS immediately terminates itself.

NOTE:  This function does not perform any kind of checking or cleanup.  Execution stops immediately.


Example:

The following example shuts down LIMS immediately.

ShutdownHard()

Returns:

The function does not return anything

 

Subroutine     

Purpose:

The Subroutine function executes a subroutine. 

Unlike the GoSub statement, this function can: 1) specify an error handling routine; 2) can trigger the called subroutine after the current routine; and 3) can pass an array of local variables to the called subroutine.

Syntax:

status = Subroutine(subroutineName, subroutineErrorHandler, postFlag, variableNamesArray, signalLastError)

subroutineName – string defining the subroutine to be triggered.

subroutineErrorHandler – string defining the subroutine to be triggered if an error occurs.  This parameter is optional.

postFlag – if set to T, the execution of the SubroutineName is delayed until the current executing code is finished.  Otherwise, the SubroutineName is executed as if the code were located in the place where the Subroutine function was called. The postFlag is optional and defaults to false.

NOTE:  If there is a Wait function in the code after the subroutine function, the Wait call stops the current process and allows any items on the pending que to process, including posted subroutines..

variableNamesArray– one dimensional array of strings defining local variables to pass on to the called subroutine.

signalLastError – This parameter is an optional Boolean.  If set to T, if a lastError is triggered during the execution of the called subroutine, the called subroutine will discontinue executing and trigger the subroutineErrorHandler, if present.. 
Otherwise lastError will not trigger the routine to discontinue execution and subroutineErrorHandler will not be triggered.  This value defaults to false if not specified.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS evaluates the postFlag.  If the input begins with T or Y, LIMS sets this input to true, otherwise the input is set to false.

•LIMS evaluates the signalError.  If the input begins with T or Y, LIMS sets this input to true, otherwise the input is set to false.

•If subroutineName is not a valid subroutine, LIMS will return status = false

•If the postFlag is true, LIMS executes the subroutine call at the end of the current process.  If false, the LIMS executes the subroutine call immediately in the process.

•If the signalError is true, LIMS executes the subroutineErrorHandler (if defined).  Additionally, if the subroutine sets the lastError to a non-null value or if the subroutineErrorHandler is invoked, the status is set to false.

 

Example:

local: var = "YES"

local: mice = "Three blind ones"

local: catLives = 9

anArray[1] = "var"

anArray[2] = "mice"

anArray[3] = "catLives"

status = Subroutine("LB-TEST", "LB-ERROR", "F", anArray)  

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

UpdateAssociatedTableFlds    

Purpose:

This function is designed to facilitate the update of user added tables that are associated with standard LIMS tables.

Syntax:

status = UpdateAssociatedTableFlds(table,keyFields,keyValues,fields,values,auditReason)

table – string defining the table to be updated.

keyFields –one dimensional array defining the key fields for the table being updated.  The key field for the parent table must be the first value.  Parent table must be a single key table (versioned tables supported).

keyValues – one dimensional array defining the key values for the records to be updated.  The key value for the parent record must be the first value.  Parent table must be a single key table (versioned tables supported).

fields – one dimensional array defining the fields to be updated.

values – one dimensional array defining the values to be populated in the associated fields.

auditReason –string to be posted to the audit trail when changes are commited.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that the defined table is valid.  If not, LIMS returns the last error: Table is not defined..

•If an audit reason was specified, LIMS will apply it to the pending transaction.

•If changes should be committed to the database, LIMS saves both the top level parent record and the record that was updated.

NOTE:  This function does commit changes to the database.


Example:

tableName = "CUSTOM_NOTES"

keyFields[1] = "ORDER_NUM"

keyValues[1] = " 0000000003"

keyFields[2] = "SEQ_NUM"

keyValues[2] = 47

fields[1] = "OBS_1"

values[1] = "Phase one successfully completed."

fields[2] = "NOTE_DATE"

values[2] = date()

auditReason = "Updating Order observations."

status = UpdateAssociatedTableFlds(tableName, keyFields, keyValues, fields, values, auditReason)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

UpdateProduct     

Purpose:

The UpdateProduct function updates, replaces, or deletes a product.

Syntax:

status = UpdateProduct(productName, productFieldNameArray, productFieldValueArray, gradeFieldNameArray, gradeFieldValueArray, stageFieldNameArray, stageFieldValueArray, specFieldNameArray, specFieldValueArray, actionType, incrementVersionFlag)

productName – the name of the product to be updated

productFieldNameArray – an array that contains the names of the fields to be updated

productFieldValueArray – an array that contains the values for the corresponding field names

gradeFieldNameArray – an array that contains the names of the fields to be updated as well as the names of the fields that identify the grades to be updated

gradeFieldValueArray – an array that contains the values for the corresponding field names

stageFieldNameArray – an array that contains the names of the fields to be updated as well as the names of the fields that identify the stages to be updated

stageFieldValueArray – an array that contains the values for the corresponding field names

specFieldNameArray – an array that contains the names of the fields to be updated as well as the names of the fields that identify the specs to be updated.  The array must include the following fields: PRODUCT, VERSION, SAMPLING_POINT, GRADE, STAGE, SPEC_TYPE, ANALYSIS, COMPONENT, CLASS, RULE_TYPE

specFieldValueArray – an array that contains the values for the corresponding field names

actionType – the action to be performed.  One of "DELETE", "REPLACE", or "UPDATE".

incrementVersionFlag – the value true if the product version should be incremented as a result of the updates (optional; the default value is true)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the productFieldNameArray and the productFieldValueArray do not have the same size, LIMS will trigger the error "Product field names and values must be the same size".

•If the gradeFieldNameArray and the gradeFieldValueArray do not have the same size, LIMS will trigger the error "Grade field names and values must be the same size".

•If the stageFieldNameArray and the stageFieldValueArray do not have the same size, LIMS will trigger the error "Stage field names and values must be the same size".

•If the specFieldNameArray and the specFieldValueArray do not have the same size, LIMS will trigger the error "Spec field names and values must be the same size".

•If the productFieldNameArray contains a field name that does not exist in the product table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid product field name specified".

•If the productFieldNameArray contains the field named "PRODUCT" more than once, LIMS will trigger the error "Only one product record can be updated at a time".

•If the productFieldNameArray contains an array index that is not present in the productFieldValueArray, LIMS will trigger the error "Missing product values array index".

•If the gradeFieldNameArray contains a field name that does not exist in the product grade table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid product grade field name specified".

•If the gradeFieldNameArray contains an array index that is not present in the gradeFieldValueArray, LIMS will trigger the error "Missing grade values array index".

•If the first element in the gradeFieldNameArray is not the field named "PRODUCT", LIMS will trigger the error "Grade data array fields must begin with PRODUCT".

•If the stageFieldNameArray contains a field name that does not exist in the product grade stage table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid product grade stage field name specified".

•If the stageFieldNameArray contains an array index that is not present in the stageFieldValueArray, LIMS will trigger the error "Missing stage values array index".

•If the first element in the stageFieldNameArray is not the field named "PRODUCT", LIMS will trigger the error "Stage data array fields must begin with PRODUCT".

•If the specFieldNameArray contains a field name that does not exist in the product specification table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid product specification field name specified".

•If the specFieldNameArray contains an array index that is not present in the specFieldValueArray, LIMS will trigger the error "Missing spec values array index".

•If the first element in the specFieldNameArray is not the field named "PRODUCT", LIMS will trigger the error "Spec data array fields must begin with PRODUCT".

•If the specified action is not one of "REPLACE", "UPDATE", or "DELETE", LIMS will trigger the error "The specified action is not valid".

•If the productFieldNameArray contains the "VERSION" field name, LIMS will use the corresponding product version number to access the product to be updated or deleted.  Otherwise, if the gradeFieldNameArray contains the "VERSION" field, LIMS will use the corresponding grade version number.  Otherwise, if the stageFieldNameArray contains the "VERSION" field, LIMS will use the corresponding stage version number.  Otherwise, if the specFieldNameArray contains the "VERSION" field, LIMS will use the corresponding spec version number.  Otherwise, LIMS will use the latest version of the product.

•If the requested action is "UPDATE" and LIMS is unable to find the product with the specified productName and version, LIMS will trigger the error "Can not update a product that does not exist".

•If the requested action is "DELETE" and LIMS is unable to find the product with the specified productName and version, LIMS will trigger the error "Can not delete a product that does not exist".

•If the productFieldNameArray contains the "VERSION" field name and if the corresponding product version number is not equal to the version number of the retrieved product, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified product version does not match the current product version.

•LIMS validates the values in the four FieldValueArrays against the definition of the fields in the corresponding FieldNameArrays.  If any field is found to be invalid, LIMS will trigger the error "invalid valid for the field".  (Valid in this context means the following.  Link fields must contain a value that exists in the corresponding parent table.  Text values must not be longer than the permitted width of the field.  List entries must be present in the corresponding list.  Values for date fields must represent a valid date or datetime.  String representations of booleans must begin with the letter T or F.  Interval values must be integers or reprensetations of datetime values.  Values for integer fields must be representations of integer values.  Values for numeric fields must be representations of numbers.)

•The following steps apply when the actionType is "DELETE".

•LIMS deletes all spec records identified by the specField arrays.  If a spec record cannot be found, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified spec does not exist".  If a stage record for a spec cannot be found, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified stage does not exist".  If a grade record for a spec cannot be found, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified grade does not exist".

•LIMS deletes all stage records identified by the stageField arrays.  If a stage record cannot be found, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified stage does not exist".  If a grade record for a stage cannot be found, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified grade does not exist".

•LIMS deletes all grade records identified by the gradeField arrays.  If a grade record cannot be found, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified grade does not exist".

•If the productField arrays are not empty or missing, LIMS will delete the product record identified by these arrays.

•The following steps apply when the actionType is "REPLACE".

•If the value of incrementVersionFlag is false, and the product to be replaced already exists in the database, LIMS will delete the existing product and save the change to the database using audit type "UpdateTable".  LIMS then creates a new product object in memory with the same name.

•The following steps apply when the actionType is "UPDATE" or "REPLACE".

•LIMS updates the product with the field values in the productFieldValueArray.

•LIMS updates the grades of the product with the field values in the gradeFieldValueArray (or creates them if they don’t already exist).

•LIMS updates the stages of the product with the field values in the stageFieldValueArray (or creates them if they don’t already exist).  If the grade for any stage does not already exist, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified grade does not exist".

•LIMS updates the specs of the product with the field values in the specFieldValueArray (or creates them with the next available order number if they don’t already exist).  If the grade for any spec does not already exist, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified grade does not exist".  If the stage for any spec does not already exist, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified stage does not exist".

•The following steps apply when the actionType is "REPLACE".

•If the value of incrementVersionFlag is true, LIMS will delete all grades, stages, and specs that are not explicitly referenced in the gradeField, stageField, or specField arrays, respectively.

•The following steps apply to all actionType values.

•If the value of incrementVersionFlag is true, LIMS will increment the version of the product record.

•If an audit reason is associated with the product table, LIMS will assign it to the pending database transaction.

•LIMS saves the changes to the database using audit type "UpdateTable" and clears the cache of static data objects.

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.

NOTE:  The "DELETE" and "UPDATE" actions can be used to delete or update any elements in the product-grade-stage-spec hierarchy without specifying the elements that do not need to be deleted or updated.  In order to use the "REPLACE" action, the entire hierarchy must be specified, including any elements that will remain unchanged.

NOTE: The component must exist on the analysis before it can be included in the product specification.


Example:

The following example updates the description of the first sampling point of the domestic grade of aspirin to "USA" without updating the version number.

myFields[1] = "PRODUCT"

myFields[2] = "GRADE"

myFields[3] = "SAMPLING_POINT"

myFields[4] = "DESCRIPTION"

myValues[1] = "ASPIRIN"

myValues[2] = "DOMESTIC"

myValues[3] = "FIRST"

myValues[4] = "USA"

status = UpdateProduct("ASPIRIN", "", "", myFields, myValues, "", "", "", "", "UPDATE", "false")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

UpdateReportedResult

Purpose:

The UpdateReportedResult function updates fields in the REPORTED_RESULT table for the specified entry.

CAUTION:  This function should not be used to modify system determined entries in the Reported Result table.  Also, this function can be used to update entries corresponding to results that are reviewed so care should be taken when applying to reviewed results.


Syntax:

status = UpdateReportedResult(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber, theGuid, fieldsArray, valuesArray, reviseFlag)

sampleNumber - the sample number of the result that is to be updated

testNumber– the test number of the result that is to be updated

resultNumber– the result number of the result that is to be updated

theGuid– the value in the GU_ID field in the REPORTED_RESULT table for the specified entry that is to be updated

fieldsArray– a one dimensional array of the fields in the REPORTED_RESULT table that will be updated

valuesArray– a one dimensional array of the values for the fields that are to be updated

reviseFlag– a Boolean indicating if the entry in the REPORTED_RESULT table is to be versioned on update

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

1.  The system verifies the sampleNumber is valid.  If the sample is not found, the following error is returned: "Sample does not exist: sampleNumber ".

2.  The system verifies the testNumber is valid.  If the test is not found, the following error is returned: "Test does not exist: testNumber ".

3.  The system verifies the resultNumber is valid.  If the test is not found, the following error is returned: "Result does not exist: resultNumber ".

4.  The system verifies the theGuid is valid.  If not found or the reported result is not the highest revision for the result name, the following error is returned: "Reported result does not exist: theGuid ".

5.  The system checks the size of the fieldsArray.  If is empty, the following error is returned: "No fields specified ".

6.  The system checks the size of the valuesArray.  If is empty, the following error is returned: "No values specified ".

7.  The system compares the fieldsArray to the valuesArray.  If they are not the same size, the following error is returned: "Fields and values arrays are not the same size ".

8.  The system updates the reported result.

NOTE:  This function does NOT save changes to the database.


Example:

sampleNumber = 236

testNumber = 349

resultNumber = 429

theGuid = rrGUID

fieldsArray[1] = "REPORTED_NAME"

valuesArray[1] = "Len (m)"

reviseFlag = "F"

status = UpdateReportedResult(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber, theGuid, fieldsArray, valuesArray, reviseFlag)

save()

 

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

UpdateSampleSpecListEntryVersion  

Purpose:

The UpdateSampleSpecListEntryVersion function updates the product Version field in the Sample Spec list table entry for the specified sample.

Syntax:

status = UpdateSampleSpecListEntryVersion(sample, entryNumber, version, updateAliquots, showProgressDialog)

sample – the sample number on which the product version will be updated

entryNumber - the ENTRY_NUMBER from the Sample Spec list table corresponding to the entry that will be updated (only one entry can be updated at a time)

version - the version of the Specification List that will be updated to the sample

updateAliquots – a Boolean flag indicating if aliquots of the sample are to be updated

showProgressDialog – a Boolean flag indicating if a progress dialog is to be displayed while the sample is being updated

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

1.  The system checks the sample.  If this fails, the system will trigger the error “Sample does not exist: sample”.

2.  The system checks the spec list on the sample.  If no spec list is found, the system will trigger the error “'No SpecList name specified for the sample”.   If the sample does have a Spec List, the system checks that the spec list is valid for the specified entry number.  If this fails, the system will trigger the error “Invalid SpecList”.

3.  The system checks the entryNumber.  If the entry is not valid, the system will trigger the error “Invalid SpecListEntry: entryNumber”. 

4.  The system checks the version for the product associated with the specification entryNumber.  If no product is found with the specified version, the system will trigger the error “Invalid Product version: product / version”. 

5.  The system updates the sample and it aliquots, if specified. If this fails, the system will trigger the error “Error committing sample record”.

This function does save changes to the database.

Example:

 

 

Sample = 12345

entryNumber = 512

version = 2

updateAliquots = "F"

showProgressDialog = "T"

status = UpdateSampleSpecListEntryVersion(sample, entryNumber, version, updateAliquots, showProgressDialog)

 

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

UpdateServerAction

Purpose:

The UpdateServerAction function updates an entry in the Server Action table.

Syntax:

status = UpdateServerAction(actionNumber, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

actionNumber – the number of the Server Action to be updated

fieldsArray – the one dimensional array of fields to be updated.

valuesArray – the one dimensional array of values  to be assigned to the fields in the fieldsArray.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•The system checks actionNumber.  If not an integer or the specified action number is not found in the Server Action table, the system will trigger the error “Invalid action number specified: actionNumber”.

•The system checks the fieldsArray size.  If the size is 0, the system will trigger the error “No fields specified”.

•The system checks that fieldsArray and valuesArray are the same size. If not, the system will trigger the error “The fields and values array must be the same size'”.

•The system checks the entries in the fieldsArray.  If a field is found that is not in the Server Action table, the system will trigger the error “Invalid field specified: field”.

•The system attempts to update the specified server action.  If this fails, the system will trigger the error “Error commiting the update”.

NOTE:  A database commit is part of this function.


Example:

 

 

The following example updates the Background Server name for the specified action:

 

actionNumber = 112

fieldsArray[1] = "SERVER_NAME"

valuesArray[1] = "BACK2"

status = UpdateServerAction(actionNumber, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

Returns:

 

The function returns true if successful. If it is not successful, it will answer false and lastError will be set with an appropriate error message.

 

ValidAnalysesAndVariationsForSampleFields     

Purpose:

The ValidAnalysesAndVariationsForSampleFields function checks the Sample Rules based on the fields and field values defined in the fields and values arrays and returns the valid analyses with variations for the specified field values based on the Sample Rule definitions only.

Syntax:

status = ValidAnalysesAndVariationsForSampleFields(fieldsArray, valuesArray, "analysesArrayName")

fieldsArray – an array of field names

valuesArray – an array containing field values for the fields specified in the fields array

analysesArrayName – the name of the array which will contain the valid analyses and variations returned by the function for the specified field values

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evaluates the analysesArrayName.  If the argument is empty, the system triggers an error and returns a Last Error: ‘No array name specified’

•LIMS evaluates size of the fieldsArray.  If the size is not > 0, the system triggers an error and returns a Last Error: ‘No fields specified’.

•LIMS evaluates size of the fieldsArray and valuesArray.  If not the same size then LIMS will trigger the error "Field and value arrays are not the same size".

•LIMS checks the Samples Rules for the specified field values and returns the array of analyses with variations.

NOTE:  This function does not save changes to the database.

 

NOTE: If the Business rule Include Non-Ruled Analyses for Sample Rules is set to true the output will also included all analyses for which there are no samples rules.


Example:

The following example checks the Sample Rules for the customer ‘ACME’

fieldsArray[1] = "CUSTOMER"

valuesArray[1] = "ACME"

anlArrayName  = "ValidAnalsArray"

status = ValidAnalysesAndVariationsForSampleFields(fieldsArray, valuesArray, anlArrayName  )

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

ValidAnalysesForSample    

Purpose:

The ValidAnalysesForSample function checks the Sample Rules based on the field values from the specified sample and returns the valid analyses for the sample.  The field valuess from the sample are compred against the fields used to defined the samples rules to determine the valid analyses.

Syntax:

status = ValidAnalysesForSample(sampleNumber, "analysesArrayName")

sampleNumber – the sample number of the sample that is to be checked

analysesArrayName – the name of the array which will contain the valid analyses returned by the function for the specified sample

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evaluates the analysesArrayName.  If the argument is empty, the system triggers an error and returns a Last Error: ‘No array name specified’

•LIMS checks the sampleNumber.  If not found LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid sample specified: sampleNumber".

•LIMS checks the Samples Rules for the specified sample and returns the array of analyses.

NOTE:  This function does not save changes to the database.

 

NOTE: If the Business rule Include Non-Ruled Analyses for Sample Rules is set to true the output will also included all analyses for which there are no samples rules.


Example:

The following example checks the Sample Rules for sample number 123:

sampleNumber = 123

anlArrayName  = "ValidAnalsArray"

status = ValidAnalysesForSample(sampleNumber, anlArrayName )

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

ValidAnalysesForSampleFields    

Purpose:

The ValidAnalysesForSampleFields function checks the Sample Rules based on the fields and field values defined in the fields and values arrays and returns the valid analyses for the specified field values.

Syntax:

status = ValidAnalysesForSampleFields(fieldsArray, valuesArray, "analysesArrayName ")

fieldsArray – an array of field names

valuesArray – an array containing field values for the fields specified in the fields array

analysesArrayName – the name of the array which will contain the valid analyses returned by the function for the specified field values

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evaluates the analysesArrayName.  If the argument is empty, the system triggers an error and returns a Last Error: ‘No array name specified’

•LIMS evaluates size of the fieldsArray.  If the size is not > 0, the system triggers an error and returns a Last Error: ‘No fields specified’.

•LIMS evaluates size of the fieldsArray and valuesArray.  If not the same size then LIMS will trigger the error "Field and value arrays are not the same size".

•LIMS checks the Samples Rules for the specified field values and returns the array of analyses.

NOTE:  This function does not save changes to the database.

 

NOTE: If the Business rule Include Non-Ruled Analyses for Sample Rules is set to true the output will also included all analyses for which there are no samples rules.


Example:

The following example checks the Sample Rules for the sample type ‘INTERNAL’

fieldsArray[1] = "SAMPLE_TYPE"

valuesArray[1] = "INTERNAL"

anlArrayName  = "ValidAnalsArray"

status = ValidAnalysesForSampleFields(fieldsArray, valuesArray, anlArrayName  )

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

ValidateFieldForTable

Purpose:

Validates a value for the field in the specified table.

Syntax:

status = ValidateFieldForTable(tableName, fieldName, fieldValue)

tableName – The name of the table to be checked

fieldname – The name of the field in the table that will be checked

fieldValue – The value to be checked in the specified field

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•Checks if the table name is present.  If not present, the system will trigger the error: “No table name specified”.

•Checks if the table name is valid.  If not valid, the system will trigger the error: “Invalid table specified: tableName”.

•Checks if the field name is present.  If not present, the system will trigger the error: No field name specified”.

•Checks if the field name is valid for the specified table. If not valid, the system will trigger the error:  “Invalid field specified: fieldname”.

•Checks if the field value is present.  If not present, the system will trigger the error: “Field value is mandatory”.

•Checks if the field Value is valid.  If not valid, the system will trigger the error: “Field value is not valid: fieldValue”.

•The system checks if the value is valid.

Example:

 

tableName = "ANALYSIS"

fieldName = "COMMON_NAME"

fieldValue = "DEFAULT"

status = ValidateFieldForTable(tableName, fieldName, fieldValue)

Returns: 

True if the fieldValue is valid for the tableName and fieldName specified. Otherwise it returns false.

 

ValidTestListsForSample    

Purpose:

The ValidTestListsForSample function checks the Sample Rules based on the fields and field values from the specified sample and returns the valid test lists.

Syntax:

status = ValidTestListsForSample(sampleNumber, "testListArrayName ")

sampleNumber – the sample number of the sample that is to be checked

testListArrayName – the name of the array which will contain the valid test lists returned by the function for the specified sample

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evaluates the testListArrayName.  If the argument is empty, the system triggers an error and returns a Last Error: ‘No array name specified’

•LIMS checks the sampleNumber.  If not found LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid sample specified: sampleNumber".

•LIMS checks the Samples Rules for the specified sample and returns the array of test lists.

NOTE:  This function does not save changes to the database.

 

NOTE: If the Business rule Include Non-Ruled Analyses for Sample Rules is set to true the output will also included all test lists for which there are no samples rules.


Example:

The following example checks the Sample Rules for sample 456

sampleNumber = 456

tlArrayName  = "ValidTListArray"

status = ValidTestListsForSample(sampleNumber, tlArrayName )

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

ValidTestListsForSampleFields    

Purpose:

The ValidTestListsForSampleFields function checks the Sample Rules based on the fields and field values defined in the fields and values arrays and returns the valid test lists for the specified field values.

Syntax:

status = ValidTestListsForSampleFields(fieldsArray, valuesArray, "testListArrayName ")

fieldsArray – an array of field names

valuesArray – an array containing field values for the fields specified in the fields array

testListArrayName – the name of the array which will contain the valid test lists returned by the function for the specified field values

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evaluates the testListArrayName.  If the argument is empty, the system triggers an error and returns a Last Error: ‘No array name specified’

•LIMS evaluates size of the fieldsArray.  If the size is not > 0, the system triggers an error and returns a Last Error: ‘No fields specified’.

•LIMS evaluates size of the fieldsArray and valuesArray.  If not the same size then LIMS will trigger the error "Field and value arrays are not the same size".

•LIMS checks the Samples Rules for the specified field values and returns the array of valid test lists.

NOTE:  This function does not save changes to the database.

 

NOTE: If the Business rule Include Non-Ruled Analyses for Sample Rules is set to true the output will also included all analyses for which there are no samples rules.


Example:

The following example checks the Sample Rules for the customer ‘ACME’

fieldsArray[1] = "CUSTOMER"

valuesArray[1] = "ACME"

tlArrayName  = "ValidTLArray"

status = ValidTestListsForSampleFields(fieldsArray, valuesArray, tlArrayName  )

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

VarValue    

Purpose:

The VarValue function retrieves the value of the specified variable.

Syntax:

variableValue = VarValue(varName)

varName –name of the variable the system will retrieve the value for.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that a variable name is specified.  If a variable name is not specified, LIMS returns the error: "Error: no variable name specified."

•LIMS sets the output variable: variableValue to the retrieved value.

NOTE: This function  does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code retrieves the value for  the variable: VN123.

varName = "VN123"
variableValue = VarValue(varName)

Returns:

The function returns the retrieved value.  It returns ERROR if an error is triggered.

 

Wait    

Purpose:

The Wait function causes the system to pause for a specified period of time.

Syntax:

status = Wait(seconds, runPostedRoutines)

seconds is the time in seconds

runPostedRoutines is a Boolean flag indicating if postedSubroutines should be processed.  If set to “F”, any posted subroutine will not be processed.  If set to “T”, posted subroutines will be processed during the Wait. If not specified, the argument defaults to True.

Comments:

A Wait call stops the current process and allows any items on the pending que to process, including posted subroutines (if runPostedRoutines = “T”).

Example:

 

‘Wait for 3 seconds

status = Wait (3)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

 

28.3.26  Lot Functions
 

Function

AddLotSource

AddSampleToLot

CloseLot

CreateLot

CreateLotResult

CreateLotSamplingPoint

DefaultLotNumber

DispositionLot

ImportLotResults

RefreshLot

RemoveLotSource

SplitLot

UpdateLotFld

UpdateLotSamplingPointFld

 

AddLotSource    

Purpose:

The AddLotSource function adds the specified source lot as a source to the specified sampling point of the specified lot.  Optionally, the specified quantity is associated with the new source.

Syntax:

status = AddLotSource(lotNumber, samplingPoint, sourceLot, quantity)

lotNumber – the lot number of the lot to which a source is to be added

samplingPoint – the sampling point to which the source is to be added (optional)

sourceLot – the lot to be identified as the source lot

quantity – the quantity to be associated with the source (optional)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that the lot with lot number equal to the value of sourceLot exists.  If it does not exist, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified source lot does not exist".

•LIMS retrieves the lot with lot number equal to the value of lotNumber.  If it does not exist, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified lot does not exist".

•If a value for samplingPoint is specified, LIMS will retrieve the sampling point whose name matches the value of samplingPoint and whose value of primary_grade is true.  If no value for samplingPoint is specified, LIMS will retrieve the sampling point that matches the values of sampling_point, product_grade, and product in the lot record.  In either case, if LIMS fails to retrieve the sampling point, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified grade sampling point does not exist on the lot".

•LIMS verifies if the specified source lot has already been assigned as a source lot for the sampling point.  If so, LIMS will trigger the error "Source lot is already assigned".

•LIMS assigns the specified source lot as a source for the sampling point.

•If a value for quantity is specified, LIMS will assign that quantity value to the source.

•If an audit reason has been specified for the lot table, LIMS will assign that reason for the pending database transaction of the lot.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

status = AddLotSource(83, "SP1", 74, 125)

Adds lot 74 as a source to sampling point "SP1" of lot 83 and associates the quantity 125 with the source.

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

AddSampleToLot    

Purpose:

The AddSampleToLot function adds a sample to a lot.

Syntax:

status = AddSampleToLot(sampleNumber, lotNumber, product, grade,  samplingPoint, version)

sampleNumber – integer defining the target sample.

lotNumber – integer defining the lot number of the target lot.

product – string defining the product.

grade – string defining the product grade.

samplingPoint – string defining the product sampling point.

Comments:

product must be defined in the Product Specifications table. 

A sample cannot be assigned to more than one lot.

NOTE:A database commit is part of this function.


Example:

status = AddSampleToLot(79, 2, "CET-PROD", "GENERIC", "CET-SP1", 1)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

CloseLot    

Purpose:

The CloseLot function closes the specified lot.

Syntax:

status = CloseLot(lotNumber)

lotNumber – the lot number of the lot to be closed

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the lot with the specified lot number.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Lot does not exist".

•If the lot is already closed, LIMS will trigger the error "Lot already closed".

•LIMS closes the lot.

•If an audit reason has been entered for the lot table, LIMS assigns it to the closed lot.

•LIMS assigns the audit type "CloseLot" to the lot.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example closes lot 57.

status = CloseLot(57)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

CreateLot    

Purpose:

The CreateLot function creates a new lot.

Syntax:

status = CreateLot(templateName, fieldArray, valueArray)

templateName – the name of the template from which the lot is to be created

fieldArray – an array that contains the names of the fields to be set

valueArray – an array that contains the values to be set

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no template exists with the specified name, LIMS will generate the error "Specified template does not exist".

•If the field and value arrays are not the same size, LIMS will trigger the error "Field array and value array must be the same size".

•If any of the field names in the fields array are link fields (foreign keys), LIMS will validate the corresponding entries in the values array.  If an invalid value is found, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid value for sample record".

•LIMS creates the new lot, assigns the values in the values array to the fields in the fields array, and updates all calculated fields.

•If an audit reason is specified for the lot table, LIMS assigns it to the new lot.

•LIMS saves the lot in the database using audit type "CreateLot".  If this fails, LIMS triggers the error "Error committing lot record".

•LIMS adds the lot to its context.

NOTE: This function does save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example creates a new lot from the template named "LOTTPL1" without assigning any field values (other than those in the template).

status = CreateLot("LOTTPL1", "", "")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

CreateLotResult    

Purpose:

The CreateLotResult function creates a lot result.

Syntax:

status = CreateLotResult(lotNumber, productGrade, stage, samplingPoint, specType, analysisName, resultName, units, resultValue, inSpecificaitonFlag, primaryInSpec)

lotNumber – integer defining the lot number of the target lot.

productGrade – string defining the product grade.

stage – string defining the product grade stage.

samplingPoint – string defining the product sampling point.

specType – string defining the product specification type.

analysisName – string definng the analysis

resultName – string defining the name of the result.

units – string defining the units of the result.

resultValue – value of the result.

inSpecificationFlag – Boolean, T, true, sets the result In Spec; F, false, sets the result out of specification.  The default values is true.

primaryInSpec – Boolean, T, true, sets the result In Spec; F, false, sets the result out of specification.  The default values is true.

Comments:

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

 

lotNumber = 2

productGrade = "GENERIC"

stage = "ONE"

samplingPoint = "CET_SP1"

specType = "NONE"

analysisName = "WEIGHT"

resultName = "Weight before"

units = "POUNDS"

value = 1.2

inSpecFlag = "T"

primaryInSpec = "T"

status = CreateLotResult(lotNumber, productGrade, stage, samplingPoint, specType, analysisName, resultName, units, resultValue, inSpecFlag, primaryInSpec)

Returns:

status =true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

CreateLotSamplingPoint    

Purpose:

The CreateLotSamplingPoint function adds a sampling point to a lot.

Syntax:

status = CreateLotSamplingPoint(lotNumber, samplingPoint, grade, fieldArray, valueArray, commitFlag)

lotNumber – the lot number of the lot for which a sampling point is to be created.

samplingPoint – the name of the sampling point to be created.

grade – the product grade for which the sampling point is to be created.

fieldArray – an array that contains the names of the fields to be updated

valueArray – an array that contains the values to be assigned to each of the fields

commitFlag – a boolean value that indicates if the sampling point should be committed to the database.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no value is specified for commitFlag, LIMS will proceed as if the value ‘T’ (true) had been specified.

•If the lot number is not specified or is zero, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid lot number specified".

•If LIMS is unable to retrieve the specified lot, LIMS will trigger the error "Lot does not exist".

•If the retrieved lot is marked as closed, LIMS will trigger the error "Lot is closed".

•If the lot already has a sampling point with the specified name and grade, LIMS will trigger the error "Lot sampling point record already exists".

•If LIMS is unable to retrieve the product record that matches the product name and product version specified in the lot record, LIMS will trigger the error "Product does not exist".

•LIMS checks if the product contains a sampling point with the specified sampling point name and grade.  If LIMS fails to retrieve this record, LIMS will trigger the error "Sampling / Grade does not exist for product".

•LIMS adds the specified sampling point to the lot.

•LIMS performs the following steps for each entry in the fieldsArray.

•If the specified field name does not exist in the lot sampling point table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid field for table".

•If the specified field value is not blank, LIMS will validate it by checking its datatype and also, if the field is a link or list field, by checking if the specifed value exists in the corresponding table or list.  If this validation fails, LIMS will trigger the error "invalid value for the field".

•LIMS updates the specified field with the specified value.

•If an audit reason was specified for the lot table, LIMS will apply that audit reason to the pending database transaction.

•If the value for commitFlag is true, LIMS will commit the changes to the database and write an entry to the audit log with audit type "UpdateLot".

•LIMS places the lot in context.

NOTE: This function save changes to the database if so requested.


Example:

The following example adds sampling point ‘TOP’ to grade ‘MEDIUM’ of lot 101 without committing the changes to the database.

f[1] = ‘DESCRIPTION’
v[1] = ‘Added via LIMS Basic’
status = CreateLotSamplingPoint(101, ‘TOP’, ‘MEDIUM’, f, v, ‘F’)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

DefaultLotNumber    

Purpose:

The DefaultLotNumber function returns the lot number of the lot that is currently open in the Lot Manager window or that is currently being imported by the importer.

Syntax:

lotNumber = DefaultLotNumber()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If a lot is currently open in Lot Manager or if a lot is currently being imported, LIMS will return its lot number.

•Otherwise, LIMS will return an empty string.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example returns the default lot number.

lotNumber = DefaultLotNumber()

Returns:

The function returns the default lot number if one is defined.  It returns an empty string otherwise.

 

DispositionLot    

Purpose:

The DispositionLot function dispositions a lot.

Syntax:

status = DispositionLot(lotNumber, lotName, samplingPoint, grade, disposition, commitflag)

lotNumber – Number of the lot to be dispositioned.

lotName – Name of the lot to be dispositioned.

samplingPoint – Sampling Point to be dispositioned.

grade – Grade to be dispositioned.

disposition – the disposition to be assigned to the lot/sampling point / grade.  This value must be a valid entry on the LOT_DISP list.

commitflag – Boolean that defines if the disposition is committed to the database or left in cache until a commit is triggered.  Default value is true, if this parameter is not specified, the disposition is committed to the database.

Comments:

When this function is trigger, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS evaluates the commit parameter.  If not specified, it is set to true.

•LIMS retrieves the lot record based on the Lot Number if specified.  If unsuccessful, LIMS populates the lastError with: "Lot record does not exist for specified lot number:" followed by the input.

•If the Lot Name is provided with the Lot Number, LIMS verifies that specified Lot Name matches the Lot Name on the lot record.  If not, LIMS populates the lastError with: "Lot record does not match the specified lot name:" followed by the input Lot Name.

•LIMS retrieves the lot record based on the Lot Name if the Lot Number is not specified.  If unsuccessful, LIMS populates the lastError with: "Lot record does not exist:" followed by the Lot Name.

•LIMS checks the status of the Lot.  If the Lot is closed, LIMS populates the lastError with: "Lot is closed:" followed by the Lot Name.

•LIMS updates the Lot disposition in cache.  If unsuccessful, LIMS populates the lastError with: "Error dispositioning lot".

•LIMS commits the disposition of the lot based on the commit flag.  If unsuccessful, LIMS populates the lastError with: "Error committing lot record".

NOTE: A database commit is a configurable part of this function.


Example:

status = DispositionLot( , "MYLOT" , "NONE", "GRADE1","APPROVE", "T")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

ImportLotResults    

Purpose:

The ImportLotResults function imports lot results from one lot to another.  Only results from the Primary grade of the source lot are imported.

Syntax:

status = ImportLotResults(lotNumber, sourceLotNumber, samplingPoint,resultNameArray, allSamplingPointsFlag)

lotNumber – (Required) integer defining the lot number of the target lot.

sourceLotNumber – (Required) integer defining the lot number of the source lot.

samplingPoint – (Required) string defining the sampling point results to be imported.

resultNameArray – (Required) one dimensional array containing the result names to be imported.

allSamplingPointsFlag – (Optional) Boolean. If true, all sampling points are imported.  If false, only the primary sampling point is imported. This value defaults to false.

analysisNameArray – (Optional) An array containing the analysis names corresponded to the result names in the resultNameArray.  Array size must match resultNameArray.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•If specified, the size of the analysisNameArray is checked against the resultNameArray.  If the sizes are different, the system triggers the error: “Result Names does not match the Analysis Names size”.

•Checks the lotNumber.  If not valid, the system triggers the error: “Lot number not valid: lotNumber”. If the lot does not exit, the system triggers the error: “Lot does not exist: lotNumber”.

•Checks the sourceLotNumber.  If not valid, the system triggers the error: “Lot number not valid: sourceLotNumber”. If the lot does not exit, the system triggers the error: “Lot does not exist: sourceLotNumber”.

•Checks the lot results for the sourceLotNumber.  If no results are found, the system triggers the error: “There are no lot results to import”.

•Checks the samplingPoint.  If not in the source lot, the system triggers the error: “Sampling point does not exist: samplingPoint”.

•The system tries to copy the specified results to the target lot.  If no results are found with the specified names, the system triggers the error: “No results to copy”.

•The system commits the results with audit reason “UpdateLot”.  If there is a problem with the commit, the system triggers the error: “Error committing lot record”.

NOTE:  A database commit is part of this function.


Example:

lotNumber = 2

sourceLotNumber = 3

samplingPoint = "CET_SP1"

resultNameArray[1] = "Before"

resultNameArray[2] = "After"

allSamplingPointsFlag = "T"

status = ImportLotResults((lotNumber, sourceLotNumber, samplingPoint,  resultNameArray, allSamplingPointsFlag)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

RefreshLot    

Purpose:

This function updates the lot information in cache based on the database information.

Syntax:

status = RefreshLot(lotNumber, allGrades)   

lotNumber – integer defining the lot number of the target lot.

allGrades – (optional) Boolean. If true, all grades are refreshed.  If false, only the primary grade is refreshed.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:
