AddAdHocProjectResult    

Purpose:

The AddAdHocProjectResult function adds an ad hoc result to a project.

Syntax:

status = AddAdHocProjectResult(projectNumber, resultName, value)

projectName – the value stored on the Project.Name field within the database for the project.

resultName – the name of the Ad Hoc Component to be added to the project.

value – entry to be posted for the Ad Hoc component.

Comments:

resultName must be defined in the Ad-hoc Components table. 

NOTE: This  function does not do a database commit.


Example:

status = AddAdHocProjectResult("CHRG-001", "AH_COLOR", "Red")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

AddProjectItem    

Purpose:

The AddProjectItem function adds an item of a particular type to the specified project.  Sets the specified field values of the added item.  If the Project Template has an Item Template defined for the Item Table, that template will be used when adding items to the Item Tahle.

Syntax:

status = AddProjectItem(projectName, itemTable, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

projectName – the name of the project to which an item is to be added

itemTable – the table in which the item is to be stored.  This can be any of the tables identified in table master as a user-defined child table of the project table.  This parameter will default to the Item Table configured on the project template if no value is specified.

fieldsArray – an array of field names for the item table

valuesArray – an array of field values for the item table

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the project whose name is equal to the value of projectName.  If the project does not exist, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified project does not exist".

•If the project has already been marked as closed, LIMS will trigger the error "Project closed".

•If the number of elements in the fieldsArray does not match the number of elements in the valuesArray, LIMS will trigger the error "Field and value array sizes do not match".

•If an Item Table is specified, LIMS creates a new project item for the table whose name is equal to the value of itemTable.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified item table is not valid for the project".

•If an Item Table is not specified, LIMS creates a new project item for the item table specified on the Project Template.

•LIMS uses the increment named "KEY_NUM" to generate a new key value for the item.

•If the specified item table has a column named "ORDER_NUMBER", LIMS will set the value of this column equal to the number of items that now exist in the table for the specified project.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

fieldsArray[1,1] = "CUSTOMER"
fieldsArray[2,1] = "SUPPLIER"
valuesArray[1,1] = "ABC"
valuesArray[2,1] = "ACME"

status = AddProjectItem("PROJ1", "COMPANIES", fieldsArray, valuesArray)

Adds an item to the project named "PROJ1" and marks it for storage in the table named "COMPANIES".  The item has customer name "ABC" and supplier name "ACME".


Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

AuthorizeProjectResult    

Purpose:

The AuthorizeProjectResult function authorizes a project result.

Syntax:

status = AuthorizeProjectResult(projName, resultNumber, commitFlag)

ProjectName – the unique identifier of a project stored in the PROJECT.NAME field.

resultNumber – the unique identifier of the result stored in the PROJECT_RESULT.RESULT_NUMBER field.

commitFlag – Boolean parameter that determines if the function immediate commits the update to the database or posts the information to cache.  This value defaults to false.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evaluates the projectName parameter.  If the project is not located, the system triggers and error and returns a Last Error: ‘Project does not exist’ followed by the parameter.

•LIMS evaluates the projectName parameter.  If the project is closed, the system triggers and error and returns a Last Error: ‘Project is closed: ’ followed by the parameter.

•LIMS evaluates the specified result.  If the current status is N, X, or A, the system triggers and error and returns a Last Error: ‘The object can not be authorized because object status is A, or X, or N.

•The system performs the update to the cache.  If the commitFlag is "T", the changes are committed to the databae.

NOTE: This function saves results to the database only if explicitly requested.


Example:

status = AuthorizeProjectResult("PROJ-55", 19, "T")

Returns:

true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

ChargeProject    

Purpose:

The ChargeProject function creates an entry in the charges table with an amount that equals the sum of all completed tests of the specified project that have not yet been charged.

Syntax:

status = ChargeProject(projectName)

projectName – the name of the project to be charged

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the project name is blank or missing, LIMS will trigger the error "No project name specified".

•LIMS retrieves the specified project.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid project name".

•LIMS creates a new charge entry using the charge code specified in the project record.

•LIMS assigns a new entry number to the charge.

•LIMS retrieves all of the project’s completed tests that have not yet been charged and updates them with the newly assigned entry number to indicate that they have been charged.

•LIMS updates the charge with the sum of the cost units of the completed tests.

•LIMS saves the charge using audit type "TableUpdate".

•LIMS saves the tests using audit type "ModifyTest".

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example accrues charges for the completed tests of the project named "MY_PROJ".

status = ChargeProject("MY_PROJ")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

CloseProject    

Purpose:

The CloseProject function closes a project. 

Syntax:

status = CloseProject (projectName)

projectName – string defiing the name of the target project.

Comments:

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

status = CloseProject("STUDY_01")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

CopyProject    

Purpose:

The CopyProject function creates a new project based on an existing project with significant control over the child information and samples to be copied.

Syntax:

newName = CopyProject(originalProject, newProjectName, fieldsArray, valuesArray, itemTablesToCopy, copySamples, sampleFieldsArray, sampleValuesArray, sampleOnlyFlag, copyChildrenFlag, mergeAliquotTests, assignOriginalComponents)

originalProject – name of the project to be copied.

newProjectName – name of the project to be created.

fieldsArray – a one dimensional array that contains the name of the project fields to be set when creating the new project.  Fields not specified will inherit their settings from the original project or state of the created project.

valuesArray – a one dimensional array that contains the values for the project fields to be set when creating the new project.

itemTablesToCopy – a 1-D array with the names of the Item Tables that contains the records to be copied to the new project.

copySamples – a Boolean parameter that determines if this function copies the samples of the target project into the project being created.  This parameter defaults to False. 

sampleFieldsArray – a one dimensional array that contains the name of the sample fields to be set when creating the samples for the new project.  Fields not specified will inherit their settings from the original sample or state of the created sample.

sampleValuesArray – a one dimensional array that contains the values for the sample fields to be set when creating the samples for the new project.

sampleOnlyFlag – a Boolean parameter that determines if this function copies the samples of the target project into new project without copying any tests or result records.  This parameter defaults to False. 

copyChildrenFlag – a Boolean parameter that determines if this function copies subsamples and aliquots of the project samples.  This parameter defaults to False. 

mergeAliquotTests – a Boolean parameter that determines if this function collapses the tests from aliquot samples into the parent samples of the target project when creating the samples for the project being created.    This parameter defaults to False.

assignOriginalComponents – a Boolean parameter that determines if this function copies all the components from the target tests when creating the samples.  If set to true, only non-optional components are created for the copied tests.  This parameter defaults to False. 

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•The LIMS evaluates the originalProject parameter.  If the value is not a valid project name, the system returns the following LastError: ‘Project does not exit:’ followed by the input value.

•The LIMS evaluates the newProjectName parameter.  If the project name already exists in the database, the system returns the following LastError: ‘Error project already exists with name:’ followed by the input value.

•The LIMS creates the new project and copies the item table(s) specified on the itemTableToCopy.  If the system fails to create the project the system returns the following LastError: ‘Error copying project:’ followed by the value of the originalProject parameter.

•The LIMS then attempts to update the project fields with the specified values using the parameters FieldsArray and ValuesArray.  If the system fails to update the project the system returns the following LastError: ‘Error setting fields on project:’ followed by the value of the newProjectName parameter.

•The LIMS copies the samples if copySample parameter is set to true.

•The LIMS copies aliquots and subsamples if the copyChildrenFlag is set to true.

•The LIMS copies aliquoted tests to the parent sample if the mergeAliquotTests flag is set to true.

•The LIMS assigns only non-optional components to the created tests if the assignOriginalComponents flag is set to true.  If false, the system copies all the components on the target tests to the created tests.

Example:

The following example copies the project 17 to project NEW_17.  All the samples are copied with the aliquots collapsed and only original components assigned.

projField[1] = "DESCRIPTION"
projField[2] = "CUSTOMER"
projVal[1] = "Monthly project for Customer 1"
projVal[2] = "CUST_01"
samField[1] = "DESCRIPTION"
samField[2] = "CUSTOMER"
samVal[1] =  "Monthly retainer sample for Customer 1"
samVal[2] = "CUST_01"
itemTablesToCopy[1] = "PRJ_CHILD1"
itemTablesToCopy[2] = "PRJ_CHILD2"

newName = CopyProject("17", "NEW_17", projField, projVal, itemTablesToCopy, "T", samField, samVal, "F", copyChildrenFlag, "T", "T")

Returns:

The function returns the value new project name if it completes successfully.  It returns the value ERROR if an error is triggered.

 

CreateProject    

Purpose:

The CreateProject function creates a project.

Syntax:

projectName = CreateProject(templateName, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

templateName – Name of the Project Template assigned to the created project.

fieldsArray – One dimensional array of field names to be populated when the project is created.

valuesArray – One dimensional array of values to be posted to the field in the corresponding position in the fieldsArray.

Comments:

Mandatory fields are not enforced, however, formula fields will execute when this functions is used..  NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.

Example:

fieldsArray[1] = "DESCRIPTION"

fieldsArray[2] = "PRODUCT"

valuesArray[1] = "Comparison of pulp content"

valuesArray[2] = "PAPER"

projectName = CreateProject("CUST-PROD", fieldsArray, valuesArray)

Returns: 

projectName = "STD-980415-1"

 

DefaultProjectName    

Purpose:

The DefaultProjectName function returns the name of the project that is currently open in the Project Manager window or that is currently being imported by the importer.

Syntax:

projectName = DefaultProjectName()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If a project is currently open in Project Manager or if a project is currently being imported, LIMS will return its name.

•Otherwise, LIMS will return an empty string.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example returns the default project name.

projectName = DefaultProjectName()

Returns:

The function returns the default project name if one is defined.  It returns an empty string otherwise.

 

ProjectResults    

Purpose:

The ProjectResults function returns an array of result values for all samples in a project for a specific result name and result status. 

Syntax:

status = ProjectResults(projectName, resultName, statusString, "resultArrayName")

projectName is the name of the project.

resultName is the name of the result for which values will be returned. 

statusString is the set of result statuses for which result values will be returned. 

resultArrayName contains the returned values.  For all samples in the named project, the formatted entry of results of resultName will be returned in the arrayName. 

Comments:

Only those results with a status matching statusString will be returned. 

This function is useful for generating summary results at a project level.  

Example:

status = ProjectResults("STD-980415-2", "LOD", "AEMRX", "resultArray")

Returns:

resultArray[1] = 16

resultArray[2] = 10

resultArray[3] = 15

 

RejectProjectResult    

Purpose:

The RejectProjectResult function rejects a project result.

Syntax:

status = RejectProjectResult(projectName, resultNumber, commitFlag)

projName – the unique identifier of a project stored in the PROJECT.NAME field.

resultNumber – the unique identifier of the result stored in the PROJECT_RESULT.RESULT_NUMBER field.

commitFlag – Boolean parameter that determines if the function immediate commits the update to the database or posts the information to cache.  This value defaults to false.

Comments:

Result status must be Entered, Modified, or Authorized. 

If commitFlag is T, the data is committed, otherwise the data is committed during the next LIMS commit to the project (the next commit may happen when a later LIMS Basic function performs a commit or when a Save operation is performed).

Example:

status = RejectProjectResult("PROJ-55",731,"T")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

RemoveProjectItem    

Purpose:

The RemoveProjectItem function removes the specified entry in an items table from the project.

Syntax:

status = RemoveProjectItem(projectName, itemTable, seqNum, commitFlag)

projectName – name of the project containing the item to be removed.

itemTable – name of the item table within the project containing the item to be removed.

seqNum – an integer representing the sequence number of the item to be removed.

commitFlag – Boolean setting defining if the function should immediately post changes to the database or if the changes should be cached until a save is triggered.  This value defaults to false if unspecified.  Values starting with "T" or "Y" are interpreted as true.

NOTE: The seqNum must be an integer. 


Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that the specified project is valid.  If the project is invalid, the LIMS returns:  "Specified project does not exist:" followed by the input value.

•LIMS verifies that the project is not closed.  If the project is closed, the LIMS returns: "Project closed:" followed by the invalid string.

•LIMS verifies that the item table specified is valid.  If the entry is not valid, LIMS returns the error: "Specified item table has no entries for the project."

•LIMS verified that the specified sequence number is valid.  If the entry is not valid, LIMS returns the error: "Specified item is not valid for the project."

•LIMS removes the item from the item table and writes the event to the audit trail.

NOTE:  This function immediately save changes to the database if the commit flag is set to true.  Otherwise, the function saves changes to cache until a save is triggered.


Example:

The following statement removes the fourth item on the Project Attach table that is associated with Project: DEMO-1.

projectName = "DEMO-1"
itemTable =  "PROJECT_ATTACH"
seqNum = 4
commitFlag = "T"

status = RemoveProjectItem(projectName, itemTable, seqNum, commitFlag)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

SetOrCreateProjectResult    

Purpose:

The SetOrCreateProjectResult function creates a project result or updates an existing result with the ability to control the entry value, unit, limits, and reportable flag.  

Syntax:

status = SetOrCreateProjectResult(projectName, resultNameOrNumber, value, units, type, minLimit, maxLimit, reportable)

projectName – the name of the project for which a result is to be updated

resultNameOrNumber – either the result name or the result number of the project result record(s) to be created or updated.

value – value to be posted to the entry field of the result.

units –value to be posted to the units field of the result.

type – value specifying the type of result.

minLimit – value to be posted to the minimum field of the result.

maxLimit – value to be posted to the maximum field of the result.

reportable – Boolean to be posted to the reportable field of the result. 

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS interprets the type. 

•If a type value is provided, LIMS uses the first character of the input for the type.

•If the type is not specified and the minLimit and MaxLimit are not specified, the type defaults to T (text).

•If the type is not specified and the minLimit or MaxLimit is specified, the type defaults to N (numeric).

•LIMS verifies the projectName is valid.  If the project is not found, LIMS returns the error: "Project does not exist:" followed by the input value.

•If the project is already closed, LIMS will trigger an error and return: "Project already closed:" followed by the specified project name.

•LIMS will perform one of the following steps for the retrieved project.

•If the specified resultNameOrNumber can be interpreted as an integer, LIMS will retrieve the project result whose result number matches this value.

•Otherwise, LIMS will retrieve all project results whose result name match the specified value.

•LIMS updates the retrieved result(s) or creates a new result if no result was retrieved.

•The LIMS updates the audit trail and the result fields as specified by the inputs. 

•If an audit reason is set for the project result table, that reason is posted to the audit trail.

•If there is no audit reason on the project result table, the audit reason set on the project table is posted to the audit trail.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.

 

CAUTION:  This function does not enforce entry types when updating the entry field.  For example, it is possible to enter text into a numeric result or a non-list value into a list result type.


Example:

The following example creates a project result (or updates an existing result if LB Result is located).

projectName = "7"
resultNameOrNumber = "LB Result"
value = 2.5
units = ""
type = "N"
minLimit = 7
maxLimit =  10
reportable = "T"

status = SetOrCreateProjectResult(projectName, resultNameOrNumber, value, units, type, minLimit, maxLimit, reportable)

Returns:

The function returns true if it completes successfully.  Otherwise, it returns false with the appropriate Last Error.

 

UpdateProjectFld    

Purpose:

The UpdateProjectld function updates a field in a project.

Syntax:

status = UpdateProjectFld(projectName, field, value, commitFlag)

projectName – String defining the target project.

field – string defining the field to be updated.

value – value to be posted to the defined field.

commitFlag – Boolean.  "T", true, commits the value to the database.  "F", false, retains the value in cache until the object is committed.  The default value is false.

Comments:

CAUTION:  This function should be used with caution as any field in the PROJECT table may be updated. 


Example:

status = UpdateProjectFld("STD-980415-1", "TITLE", "Comparison of Vendors", "T")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

UpdateProjectFlds    

Purpose:

This function updates multiple field values for a single project.

Syntax:

status = UpdateProjectFlds(projectName, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

projectName – string defining the name of the target project.

fieldsArray – one dimensional array of project field names.

valuesArray – one dimensional array of values to populate into the corresponding fields.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•The LIMS compares the fieldsArray with the valuesArray inputs.  If they are not the same size, LIMS returns the last error: Error: field and value array must be the same size.

•LIMS retrieves the specified project.  If this fails, LIMS returns the last error: Project does not exist.

•LIMS evaluates the entries in the fieldsArray.  If an invalid field is found, LIMS returns the last error:  "Invalid field for table: Project."

•LIMS evaluate the entries in the valuesArray.  If an invalid value is found, LIMS returns the last error: "Invalid value for project record: " + <value> + "for field: " + <field>.

•LIMS updates the fields in cache.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.

Example:

projectName = "DEMO_123"

fieldsArray[1] = "DESCRIPTION"

fieldsArray[2] = "CUSTOMER"

valuesArray[1] = "Function demonstration"

valuesArray[2] = "ENTRY_1"

status = UpdatePorjectFlds(projectName, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

Returns:

status = true if function is successful, otherwise false.

 

UpdateProjectItemFld    

Purpose:

The UpdateProjectItemFld function updates a field in an item record of a project.

Syntax:

status = UpdateProjectItemFld(projectName, itemTable, seqNum, field, value, commitFlag)

projectName – the name of the project to be updated

itemTable – the name of the item table to be updated

seqNum – the sequence number of the record to be updated in the item table

field – the name of the field to be updated

value – the value to be stored in the specified field

commitFlag – the value True if the change should be saved in the database, and the value False otherwise

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the value of commitFlag begins with the letter T or Y, LIMS will interpret it as the value True.  Otherwise, LIMS will interpret it as the value False.

•LIMS retrieves the project with the specified name.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified project does not exist".

•If the project is marked as closed, LIMS will trigger the error "Project closed".

•LIMS retrieves all items for the specified project and item table.  If no items are found, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified item table has no entries for the project".

•LIMS finds the entry that has the specified sequence number.  If no item with that sequence number is found, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified item table is not valid for the project".

•If the item table does not have a field with the specified field name, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid field for table".

•If the specified field is a link field, and if the specified value is not blank and does not exist in the corresponding linked table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid value for item record".

•LIMS updates the specified item as follows:

•If the specified field is a date field, and if the specified value cannot be interpreted as a valid date, LIMS will apply a null value instead.

•If the specified field is a boolean field, LIMS will apply the value True if and only if the specified value argument begins with the letter "T".  Otherwise, LIMS will apply the value False.

•If the specified field is an interval field, and if the specified value is blank, LIMS will apply the value 0 (zero) instead.

•Otherwise, LIMS will apply the value as specified.

•If an audit reason is specified for the project table, LIMS will apply it to the pending transaction.

•If the value of commitFlag is begins with the letter "T" or "Y", LIMS will save the change to the project in the database.  The audit type "UpdateProject" will be applied to the transaction.  If the database update fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Error committing project".

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database if the commitFlag is True


Example:

The following example relates to a scenario in which LIMS is configured to allow projects to have related documents.  These related documents are stored in an items table named "RELATED_DOCS".  The number of pages of document 4 of project "PROJ1" is being updated to 18. The change is immediately saved to the database.

status = UpdateProjectItemFld("PROJ1", "RELATED_DOCS", 4, "NUMBER_OF_PAGES", 18, True)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

UpdateProjectResultFld    

Purpose:

The UpdateProjectResultFld function updates the value of a field for a project result record.

Syntax:

status = UpdateProjectResultFld(projectName, resultNameOrNumber, field, value, commitFlag)

projectName – the name of the project for which a result is to be updated

resultNameOrNumber – either the result name or the result number of the project result record(s) to be updated.

field –the name of the project result field to be updated

value – the value to be stored in the specified field

commitFlag – Boolean setting defining if the function should immediately post changes to the database or if the changes should be cached until a save is triggered.  This value defaults to false if unspecified.  Values starting with "T" or "Y" are interpreted as true.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the project whose name is equal to the specified project name.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger an error and return: "Project does not exist:" followed by the specified project name.

•If the project is already closed, LIMS will trigger an error and return: "Project already closed:" followed by the specified project name.

•LIMS will perform one of the following steps for the retrieved project.

•If the specified resultNameOrNumber can be interpreted as an integer, LIMS will retrieve the project result whose result number matches this value.

•Otherwise, LIMS will retrieve all project results whose result name match the specified value.

•LIMS performs the following steps for each retrieved result record.

•If the project result table does not have a field whose name is equal to the specified field name, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid field for table".

•If the specified field is a link field, and if the specified value does not appear in the linked table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid value for result record".

•LIMS converts the specified value to the appropriate data type for the specified field and updates the batch result.

•If an audit reason is specified for the project result table, LIMS will apply it to the pending database transaction.  Otherwise, if an audit reason is specified for the batch table, LIMS will apply that reason instead.

•If the value true is specified for the commit flag, LIMS will save the changes to the database and write a record to the audit trail with audit type "UpdateProject".  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error: "Error committing sample record".

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database immediately if the commit flag is true.  If the flag is set to false, this function posts changes to cache until a save is triggered.


Example:

The following example sets the description of each project result with project name "PROJECT1" and result name "AVGCONC".

Status = UpdateProjectResultFld( "PROJECT1" , "LB Result" , "FORMATTED_ENTRY" , "37", "T")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

 

28.3.36  Prompt Functions
Prompt functions are used to interact with the user when the code is running.  They may prompt the user to make a choice or they may simply keep the user informed of the status of the code being run, such as using a progress dialog or message box.

NOTE:  These Prompt functions do not perform actions on the database therefore there are no changes to save when these functions are used.

 

 

Function

BrowseForRecord

BrowseSelect

BrowseSelectSample

CloseProgressDialog

InputBox

InputPassword

MsgBox

MsgBoxHtml

MultiColumnSelectFromArray

MultiSelectDialog

MultiSelectFromArray

OpenHtmlDisplayDialog

OpenProgressDialog

PromptForConstants

PromptForDate

PromptForDateTime

PromptForDirectory

PromptForFile

PromptForInteger

PromptForListEntry

PromptForLongText

PromptForNumber

PromptForPrinter

PromptForYesNo

SelectFromArray

StatusMessage

UpdateProgressDialog

 

BrowseForRecord    

Purpose:

The BrowseForRecord function displays a select dialog to the user populated with qualifying records from the specified table.

Syntax:

status = BrowseForRecord(table, title, whereClause)

table – the name of the table the LIMS should use to construct the selection options on the prompt.  This input is case sensitive.

title – the string to be displayed in the title bar of the prompt presented to the user.

whereClause – a string defining the restriction .

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the entries from the specified list and displays these entries in a select dialog with the specified title.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code prompts the user to ‘Pick one’ from a list of analyses that have a Common Name of Impurity:

table = "ANALYSIS"
title = "Pick one"
whereClause = "COMMON_NAME = ‘IMPURITY’"
status = BrowseForRecord(table, title, whereClause))

Returns:

The function returns the value of true if a record is selected.  The function returns the value false if the prompt is canceled or no record is selected.

 

BrowseSelect    

Purpose:

The BrowseSelect function displays a select dialog with a list of values from a field in a table and selects a table record based on the value chosen by the user.

Syntax:

status = BrowseSelect(tableName, displayField, title, whereClause)

tableName – the name of the table to be browsed.  Value must be capitalized.

displayField – the field in the table to display.  Value must be capitalized.

title – displayed in the title bar of the select dialog.

whereClause – where clause to be applied to the browse to restrict the records presented.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS collects the valid records based on the input and displays a select dialog with the title as specified.

•When the select dialog is closed the LIMS performs one of the following actions:

•If the OK button was select, the highlighted record is placed in context and the status is set to True.

•If the Cancel button was select, no record is placed into context and the status is set to False.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example browses the Common Name table on all the records that begin with the letters: IMP.

tableName = "COMMON_NAME"
displayField = "NAME"
title = "Pick a Common Name"
whereClause = "NAME LIKE ‘IMP%’ "

status = BrowseSelect(tableName, displayField, title, whereClause)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered or if the select dialog is closed with Cancel.

 

BrowseSelectSample    

Purpose:

The BrowseSelectSample function opens the LIMS Sample Prompter with the specified sample status settings and restricted by a where clause.

Syntax:

status = BrowseSelectSample(statusString, whereClause)

statusString – list of sample status setting to be active on the Sample Prompter.

whereClause – string defining the where clause that restricts the browse sample and the selectable samples.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS open the Sample Prompter with the specified status boxes checked.  The sample browse is restricted to qualifying samples based on the where clause.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens the Sample Prompter restricted to samples with a description starting with ‘M’ and the following active:
Unreceived, Incomplete, In Progress, Complete.

statusString = "U|I|P|C"
whereClause = "DESCRIPTION like ‘M%’"

status = BrowseSelectSample(statusString, whereClause)

NOTE:  Unreceived U, Incomplete I, In progress P, Complete C, Authorized A, Rejected R, Canceled X.


Returns:

The function returns the value true if a sample is selected on the prompt.  It returns the value false if the Sample Prompter is canceled.

 

CloseProgressDialog    

Purpose:

The CloseProgressDialog function closes a progress dialog.

Syntax:

status = CloseProgressDialog()

Comments:

 

Example:

status = CloseProgressDialog()

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

InputBox    

Purpose:

The InputBox function displays a dialog box that prompts the user for input.

Syntax:

x = InputBox(prompt, title, default)

prompt – text displayed in the dialog box.

title – text displayed in the title bar of the dialog box (optional).

default – default response if no other input is entered.

Comments:

 

Example:

x = InputBox("What is your name?", "NAME", " ")

Returns:

x contains the information typed by the user.

 

InputPassword    

Purpose:

The InputPassword function displays a dialog box that prompts the user for a password. 

Syntax:

x = InputPassword(prompt)

Comments:

This function is used when a password is needed for security reasons.  The password is displayed as asterisks.

Example:

x = InputPassword(prompt)

Returns:

x contains the information typed by the user.

 

MsgBox    

Purpose:

This function displays a message in an information box. It has been enhanced to include an optional timeout parameter, which, if specified in milliseconds, closes the message box after the specified number of milliseconds have elapsed if the user has not responded within the stipulated time.

Syntax:

MsgBox(string, milliSeconds)

string – the string value to be displayed in the information box.

milliSeconds – optional parameter (integer) defining the number of milliseconds after which the message box will be closed provided the user has not responded.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS determines if a string has been provided to be displayed.

•If the above condition is true, then LIMS verifies if the optional milliseconds parameter has been provided and if it is an integer greater than zero. If not, then the milliseconds parameter is not considered. In this case, the user must click the OK button to continue.

•If a valid integer value is provided as milliseconds, then LIMS displays the message box and continues to display it till the specified number of milliseconds have elapsed. If there is no user response within the stipulated time, then LIMS automatically closes the message box.

•If a valid milliseconds value is not provided, then the message box simply displays the specified string and remains active until the user responds to it.

CAUTION:  M0366 WebLIMS does not support the millisecond parameter.  As a result, this parameter is ignored in the Web environment.


Example:

The following example displays the message "Hello, World" and continues to display it for 5 seconds (5,000 milliseconds). If there no user intervention within this period, then this message box is automatically closed.

MsgBox("Hello, World", 5000)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully, else, it returns false if an error is triggered.

 

MsgboxHtml  

Purpose:

The MsgboxHtml function opens the an information box displaying a specified string with defined formatting.

Syntax:

MsgboxHtml(htmlString, optionalWidth, optionalHeight)

htmlString – text string with HTML formatting.

optionalWidth – integer defining the width of the displayed information box.

optionalHeight – integer defining the height of the displayed information box.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•The LIMS opens an information box with the defined text.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens an information box with the text "This should be bold text" set in bold font.

htmlString = "<strong> This should be bold text </strong>"
optionalWidth = 100
optionalHeight = 300
MsgboxHtml(htmlString, optionalWidth, optionalHeight)

Returns:

The function does not return a value.

 

MultiColumnSelectFromArray    

Purpose:

The MultiColumnSelectFromArray function prompts the user to select zero, one, or more rows from a grid that has one or more columns of values.  The Query Select Dialog opened when using the MultiColumnSelectFromArray or SQLSelect Lims Basic functions can search text in the array. Refer to the SQLSelect function for more on using this search capability.

Syntax:

numSelections = MultiColumnSelectFromArray(array, title, "selectionArrayName", headerArray)

array – the array of values from which the user is prompted to select.  This array can have either one or two dimensions.  The first dimension is the row number and the second dimension is the column number.

title – the title to be displayed on the window

selectionArrayName – the name of the array in which the selected rows should be copied

headerArray – an optional 1-D array of column headers to be displayed above the grid

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies the correct number of arguments for each element.  If any element has more than two dimensions, LIMS will trigger the error "Input array has more than two dimensions".

•LIMS opens a window in which it displays the rows to be selected.  Any array element that has only one dimension will be displayed in column 1.  A blank will be shown in any column for which no array element is specified.  Blank rows will not be shown, unless explicitly specified in the array.

•The user may select one or more rows.  The user may select all rows by pressing the Select All button.  LIMS waits for the user to press either the OK button or the Cancel button.

•If the user presses the Cancel button, LIMS will return the value zero.

•If the user presses the OK button, LIMS will return the total number of rows selected by the user.  If this number is greater than zero, the selected rows will be stored in an array whose name is equal to the specified selection array name.

NOTE:  If no rows are selected, the selection array will not be created.


Example:

The following example illustrates how the system handles missing array elements.  The example causes the system to display a grid with two rows and three columns and 3 column headers.  The top-left cell will have the value "A" and the bottom-right cell will have the value "Z".  The other four cells will be blank.

In other words, missing columns are shown, but missing rows are not.

myArray[1,1] = "A"
myArray[3,3] = "Z"
myHeaderArray [1] = "Header 1"
myHeaderArray [2] = "Header 2"
myHeaderArray [3] = "Header 3"
myTitle = "Select a row"

num = MultiColumnSelectFromArray(myArray, myTitle, "myResult", myHeaderArray)

If the user selects the second row only, the result will be as follows:
– num will have the value 1.
– myResult[1,1] and myResult[1,2] will have the value "".
– myResult[1,3] will have the value "Z".

Returns:

The function returns the number of rows selected by the user if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

MultiSelectDialog    

Purpose:

The MultiSelectDialog function opens a two-column, multi-select dialog.

Syntax:

numSelections = MultiSelectDialog(sourceArray, sourceTitle, selectedArray, selectedTitle, dialogTitle, "selectionArrayName")

sourceArray – an array that contains the elements that are available to be selected

sourceTitle – the label to be displayed above the list of unselected items

selectedArray – an array that contains the elements that should be shown as already selected

selectedTitle – the label to be displayed above the list of selected items

dialogTitle – the title to be displayed in the title bar of the window

selectionArrayName – the name of the array in which the selected items should be stored when the user presses the OK button.  This variable is a string variable that contains the name of an array; the variable is not the array itself.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS creates a two-column, multi-select dialog with the specified dialogTitle in the title bar of the window.

•LIMS displays the elements in the sourceArray in the column on the left along with the sourceTitle.  Elements that are present in both the sourceArray and the selectedArray are not displayed in the column on the left.

•LIMS displays the elements in the selectedArray in the column on the right along with the selectedTitle.

•LIMS opens the dialog and waits for the user to move elements between the two columns and press the OK or Cancel button.

•If the user presses the Cancel button, LIMS will set the variable DialogCanceled equal to true and trigger the error "User canceled the dialog".

•If the user presses the OK button, LIMS will set the variable DialogCanceled equal to false and store the elements in the column on the right (i.e., the selected items) in an array whose name is equal to the value of selectionArrayName.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example allows the user to select from the elements A, B, and C.  Element B will be shown as the default selection.  The first selection made by the user is shown in a message box.  Note that it is not necessary for element B to be included in the myList array.

myList[1] = "A"
myList[2] = "B"
myList[3] = "C"
mySelection[1] = "B"
numSelections = MultiSelectDialog(myList, "Left", mySelection, "Right", "Select some values", "yourSelection")
if (not DialogCanceled) then
    if (numSelections > 0) then
        MsgBox(yourSelection[1])
    end if
endif

Returns:

The function returns the number of selected elements if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

The system variable: DialogCanceled is set to true if the dialog is closed using the Cancel button; otherwise true.

 

MultiSelectFromArray    

Purpose:

The MultiSelectFromArray function prompts the user to select one or more values from an array.

Syntax:

numSelections = MultiSelectFromArray(array, title,"selectionArrayName",multiSelectFlag)

array – an array that contains the elements that are available for selection.

title – the title to be displayed in the title bar of the window

selectionArrayName – the name of the array in which the selected items should be stored when the user presses the OK button.  This variable is a string variable that contains the name of an array; the variable is not the array itself.

multiSelectFlag – the value true if the user should be permitted to select more than one value from the array (optional; the default is false)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the value of multiSelectFlag is true, LIMS will open a dialog with a list box from which the user is permitted to select one or more entries.  The elements in the specified array will be displayed as the available entries.  The specified title will be displayed in the title bar of the window.

•If the value of multiSelectFlag is false, LIMS will open a dialog with a list box from which the user is permitted to select no more than one entry.  The elements in the specified array will be displayed as the available entries.  The specified title will be displayed in the title bar of the window.

•If the user makes at least one selection and presses the OK button, LIMS will save the selections in an array whose name is equal to the value of selectionArrayName.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example allows the user to select one element from the elements A, B, and C.  The selection made by the user is shown in a message box.

myList[1] = "A"
myList[2] = "B"
myList[3] = "C"
num = MultiSelectFromArray(myList, "Select One", "yourSelection", "F")
if (num > 0) then
    MsgBox(yourSelection[1])
endif

Returns:

The function returns the number of selected elements if it completes successfully.  It returns the value zero if the user cancels or makes no selections..

 

OpenHtmlDisplayDialog    

Purpose:

This function displays an HTM dialog with user configurable button.

Syntax:

buttonIndex = OpenHtmlDisplayDialog(htmlString, width, height, buttonLabelsArray, "idValueArrayName")

htmlString – html string to be displayed within the dialog.

width – integer defining the default width of the dialog.

height – integer defining the default height of the dialog.

buttonLabelsArray –array defining the labels of the dialog button.

idValueArrayName – the name of the output array which will contain the values from the input elements in the HTML dialog at the time the dialog is closed. Input elements are identified in the HTML.  This parameter is optional.

NOTE: This function does not support Select type elements (drop downs). Also, not all Input elements behave the same way in the web client as the Windows client.


Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•The system evaluates the htmlString input.  If empty, the system returns the last error: “No html specified”.

•The system evaluates the buttonLabelsArray input.  If there are no entries in the array, the system returns the last error: “No button labels specified”.

•If there are more than 3 entries in the buttonLabelsArray, the system returns the last error: “Too many button labels specified.”

•The system displays the HTLM display dialog.  If specified the <INPUT> elements are analyzed and values returned in the output array.

Example:

db = CHR(34)

htmlString = " <html> <form> First name: <input type=" & db & "text" & db & " name="

htmlString = htmlString & db & "firstname" & db & "id=" & db & "name1" & db & "><br> "

htmlString = htmlString & "Last name: <input type=" & db & "text" & db & " name=" & db

htmlString = htmlString & "lastname" & db & " id=" & db & "name2" & db  

htmlString = htmlString & " maxlength=" & db & "10" & db & "><br></form> </html>"

width = 400

height = 800

buttonLabelsArray[1] = "Enter"

buttonLabelsArray[2] = "Cancel"

buttonIndex = OpenHtmlDisplayDialog(htmlString, width, height,buttonLabelsArray , "idValueArrayName")

Returns:

The variable buttonIndex returns an integer defining the button selected to close the HTML dialog.  If the dialog could not be opened by LIMS, the buttonIndex is populated with ERROR.

 

OpenProgressDialog    

Purpose:

The OpenProgressDialog function displays a progress dialog.

Syntax:

status = OpenProgressDialog(title, message, showPercentage, allowCancelFlag)

title – the text to be displayed in the title bar of the dialog

message – the text to be displayed in the main body of the dialog

showPercentage – a boolean flag that indicates whether a percentage should be displayed.  This argument is optional.  If not specified, the value "F" (false) will be used.

allowCancelFlag – a boolean flag that indicates whether the user should be permitted to cancel the current operation.  This argument is optional.  If not specified, the value "T" (true) will be used.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If a progress dialog is already open, LIMS will return false without triggering an error.

•LIMS displays a progress dialog with the specified title and message.  If the value of showPercentage is true, a percentage will be shown.  If the value of allowCancelFlag is true, a Cancel button will be shown.

Example:

The following example displays a progress dialog with a percentage and without a Cancel button.

status = OpenProgressDialog("Progress Dialog", "Testing 1, 2, 3", "T")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

PromptForConstants   

Purpose:

This function will prompt for one or more LIMS Constants. The dialog displays the specified constants so the user can update the entries. If no User Constants exist the value of the LIMS Constant will be displayed.  When the user clicks OK to the dialog the appropriate Constants will be updated and/or new constants will be created.

Syntax:

status = PromptForConstants(title, constantsArray, userName, "valuesArrayName")

title – the title to be displayed in the Prompt dialog

constantsArray – A one or two dimensional array of LIMS constant names to be retrieved. If the array is two-dimensional, it is assumed that the constant names are in the first column.

username – The name of the user that will have user constants updated or created.  If no user is specified the LIMS Constants table will be updated with the new values.

valuesArrayName – If the function is successful and the user presses the OK button, then the new values for the LIMS Constants specified will be returned in this array.

Comments:

When this function is invoked, LIMS performs the following activities:

•If no constants are specified, LIMS returns false and sets lasterror to "No LIMS Constants specified".

•LIMS verifies that the constants specified are valid LIMS Constants. If not, status will be set to false and lasterror will be set to "Invalid LIMS Constant specified."

•LIMS displays a user dialog with a field for each LIMS Constant specified. The dialog will have an OK and Cancel button.

•The label for each field will be the "pretty"  name of the LIMS Constant (i.e. the name of conatant without underscores using mixed cased), or if the NLS entry LIMS_Constant_<constant name>  exists, then the NLS entry will be used.

The type of field for each constant will be dictated by the Field Type, Link Table, List Key and Where Clause fields in the LIMS Constant table.

If Cancel is pressed by the user, then the function will return false , newValuesArray will be empty and lasterror set to "Cancelled by user".

If OK is pressed, LIMS will create or update the User Constants for the specified user.

Note: Role constants can be updated by logging in as the role.

Example:

The following example will display a dialog with two fields labeled "My Visual Workflows" and "Last Search". The dialog will display the current value from the User Constants table for the specified user.

constantArray[1] = "MY_VISUAL_WORKFLOWS"

constantArray[2] = "LAST_SEARCH"

status = PromptForConstants(constantArray, "newValuesArray", True)

Returns:

status = true if the dialog is displayed and the user presses OK, otherwise false.

 

PromptForDate    

Purpose:

The PromptForDate function prompts the user to enter a date.

Syntax:

dt = PromptForDate(title, default)

title – the prompt title to be displayed in the dialog

default – the default date value to be displayed in the dialog

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no default date is specified, LIMS will use the current date as the default date.

•If the default date cannot be interpreted as a valid date, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid default date value".

•LIMS prompts for a date using the specified prompt title and default date.

•If the user presses the OK button in the dialog, LIMS will return the entered date.

•Otherwise (i.e., if the user presses the Cancel button), LIMS will return an empty string.

Example:

The following example causes LIMS to prompt for a date with prompt title "Please enter a date" and today’s date as the default.

dt = PromptForDate("Please enter a date", "")

Returns:

The function returns a date if the user presses the OK button; an empty string if the user presses the Cancel button, and the value false if an error is triggered.

 

PromptForDateTime    

Purpose:

The PromptForDateTime function prompts for a date time value.

Syntax:

dateTime = PromptForDateTime(title, default)

title – the prompt text to be shown (optional)

default – the default value to be displayed (optional)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no default value is specified, LIMS will use the current date and time as the default value.

•If the specified default value does not represent a valid date and time, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid default date time value".

•LIMS opens a Date Time Prompt Dialog, displays the specified title and default value, and waits for the user to press OK or Cancel.

•If the user presses Cancel, LIMS will return a null value.

•If the user presses OK, LIMS will return the value entered by the user.

Example:

The following example prompts for an approval date.

dateTime = PromptForDateTime("Approval Date", "")

Returns:

The function returns a date time value.

 

PromptForDirectory    

Purpose:

The PromptForDirectory function opens the Windows browse dialog.  When confirmed with OK, the system returns the directory the dialog was focused on.

NOTE: This function is not available in the web client.


Syntax:

directoryName = PromptForDirectory(Title, default)

Title – the string value enter for this parameter is used as the title of the Windows browse dialog without any additional formatting.

default – optional parameter to define the starting point of the folder browse.  If not specified, the browse start defaults to the LIMS working directory.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS determines if a title has been provided.

•If the title is empty or not provided, the LIMS opens the Browse For Folder dialog with the title: Save As.

•If a title is provided, the LIMS opens the Browse For Folder dialog with the specified title.

•When the Browse For Folder dialog is closed LIMS returns the folder if OK was selected. 
LIMS returns the directoryName = ERROR if Cancel was selected or the window was closed using the X in the upper right of the Browse For Folder dialog.  In both of these cases, the DialogCanceled variable is set to true.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens the Windows browse dialog with the title abc.  When the dialog is closed, the path of the directory is returned in the variable: directoryName.

Title = "abc"

Default = curdir()

directoryName = PromptForDirectory(title, default)

Returns:

The function returns the path of the directory if it completes successfully.

The system variable: DialogCanceled is set to true if the dialog is closed using the Cancel button; otherwise true.

 

PromptForFile    

Purpose:

The PromptForFile function prompts for a file name.

Syntax:

file = PromptForFile(title, default)

title – the prompt text to be shown (optional)

default – the default value to be displayed (optional)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS opens a File Dialog, displays the specified title and default value, and waits for the user to press OK or Cancel.

•If the user presses Cancel, LIMS will return a null value.

•If the user presses OK, LIMS will return the value entered by the user.

Example:

The following example prompts for the name of a report file.

file = PromptForFile("Report File", "")

Returns:

The function returns a string that represents a file name.

 

PromptForInteger    

Purpose:

The PromptForInteger function prompts for an integer value.

Syntax:

 

 

integer = PromptForInteger(minVal, maxVal, default, title)

minVal – the minimum integer value to be permitted (the default is 0 if no value is specified)

maxVal – the maximum integer value to be permitted (the default is 1 000 000 000 if no value is specified)

default – the default value to be displayed (the default is 0 if no value is specified)

title – the prompt text to be shown (optional)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the specified minimum value is greater than the specified maximum value, LIMS will trigger the error "Min value greater than max value".

•LIMS opens an Integer Prompt Dialog, displays the specified title and default value, and waits for the user to press OK or Cancel.

•If the user presses Cancel, LIMS will return a null value.

•If the user presses OK, LIMS will return the value entered by the user.

Example:

The following example prompts for a number of copies between 1 and 5 with a default value of 1.

integer = PromptForInteger(1, 5, 1, "Number Of Copies")

Returns:

The function returns an integer.

 

PromptForListEntry    

Purpose:

The PromptForListEntry function displays a set of list entries for the use to select from.

Syntax:

ans = PromptForListEntry(listName, title)

listName – the name of the list the LIMS should use to construct the selection options on the prompt.

title – the string to be displayed in the title bar of the prompt presented to the user.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the entries from the specified list and displays these entries in a select dialog with the specified title.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code prompts the user to select an entry from the list: ADHOC_TYPE.  The Prompt has the title: Pick one.

listName = "ADHOC_TYPE"
title = "Pick one"
ans = PromptForListEntry(listName, title)

Returns:

The function returns the value of the selected entry if one is selected.  The function returns the null value if the prompt is canceled.

The system variable: DialogCanceled is set to true if the dialog is closed using the Cancel button; otherwise true.

 

PromptForLongText    

Purpose:

The PromptForLongText function displays a configurable prompt to the user.  The configuration options include a default text value and a maximum restriction on the input text.

Syntax:

ans = PromptForLongText(title, default, maxsize)

title – the string to be displayed in the title bar of the prompt presented to the user.

Default – string to be inserted into the prompt entry field when the prompt is presented to the user.

maxSize – integer limiting the number of characters accepted by the prompt.  This valued defaults to 254 characters if no value is specified.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS displays the prompt dialog with the specified default text and title.  The system limits entry into the prompt as defined by the maxSize.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code prompts the user with the title: Enter Information.  The default string is displayed in the entry field when the prompt is presented.  The entry is limited to a 500 characters.

Title = "Enter information"
default = "The following information was collected during the inspection process."
maxSize = 500
ans = PromptForLongText(title, default, maxSize)

Returns:

The function returns the value entered in the prompt entry field if the user closes the prompt with OK.  The function returns the null value if the prompt is canceled.

 

PromptForNumber    

Purpose:

The PromptForNumber function prompts for a numeric value (not necessarily an integer).

Syntax:

num = PromptForNumber(minVal, maxVal, default, title)

minVal – the minimum value to be permitted (the default is 0 if no value is specified)

maxVal – the maximum value to be permitted (the default is 1 000 000 000 if no value is specified)

default – the default value to be displayed (the default is 0 if no value is specified)

title – the prompt text to be shown (optional)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the specified minimum value is greater than the specified maximum value, LIMS will trigger the error "Min value greater than max value".

•LIMS opens a Numeric Prompt Dialog, displays the specified title and default value, and waits for the user to press OK or Cancel.

•If the user presses Cancel, LIMS will return a null value.

•If the user presses OK, LIMS will return the value entered by the user.

Example:

The following example prompts for a magnification between 1.5 and 3.5 with a default of 2.

num = PromptForNumber(1.5, 3.5, 2, "Magnification")

Returns:

The function returns a number.

 

PromptForPrinter    

Purpose:

The PromptForPrinter function prompts the user to select a printer name from a list of available printers.

Syntax:

printerName = PromptForPrinter(promptString)

promptString – the prompt title to be displayed

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no prompt string is specified, LIMS will use the value identified by the NLS key "LIMSBasicSelectPrinter" instead.

•LIMS builds a list of all printers that are defined in the Microsoft Windows operating system environment in which LIMS is currently executing.

•LIMS prompts the user to select a printer from this list and uses the prompt string as the title.

•If the user selects a printer name, LIMS will return it.

•Otherwise, LIMS will return an empty string.

Example:

The following example will cause LIMS to display the prompt "Select a printer for the report" along with a list of available printers.

printerName = PromptForPrinter("Select a printer for the report")

Returns:

The function returns a string.

 

PromptForYesNo    

Purpose:

The PromptForYesNo function prompts for either a Yes or a No answer.

Syntax:

answer = PromptForYesNo(title)

title – the prompt text to be shown (optional)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS displays the specified title in a messagebox with a Yes button and a No button, and waits for the user to press one of those buttons.

•If the user presses the Yes button, LIMS will return the value "Yes".

•Otherwise, LIMS will return the value "No".

Example:

The following example prompt the user if entries should be saved.

answer = PromptForYesNo("Save Entries?")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

SelectFromArray    

Purpose:

The SelectFromArray function displays an array of items in a select dialog and returns the item selected by the user.

Syntax:

x = SelectFromArray(array, title)

array is a two-dimensional array.  The first column of the array is the label displayed in the dialog.  The second column is the value associated with the label.

title is the text that is displayed in the title bar of the  dialog box.

Comments:

 

Example:

•a[1,1] = "Choice 1"

•a[1,2] = 1

•a[2,1] = "Choice 2"

•a[2,2] = 2

•x = SelectFromArray(a, "Make your choice please.")

Returns:

x contains the item selected by the user.

 

StatusMessage    

Purpose:

The StatusMessage function displays a messge in the status bar of the active LIMS window.

Syntax:

status = StatusMessage(message)

message is displayed in the status bar.

Comments:

 

Example:

status = StatusMessage("Configuration specific test to be displayed.")    

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

UpdateProgressDialog    

Purpose:

The UpdateProgressDialog function updates the message and progress bar in a  progress dialog.

Syntax:

status = UpdateProgressDialog(message, percentage)

message is displayed in the dialog box.

percentage is the percent complete and controls how much of the progress bar is complete.

Comments:

 

Example:

status = UpdateProgressDialog("Testing 4, 5, 6",  20)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

 

28.3.37  Reporting Functions
 

Function

CrystalClearCache

CrystalDescribeReports

CrystalInjectNLS

CrystalSetTableLocations

OpenPDFWindow

PrintCrystalReport

PrintLabels

PrintMultipleLabels

PrintRaw

RunReport
