CrystalClearCache

Purpose:

The CrystalClearCache function clears the Crystal Report templates (.rpt) cached for the current LabWare login session.

Syntax:

status = CrystalClearCache()

Comments:

 

Example:

The following example will clear the Crystal Report template cache for the current LabWare login session.

status = CrystalClearCache()

Returns:

The status variable is set to True if the function completes successfully, else status is set to False.

 

CrystalDescribeReports

Purpose:

The CrystalDescribeReports function creates a text file with information on the Crystal Reports Report Objects in the main report and the sub-reports of a Crystal Reports template file.

Syntax:

status = CrystalDescribeReports(dirInString, dirOutString, showProgress)

dirInString – directory location of the target reports.

dirOutString – directory location of the text files.

showProgress – a Boolean flag that can be used to control the display of a progress dialog (optional).

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•If the dirInString is not valid, the status is set to “False” and the lastError variable is set to “Specified input directory does not exist:  <dirInString>”.

•If the dirOutString is not valid, the status is set to “False” and the lastError variable is set to “Specified output directory does not exist:  < dirOutString >”.

•Displays a progress dialog if showProgress is set to “T”.  The title bar displays the text ‘DescribeReports”.

•Creates a text file (.txt) in the dirOutString directory for each of the reports in the dirInString directory.  The name of the text file will be the same as the name of the .rpt file that it describes.

Example:

The following example will create a .txt file in the “reports_post” directory for each of the .rpt files in the “reports_pre” directory.  A progress dialog will be displayed during the operation.

dirInString = “c:\LabWare_dev\reports_pre\”

dirOutString = “c:\LabWare_dev\reports_post\”

showProgress = “T”

status = CrystalDescribeReports(dirInString, dirOutString, showProgress)

Returns:

The status variable is set to True if the function completes successfully, else status is set to False.

 

CrystalInjectNLS

Purpose:

The CrystalInjectNLS function inserts National Language Support (NLS) for Text Objects in Crystal Reports template files (.rpt).

Syntax:

status = CrystalInjectNLS(dirInString, dirOutString, showProgress)

dirInString – directory location of the target report files.

dirOutString – directory location of the modified report files.

showProgress – a Boolean flag that can be used to control the display of a progress dialog (optional).

Comments:

The function performs the following:

1.  If the dirInString is not valid, the status is set to “False” and the lastError variable is set to “Specified input directory does not exist:  <dirInString>”.

2.  If the dirOutString is not valid, the status is set to “False” and the lastError variable is set to “Specified output directory does not exist:  < dirOutString >”.

3.  Displays a progress dialog if showProgress is set to “T”.  The title bar displays the text ‘Injecting NLS Keys into Reports”.

4.  Performs the following for each of the report files in the dirInString:

•Creates and injects NLS keys into the Text Objects in the report file.  Note:  The NLS key will be truncated at 50 characters.

•Logs errors to the “rpt_inject_nls_keys.log” file in the LabWare client directory.

•Updates the “xx_NLS_ReportText.txt” file in the LabWare client directory with the NLS key and NLS description.

•Copies the modified report file to the dirOutString.

5.  Updates the “InjectNLSKeysIntoReports.log” file with summary information before displaying the log file to the user.

NOTE:  The function will only modify Crystal Reports Text Objects.  Text values in Crystal Reports Formulas will not be updated.  The Crystal Reports Callback function NLS can be used to handle text values in formulas.


Example:

The following example will create a xx_NLS_ReportText.txt file in the LabWare client directory and copy a modified version each of the .rpt file in the “reports_pre” directory to the “reports_post”.  A progress dialog will be displayed during the operation.

dirInString = “c:\LabWare_dev\reports_pre\”

dirOutString = “c:\LabWare_dev\reports_post\”

showProgress = “T”

status = CrystalInjectNLS(dirInString, dirOutString, showProgress)

Returns:

The status variable is set to true if the function completes successfully, else status is set to false.

 

CrystalSetTableLocations    

Purpose:

The CrystalSetTableLocations function is used to update the data source location of the main report and sub-reports in the Crystal Report template files to the data source location of the currently logged in user.

Syntax:

status = CrystalSetTableLocations(dirInString, dirOutString, showProgress, setAllFlag)

dirInString – directory location of the target Crystal Report template files.

dirOutString – directory location of the modified report files.

showProgress –Boolean flag that is used to control the display of a progress dialog (optional).

setAllFlag – a Boolean flag that is used to control whether the data source location for the main report and all sub-reports are updated.  If set to True, the data source location of the main report and all sub-reports are set to the data source location of the currently logged in user.  If set to False, the data source of the main report and sub-reports with the same data source location as the main report will be updated to the data source location of the currently logged in user.  Sub-reports with a data source location different to that of the main report will not be updated.  The default value False is applied if the Boolean Flag is not specified.

Comments:

For those special reports that intentionally point to multiple databases, this function should not be applied.

Example:

dirInString = “c:\LabWare_dev\reports_pre\”

dirOutString = “c:\LabWare_dev\reports_post\”

showProgress = “T”

setAllFlag = “T”

status = CrystalSetTableLocations(dirInString, dirOutString, showProgress, setAllFlag)

Returns:

The status variable is set to true if the function completes successfully, else status is set to false.

 

DisplayReport    

Purpose:

The DisplayReport function displays a Crystal Report in the Crystal Report Viewer window.

Syntax:

status = DisplayReport(reportName, argArray, valArray, languagePrefix, locale)

reportName – a string representing the name of the report query tag to be displayed.

The argArray and valArray arrays are used to specify the filter criteria for record selection for the report.

argArray – one dimensional array of filter criteria field names (optional).

valArray – one dimensional array of filter criteria values (optional).

languagePrefix – the language prefix for the NLS translation to be displayed for Text Objects in the Crystal Report (optional).

locale – the Preferred Viewing Local to be passed from LabWare to Crystal Reports.  The Crystal Reports functions “ContentLocale” and “SelectionLocale” will be set to the specified value.

Comments:

The function performs the following:

1.  Compares the size of the argArray and valArray.  If not the same size, the lastError variable is set to “Error:  Arguments and value are not the same size”.

2.  If a valid Query Tag does not exist for the specified reportName, the lastError is set to “Query tag does not exist:  <reportName>”.

3.  Evaluatees languagePrefix.  If the language prefix is not specified for the function, the language prefix for the logged in User will be applied.

Example:

The following example displays the Sample Summary report for sample 49 with the German NLS translation displayed as the text in Text Objects in the report.  The Crystal Reports functions “ContentLocale” and “SelectionLocale” will be set to “de_DE” respectively.

reportName = “SAMPLE_SUMMARY”

argArray[1] = “SAMPLE.SAMPLE_NUMBER”

valArray[1] = 49

languagePrefix = “DE_”

locale = “ceLocaleGerman”

status = DisplayReport(reportName, argArray, valArray, languagePrefix, locale)

Returns:

The status variable is set to true if the function completes successfully, else status is set to false.

 

OpenPDFWindow   

Purpose:

The OpenPDFWindow function opens a PDF Viewer window.

Syntax:

status = OpenPDFWindow(reportFileName, reportNumber)

reportFileName – the name of the report file to be opened

reportNumber – the report number of the report to be opened

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

If no argument values are specified, LIMS opens a PDF Viewer window without opening a report file in it.

If a report number is specified, and no report exists in the "REPORTS" table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid reportNumber specified".  If the report number does exist, LIMS will open the corresponding report in a PDF Viewer window.

If no report number is specified, and the file indicated by the reportFileName argument does not exist, LIMS will trigger the error "The reportFileName does not exist".  If the file does exist, LIMS will open it in a PDF Viewer window.

If LIMS is unable to open the PDF Viewer window, it will trigger the error "Unable to open the LIMS PDF Viewer".

If values are specified for both arguments, the reportFileName argument will be ignored.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens report number 183.

status = OpenPDFWindow("", 183)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

PrintCrystalReport     

Purpose:

This function generates a report using the Crystal Reports template and allows the passing of supported arguments.

Syntax:

status = PrintCrystalReport(crystalReport, args, argValues, outputFileName, printerName, reportTitle, languagePrefix, locale).

crystalReport – text parameter that specifies the name of the Crystal Reports (.rpt) file.

The args and argValues arrays are used to specify the filter criteria for record selection for the report.

args – one dimensional array used to specify the table_name.field_name for the filter criteria.

argValues – one dimensional array used to specify the filter values.

outputFileName – text parameter that specifies the file path and the name for the .lim file that will be generated.

printerName – text parameter that specifies the printer name to submit the printer job to.  If the value of this argument is set to DEFAULT or is not specified, the default printer will be used.  If printerName = “NO-PRINT” then the output file will be generated however no print operation will take place.

reportTitle – text parameter that specifies the title of the report.

languagePrefix – the language prefix for the NLS translation to be displayed for Text Objects in the Crystal Report (optional).

locale – the Preferred Viewing Local to be passed from LabWare to Crystal Reports.  The Crystal Reports functions “ContentLocale” and “SelectionLocale” will be set to the specified value.

Comments:

The function performs the following:

•Compares the size of the args and argValues.  If not the same size, the lastError variable is set to “Error:  field and value arrays must be the same size”.

•If the crystalReport is invalid, the lastError variable is set to “File does not exist:  <reportName>”.

•If the value of the reportName does not contain a directory (i.e., does not contain the “\” character, LabWare will add the path of the report files directory to the report name.

•Evaluates languagePrefix.  If the language prefix is not specified for the function, the language prefix for the logged in User will be applied.

Example:

The following example prints a Sample Summary report for Sample 49 with the German NLS translation applied to Text Objects in the Crystal Report to “PRINTER_A”.  The “Sample_Summary.lim” file will be saved to the LabWare client directory.  The Crystal Reports functions “ContentLocale” and “SelectionLocale” will be set to “de_DE” respectively.

sampleNumber = 49

crystalReport = “samplesummary.rpt”

args[1] = “SAMPLE.SAMPLE_NUMBER”

argValues[1] = 49

outputFileName = “Sample_Summary.lim”

printerName = “PRINTER_A”

reportTitle = “Sample Summary”

languagePrefix = “DE_”

locale = “ceLocaleGerman”

status = PrintCrystalReport(crystalReport, args, argValues, outputFileName, printerName, reportTitle, languagePrefix, locale).

Returns:

The status variable is set to true if the function completes successfully, else status is set to false.

 

PrintLabels    

Purpose:

The PrintLabels function prints bar code labels on a bar code printer.

Syntax:

status = PrintLabels(labelName, numLabels, tableName, tableKeyValue)

Comments:

labelName is the name of the label in the Label Printer table.

tableName and tableKeyValue are the table and table key value for the formula fields and are only valid for single keyed tables.  Label fields must be of type Status, Field, or Formula. LIMS Basic statements for the Formula fields reference the tableName and tableKeyValue.  tableName must be capitalized.

Example:

labelName = "SAMP_LABEL"

numLabels = 2

tableName = "SAMPLE"

tableKeyValue = 132    ‘ Sample number is the key field in the sample table

status = PrintLabels(labelName, numLabels, tableName, tableKeyValue )

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

PrintMultipleLabels    

Purpose:

This function prints multiple bar code labels within the same print job.

Syntax:

Status = PrintMultipleLabels(labelNameArray, numLabels, tableNameArray, tableKeyArray, printJobName, printerName, prntRaw)

labelNameArray – string defining the record on the Label Printer table to use to generate the labels for the corresponding record.

numLabels – integer defining the number of labels to generate for each object.

tableNameArray – one dimensional array defining the tables of the records to be printed.

tableKeyArray – one dimensional array defining the key values for the records to be printed.

printJobName – the title to be shown as the job name in the print spooler.

printerName – the name of the printer on which the data is to be printed. 

NOTE: It is recommended to leave this parameter unspecified if printRaw is set to true.  In this case, the label printer name will be retrieved from the LIMS client configuration.

prntRaw – optional Boolean defining the type of print job.  This value defaults to false if not specified.  When set to true, the print job is label printer compatible.  When false, the print job is standard windows printer compatible.

Comments:

 

Example:

printJobName = GetIncrement("BarcodeJob")

printJobName = "Barcode set: " + str(printJobName)

labelNameArray[1] = "SAM_LABEL"

labelNameArray[2] = "TEST_LABEL"

tableNameArray[1] = "SAMPLE"

tableNameArray[2] = "TEST"

tableKeyArray[1] = "289"

tableKeyArray[2] = "593"

prntRaw = "T"


Status = PrintMultipleLabels(labelNameArray, numLabels, tableNameArray, tableKeyArray, printJobName,  , prntRaw)

Returns:

status = true if the function is successful, otherwise false.

 

PrintRaw    

Purpose:

The PrintRaw function prints a data string to a Windows printer in raw format.

Syntax:

status = PrintRaw(printer, jobname, stringData)

printer – the name of the printer on which the data is to be printed. 

jobname – the title to be shown as the job name in the print spooler

stringData – the data to be printed

NOTE: The Data to be printed should contain printer control charcters that will be recognized by the printer specified in the printer argument.


Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If a printer name is specified, and it has not been defined in the Windows operating system as a printer, LIMS will trigger the error "Printer does not exist".

•LIMS opens a job with specified job name on the Windows print spooler for the specified printer, and sends it the string data to be printed in raw format.  (This step is performed using Windows API calls.  If any API call fails, LIMS will return the value False without displaying a LIMS error message in addition to any message that Windows may already have displayed.)

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example prints the text "Hello World" on the printer named "HP Deskjet 650" with job name "My Print Job".

status = PrintRaw("HP Deskjet 650", "My Print Job", "Hello World")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

PrintReport    

Purpose:

The PrintReport function prints a secure report on a printer.

Syntax:

status = PrintReport(reportNumber, printerName)

reportNumber – the report number of the report to be printed

printerName – the name of the printer on which the report is to be printed. If set to "DEFAULT" then Windows default printer is used.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no record exists in the "REPORTS" table for the specified report number, or if the report file specified in the record does not exist, LIMS will trigger the error "Print report failed".

•LIMS asks the operating system to print the specified file, based on the file type association.  (Exception: for PDF files, LIMS will use Acrobat regardless of file type association.)  If an error status is returned, LIMS will trigger the error "Print report failed".

•LIMS increments the print count for the report and saves this update in the database.

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example prints report number 19 on the "LAB108" printer.

status = PrintReport(19, "LAB108")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

RunReport    

Purpose:

The RunReport function is used to generate the specified report.

Syntax:

status = RunReport(reportName, argArray, valArray, filename, printerName, reportNumber, numCopies, languagePrefix, locale)

reportName – text parameter that specifies the name of the report query tag to be executed.

The argArray and valArray arrays are used to specify the filter criteria for record selection for the report.

argArray – one dimensional array used to specify the table_name.field_name for the filter criteria.

valArray – one dimensional array used to specify the filter values.

filename – text parameter that specifies the file name under which the report that is generated should be saved.  If no path is included in the file name, it will be stored in the LabWare client directory.  Valid file extensions are .lim, .doc, .txt, .rtf, .csv, .xml, .html, .xls and .xlsx.

printerName – text parameter that specifies the printer name to submit the printer job to. If set to "DEFAULT" then Windows default printer is used.

reportNumber – a number that identifies a Secure Report.

numCopies – integer defining the number of copies to be generated.

languagePrefix -  the language prefix for the NLS translation to be displayed for Text Objects in the Crystal Report (optional).

locale – the Preferred Viewing Local to be passed from LabWare to Crystal Reports.  The Crystal Reports functions “ContentLocale” and “SelectionLocale” will be set to the specified value.

Comments

This function performs the following:

1.  Compares the size of the argArray and valArray.  If not the same size, the lastError variable is set to “Number for arguments does not equal the number of values”.

2.  If the reportName is invalid, the lastError variable is set to “Query Tag does not exist:  <reportName>”.

•If the value of the reportName does not contain a directory (i.e., does not contain the “\” character, LabWare will add the path of the report files directory to the report name.

3.  If a report number is specified, LabWare performs the following actions:

•LabWare will retrieve the information about the Secure Report from the database.  If this fails, LabWare will trigger the error “Invalid report number specified”.

•If a file name is specified, LabWare will update the database records with that file name.

•If no file name is specified, LabWare will use the report file name form the database record instead of the file name argument in the subsequent steps.

4.  If the language prefix is not specified for the function, the language prefix for the logged in User will be applied.

5.  LabWare runs the report in the background and saves the output to disk as follows:

•If the file name includes a directory path, LabWare will store the output using that file name.

•If the file name does not include a directory path, LabWare will store the output using that file and place it in the LabWare client directory.

•If no file name was specified, LabWare will use the report name specified in the query tag and replace the file type with “.lim”.  LabWare will select a directory path as follows.

•If the report name specified in the query tag includes a directory path, LabWare will store the output file in that location.

•If the report name specified in the query tag does not include a directory path, LabWare will store the output file in the current default directory.

•If a printer name was specified, LabWare will print the output file on that printer.

•If the process of running and/or printing the report fails, LabWare will trigger the error “Error” running report.

 

NOTE:  When a report number is specified, LabWare will log the reporting action to the database and perform a database commit.  Otherwise, this function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example prints two copies of the Sample Summary report for Sample 49 with the German NLS translation applied  to “PRINTER_A”.  The Crystal Reports functions “ContentLocale” and “SelectionLocale” will be set to “de_DE” respectively.  The “sampleSummary.lim” file will be saved to the LabWare client directory.  LabWare will update the database with the Secure Reports information for this action. 

reportName = “SAMPLE_SUMMARY”

argArray[1] = “SAMPLE.SAMPLE_NUMBER”

valArray[1] = 49

filename = “sampleSummary.lim”

printerName = “PRINTER_A”

reportNumber = 7843

numCopies = 2

languagePrefix = “DE_”

locale = “ceLocaleGerman”

status = RunReport(reportName, argArray, valArray, filename, printerName, reportNumber, numCopies, languagePrefix, locale)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

RunTableManagerReports   

Purpose:

This function generates reports for the tables in Table Master that qualify as defined by the whereClause.

Syntax:

Status = RunTableManagerReports(whereClause, dirName, printerName)

whereClause – text parameter defining the restriction on the tables evaluated by this function.

dirName – text parameter defining the path that PDF copies of the generated reports are posted to.

printerName – text parameter defining the printer the reports are submitted to. If set to "DEFAULT" then Windows default printer is used.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS creates a log defining reporting activities in the dirName directory: TableManagerReports.log.  If dirName argument is not provided then the working directory is used.

•If a dirName is provided, the LIMS generates the report(s) and posts a PDF copy to the specified directory.

•If a table qualifies for reporting, and no report is specified within Table Master, the system posts an entry to the log: " : table has no report name"

•If a table qualifies and has a report and there are no records on the table, the system posts an entry to the log: " : table is empty"

•If a printerName is specified, the system submits the report to the prints.  If no printerName is specified, the system does not submit the report to a printer.

•The system generates the report and verifies that it was successful.  If successful, the system posts an entry to the log:  " : report ran successfully".  If not successful, the system posts an entry to the log:  " : error occurred while running report".

Example:

The following LIMS Basic generates the Customer report for all customer records:

whereClause = "where name = 'CUSTOMER'"

dirName = curdir() + "\RTMR\"

printerName = ""

status = RunTableManagerReports(whereClause,dirName,printerName)

Returns:

If successful, the function returns status = true.  If not, the function returns status = false.

 

 

28.3.38  Result Functions

AddAdHocResult    

Purpose:

The AddAdHocResult function adds an add hoc component with a formatted value and allows inclusion of additional arguments.

Syntax:

status = AddAdHocResult(sampleNumber, testNumber,resultName,value, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

sampleNumber –  The number of the sample for which ad hoc result is added

testNumber – The number of the test to which ad hoc component is added

resultName – The name of the ad hoc component.  The name must correspond to an entry in the Ad Hoc Components table.

value - The value entered for the ad hoc component (This is not the formatted value)

fieldsArray – the array of fields from the RESULT table.

valuesArray – the array of values assigned to the fields of the RESULT table .

Comments:

NOTE: A database commit is not part of this function.


Example:

sampleNumber = 8
testNumber = 9
resultName = M0282_ADHOC
Value = 8
fieldsArray[1,1] = "MINIMUM"
fieldsArray[2,1] = "MAXIMUM"

valuesArray[1,1] = "1"
valuesArray[2,1] = "10"

status = AddAdHocResult(sampleNumber, testNumber,resultName,value, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

Returns:

The function returns true when successful, false when unsuccessful.

 

AssignOptionalResults    

Purpose:

The AssignOptionalResults function marks the specified optional results as mandatory.

Syntax:

status = AssignOptionalResults(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNamesArray)

sampleNumber – the sample number of the sample whose optional results are to be made mandatory

testNumber – the test number of the test whose optional results are to be made mandatory

resultNamesArray – the array of result names that are to be made mandatory

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the sample whose value of sample number is equal to sampleNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample does not exist".

•From this sample, LIMS retrieves the test whose value of test number is equal to testNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Test does not exist".

•If the test has already been reviewed or canceled, LIMS will trigger the error "Cannot assign to reviewed or canceled tests".

•LIMS creates result records for all optional results of the retrieved test that do not yet have a result record.  LIMS marks as mandatory all of the optional results whose name matches one of the names in the resultNamesArray.  LIMS adds those result records to its context.  If no result records exist for any of the specified result names, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified result does not exist on the test".

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

resultNamesArray = "WT_BEFORE"
resultNamesArray = "WT_AFTER"

status = AssignOptionalResults(1404, 2033, resultNamesArray)

Marks the "WT_BEFORE" and "WT_AFTER" optional results as mandatory for test 2033 of sample 1404.

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

AuthorizeResult    

Purpose:

The AuthorizeResult function authorizes the specified result.

Syntax:

status = AuthorizeResult(sampleNumber,testNumber, resultNumber)

sampleNumber – the sample number of the result to be authorized

testNumber – the test number of the result to be authorized

resultNumber – the result number of the result to be authorized

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the sample whose sample number is equal to the value of sampleNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample does not exist".

•LIMS retrieves the test whose test number is equal to the value of testNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Test does not exist".

•LIMS retrieves the result whose result number is equal to the value of resultNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the result "Result does not exist".

•LIMS determines if the result can be authorized by the current user.  If present, the CONTINUE_AUTHORIZE status rule is processed.  If the result cannot be authorized, LIMS will trigger an error using the status rule message as the error message.

•LIMS authorizes the result.

•LIMS sets the audit type of the sample equal to "Authorize".

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example authorizes result 5641, which belongs to test 3382, which in turn belongs to sample 997.

status = AuthorizeResult(997,3382,5641)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

CalculateResult    

Purpose:

The CalculateResult function forces a result calculation to occur.

Syntax:

ans = CalculateResult(resultNumber)

resultNumber – integer defining the target result number.

Comments:

The result record must be in context.

NOTE:This  function does not do a database commit.


Example:

 

resultNumber = SELECT RESULT.RESULT_NUMBER

WHERE RESULT_NUMBER = 324

ORDER BY RESULT_NUMBER

ans = CalculateResult(resultNumber)

Returns:

ans = 50

 

CancelResult    

Purpose:

The CancelResult function cancels a result in a test.

Syntax:

status = CancelResult (sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber)

sampleNumber – Sample Number of the target parent sample.

testNumber – Test Number of the target parent test.

resultNumber – Result Number of the target result.

Comments:

Result status must be Not Entered, Entered, or Modified.  Test status must be incomplete or In-progress.  Sample status must be Unreceived, Incomplete ir In Progress

NOTE: This  function does not do a database commit.

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and CONTINUE_CANCEL status rule is checked before this function is performed.


Example:

status = CancelResult (74, 89, 200)

Returns:

status =  true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

ConvertResultToNumeric    

Purpose:

The ConvertResultToNumeric function converts a calculated or list type result to numeric.  This function changes the RESULT_TYPE field of a result from K or L to N and converts the value of  the FORMATTED_ENTRY field of the result to numeric.

Syntax:

status = ConvertResultToNumeric(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber)

sampleNumber – Sample Number of the parent sample.

testNumber – Test Number of the parent test.

resultNumber – Result number of the target result.

Comments:

This function must be executed from an analysis calculation or format calculation.  This function is typically used to update the formatted_result for reporting purposes.

NOTE: This function does not perform a database commit.


Example:

status = ConvertResultToNumeric(1, 22, 85)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

ConvertResultToText    

Purpose:

The ConvertResultToText function converts a calculated or list type result to text.  This function changes the RESULT_TYPE field of a result from K or L to T and converts the value of  the FORMATTED_ENTRY field of the result to text.

Syntax:

status = ConvertResultToText(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber)

sampleNumber – Sample Number of the parent sample.

testNumber – Test Number of the parent test.

resultNumber – Result number of the target result.

Comments:

This function must be executed from an analysis calculation or format calculation.  This function is typically used to update the formatted_result for reporting purposes.

NOTE: This function does not perform a database commit.


Example:

status = ConvertResultToText(1, 22, 85)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

GetResultsFormattedEntry    

Purpose:

The GetResultsFormattedEntry function retrieves the values stored in the FORMATTED_ENTRY field for the defined result records and populates the user defined array.

NOTE: Date type results are stored in the format defined by the Business Rule for Date results using  – as the separator. This function returns the date results as they are stored in the database not using the NLSed date format.


Syntax:

status = GetResultsFormattedEntry(sample, testNumberOrAnalysisName, component, "resultArrayName")

Sample – the sample number of the target sample as an integer.

TestNumberOrAnalysisName –the test number of the target test as an integer.

component – the name of the target result

"resultArrayName" – the name of the 1 dimensional array used by LIMS to return the result value

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that the Sample Number provided is a valid entry.  If there is no sample with the provided sample number, the LIMS returns:
"Sample does not exist: " followed by the sample number.

•LIMS verifies that the Test Number provided is a valid entry.  If there is no test with the provided test number, the LIMS returns:
"Test does not exist: " followed by the test number.

•LIMS verifies that the identified test has a result with the provided name.  If there is no result with the provided name on the test, the LIMS returns:
"The test in context does not have the specified result:" followed by the name provided.

•LIMS retrieves the results and populates the return array.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following statement retrieves the formatted entries for the Entry1 results for sample 1, test 1.  The values are inserted into a one dimensional array named ReturnArray.

status = GetResultsFormattedEntry (1,1, "Entry1", "ReturnArray")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

This function also returns an array populated with the retrieved formatted entry values.

 

RejectResult    

Purpose:

The RejectResult function rejects the specified result.

Syntax:

status = RejectResult(sampleNumber,testNumber, resultNumber)

sampleNumber – the sample number of the result to be rejected

testNumber – the test number of the result to be rejected

resultNumber – the result number of the result to be rejected

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the sample whose sample number is equal to the value of sampleNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample does not exist".

•LIMS retrieves the test whose test number is equal to the value of testNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Test does not exist".

•LIMS retrieves the result whose result number is equal to the value of resultNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the result "Result does not exist".

•LIMS determines if the result can be rejected by the current user.  If present, the CONTINUE_REJECT status rule is processed.  If the result cannot be rejected, LIMS will trigger an error using the status rule message as the error message.

•LIMS rejects the result.

•LIMS sets the audit type of the sample equal to "Reject".

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example rejects result 5641, which belongs to test 3382, which in turn belongs to sample 997.

status = RejectResult(997,3382,5641)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

RejectResults

Purpose:

The RejectResults function rejects the specified result.

Syntax:

status = RejectResults(sampleNumber,testNumber, resultNumberArray)

sampleNumber – the sample number of the results to be rejected

testNumber – the test number of the results to be rejected

resultNumberArray – one dimensional array of result numbers to be rejected

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•The system retrieves the sample whose sample number is equal to the value of sampleNumber.  If this fails, the system will trigger the error "Sample does not exist".

•The system retrieves the test whose test number is equal to the value of testNumber.  If this fails, the system will trigger the error: "Test does not exist".

•The system retrieves the result whose result numbers is equal to the values in resultNumberArray.  If this fails, the system will trigger the error: "Result does not exist".

•The system determines if the result can be rejected by the current user.  If present, the CONTINUE_REJECT status rule is processed.  If the result cannot be rejected, the system will trigger an error using the status rule message as the error message.

•The system rejects the results.

•The system sets the audit type of the sample equal to "Reject".

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database


Example:

 

 

 

sampleNumber = 997

testNumber = 3382

resultNumberArray[1] = 5641

resultNumberArray[2] = 5648

 

status = RejectResults(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumberArray)

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

ReplicateResult    

Purpose:

The ReplicateResult function creates result replicates.

Syntax:

status = ReplicateResult(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNameOrNumber, numReps, maxReps, commitFlag)

Comments:

numReps is the number of result replicates to create. 

maxReps is the maximum number of replicates for the result. 

If commitFlag is T, the data is committed, otherwise the data is committed during the next LIMS commit to the sample (the next commit may happen when a later LIMS Basic function performs a commit or when a Save operation is performed).

Example:

status = ReplicateResult(48, 103, "Before", 2, 5, "T")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise. 

 

ResetResult    

Purpose:

This function resets a specified result.  That means that the entry is set to null and the status is restored to ‘N’, not entered.  However the sample status will remain in-progress because the sample has been started.

Syntax:

Status = ResetResult(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber, auditReason)

sampleNumber – integer; sample number of the parent sample of the target result.

testNumber – integer; test number of the parent test of the target result.

resultNumber – integer; result number of the target result.

auditReason – string posted as reason to the audit trail on commit.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•If LIMS is unable to find the sample with the specified sample number, it will trigger the error "Sample does not exist".

•LIMS evaluates the sample.  If it is authorized, rejected, or canceled, LIMS returns the last error: Sample is reviewed or canceled.

•If LIMS is unable to find the test with the specified test number and sample number, it will trigger the error "Test does not exist".

•LIMS evaluates the test.  If it is rejected, authorized, or canceled, LIMS returns the last error: Test is reviewed or canceled.

•If LIMS is unable to find the result, LIMS returns the last error: Result does not exist.

•LIMS resets the result.  If the task fails, LIMS returns the last error: Result could not be reset.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

Status = ResetResult(137, 4916, 22987, "Needed to reset")

Returns:

Status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

RestoreResult    

Purpose:

The RestoreResult function restores the status of a result to its previous value.

Syntax:

status = RestoreResult(sampleNumber,testNumber,resultNumber)

sampleNumber – the sample number of the result to be restored

testNumber – the test number of the result to be restored

resultNumber – the result number of the result to be restored

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If LIMS is unable to find the sample with the specified sample number, it will trigger the error "Sample does not exist".

•If LIMS is unable to find the test with the specified test number and sample number, it will trigger the error "Test does not exist".

•If LIMS is unable to find the result with the specified sample number, test number, and result number, it will trigger the error "Result does not exist".

•LIMS verifies that the result can be restored by checking the business rules, the function privileges, and the CONTINUE_RESTORE status rule.  If the result cannot be restored, LIMS triggers an error with the reason as the error message.

•LIMS restores the result by copying the value of the OLD_STATUS field to the STATUS field and then updates any affected calculations of the sample.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger an error with the reason as the error message.

•LIMS associates the audit type "RestoreResult" with the pending changes to the sample.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example restores result 7821 of test 841 of sample 24.

status = RestoreResult(24, 841, 7821)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

Result    

Purpose:

The Result function returns the value of the FORMATTED_ENTRY field in a result. 

NOTE: Date type results are stored in the format defined by the Business Rule for Date results using  – as the separator. This function returns the date results as they are stored in the database not using the NLSed date format.


Syntax:

x = Result(sampleNumber, testNumberOrAnalysisName, componentName)

Comments:

componentName is case sensitive.  If there are multiple result replicates, the result from the first replicate is returned.

Example:

x = Result(10, "COLOR", "Whiteness")  

Returns:

x = 8.0

 

ResultFld    

Purpose:

The ResultFld function returns the value of a result field in a test.

NOTE: If using this function with the ENTRY or FORMATTED_ENTRY field for date type results, this function returns the date results as they are stored in the database not using the NLSed date format. Date results are stored in the format defined by the Business Rule for Date results using – as the separator.


Syntax:

x = ResultFld(sampleNumber, testumberOrAnalysisName, componentName,  field)

Comments:

componentName is case sensitive.  field is any field in the Result table.

Example:

x = ResultFld(10, "COLOR", "Whiteness", "UNITS")

Returns:

x = "NONE"

 

ResultFlds    

Purpose:

The ResultFlds function returns an array containing the values of a result field for all the result records in a test.

NOTE: If using this function with the ENTRY or FORMATTED_ENTRY field for date type results, this function returns the date results as they are stored in the database not using the NLSed date format. Date results are stored in the format defined by the Business Rule for Date results using – as the separator.


Syntax:

status = ResultFlds(testNumber, fieldName, "arrayName")

Comments:

Values are returned in result.order_number order.  If field is invalid, null values are returned.

Example:

status = ResultFlds(97,  "UNITS",  "unitsArray")

Returns:  

unitsArray[1] = "GRAMS"  

unitsArray[2] = "PERCENT"

 

SetMaxLimit    

Purpose:

The SetMaxLimit function sets the MAX_LIMIT field for the specified result.

Syntax:

status = SetMaxLimit(resultNumber, maxVal, recheckLimitsFlag)

resultNumber – The result number for the targeted result.

maxVal – The numerical value to be inserted into the MAX_LIMIT field.

recheckLimitsFlag – Boolean controlling system behavior after inserting maxVal into the MAX_LIMIT field. 

NOTE:  If no value is specified for the recheckLimitFlag, the value defaults to True if not specified.  Entries starting with T or Y are interpreted as True.  All other entries are interpreted as False.


Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that the sample specified on the result record is valid.  If the sample is not valid, the LIMS returns the last error:
"The sample for the specified result does not exist:" followed by the result number.

•LIMS then verifies the result number specified.  If the result number is not valid, the LIMS returns the last error:
"The specified result does not exist:" followed by the result number.

•The LIMS then updates the RESULT.MAX_LIMIT field.

•The LIMS then evaluates the recheckLimitFlag.

•If the flag is interpreted as true, the system triggers a specification check for the result.

•If the flag is interpreted as false, the system does not trigger a specification check for the result.

The LIMS updates the audit trail.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code changes the Max_Limit on result number 1 for the current value to 16 and then triggers a specification check.

resultNumber = 1
maxVal = 16
recheckLimitsFlag = "T"
status = SetMaxLimit(resultNumber, MaxVal, recheckLimitsFlag)

Returns:

The function returns the status True if completed successfully.  This function returns the status False if not completed successfully. 

 

SetMinLimit    

Purpose:

The SetMinLimit function sets the MIN_LIMIT field for the specified result.

Syntax:

status = SetMinLimit(resultNumber, minVal, recheckLimitsFlag)

resultNumber – The result number for the targeted result.

minVal – The numerical value to be inserted into the MIN_LIMIT field.

recheckLimitsFlag – Boolean controlling system behavior after inserting maxVal into the MIN_LIMIT field.  This values defaults to True.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that the sample specified on the result record is valid.  If the sample is not valid, the LIMS returns the last error:
"The sample for the specified result does not exist:" followed by the result number.

•LIMS then verifies the result number specified.  If the result number is not valid, the LIMS returns the last error:
"The specified result does not exist:" followed by the result number.

•The LIMS then updates the RESULT.MIN_LIMIT field.

•The LIMS then evaluates the recheckLimitFlag.

•If the flag is interpreted as true, the system triggers a specification check for the result.

•If the flag is interpreted as false, the system does not trigger a specification check for the result.

•The LIMS updates the audit trail.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code changes the Max_Limit on result number 1 for the current value to 10 and then triggers a specification check.

resultNumber = 1
minVal = 10
recheckLimitsFlag = "T"
status = SetMinLimit(resultNumber, MinVal, recheckLimitsFlag)

Returns:

The function returns true if it completes successfully.  Otherwise, it returns false with the appropriate Last Error.

 

SetOrCreateResult    

Purpose:

The SetOrCreateResult function creates a result or updates an existing result with the ability to control the entry value, unit, limits, and reportable flag.  

Syntax:

status =  SetOrCreateResult(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultName, value, units, minLimit, maxLimit, commitFlag, newReplicate, triggerCalcsFlag, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

sampleNumber - (Required) The sample number associated with the result to be updated or created.

testNumber – (Required) the test number associated with the result to be updated or created.

resultName – (Required) the name of the result which is to be updated

value – (Required) value to be posted to the entry field of the result.

units – (Optional) value to be posted to the units field of the result.

minLimit – (Optional) value to be posted to the minimum field of the result.

maxLimit – (Optional) value to be posted to the maximum field of the result.

commitFlag – (Optional) Boolean that determines if the result information is posted to cache awaiting the next save or committed immediately.  The default value is false.

newReplicate – (Optional) Boolean to determine if the system should update an existing result if located based on the result name or add a new replicate of the result.  The default value is false.

triggerCalcsFlag – (Optional) Boolean, T, true, and dependent calculations are triggered (only works if dependent calculation is set to auto-calculate).  F, false and the dependent calculations are not triggered.  The default value is false.

fieldsArray – (Optional) one dimensional array of Result table fields.

valuesArray– (Optional) one dimensional array of values to be populated into the corresponding Result fields.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evaluates the CommitFlag parameter.  If the parameter is not specified, LIMS sets the value to false.

•LIMS evaluates the newReplicate parameter.  If the parameter is not specified. LIMS set the value to false.

•LIMS verifies the sampleNumber is valid.  If the sample is not found, LIMS returns the last error: "Sample does not exist: sampleNumber ".

•If the sample is already reviewed (status = X, A, or R), LIMS returns the last error: "Sample is reviewed or canceled: sampleNumber ".

•LIMS evaluates the ResultName.  If not result name is specified, LIMS returns the last error: "Result name must be specified."

•LIMS verifies the testNumber is valid.  If the test is not found, LIMS returns the last error: "Test does not exist: testNumber ".

•If the test is already reviewed (status = X, A, or R), LIMS returns the last error: "Test is reviewed or canceled: testNumber ".

•LIMS check the size of the fieldsArray and valuesArray.  If the sizes do not match, LIMS returns the last error: "Fields and Values arrays must be the same size ".

•If the result name is not located or the newReplicate flag is True, then the system creates a new result and populates the entry.

•If the result is located and the newReplicate flag is False, then the system updates the located result.

•LIMS verifies that the existing result or new result has been updated.  If it has not been, LIMS returns the last error: "Result could not be added."

•The LIMS updates the audit trail and the result fields as specified by the inputs.

•If an audit reason is set for the result table that reason is posted to the audit trail.

•If there is no audit reason on the result table, the audit reason set on the sample table is posted to the audit trail.

•If there is an error when committing the result, LIMS returns the last error: "Error committing sample record".

NOTE: This function does save changes to the database based on the setting of the commitFlag.

Example:

The following example creates a result LB Result and sets the value entry, units, MIN_LIMIT, and MAX_LIMIT.

sampleNumber = 1342

testNumber = 157392

resultName = "LB Result"

value = 7.5

units = "GM"

minLimit = 7

maxLimit =  10

commitFlag = "T"

newReplicate = "F"

fieldsArray[1] = "ATTRIBUTE_1"

valuesArray[1] = "FINAL"

status = SetOrCreateResult(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultName, value, units, minLimit, maxLimit, commitFlag, newReplicate, "T",fieldsArray, valuesArray)

Returns:

The function returns true if successful. If it is not successful, it will answer false and lastError will be set with an appropriate error message.

 

SetResult    

Purpose:

The SetResult function sets a result value.

Syntax:

status = SetResult(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber, value, commitFlag)

sampleNumber – sample number of the parent sample of the target result.

testNumber – test number of the parent test of the target result.

resultNumber – result number of the target result.

value – value to set the result entry field to.

commitFlag – Boolean that determines if the value is committed to the database.  If set to F (false), the value is committed the next time a commit for the object is triggered.

Comments:

Result status must be Not Entered, Entered,or Modified.

Example:

status = SetResult(24, 79, 158, "Green", "T")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

SetResultUnits    

Purpose:

The SetResultUnits function sets the UNITS field of a result.  

Syntax:

status = SetResultUnits(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber, unitCode)

Comments:

If the new units are of the same unit type as the old units, unit conversion takes place.  If the units are not of the same unit type, unit conversion does not take place.  Units must be defined in the Units table.

This function must be executed from an analysis calculation or format calculation.

NOTE: This function does not perform a database commit.

If invalid units are specified an error message will be added to the Error.log.


Example:

status = SetResultUnits(24, 79, 158, "PPM")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

TriggerFormatCalculation    

Purpose:

The TriggerFormatCalculation function causes format calculations on the defined sample(s), tests(s), or results(s) to be triggered.

Syntax:

status = TriggerFormatCalculations(sampleNumberArray, testNumberArray, resultNumberArray, setFormattedEntryFlag)

SampleNumberArray – an array containing a list of sample numbers

TestNumberArray – an array containing a list of test numbers

ResultNumberArray – an array containing a list of result numbers

setFormattedEntryFlag – a boolean defining if the triggered format calculations are permitted to update the Formatted Entry field on the sample.  The default value is False.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•The system verifies that the values in the configured array are valid.  If the array contains an invalid number the system returns a status of false and the appropriate error message:

•Input: Invalid Sample Number; Last Error: <null>

•Input: Invalid Test Number; Last Error: Specified sample for test number does not exist: B

•Input: Invalid Result Number; Last Error: Specified sample for result number does not exist: C

•The system verifies that only one array is configured.  If more than one array set is provided, that is, sample numbers and test numbers are provided, the system return a status of false and the appropriate error message:

•Input: Sample Number and Test Number; Last Error: Error: Test or Result numbers cannot be specified if sample numbers are specified.

•Input: Sample Number and Result Number; Last Error: Error: Test or Result numbers cannot be specified if sample numbers are specified.

•Input: Test Number and Result Number; Last Error: Error: Sample or Result numbers cannot be specified if, test numbers are specified.

•The system triggers the format calculation for each of the collected results.  If successful, the status is returned as true.

NOTE:  This function save changes to the database as defined by the triggered Format Calculations.

 

CAUTION: If a result does not have a value enterd for the Formatted Entry, this LIMS Basic function will not trigger the Formatted Calculation for that result.


Example:

The following example triggers all the Format Calculations on all the samples with the description: Testing:  Any required changes to the Formatted Entry field are executed.

Qry = "Select sample_number from sample where description = ‘Testing’"
status = SQL(qry, "samNum", , "F")
setFormattedEntryFlag = "yes"
status = TriggerFormatCalculations(samNum, , , setFormattedEntryFlag)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

UpdateNumericEntry    

Purpose:

The UpdateNumericEntry function updates the NUMERIC_ENTRY field of a result.

Syntax:

status = UpdateNumericEntry(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber,  numericValue,commitFlag)

Comments:

This function must be executed from an analysis calculation or format calculation. This function is typically used to alter the numeric entry for graphing purposes (i.e. result entries < 10 set to 5).

commitFlag – the value true is the change should be saved in the database (optional; the default value is false)

Example:

status = UpdateNumericEntry(15, 20, 23, 7)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

UpdateResultFld    

Purpose:

The UpdateResultFld function updates a field in a result.

Syntax:

status = UpdateResultFld(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber, field, value, commitFlag)

sampleNumber – sample number of the parent sample of the target result.

testNumber – test number of the parent test of the target result.

resultNumber – result number of the target result.

field – result field to be updated.

value – value to be posted to the specified field.

commitFlag – T, the data is committed, otherwise the data is committed during the next LIMS commit to the sample (the next commit may happen when a later LIMS Basic function performs a commit or when a Save operation is performed)

Comments:

CAUTION:  This function should be used with caution as any field in the RESULT table may be updated. 


Example:

status = UpdateResultFld(48, 103, 379, "NAME", "Dish Weight", "T")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

UpdateResultFlds

Purpose:

Updates multiple fields on a specified result.

Syntax:

status = UpdateResultFlds(sampleNumber, testNumber,resultNameOrNumber,fieldsArray, valuesArray,commitFlag)

sampleNumber – sample number corresponding to the result to be updated.

testNumber – test number corresponding to the result to be updated.

resultNameOrNumber – result name or number corresponding to the result to be updated.

fieldsArray – one dimensional array of result fields to be updated.

valuesArray – one dimensional array of values to be posted to the specified fields.

commitFlag – Boolean, If T, the data is committed, otherwise the data is committed during the next commit to the sample (the next commit may happen when a later LIMS Basic function performs a commit or when a Save operation is performed)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•The system compares the fieldsArray with the valuesArray.  If they are not the same size, the system returns the last error: Error: field and value arrays must be the same size.

•The system retrieves the specified sample. If unsuccessful, the system returns the last error: “Sample does not exist: sampleNumber”.

•The system retrieves the specified test. If unsuccessful, the system returns the last error: “Test does not exist: testNumber”.

•The system retrieves the specified result. If unsuccessful, the system returns the last error: “Result does not exist: resultNameOrNumber”.

•The system evaluates the specified fields.  If there is a field not on the Test table, the system returns the error:  “Invalid field for table: Result”.

•The system evaluates the specified values.  If there is an invalid value, the system returns the error:  “Invalid value for result record: <value> for field: <field>”.

•The system updates the field values in cache if the commitFlag is false, otherwise it is committed to the database.  The system also assigns the audit type "ModifyResult" to the pending transaction. If the commit fails, the system returns the error:  “Error committing sample record”

CAUTION:  This function should be used with caution as any field in the RESULT table may be updated.


Example:

sampleNumber = 48

testNumber = 103

resultNameOrNumber = 379

fieldsArray[1] = "REPORTED_NAME"

valuesArray[1] = “Dish Weight”

commitFlag = “T”

status = UpdateResultFlds(sampleNumber, testNumber,resultNameOrNumber,fieldsArray, valuesArray,commitFlag)

Returns: 

The function returns true if successful, false otherwise

 

28.3.39  RTF Note Functions
 

Function

AppendToRTFNote

DisplayRTFNote

EditRTFNote

 

AppendToRTFNote    

Purpose:

The AppendToRTFNote function.

Syntax:

newNoteNumber = AppendToRTFNote(noteNumber, appendText)

noteNumber – an integer value that identifies the note to which text should be appended

appendText – the text to be appended to the note

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the note from the database.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid note specified".

•If the value of appendText is blank or omitted, LIMS will trigger the error "No text specified to append".

•LIMS creates a new note record and copied the note type and reportable flag from the old note to the new note.

•LIMS appends the specified text to the contents of the old note and stores the result in the new note.

•LIMS saves the new note to the database.

•LIMS returns the note number of the new note.

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example appends the text "Reviewed on April 29, 2005" to the note with note number 243.

newNoteNumber = AppendToRTFNote(243, "Reviewed on April 29, 2005")

Returns:

The function returns the note number of the new note if it completes successfully.  It returns the value "ERROR" if an error is triggered.

 

DisplayRTFNote    

Purpose:

The DisplayRTFNote function displays the specified RTF Note.

Syntax:

status = DisplayRTFNote(noteNumber, label)

noteNumber – the note number that identifies the note to be displayed

label – the title to be displayed on the RTF Note window

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the RTF note that has the specified note number.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid note specified".

•LIMS displays the note in a popup window and uses the specified label as the title in the window.  The OK button is disabled.

•LIMS waits for the user to press the Cancel button.

This function does not permit the user to make changes to the note.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example displays note 413 in a window with title "Sample Comments".

status = DisplayRTFNote(413, "Sample Comments")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

EditRTFNote    

Purpose:

The EditRTFNote function allows the user to create a new RTF note or update an existing RTF note.

Syntax:

newNoteNumber = EditRTFNote(noteNumber, label, noteType, reportable,templateNote, interactiveFlag. rtfText)

noteNumber – the number that identifies the note to be edited (0 for a new note)

label – the title to be displayed in the window

noteType – the value to be assigned to the note type field of the note

reportable – the value to be assigned to the reportable field of the note

templateNote – the number that identifies the Note Template to be used when creating a new note

NOTE: Use of Note Templates requires module M0253 RTF Templates.

interactiveFlag – the value true if a dialog should be displayed to allow the user to update the note, and the value false otherwise.  If no value is specified, no dialog will be displayed.

rtfText – the rich text to be placed in the note.  This argument is optional.  If specified, all existing content will be replaced with the value of this argument.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the specified note number is greater than zero, LIMS will retrieve the note with that note number.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid note specified."

•If the specified note number is not greater than zero, LIMS will create a new note object (in memory; not in the database) and assign it a new note number.

•If the specified note number and the specified template note number are both greater than zero, LIMS will trigger the error "Cannot specify a template for an existing note."

•If a template note number is specified, LIMS will retrieve the template with that number.

•If the retrieval fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid note specified."

•If the retrieval succeeds, LIMS will copy the contents of the template note to the new note.

•If a reportable flag is specified, the reportable flag of the note is set equal to this value.

•If a note type is specified, the note type of the note is set equal to this value.

•If a value is specified for noteRTF, LIMS will replace the entire contents of the note with this value.

•If the specified interactive flag is true, LIMS will perform the following steps.

•LIMS will open a dialog to display the note.

•LIMS will wait for the user to press either the OK button or the Cancel button.

•If the user presses the Cancel button, LIMS will return the specified note number.  (For example, if the value zero was specified to create a new note, LIMS will return the value zero instead of the note number of the newly created note object.  The newly created note will be discarded without writing it to the database.)

•If the user presses the OK button without making changes, LIMS will return the specified note number.

•If the note is a new note, LIMS will insert it into the database, and return the newly assigned note number.

•If the note is an existing note, and no changes were made to the note (by the user or by specifying values for reportable flag and/or note type that are different from the values stored in the note), LIMS will return the specified note number.

•If the contents of the note have not changed, i.e., if only the reportable flag and/or note type were changed, LIMS will save the changes to the database and return the specified note number.

•Otherwise, i.e., if the contents of the note were modified, LIMS will create a new note by copying the fields values from the modified note, assign it a new note number, save it in the database, and return the new note number.  (The existing note is left unchanged.)

NOTE: When a template is used, LIMS copies the contents of the template note into the new note.  The values of reportable flag and note type are not copied from the template.  If these fields need to be pre-populated in the new note, their values should be passed using the function arguments.

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example changes the value of the reportable flag of note 27 to true without displaying a dialog.  If the reportable flag of that note is not already true, LIMS will save the change to the database.  Either way, LIMS will return the value 27.

newNoteNumber = EditRTFNote(27, "", "", "T", 0, "F")

The following example prompts the user to update note 31.  If the user does make a change and then presses the OK button, and if the current increment value for the LIMS_NOTES table is 74, LIMS will create a new note with number 75, save it to the database, and return the value 75.

newNoteNumber = EditRTFNote(31, "Enter Comment", "", "", 0, "T")

The following example creates a new note by using note 64 as a template.  Suppose the current increment value for the LIMS_NOTES table is 78.  If the user presses Cancel, the value zero will be returned.  If the user presses OK, a new note will be created in the database with note number 79 and this new note number will be returned.  In both cases, the current increment will become 79.

newNoteNumber = EditRTFNote(0, "Enter Comment", "", "", 64, "T")

The following example creates a blank note in the database without displaying a window.

newNoteNumber = EditRTFNote(0, "", "", "", 0, "F")

Returns:

The function returns a note number if it completes successfully.  It returns the value "ERROR" if an error is triggered.

 

28.3.40  Sample Functions
