AliquotSample    

Purpose:

The AliquotSample function aliquots a sample.  The fields that are copied from the parent sample to the aliquot are determined by the Aliquot Template.  If no Aliquot template is defined on the parent sample then then Sample Template on the parent is used.

Syntax:

newSampleNumber = AliquotSample(sampleNumber, testNumberArray)

sampleNumber – integer defining the sample number of the target sample.

testNumberArray – one dimensional array of integers defining the test numbers of the target tests.

Comments:

Sample status must be Unreceived, Incomplete or In Progress.

NOTE: This  function does not do a database commit.


Example:

 

testNumberArray[1] = 90

testNumberArray[2] = 91

newSampleNumber = AliquotSample(95, testNumberArray)

Returns:

newSampleNumber = 102

 

AliquotTest    

Purpose:

The AliquotTest function aliquots (copies) the specified source test from the specified source sample to the specified target sample.

Syntax:

status = AliquotTest(targetSampleNumber, sourceSampleNumber, sourceTestNumber)

targetSampleNumber – the sample number of the sample to which the test should be aliquoted

sourceSampleNumber – the sample number of the sample from which the test should be aliquoted

sourceTestNumber – the test number of the test to be aliquoted

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the sample whose value of sample number is equal to targetSampleNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample does not exist".

•If the target sample has been reviewed or canceled, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample is reviewed or canceled".

•LIMS retrieves the sample whose value of sample number is equal to sourceSampleNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample does not exist".

•If the source sample has been reviewed or canceled, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample is reviewed or canceled".

•LIMS retrieves from the source sample the test whose value of test number is equal to sourceTestNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "No test to aliquot".

•If the status of the source test is not equal to "I", LIMS will trigger the error "Test has the wrong status".

•LIMS updates the field named "ALIQUOTED_TO" of the source test with the sample number of the target sample.

•LIMS retrieves all tests of the source sample that have the same analysis name as the source test, i.e., LIMS retrieves all replicates of the source test.  If the replicates are not numbered consecutively, LIMS renumbers them starting with the number 1.

•LIMS saves the source sample using audit type "AliquotTest".

•LIMS copies the source test record and all of its result records to the target sample.

•LIMS retrieves all tests of the target sample that have the same analysis name as the target test, i.e., LIMS retrieves all replicates of the target test.  If the replicates are not numbered consecutively, LIMS renumbers them starting with the number 1.

•LIMS saves the target sample using audit type "AliquotTest".

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

status = AliquotTest(782, 688, 43)

Aliquots the test with test number 43 from sample 688 to sample 782.

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

AliquotTestsOnSample  

Purpose:

The AliquotTestsOnSample function will aliquot the tests on the selected sample.  It can be used to automatically aliquot tests based on sample events like logged, received, or prepared.

Syntax:

status = AliquotTestsOnSample(sampleNumber, "newTestsArrayName")

sampleNumber - (Required) The sample number of the sample to be received.

newTestsArrayName – (Array, Optional) A 2 dimensional array which returns the aliquot sample number in column 1 and the new test number assigned to the aliquot in column 2.

 

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evaluates the specified sample number.  If this sample does not exist, the system triggers an error and the Last Error is set to: "Invalid sample number specified".

•LIMS evaluates the Aliquot_On setting on the Sample Template.  If the sample is set to not aliquot on demand, the system triggers an error and the Last Error is set to: "The sample is not configured to auto aliquot on demand".

NOTE: This function does save changes to the database.

Example:

sampleNumber = 1248
newTestsArray = "aliqArray"

status = AliquotTestsOnSample(sampleNumber, newTestsArray)

Returns:

The function returns true if successful. If it is not successful, it will answer false and lastError will be set with an appropriate error message.

 

AssignTestandResultNumbers    

Purpose:

The AssignTestandResultNumbers function updates the tests and results that are currently assigned to the sample in cache but not yet committed to the database.  This function assigns the test numbers to the tests and result numbers to the results.

Syntax:

status = AssignTestAndResultNumbers(sampleNumber)

Comments:

NOTE: This function does not assign result number to optional results unless the analysis is configured to create option results in the database.


Example:

status = AssignTestAndResultNumbers(233)

Returns:

Status = true if successful, otherwise false.

 

AuthorizeSample    

Purpose:

The AuthorizeSample function authorizes a sample.

Syntax:

status = AuthorizeSample(sampleNumber, allTestsFlag)

sampleNumber – integer defining the sample number of the target sample.

allTestsFlag – Boolean; if T, true, all tests and results are authorized, unless a test is found that cannot be authorized.  If F, false, the tests and results are not set to authorized.  The default setting is false.

Comments:

Sample status must be Complete or Rejected (In-Progress samples may be authorized if business rules allow it).  

This  function does not do a database commit. 

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and CONTINUE_AUTHORIZE status rule is checked before this function is performed.


Example:

status = AuthorizeSample(742, "T")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

CancelSample    

Purpose:

The CancelSample function cancels a sample and all of the tests in the sample.

Syntax:

status = CancelSample(sampleNumber)

sampleNumber – Sample Number of the target sample.

Comments:

Sample status must be Unreceived, Incomplete, In Progress or Complete.

NOTE: This  function does not do a database commit.

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and CONTINUE_CANCEL status rule are checked before this function is performed


Example:

status = CancelSample(748)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

ChangeSampleLocation    

Purpose:

The ChangeSampleLocation function changes the location field in a sample.

Syntax:

status = ChangeSampleLocation(sampleNumber, location)

sampleNumber – Sample Number of the target sample.

location – New location of the sample.

Comments:

location must exist in the Locations table.

NOTE: This function does not do a database commit.


Example:

status = ChangeSampleLocation(741, BIO_LAB)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

CheckLimits    

Purpose:

The CheckLimits function (re)checks the specified sample against its associated product specifications.

NOTE: Control limits are not checked.


Syntax:

status = CheckLimits(sampleNumber)

sampleNumber – the sample number of the sample to be checked

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the sample for the specified sample number.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample does not exist".

•LIMS checks the sample, as well as all of its tests and results, against the specifications for the product specified in the sample record.  The "in spec" and "in control" flags are updated as necessary.

•If an audit reason is specified for the sample table, LIMS will associate that reason with the sample.

•LIMS sets the audit type for the sample equal to "ModifySample".

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.

 

TIP: This function can be useful to force spec checks if a Product spec is updated.


Example:

The following example checks the results of sample 763 against the corresponding specifications.

status = CheckLimits(763)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

CheckSampleRules    

Purpose:

The CheckSampleRules function is used to check the sample field entries to the expected values recorded within the Sample Rules Manager based on the specified analysis.  

Syntax:

status = CheckSampleRules(analysisName, sampleNumber)

analysisName – the name of the analysis

sampleNumber – the number of the sample to be check.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evalues the input: analysisName.  If the input is not specified, the system triggers an error and the Last Error is set to: "There is no analysis specified".

•LIMS evaluates the input: analysisName.  If the record is not located, the system triggers an error and the Last Error is set to: "The specified analysis does not exist:" followed by the input.

•LIMS evaluates the input: sampleNumber.  If the sample is not located, the system triggers an error and the Last Error is set to: "The specified sample does not exist:" followed by the input.

•LIMS evaluates the container defined on the Test against the sample’s Sample Type. 

•If the container is not valid for the sample type, the system returns false.

•If the container is valid for the Sample Type, then system returns true.

NOTE:  This function does not save to the database.


Example:

analysisName = "MICROARRAY"
sampleNumber = 3611

status = CheckSampleRules(analysisName, sampleNumber)

Returns:

status = true if the test containers pass the CheckSampleRule and false if the contain does not. 

 

CheckSpec    

Purpose:

The CheckSpec function checks a sample against a product specification.

Syntax:

status = CheckSpec(sampleNumber, product, version, grade, samplingPoint, stage,
specType, analysisName, componentName, "gradeArrayName", includeRejected)

sampleNumber – integer defining the sample number of the target sample.

product – string defining the product record to use during the Spec Check.

version – integer defining the version of the product record to use during the Spec Check.

grade – string definng the product grade to use during the Spec Check.  (Optional)

samplingPoint – string defining the sampling point to use during the Spec Check. (Optional)

stage – string defining the stage to use during the Spec Check. (Optional)

specType –  string defining the specification type to use during the Spec Check.  (Optional)

analysisName – string defining the analysis to be evaluated.  (Optional)

componentName – string defining the component to be evaluated. (Optional)

"gradeArrayName" – name of the array returning the grades and the evaluations.
column 1 – Grade
column 2 – Sampling Point
column 3 – evaluation

includeRejected – Boolean; T, true, and the system evaluates rejected result, F, false, and the system does not.  The default value is false.

Comments:

gradeArrayName is an array containing the results of the spec check.

NOTE:  If only the sampleNumber, product and version arguments are specified (in addition to the gradeArrayName and includeRejected arguments) the function will check all grades and sampling points.


 

Example:

Example 1:

status = CheckSpec(22, "PAPER", 1, "TYLER_B, "TANK_1", "FINAL",  "NONE, "LOD", "LOD", , "T")

Example 2:

status = CheckSpec(22, "PAPER", 1, , , , , , , "evalArray", "T")

evalArray[1,1] = "GRADE_1"

evalArray[2,1] = "GRADE_2"

evalArray[3,1] = "GRADE_3"

evalArray[4,1] = "GRADE_4"

evalArray[1,2] = "SAM_PT_1"

evalArray[2,2] = "SAM_PT_2"

evalArray[3,2] = "SAM_PT_3"

evalArray[4,2] = "SAM_PT_4"

evalArray[1,3] = "T"

evalArray[2,3] = "T"

evalArray[3,3] = "F"

evalArray[4,3] = "F"

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

CompositeSamples    

Purpose:

The CompositeSamples function generates a composite sample from all the samples in the specified sample array.

Syntax:

sampleNumber = CompositeSamples(sampleArray)

sampleArray – the array that contains the sample numbers to be composited

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the sample array contains a sample number that is not present in the database, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified sample does not exist".

•If the sample array does not contain at least two sample numbers, LIMS will trigger the error "Error: must select more than one sample".

•If none of the samples have a composite template, LIMS will trigger the error "Error: no composite template specified".

•If any two samples have a different composite template, LIMS will trigger the error "Error: more than one composite template specified".

•LIMS creates a new composite sample.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Error: composite failed".

•LIMS adds the new sample to its context and returns the sample number of the new sample.

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example creates a new composite sample from the samples with sample number 301 and 302.

mySamples[1] = 301
mySamples[2] = 302
sampleNumber = CompositeSamples(mySamples)

Returns:

The function returns the sample number of the new sample if it completes successfully.  It returns the value zero if an error is triggered.

 

CopySample    

Purpose:

The CopySample function logs in a new sample by copying from an existing sample.

Syntax:

newSampleNumber = CopySample(sampleNumber, fieldsArray, valuesArray, sampleOnlyFlag, copyChildrenFlag)

sampleNumber – the number of the existing sample to be copied

fieldsArray – the array of field names to be overridden

valuesArray – the array of corresponding field values to be assigned

sampleOnlyFlag – false if the tests of the existing sample are to be copied to the new sample (the default is false)

copyChildrenFlag – true if the aliquots of the existing sample are to be copied to the new sample (the default is false)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the sample with the specified sample number.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample does not exist".

•LIMS logs in a new sample as a copy of the retrieved sample.

•If the value of sampleOnlyFlag is false, LIMS will copy the tests from the original sample to the new sample.

•If the value of copyChildrenFlag is true, LIMS will copy any aliquots from the original sample to the new sample, set their audit type to "LogSample", and add them to its context.

•If a fieldsArray is specified, LIMS will assign the values in the valuesArray to the corresponding fields in the fieldsArray of the new sample.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example logs in a new sample from the existing sample with sample number 231.  All fields from the existing sample are copied, with the exception of the customer field, which is overridden with the value "ACME".  The tests are copied, but the aliquots are not.

myFields[1] = "CUSTOMER"
myValues[1] = "ACME"
myNewSampleNum = CopySample(231, myFields, myValues, false, false)

Returns:

The function returns the sample number of the new sample if it completes successfully.  It returns the value "ERROR" if an error is triggered.

 

CreateContainerAliquots   

Purpose:

The CreateContainerAliquots function will aliquot the tests based on container group and lab.   It can be used to make an aliquot for each of the necessary containers which satisfy the volume requirements of a test.

NOTE: Use of this function requires that a container type be specified in the analysis definition for all tests on the sample.

Syntax:

status = CreateContainerAliquots(sampleNumber, moveTestsFlag)

sampleNumber– the Sample Number

moveTestsFlag– A Boolean, it is set to true for grouping; defaults to True.

Comments

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evaluates the moveTestsFlag.  If a value is not specified, the system defaults this input to True.

•LIMS evaluates the input sample number.  If the sample is not valid, the system triggers an error and the Last Error is set to: "Invalid sample specified:" followed by the input sample number.

•LIMS aliquots the tests.  Tests with the same container group and lab are grouped together if the moveTestsFlag is interpreted as True.  IF the affected Container Type does not have a Container Group configured the function triggers an error and the Last Error is set to: Aliquots could not be created.

•LIMS verifies that the aliquots were made.  If no aliquot was generated, the system triggers an error and the Last Error is set to: "Aliquots could not be create".

NOTE: This function does save changes to the database. 


 

Tests are grouped by container group  and lab.  The container group field is defined n the CONTAINER_TYPE table.  The minimum number of containers are assigned ,after the flag for destructive is set to true. 

Example:

SampleNumber = "58"

moveTestsFlag ="Y"

Status  =  CreateContainerAliquots(sampleNumber,moveTestsFlag)

Returns:

 

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

DefaultSampleNumber    

Purpose:

The DefaultSampleNumber function returns the sample number of the most recently accessed sample.

Syntax:

sampleNumber = DefaultSampleNumber()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If a default sample has been defined, LIMS will return its sample number.

•Otherwise, LIMS will return an empty string.

NOTE: A sample is defined as the default number when the user selects it in a Sample Prompter dialog or in any of the following windows: Batch Manager, Folder Manager, Investigation Manager, Lot Manager, Project Manager, or the Sample Interface.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example returns the default sample number.

sampleNumber = DefaultSampleNumber()

Returns:

The function returns the default sample number if one is defined.  It returns an empty string otherwise.

 

FastCancelSamples    

Purpose:

The FastCancelSamples function cancels the specified samples and their tests and results.  There are Boolean arguments that indicate if the cancel operation will operate only at database level or whether the action will include an audit trail.

Syntax:

status = FastCancelSamples(sampleNumsArray, dbCancelSampleFlag, dbCancelTestsFlag, dbCancelResultsFlag, auditReason)

sampleNumsArray – a 1-D array containing Sample Numbers to be cancelled. All samples must be Unreceived or Incomplete.

dbCancelSampleFlag- Boolean argument which indicates if cancel operation will be at the database level or whether audit reason is applied.  If set to "T", no audit records are created for the sample objects and all Cancel actions are bypassed (e.g. no events occur and no checking of business rules, function privileges and status rules.) The Status is updated in the database to X for all affected samples, tests and results.

dbCancelTestsFlag- Boolean argument which indicates if cancel operation will be at the database level or whether audit reason is applied.  If set to "T", no audit records are created for the test objects and all Cancel actions are bypassed (e.g. no events occur and no checking of business rules, function privileges and status rules.)

dbCancelResultsFlag- Boolean argument which indicates if cancel operation will be at the database level or whether audit reason is applied.  If set to "T", no audit records are created for the result objects and all Cancel actions are bypassed (e.g. no events occur and no checking of business rules, function privileges and status rules.) 

auditReason- an audit reason to be applied to the audit transactions that are written to the databse.  An audit reason can only be used if dbCancelSampleFlag = "F".  If  dbCancelSampleFlag = "T" no audit records are written to the database.

NOTE: If dbCancelSampleFlag is set to "T" then the dbCancelTestsFlag and dbCancelResultsFlag must be set to "T".  Similarly, if dbCancelTestsFlag is set to "T" then the dbCancelResultsFlag must be set to "T"

At least one of the arguments must be set to "T" or the function will not do anything.


Comments:

Sample statuses must be Unreceived or Incomplete. This function prefomrs the following steps:

•LIMS verifies that the sampleNumsArray has been specified.  If this check fails, the function triggers an error and the Last Error is set to:
"Error: No sample numbers specified".

•LIMS checks the dbCancelSampleFlag argument, If this flag is true and an audit reason has been specified, the function triggers an error and the Last Error is set to:
"Error: An audit reason can''t be specified if the samples are directly canceled at the db level"

•LIMS checks the dbCancelTestsFlag and dbCancelResultsFlag arguments, If either flag is set to False and the dbCancelSampleFlag is true, the function triggers an error and the Last Error is set to:
"Error: Tests and results must be at the db level if the samples are directly canceled at the db level'"

•LIMS checks the dbCancelResultsFlag argument, If the flag is set to False and the dbCancelTestsFlag is true, the function triggers an error and the Last Error is set to:
"Error: Results must be at the db level if the tests are directly canceled at the db level"

•LIMS verifies that the sample numbers provided are valid.  If an invalid numer is found, the function triggers an error and the Last Error is set to:
"Error: Invalid sample numbers specified"

•LIMS checks that the sample statuses.  If there is a sample with status other than Unreceived or Incomplete, the function triggers an error and the Last Error is set to:
"Error: Can only cancel unreceived and incomplete samples"

•LIMS cancels the samples.  The status fields are set to X for a samples, tests and results.  Normal events for the cancel operation may happen for samples and/or tests, depending on the dbCancelSampleFlag and dbCancelTestsFlag arguments.

 

NOTE: This  function does do a database commit.


Example:

SnumArray[1] = 5686

SnumArray[2] = 5687

dbCancelSampleFlag = "F"

dbCancelTestsFlag = "F"

dbCancelResultsFlag = "T"

auditReason = "Testing LB"

status = FastCancelSamples(SnumArray, dbCancelSampleFlag, dbCancelTestsFlag, dbCancelResultsFlag, auditReason)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

FullReactivateSample    

Purpose:

The FullReactivateSample function reactivates the specified sample along with the specified tests of that sample.

Syntax:

status = FullReactivateSample(sample, testNumbers)

sample – the sample number of the sample to be reactivated

testNumbers – a one dimensional array that contains the test numbers to be reactivated

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the sample with the specified sample number.  If this fails, LIMS returns the error: "Sample does not exist".

•LIMS verifies that test number were specified.  If no test numbers were specified, the LIMS returns the last error: "No test numbers specified."

•LIMS determines if the sample can be reactivated.  If it cannot, LIMS will trigger an error that explains the reason.

•LIMS reactivates the sample.  For each of the sample’s tests, if its test number matches one of the elements in the array of test numbers, LIMS will reactivate it.

•LIMS assigns the audit type "ReactivateSample" to the pending database transaction.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

Suppose sample 513 has tests 718 and 719.  The following example reactivates sample 513 and test 718.  Test 717 does not belong to the sample and will not be reactivated.  Test 719 will not be reactivated either, because it is not included in the array of test numbers.

mytests[1] = 717
mytests[2] = 718
status = FullReactivateSample(513, mytests)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

GetLastSampleGroupNumber    

Purpose:

The GetLastSampleGroupNumber function returns the sample number of the first sample in the most recently logged group of samples.

Syntax:

num = GetLastSampleGroupNumber()

Comments:

 

A group is all samples logged while the Sample Interface is open.

This command is typically used in generating Text Id’s on a sample template.

Example:

num = GetLastSampleGroupNumber()

Returns:

num = 248

 

InControl    

Purpose:

The InControl function marks the specified result as in control.

CAUTION:  This function should be used with caution since it overrides system determined values.


Syntax:

status = InControl(resultNumber, override)

resultNumber – the result number of the result to be marked as in control

override – the value true if the product control limits should be overridden.  The default is false.

CAUTION: If override is set to T then any entries in the RESULT_SPEC table related to this result will be deleted since the Override operation is overriding any spec checking. 


Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no sample is found with a result having the specified result number, LIMS will trigger the error "Error: invalid result was specified".

•LIMS marks the result as in control and applies the override.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database. This function must be executed from an analysis calculation or format calculation.


Example:

The following example marks result 5812 as in control and overrides the product control limits.

status = InControl(5812, "T")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

InSpec    

Purpose:

The InSpec function can override product specifications and set the result IN_SPEC field to true. 

CAUTION:  This function should be used with caution since it overrides system determined values.


Syntax:

status = InSpec(resultNumber, overrideFlag)

resultNumber – Result number of the result that will be modified

overrideFlag – Boolean, determines whether the product specifications can be overridden.

reportedResultsOverride – a Boolean which determines whether the reported results can be overridden

CAUTION: If overrideFlag is set to T then all the Result_Spec records are removed except the one that matches the Result record. This entry will have Spec Code set to 0 since the Override operation is overriding any spec checking.  This change allows systems to query against the Result_Spec table for all results.


Comments:

This function should be executed from an analysis calculation or format calculation. The function performs the following steps:

1.  The system checks the resultNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error “Error: invalid result was specified: resultNumber”.

2.  The system sets the In Spec flag to T for the result, Override = T and SPEC_OVERRIDE is set to T.

NOTE: When this function is called, a field in the RESULT table called SPEC_OVERRIDE will be set to the value of the overRideFlag. The default value for the SPEC_OVERRIDE field is F.


Example:

resultNum = 48765

overrideFlag = "T"

reportedResultsOverride = "T"

status = InSpec(resultNum, overrideFlag, reportedResultsOverride)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

LogSample  

Purpose:

The LogSample function logs a sample and returns the new sample number.

Syntax:

sampleNumber = LogSample(templateName, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

templateName – string define the Sample Login Template to be used to create the sample.  Fields specified must be template fields.

fieldsArray –  One dimensional array of the Sample fields to be populated.

valuesArray – One dimensional array of the values to use to populate the corresponding fields.

Comments:

Fields specified must be template fields. 

 

Mandatory entries are not enforced.  Template formulae are executed. 

Sample template should set "Edit Tests" to False so the Test Editor does not display.

NOTE: A database commit is part of this function.


Example:

fieldsArray[1] = "CUSTOMER"

fieldsArray[2] = "SAMPLE_NAME"

valuesArray[1] = "NIXON-D"

valuesArray[2] = "Low pulp"

sampleNumber = LogSample("PAPER", fieldsArray, valuesArray)

Returns:

sampleNumber = 52

 

MakeSubSample    

Purpose:

The MakeSubSample function makes a sub sample of a sample.

Syntax:

subSampleNumber = MakeSubSample(sampleNumber, analysesArray)

sampleNumber – the sample number of the sample from which a sub sample is to be created.

analysesArray – an array of analysis names whose tests are to be copied from the sample to the sub sample.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the specified sample does not exist, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample does not exist".

•LIMS makes a sub sample of the specified sample and copies all tests whose name is equal to one of the elements in the analysesArray.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Error creating SubSample".

•If an audit reason is specified for the sample table, LIMS will assign it to the new sample.

•LIMS assigns the audit type "CreateSubSample".

•LIMS adds the sample to its context.

NOTE: This function does save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example makes a sub sample of sample 4482 and copies the tests with name "LOD" and those with name "POTENCY".

an[1] = "LOD"
an[2] = "POTENCY"
subSampleNumber = MakeSubSample(4482, an)

Returns:

The function returns the sample number of the created sample if it completes successfully.  It returns the value 0 if an error is triggered.

 

OutOfControl    

Purpose:

The OutOfControl function marks the specified result as out of control.

CAUTION:  This function should be used with caution since it overrides system determined values.


Syntax:

status = OutOfControl(resultNumber, override)

resultNumber – the result number of the result to be marked as out of control

override – the value true if the product control limits should be overridden.  The default is false.

CAUTION: If override is set to T then any entries in the RESULT_SPEC table related to this result will be deleted since the Override operation is overriding any spec checking. 


Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no sample is found with a result having the specified result number, LIMS will trigger the error "Error: invalid result was specified".

•LIMS marks the result as out of control and applies the override.

NOTE:  This function does not save changes to the database.

This function must be executed from an analysis calculation or format calculation.


Example:

The following example marks result 5812 as out of control and overrides the product control limits.

status = OutOfControl(5812, "T")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OutOfSpec    

Purpose:

The OutOfSpec function can override the product specifications and set the IN_SPEC field of the result to false. 

CAUTION:  This function should be used with caution since it overrides system determined values.


Syntax:

status = OutOfSpec(resultNumber, overrideFlag)

resultNumber – the result number of the result to be marked as out of specification.

overrideFlag – the value true if the product specifications should be overridden.  The default is false.

reportedResultsOverride – a Boolean which determines whether the reported results can be overridden.

CAUTION: If overrideFlag is set to T then all the Result_Spec records are removed except the one that matches the Result record. This entry will have Spec Code set to 0 since the Override operation is overriding any spec checking.  This change allows systems to query against the Result_Spec table for all results.


Comments:

The overrideFlag determines whether the product specifications can be overridden.  This function should be executed from an analysis calculation or format calculation.

The function performs the following steps:

1.  The system checks the resultNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error “Error: invalid result was specified: resultNumber”.

2.  The system sets the In Spec flag to F for the result.

NOTE:  When this function is called, a field in the RESULT table called SPEC_OVERRIDE will be set to the value of the overrideFlag. The default value for the SPEC_OVERRIDE field is F.


Example:

resultNum = 57373

overrideFlag = "T"

reportedResultsOverride = "T"

status = OutOfSpec(resultNum, overrideFlag, reportedResultsOverride)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

PartialRestoreSample

Purpose:

Restores the sample with the identified tests.

Syntax:

status = PartialRestoreSample(sampleNumber, testNumbersArray)

sampleNumber - The sample_number of the sample to restore

testNumbersArray - A single dimensional array containing the test numbers to restore.  The test numbers must belong to the sampleNumber specified. If the testNumberArray is blank or not specified, only the sample will be restored.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•LabWare checks if sampleNumber is valid.  If not an error is triggered: “object is not a sample”

•LabWare checks if sampleNumber exists.  If not an error is triggered: “Sample does not exist:”

•LabWare checks the test numbers are valid test numbers for the specified sample.  If not an error is triggered: “Invalid test number specified:”

•LabWare restores the sample and any test number identified in the array.

Example:

 

 

sampleNumber = 25

testNumbersArray[1] = 154

testNumbersArray[2] = 156

 

status = PartialRestoreSample(sampleNumber, testNumbersArray)

Returns:

 

The function returns the status of True if it completes successfully.  It returns the status of False if an error is triggered.  LastError contains the reason for failure.

 

PutSampleOnHold  

Purpose:

The PutSampleOnHold function puts a sample on hold (changes the sample status to Hold).

  

Syntax:

status = PutSampleOnHold(sampleNumber)

sampleNumber – the number of the sample to put on hold.

Comments:

Only samples with status Incomplete or Unreceived can be put on Hold.

This function does Not save changes to the database.

Example:

The following example changes the status of sample 5547 to Hold.

sampno = 5547

status = PutSampleOnHold(sampno)

Returns: 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

ReactivateSample    

Purpose:

The ReactivateSample function changes sample status to its previous status of In Progress or Complete.  This makes the sample available to the user for modifications.  If allTestsFlag is true, this is a full reactivate and test status is changed from Authorized or Rejected to their previous status of Complete. This makes the tests available to the user for modifications

Syntax:

status = ReactivateSample(sampleNumber, allTestsFlag)

sampleNumber – the sample number of the sample to be reactivated

allTestsFlag – a Boolean indicating if all tests on the sample are to be reactivated

Comments:

Sample status must be Authorized or Rejected. 

If the allTestsFlag is true then sample, test and result statuses will be reactivated (i.e. a full reactivate), if the allTestsFlag is false then only the Sample status will be reactivated.

NOTE:This  function does not perform a database commit.

NOTE:   The business rules, function privileges and CONTINUE_REACTIVATE status rule is checked before this function is performed.

NOTE:  A full reactivate will restore canceled tests and results to their old status.  This will inlcude any tests and results that may have been canceled prior to a review processnot ONLY those tests and results that may have been canceled when the sample was reviewed.


Example:

status = ReactivateSample(200, "T") 

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

ReceiveSample    

Purpose:

The ReceiveSample function changes a sample status to Incomplete.

Syntax:

status = ReceiveSample(sampleNumber , commitFlag)

sampleNumber - (Required) The sample number of the sample to be received.

commitFlag – (Boolean, Optional) If set to true, a commit is performed to save changes to the database.  The default value is False.

Comments:

Sample status must be Unreceived.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database unless the commitFlag = True.


Example:

sampleNumber = 741
commitFlag = "T"

status = ReceiveSample(sampleNumber, commitFlag)

Returns:

The function returns true if successful. If it is not successful, it will answer false and lastError will be set with an appropriate error message.

 

ReceiveSamples    

Purpose:

The ReceiveSamples function changes the status of the specified samples from unreceived to incomplete or complete depending on the existence of assigned tests.

Syntax:

status = ReceiveSamples(sampleIds, commitFlag)

SampleIds – (Required) One dimensional array of integers defining the sample numbers of the samples to be received.

commitFlag – (Boolean, Optional) If set to true, a commit is performed to save changes to the database.  The default value is False.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that the SampleIds array has entries.  If there is no sample ids, the LIMS return:
"Error: No sample ids".

•LIMS verifies that the samples have not been canceled or reviewed.  If any sample has been canceled or reviewed, the LIMS returns:
"Error: Cannot receive samples that are reviewed or canceled'"

•LIMS verifies that the sample numbers provided are valid.  If there is no sample with the provided sample number, the LIMS return:
"Error: One or more sample ids were invalid:"

•LIMS retrieves the samples specified and receives the collected samples.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database unless the commitFlag = True.


Example:

The following code receives sample 2 and 15 if the samples were not already received.

SampleIds[1] = 2
sampleIds[2] = "15"
commitFlag = "T"
status = ReceiveSamples (sampleIds, commitFlag)

Returns:

The function returns true if successful. If it is not successful, it will answer false and lastError will be set with an appropriate error message.

 

RefreshSample    

Purpose:

The RefreshSample function refreshes a sample by replacing the data in memory with the data stored in the database.

Syntax:

status = RefreshSample(sampleNumber)

sampleNumber – the sample number of the sample to be refreshed

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no sample is found with the specified sampleNumber, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample does not exis".

•LIMS retrieves all field values for the sample from the database.

•For each test that is currently in memory for the sample, LIMS retrieves all field values of the test from the database.

•For each result that is currently in memory for the sample, LIMS retrieves all field values of the result from the database.

NOTE:  This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example refreshes sample 3811.

status = RefreshSample(3811)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

RejectSample    

Purpose:

The RejectSample function rejects a sample and the tests associated with a  sample.

Syntax:

status = RejectSample(sampleNumber, allTestsFlag)

Comments:

Sample status must be In Progress, Complete or Authorized.

If the allTestsFlag is true then sample, tests and results will be rejected, if the allTestsFlag is false then only the Sample will be rejected.

NOTE: This  function does not perform a database commit.

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and CONTINUE_REJECT status rule is checked before this function is performed.


Example:

status = RejectSample(731)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

ReleaseSample    

Purpose:

The Release Sample function is used to release a sample.

Syntax:

status = Release Sample (sampleNumber, releaseTestsFlag)

sampleNumber – Number of the Sample to be released

releaseTestsFlag –  A Boolean indicating whether the tests are to be released or not.  If true the tests are released as well as the sample.  However, the function will not release tests unless they are completed, authorized, canceled or rejected.

Comments:

NOTE:  A database commit is not part of this function.

Example:

The following example is used to release Sample Number 1

Sample Number = 1

Release Tests Flag = "Y"

status = Release Sample (sample Number, release Tests Flag)

Returns:

 

status = true if the function is successful, false otherwise.

 

Resample    

Purpose:

The ReSample function creates a resample.

Syntax:

id = ReSample(sampleNumber, testScope, auditReason) 'testScope A = All Tests, N = No Tests, F = Failed Tests

Comments:

id is the new sample number. If the function fails, id = 0 is returned. 

testScope is variable indicated the resample type: "A" indicates assign All tests from the original sample to resample.  "N" indicates assign No tests from the original sample to the resample.  "F" indicates assigned all Failed tests from the original sample to the resample.

NOTE: A database commit is part of this function.


Example:

id = ReSample(48, "A", "Customer Request")

Returns:

id = 62

 

RestoreSample    

Purpose:

The RestoreSample function restores a canceled sample its previous status of Unreceived, Incomplete, In Progress, or Complete. Any canceled tests in the sample are restored as well.

Syntax:

status = RestoreSample(sampleNumber)

Comments:

Sample status must be Canceled to be restored.

NOTE: This  function does not perform a database commit.

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and CONTINUE_RESTORE status rule is checked before this function is performed.


Example:

status = RestoreSample(745)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

SampleFieldValuesFromRules  

Purpose:

The SampleFieldValuesFromRules function is used to get the valid values for the specified field from the Sample Rules table based on the analyses present on the specified sample.  All values for the field that meet the Samples Rules for that field for all analyses on the sample are returned in an array.   

Syntax:

status = SampleFieldValuesFromRules(sampleNumber, fieldName, "arrayName")

sampleNumber –Sample Number which will be checked

fieldname – Field name in Sample Rules table that will be searched.

arrayName – The name of the array that will contain the values retrieved from the Sample Rules. 

Example:

sampleNumber = 100

fieldname = "CONTAINER_TYPE"

arrayName = "ContainerTypesArray"

 

status = SampleFieldValuesFromRules(sampleNumber, fieldName, arrayName)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise. 

 

SampleFld    

Purpose:

The SampleFld function returns the value of a sample field.

Syntax:

x = SampleFld(sampleNumber, field)

Comments:

field is any field in the Sample table.

Example:

x = SampleFld(943, "CUSTOMER") 

Returns:

x = "CIA"

 

SetSampleDisplayFields    

Purpose:

The SetSampleDisplayFields function sets the fields that will be displayed in the right-hand pane when a sample is selected in a window such as the Folder Manager window or the Sample Drill Down window.

Syntax:

status = SetSampleDisplayFields(fieldNames)

fieldNames – an array in which each element is the name of a field in the sample table.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that each field name exists in the sample table.  If at least one field does not exist in the sample table, LIMS will trigger the error "Field does not exist in the sample table".

•LIMS sets the display fields for the sample table equal to the specified field names.

It is acceptable to specify an empty array.  In that case, all fields will be displayed.

NOTE:  This specified field names will remain in effect until they are modified or until the user logs out, whichever happens first.


Example:

The following example causes the system to show only the sample number and customer name when a sample is selected in Folder Manager.

myFields[1] = "SAMPLE_NUMBER"
myFields[2] = "CUSTOMER"
status = SetSampleDisplayFields(myFields)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

SyncSamples

Purpose:

The SyncSamples function copies fields from a specified sample to the specified sample numbers.

Syntax:

status = SyncSamples(samplesArray, fieldsArray, sourceSample, auditReason, commitFlag)

samplesArray – the array of the sample numbers to be synced with the sourceSample. If the array is a 2D array, only the first column is used.

fieldsArray – the array of field names to be synced.  If the array is a 2D array, only the first column is used.

sourceSample – the sample to which the other samples are synced.  If that sample is in context, the values from context are used.  Otherwise, the values from the database are used.

auditReason – the audit reason to be assigned to the updates.

commitFlag – a Boolean flag that if true will commit the data when the function completes. Otherwise, the data is committed during the next LIMS commit to the sample (the next commit may happen when a later LIMS Basic function performs a commit or when a Save operation is performed).  If not specified, the default is true.

NOTE: This function will copy null values i.e. if the field value in the source sample is null, the null value will be copied to the samples in the Samples Array.


Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•The system checks the sourceSample.  If this fails, the system will trigger the error " The specified sample does not exist: sourceSample ".

•The system checks the fields in the fieldsArray parameter.  If an invalid field is found, the system will trigger the error: “Field does not exist in the Sample table: field”.

•The system will assign the values from the sourceSample to the corresponding fields in the fieldsArray of the samples in the samplesArray. If an error occurs when updating a sample, the system will trigger the error “Error committing sample”.

Example:

The following example copies the Location from sample 120 to the samples 123 and 124.

samplesArray[1] = 123
samplesArray[2] = 124
fieldsArray[1] = " LOCATION "
sourceSample = 120
auditReason = “Syncing Location”
commitFlag = “T”
status = SyncSamples(samplesArray, fieldsArray, sourceSample, auditReason, commitFlag)

Returns:

The function returns true if successful. If it is not successful, it will answer false and lastError will be set with an appropriate error message.

 

TakeSampleOffHold    

Purpose:

The TakeSampleOffHold function takes a sample of hold (changes the sample status back to previous value). 

Syntax:

status = TakeSampleOffHold(sampleNumber)

sampleNumber – the number of the sample to take off hold.

Comments:

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example changes the status of sample 5547 from Hold back to its previous status.

sampno = 5547

status = TakeSampleOffHold(sampno)

Returns: 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

UnreceiveSample    

Purpose:

The UnreceiveSample function changes sample status to Unreceived.

Syntax:

status = UnreceiveSample(sampleNumber)

Comments:

Sample status must be Incomplete.

This function does not perform a database commit.

Example:

status = UnreceiveSample(48)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

UnreleaseSample    

Purpose:

The UnreleaseSample function Unreleases a sample.

Syntax:

status = UnreleaseSample(sampleNumber)

Comments:

Sample must be Released.

NOTE: This  function does not do a database commit.

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and CONTINUE_UNRELEASE status rule is checked before this function is performed.


Example:

status = UnreleaseSample(49)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

UpdateSampleFld    

Purpose:

The UpdateSampleFld function updates a field in a sample.

Syntax:

status = UpdateSampleFld(sampleName, field, value, commitFlag)

sampleName – sample number of the target sample.

field – sample field to be updated.

value – value to be posted to the specified field.

commitFlag – T, the data is committed, otherwise the data is committed during the next LIMS commit to the sample (the next commit may happen when a later LIMS Basic function performs a commit or when a Save operation is performed).

Comments:

CAUTION: This function should be used with caution as any field in the SAMPLE table may be updated. 

 

NOTE: This function does not check sample status i.e. changes can be made to samples that are authorized, rejected or cancelled.  If you want status to be considered you must put code into your routine to check for status first.


Example:

status = UpdateSampleFld(843,"PRODUCT","CHOCOLATE", "T")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

UpdateSampleFlds    

Purpose:

This function support the update of multiple fields on the sample sample.

Syntax:

status = UpdateSampleFlds(samplesNumber,fieldsArray,valuesArray)

sampleNumber – integer defining the sample number of the target sample.

fieldsArray – one dimensional array of Sample table fields.

valuesArray – one dimensional array of values to be populated into the corresponding fields.

commitFlag - Boolean that determines if the information is posted to cache awaiting the next save or committed immediately.  The default value is false.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•The system compares the fieldsArray to the valuesArray.  If they are not the same size, the system returns the last error: "Error: field and value arrays must be the same size".

•The system locates the targeted sample.  If the sample is not found, the system returns the last error: "Sample does not exist" + <sampleNumber>.

•The system evaluates the entries in the fieldsArray.  If an invalid field is found, the system returns the last error: "Invalid field for table: Sample".

•The system evaluates the entries in the valuesArray.  If an invalid entry is found, the system return the last error: "Invalid value for sample record" + <value> + "for field" + <field>.

The system updates the field values for the target sample in cache.

Example:

sampleNumber = 2397

fieldsArray[1] = "SAMPLE_NAME"

fieldsArray[2] = "COLLECTION_METHOD"

valuesArray[1] = "Function demo"

valuesArray[2] = "DEMO_1"

commitFlag = "T"

Status = UpdateSampleFlds(sampleNumber, fieldsArray, valuesArray, commitFlag)

Returns:

The function returns the status of True if it completes successfully.  It returns the status of False if an error is triggered.

 

UpdateSamplesFlds

Purpose:

This function support the update of multiple fields on multiple samples.  The same fields and field values are applied to all samples.

Syntax:

status = UpdateSamplesFlds(samplesArray,fieldsArray,valuesArray,commitFlag)

samplesArray – one dimensional array of Sample numbers to be updated.

fieldsArray – one dimensional array of Sample table fields to be updated.

valuesArray – one dimensional array of values to be populated into the corresponding fields.

commitFlag - Boolean that determines if the information is posted to cache awaiting the next save or committed immediately.  The default value is false.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•The system checks the size of the fieldsArray to the valuesArray.  If either is 0, the system returns the last error: "fields and values must be specified".

•The system compares the fieldsArray to the valuesArray.  If they are not the same size, the system returns the last error: " field and value arrays must be the same size".

•The system locates the targeted sample.  If the sample is not found, the system returns the last error: "Sample does not exist" + <sampleNumber>.

•The system evaluates the entries in the fieldsArray.  If an invalid field is found, the system returns the last error: "Invalid field for table: Sample".

•The system evaluates the entries in the valuesArray.  If an invalid entry is found, the system return the last error: "Invalid value for sample record" + <value> + "for field" + <field>.

•The system updates the field values for the target sample in cache or in the database depending on the commitFlag setting.

Example:

 

 

samplesArray[1] = 99

samplesArray[2] = 101

fieldsArray[1] = "ASSIGNED_OPERATOR"

valuesArray[1] = "AUTO"

commitFlag = "T"

status = UpdateSamplesFlds(samplesArray,fieldsArray,valuesArray,commitFlag)

Returns:

 

The function returns the status of True if it completes successfully.  It returns the status of False if an error is triggered.

AssignedGroups    

Purpose:

The AssignedGroups function fills a specified array with the names of the groups that are currently in effect for the current user.  This includes all mandatory groups as well as any optional groups that may have been explicitly added by the user in the current login session.

Syntax:

status = AssignedGroups("groupArrayName")

groupArrayName – the name of the array to be populated with group names

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS creates a new array variable with the specified name and populates it with the group names that are currently in effect.

•LIMS does not sort the array of group names.

NOTE: This function does not make changes or save them to the database.


Example:

Suppose the current user is assigned to the group "MICRO" and has optional groups "CHEM_A" and "CHEM_B".  Suppose the user has added the optional group "CHEM_A" but not the optional group "CHEM_B".  The following example will fill the array named "myGroups" with two elements having the values "MICRO" and "CHEM_A", respectively.

status = AssignedGroups("myGroups")

Returns:

The function returns the value true.

 

AssignOptionalGroups    

Purpose:

The AssignOptionalGroups function adds the specified optional groups to the logged user’s current session.

Syntax:

status = AssignOptionalGroups(groupNamesArray)

groupNamesArray – the name of the 1 dimensional array that defines the optional groups to be added.  The value of the group not the group description must be specified.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies a group name has been specified.  If no input array has been specified, the LIMS returns: "No group names specified."

•LIMS verifies that each group name provided in the group names array is valid.  If a string in the group names array is not a optional group name, the LIMS returns:
"Group name not a valid optional group:" followed by the invalid string.

•LIMS updates the cache adding the specified optional groups for the currently logged user.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following statement adds the optional groups: Optional 1 and Optional 2 to the user’s current session.

groupNamesArray[1] = "OPT1"
groupNamesArray[2] = "OPT2"
status = AssignOptionalGroups(groupNamesArray)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

AuditReasonForObject    

Purpose:

The AuditReasonForObject function returns the audit reason that was specified for an object.

Syntax:

reason = AuditReasonForObject(tableName, keyFieldValue)

tableName – the name of the table that contains the record whose audit reason is to be returned

keyFieldValue – the primary key value of the record whose audit reason is to be returned

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the key value is blank or missing, LIMS will trigger the error "No key value specified".

•If the record identified by the table name and key value is not in context, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified object does not exist, or is not in context".

•If the record is in context, but no audit reason was specified, LIMS will return an empty string.  Otherwise, LIMS will return the audit reason.

NOTE:  This function was designed to be used with the SetAuditReasonForObject function but it can be used to retrieve the audit reason that has been seet by other means.


Example:

The following example obtains the audit reason for the analysis named "LOD".

reason = AuditReasonForObject("ANALYSIS", "LOD")

Returns:

The function returns the audit reason if it completes successfully.  It returns the value "ERROR" if an error is triggered.

 

ChangePassword    

Purpose:

The ChangePassword function opens the Change Password dialog of the LIMS.  When the dialog is closed, the function returns a value indicating if the password has been changed or not.

Syntax:

status = ChangePassword()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS caches the current password for the logged user.

•LIMS opens the Change Password dialog.

•When the Change Password dialog is closed, LIMS determines if the password has been changed.

•If the password has been changed, LIMS returns a status of True.

•If the password has not been changed, LIMS returns a status of False.

NOTE:  This function does not save changes to the database; however, the user’s entry in the Change Password dialog is posted to the database.


Example:

The following code opens the Change Password dialog for the current user and monitors if the user actually changes the password.

Status = ChangePassword()

Returns:

The function returns the status True if the user changes the password and the status False if the user does not change the password. 

 

ChangeSigningPassword   

Purpose:

This function opens the Change Signing Password dialog of the LIMS. When the dialog is closed, the function returns a value indicating if the password has been changed or not.

Syntax:

status = ChangeSigningPassword()

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS caches the current signing password for the logged user.

•LIMS opens the "Change Signing Password" dialog.

•After the "Change Signing Password" dialog is closed, LIMS determines if the signing password has been changed.

•If the signing password has been changed, LIMS returns a status of True; if not, LIMS returns the status False.

Example:

The following example opens the "Change Signing Password" dialog for the current user and verifies if the password has been changed or not.

status = ChangeSigningPassword()

Returns:

This function returns True if the password has been changed, else, it returns the status False.

 

CheckToken      

Purpose:

The CheckToken function verifies the validity of the user’s token.

Syntax:

status = CheckToken(userName)

userName – the name of the user whose token is to be checked.  (The default value is the username of the current user.)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If a value for userName is specified, LIMS will retrieve the record for that user.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified user is not valid".

•If no value for userName is specified, LIMS will use the record of the current user.

•LIMS checks the token of the specified user.  If necessary, LIMS will prompt the user to insert the token.  If the token is found to be invalid, a message box will be displayed and LIMS will trigger the error "Token check failed".

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example checks the validity of the token for the user named "JSMITH".

status = CheckToken("JSMITH")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered or if the token is invalid.

 

ConfirmUser    

Purpose:

The ConfirmUser function optionally prompts a user for a password.  A failure to enter a valid password is logged in the PSWD_FAILURE_LOG table.

Syntax:

status = ConfirmUser(activity, forcePromptFlag)

activity is a string describing the activity that called the function and is part of the log record if the user types an incorrect password. 

If forcePromptFlag is false and the businessRule: Use Default Electronic Records = False, no password prompt is displayed and the function returns true and continues.  If the forcePromptFlag is true or the businessRule: Use Default Electronic Records =True then a password prompt is always displayed when this function is called.

Comments:

 

Example:

status = ConfirmUser("Testing LIMS Basic code.", "T")

Returns:

status = true if the correct password is entered or no prompt displayed, false otherwise.

 

CurrentUserRole    

Purpose:

The CurrentUserRole function returns the role the current user is logged into the system with.

Syntax:

role = CurrentUserRole()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

LIMS retrieves the user role used by the account currently logged into LIMS and populates the variable: role.  If the current user account is not using a role, the system returns a null value.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code returns the role of the logged user.

role = CurrentUserRole()

Returns:

The function returns the role of the currently logged user account.

 

CurrentUserRoles

Purpose:

The CurrentUserRoles function returns the available roles for the logged in user.

Syntax:

status = CurrentUserRoles("roleNameArray")

roleNameArray – the name of the 1 dimensional array which will contain the role names to which the current logged in user has access.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, the system performs the following:

•The system verifies a user is currently logged into the system; if not, then the function returns false.

•The system check the user roles and returns the roles available to the logged in user in the output array.

Example:

status = CurrentUserRoles("roleNameArray")

Returns:

The function returns true if successful. If it is not successful, it will answer false if no user is logged into the LIMS when the function is called.

 

DisableUser    

Purpose:

The DisableUser function sets the specified user account to disabled, preventing additional logging by that user account.

Syntax:

status = DisableUser(userName, auditReason)

userName – the name of the user account to be disabled.

auditReason – the string to be entered in the audit trail as a reason for disabling the user account.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that the active account has the functional privilege: DisableUser.  If the account does not have the functional privilege assigned, then the LIMS returns the last error: "No privilege for the operation."

•LIMS verifies that a target account has been specified.  If no account has been specified, the LIMS returns the last error: "No user name specified"

•LIMS verifies that the target account is a valid user account.  If the target string is not a valid user account, the LIMS returns the last error:
"Invalid user specified:" followed by the input value.

•LIMS updates the user account setting and writes the event to the audit trail using the provided audit reason.

NOTE: This function saves a change to the database.


Example:

The following code disables the user account: DEMO_B and enters "Documentation" as the audit reason.

userName = "DEMO_B"
auditReason = "Documentation"
status = DisableUser(userName, auditReason)

Returns:

The function returns the status True if the account is disabled (and if it was already disabled).  This function returns false if an error is triggered. 

 

EnableUser    

Purpose:

The EnableUser function sets the specified user account to enabled allowing logging by that user account.

Syntax:

status = EnableUser(userName, auditReason)

userName – the name of the user account to set Disabled: false.

auditReason – the string to be entered in the audit trail as a reason for enabling the user account.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that the active account has the functional privilege: EnableUser.  If the account does not have the functional privilege assigned, then the LIMS returns the last error: "No privilege for the operation."

•LIMS verifies that a target account has been specified.  If no account has been specified, the LIMS returns the last error: "No user name specified"

•LIMS verifies that the target account is a valid user account.  If the target string is not a valid user account, the LIMS returns the last error: "Invalid user specified:" followed by the input value.

•LIMS updates the user account setting and writes the event to the audit trail using the provided audit reason.

NOTE: This function does save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code enables the user account: DEMO_B and enters "Documentation" as the audit reason.

userName = " DEMO_B"
auditReason = "Documentation"
status = EnableUser(userName, auditReason)

Returns:

The function returns the status True if the account is enabled (and if it was already enabled).  This function returns false if an error is triggered. 

 

EncryptPassword    

Purpose:

The EncryptPassword function encrypts an unencrypted password string.

Syntax:

encryptedString = EncryptPassword(unencryptedString)

unencryptedString – the password to be encrypted

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no string is specified, LIMS will return an empty string.

•LIMS encrypts the specified string and returns the result.

Example:

The following example encryps the value "cats".

encryptedString = EncryptPassword("cats")

Returns:

The function returns a text value

 

ERecordPrompt    

Purpose:

This function displays the E-Record Dialog which includes: 1) a meaning; 2) Audit Reason field; 3) Password field.

Syntax:

status = ERecordPrompt(meaning, reasonList, defaultReason)

meaning – string displayed on the dialog as the meaning of the event.

reasonList – string defining the LIMS list used to populated the Audit Reason dropdown list.

defaultReason – string defaulted in the Audit Reason field.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•The LIMS retieves the List specified by the reasonList parameter.  If unsuccessful, LIMS returns the last error: Invalid audit list.

•If the LIMS is unable to locate the current users User record, the LIMS returns the last error: The user record does not exist or is removed.

•The LIMS verifies the account is not disabled.  If disabled, the LIMS returns the last error: User login is disabled.

•LIMS verifies that the password has not expired.  If expired, the LIMS returns the last error: Password has expired.

•LIMS verifies the entered password against the user record.  If it does not match, LIMS returns the last error:  Password is not valid.

•If the dialog is closed without a password, the system sets the dialogCancel variable to true.

Example:

status = ERecordPrompt("Demo of LIMS Basic", "AUDIT_TEXT", "Configured audit default")

Returns:

status = true if the function is successful, otherwise false.

The system variable: DialogCanceled is set to true if the dialog is closed using the Cancel button; otherwise true.

 

ESignatureRecords  

Purpose:

The ESignatureRecords function displays a set of objects in a single E-Signature Dialog for audit reason assignment and signature.

Syntax:

status = ESignatureRecords(table, keyFields, keyValues, meaning, reasonRequired, reasonList, defaultReason, allowCancel)

table – the name of the table to which the target objects belong;

keyFields- one dimensional array defining the key fields of the objects.

1. VERSION is an invalid entry.

2. Parent table key must be the first key entered

keyValues- An array listing the key values to be signed

meaning- String displayed in the upper pane of the E-Signature Dialog defining the meaning of the signature

reasonRequired- Boolean; if True, the Audit Reason field is mandatory, if false, the field is not mandatory.  The default value is false

reasonList- string defining the List table entry that populates the dropdown list on the E-Signature Dialog

defaultReason- string that is defaulted into the Audit Reason field

allowCancel- A Boolean indicating if the Cancel button is displayed in the Esig dialog.  This argument is only for use with RESULT records since a special dialog is displayed which displays only Result Number and Name column headers and is not applicable to other records.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS checks the reasonList parameter.  If the list is not located, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid audit list: reasonList".

•LIMS evaluates the keyFields input.  If the array includes the Version field, LIMS will trigger the error "Error: can't specify version as a key field".  If the parent table key field is not the first entry, LIMS will trigger the error "error: Parent table key must be the first entry specified by the user".

•LIMS gets the parent record for each specified key values.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "The record does not exist:". 

•LIMS checks to see if the parent record has changes. If no changes are found, LIMS will trigger the error "The record does not have pending changes:".

•LIMS displays the E-Signature Dialog.

Function only works if the Parent Table is a single key table.

Example:

 

 

table = "SAMPLE"

keyfields[1] = "SAMPLE_NUMBER"

keyvalues[1] = 736

keyvalues[2] = 737

keyvalues[3] = 738

keyvalues[4] = 739

keyvalues[5] = 740

meaning = "LB DEMO"

reasonRequired = "T"

reasonList = "AUDIT_REASON"

defaultReason = "Default reason set by LB"

allowCancel = “F”

status = ESignatureRecords(table, keyFields, keyValues, meaning, reasonRequired, reasonList, defaultReason, allowCancel)

Returns:

 

Status = True if successfully displays E-Signature Dialog, false otherwise.

The system variable: DialogCanceled is set to true if the dialog is closed using the Cancel button; otherwise false.

 

GetAccessFunctionIds   

Purpose:

This function gets all the access function ids assigned to the named user. If no user name is specified, then it returns all known access function ids defined in the system.

Syntax:

status = GetAccessFunctionIds(userName, "outputArrayName")

userName – the user id of the user whose assigned access function ids are required.

outputArrayName – the name of the output array containing the returned access function ids.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS verifies if a user name has been specified; if not, then LIMS checks the database for all access function ids defined in the system and populates the output array with the same.

•If a user name has been specified, then LIMS verifies if the given user name is a valid one; if not, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "User <user name> does not exist", where <user name> is the given user name.

•If the given user name is valid, then LIMS checks the database for all access function ids assigned to the user and populates the output array with the same.

Example:

The following example checks all access function ids assigned to the user "system" and returns the same in the specified output array "outArray".

status = GetAccessFunctionIds("SYSTEM", "outArray") returns:

outArray[i] = <Access Function Id>,

for i number of rows, each row containing a distinct access function id, where i = the number of access function ids defined for the user "SYSTEM".

Returns:

This function returns True if the function is successful, else, it returns the status False.

 

GetGroups  

Purpose:

This function gets all the groups assigned to the named user. If no user name is specified, then it returns all known groups defined in the system.

Syntax:

status = GetGroups(userName, "outputArrayName")

userName – the user id of the user whose assigned groups are required.

outputArrayName – the name of the output array containing the returned groups.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS verifies if a user name has been specified; if not, then LIMS checks the database (the list entries) for all groups defined in the system and populates the output array with the same.

•If a user name has been specified, then LIMS verifies if the given user name is a valid one; if not, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "User <user name> does not exist", where <user name> is the given user name.

•If the given user name is valid, then LIMS checks the database for all groups assigned to the user and populates the output array with the same.

Example:

The following example checks all groups assigned to the user "system" and returns the same in the specified output array "outArray".

status = GetGroups("SYSTEM", "outArray") returns:

outArray[1] = "DEFAULT"

outArray[2] = "SYSTEM".

Returns:

This function returns True if the function is successful, else, it returns the status False.

 

GetRoles  

Purpose:

This function gets all the roles assigned to the named user. If no user name is specified, then it returns all known roles defined in the system.

Syntax:

status = GetRoles(userName, "outputArrayName")

userName – the user id of the user whose assigned roles are required.

outputArrayName – the name of the output array containing the returned roles.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS verifies if a user name has been specified; if not, then LIMS checks the database (the users table) for all roles defined in the system and populates the output array with the same.

•If a user name has been specified, then LIMS verifies if the given user name is a valid one; if not, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "User <user name> does not exist", where <user name> is the given user name.

•LIMS then checks the database to find out if the given user uses any roles; if not, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "User <user name> does not use roles", where <user name> is the given user name.

•If the given user name is valid and it uses roles, then LIMS checks the database for all roles assigned to the user and populates the output array with the same.

Example:

The following example checks all roles assigned to the user "JOHNS" and returns the same in the specified output array "outArray".

status = GetRoles("JOHNS", "outArray") returns:

outArray[i] = <Role name>,

for i number of rows, each row containing a distinct role, where i = the number of roles defined for the user "JOHNS".

Returns:

This function returns True if the function is successful, else, it returns the status False.

 

GroupClause    

Purpose:

The GroupClause function returns the clause that is appended to SQL queries to ensure that the current user can access only those records that belong to groups assigned to the user.

Syntax:

clause = GroupClause()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If a user is currently logged in, LIMS will return the group clause for that user.

•Otherwise, LIMS will return an emptry string.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example returns the group clause for the current user.

clause = GroupClause()

Returns:

The function returns the group clause if a user is currently logged in.  It returns an empty string otherwise.

 

HasGroupAccess   

Purpose:

This function determines if the active user has the specified Group configured on their account or on their active role.

Syntax:

status = HasGroupAccess(groupName)

groupName – text parameter with the key value for the Group to be checked.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS evaluates the specified group name.  If no group name was specified, LIMS sets the LastError variable to:  "No group name specified."

•LIMS checks the user’s profile for access to the group name.

Example:

groupName = "SYSTEM"

status = HasGroupAccess(groupName)

Returns:

This function returns true if the user has access to the specified group and false if the user does not have access to the specified group.

 

IsUserCertifiedForAnalysis    

Purpose:

The IsUserCertifiedForAnalysis function returns a Boolean indicating whether  a user is certified to run an analysis.

Syntax:

status = IsUserCertifiedForAnalysis(userName, analysisName)

Comments:
