The user must have the analysis assigned in the User Configuration to be certified for the analysis.

Example:

status = IsUserCertifiedForAnalysis("SMITH-A", "FAT-SOX") 

Returns:

status = true if the user is certified to run the analysis, false otherwise.

 

IsUserCertifiedForInstrument    

Purpose:

The IsUserCertifiedForInstrument function returns a Boolean indicating  whether a user is certified to operate an instrument.

Syntax:

status = IsUserCertifiedForAnalysis(userName, instrumentName)

Comments:

The user must have the instrument assigned in the User Configuration to be  certified for the instrument.

Example:

status = IsUserCertifiedForInstrument("SMITH-A", "GC-1") 

Returns:

status = true if the user is certified to run the instrument, false otherwise.

 

PromptForAuditReason    

Purpose:

The PromptForAuditReason function promps the user for entering a reason for a pending database transaction.

Syntax:

status = PromptForAuditReason(promptText, tableName)

promptText – the prompt text to be displayed in the prompt window

tableName – the name of the table for which the user is being prompted

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS opens the audit reason dialog and displays the requested prompt text.

•If the user does not enter a reason, LIMS will trigger the error "No Audit Reason set".

•If no table is specified, LIMS will associate the reason with all tables.  Otherwise, LIMS will associate the reason with the specified table.

•LIMS sets the local variable promptreason equal to the entered reason.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.

NOTE:  This function puts a variable called promptreason into context which contatins the audit reason entered at the prompt.


Example:

The following example prompts the user for a reason and then creates a new lot from the template named "LOTTPL1".

status = PromptForAuditReason("Reason for new lot", "LOT")
IF (status) THEN
    status = CreateLot("LOTTPL1", "", "")
ELSE
    msgbox("Aborted")
ENDIF

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

PromptForIdentity    

Purpose:

The PromptForIdentity function prompts for an identity string, i.e., a string that satisfies the rules for names of records in Table Manager.

Syntax:

newIdentity = PromptForIdentity(title, maxSize, default)

title – the prompt text to be shown (optional)

maxSize – the maximum permitted length of the identity string (the default is 10 if no value is specified)

default – the default value to be displayed (optional)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS opens an Identity Prompt Dialog, displays the specified title and default value, and waits for the user to press OK or Cancel.

•If the user presses Cancel, LIMS will return a null value.

•If the user presses OK, LIMS will return the value entered by the user.

Example:

The following example prompts for a name of up to 10 characters.

newIdentity = PromptForIdentity("Name", 10, "")

Returns:

The function returns a text string that represents an identity.

 

RemoveAccessFunctionIds   

Purpose:

This function removes all the given access function ids from all the given users, except for those given users who use roles.

Syntax:

status = RemoveAccessFunctionIds(userNameArray, functionIdArray)

userNameArray – input array containing user ids of users from whom the access function ids are to be removed.

functionIdArray – input array containing the defined access function ids to be removed from the array of users.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS verifies if the input user name array is an actual array; if not, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "Parameter 1 is not an array.".

•LIMS verifies if the input function id array is an actual array; if not, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "Parameter 2 is not an array.".

•LIMS verifies if either of the input arrays, viz., the user name array or the function id array is empty; if yes, then LIMS stops the execution and returns false.

•LIMS takes the users given in the user name input array one by one and verifies if they are valid user names; if any one of them is found to be invalid, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "User <user name> does not exist", where <user name> is the given user name.

•LIMS also verifies if any of the given users uses roles; if true, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "User <user name> uses roles", where <user name> is the given user name.

•If all verifications are satisfactorily through, then LIMS checks each of the users defined in the user input array one by one and verifies whether each of the access function ids (defined in the input function id array) are assigned to the user. If any such are found to be assigned, then LIMS removes the assigned function id from the user and saves the change to the database.

Example:

The following example removes the access functions ids given in the input array "functionArray" ("AuthorizeBatchResults", "AuthorizeProjectResults", "AuthorizeResult", "AuthorizeSample" and "AuthorizeTest") from each of the users "JANES" and "JOHNS" given in the input array "userArray" and saves the changes to the database.

userArray[1] = "JANES"

userArray[2] = "JOHNS"

 

functionArray[1] = "AuthorizeBatchResults"

functionArray[2] = "AuthorizeProjectResults"

functionArray[3] = "AuthorizeResult"

functionArray[4] = "AuthorizeSample"

functionArray[5] = "AuthorizeTest"

 

status = RemoveAccessFunctionIds(userArray, functionArray)

Returns:

This function returns True if it is successful, else, it returns the status False.

 

RemoveGroups   

Purpose:

This function removes all the given group names from all the given users, except for those given users who use roles.

Syntax:

status = RemoveGroups(userNameArray, groupNamesArray)

userNameArray – input array containing user ids of users from whom the groups are to be removed.

groupNamesArray – input array containing the defined group names to be removed from the array of users.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS verifies if the input user name array is an actual array; if not, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "Parameter 1 is not an array.".

•LIMS verifies if the input group names array is an actual array; if not, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "Parameter 2 is not an array.".

•LIMS verifies if either of the input arrays, viz., the user name array or the group names array is empty; if yes, then LIMS stops the execution and returns true.

•LIMS takes the users given in the user name input array one by one and verifies if they are valid user names; if any one of them is found to be invalid, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "User <user name> does not exist", where <user name> is the given user name.

•LIMS also verifies if any of the given users uses roles; if true, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "User <user name> uses roles", where <user name> is the given user name.

•If all verifications are satisfactorily through, then LIMS checks each of the users defined in the user input array one by one and verifies whether each of the group names (defined in the input group names array) are assigned to the user. If any such are found to be assigned, then LIMS removes the assigned group name from the user and saves the change to the database.

Example:

The following example removes the defined groups given in the input array "groupsArray" ("AUTH" and "TEST") from each of the users "JANES" and "JOHNS" given in the input array "userArray" and saves the changes to the database.

userArray[1] = "JANES"

userArray[2] = "JOHNS"

 

groupsArray[1] = "AUTH"

groupsArray[2] = "TEST"

 

status = RemoveGroups(userArray, groupsArray)

Returns:

This function returns True if it is successful, else, it returns the status False.

 

RemoveOptionalGroups    

Purpose:

The RemoveOptionalGroups function removes the specified optional groups from the logged user’s current session.

Syntax:

status = RemoveOptionalGroups("groupNamesArray")

groupNamesArray – the name of the 1 dimensional array that defines the optional groups to be removed.  The value of the group not the group description must be specified.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that an input array has been specified.  If no input array has been specified, the LIMS returns: "No group names specified."

•LIMS verifies that each group name provided in the group names array is valid.  If a string in the group names array is not a optional group name, the LIMS returns:
"Group name not a valid optional group:" followed by the invalid string.

•LIMS updates the cache setting for the optional groups for the currently logged user.

NOTE:  This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following statement removes the optional groups: Optional 1 and Optional 2 from the users current session.

groupNamesArray[1] = "OPT1"
groupNamesArray[2] = "OPT2"
status = RemoveOptionalGroups(groupNamesArray)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

RemoveRoles  

Purpose:

This function removes all the given roles from all the given users, except for those given users who do not use roles.

Syntax:

status = RemoveRoles(userNameArray, roleNamesArray)

userNameArray – input array containing user ids of users from whom the roles are to be removed.

roleNamesArray – input array containing the defined roles to be removed from the array of users.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS verifies if the input user name array is an actual array; if not, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "Parameter 1 is not an array.".

•LIMS verifies if the input role names array is an actual array; if not, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "Parameter 2 is not an array.".

•LIMS verifies if either of the input arrays, viz., the user name array or the role names array is empty; if yes, then LIMS stops the execution and returns true.

•LIMS takes the users given in the user name input array one by one and verifies if they are valid user names; if any one of them is found to be invalid, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "User <user name> does not exist", where <user name> is the given user name.

•LIMS also verifies if any of the given users uses roles; if NOT, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "User <user name> does not use roles", where <user name> is the given user name.

•If all verifications are satisfactorily through, then LIMS checks each of the users defined in the user input array one by one and verifies whether each of the role names (defined in the input role names array) are assigned to the user. If any such are found to be assigned, then LIMS removes the assigned role name from the user and saves the change to the database.

Example:

The following example removes the defined roles given in the input array "rolesArray" ("AUTHMANAGER" and "PROJECTMANAGER") from each of the users "JANES" and "JOHNS" given in the input array "userArray" and saves the changes to the database.

userArray[1] = "JANES"

userArray[2] = "JOHNS"

 

rolesArray[1] = "AUTHMANAGER"

rolesArray[2] = "PROJECTMANAGER"

 

status = RemoveRoles(userArray, rolesArray)

Returns:

This function returns True if it is successful, else, it returns the status False.

 

ResetAuditReason    

Purpose:

The ResetAuditReason function resets the audit reason for the specified table.

Syntax:

status = ResetAuditReason(tableName)

tableName – the name of the table for which the reason is to be reset, or blank if the audit reason for all tables is to be reset.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If a table name is specified, LIMS will reset the audit reason for that table back to an empty string.

•If no table name is specified, LIMS will reset the "generic" reason that is applied to all tables.  (Reasons for individual tables will not be reset.)

This function does not save changes to the database.

Example:

The following example resets the audit reason for the sample table.

status = ResetAuditReason("SAMPLE")

Returns:

The function returns the value true.

 

ResetPassword    

Purpose:

The ResetPassword function changes the target account’s password to the specified password.

Syntax:

status = ResetPassword(userName, newPassword, auditReason)

userName – the name of the user record that is to be reset.

newPassword – string value to be used as the new password.

auditReason – reason for change that is posted to the audit trail.

NOTE:  This function will reset a user account’s password to the specified password regardless of the business rule settings.


Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that the current user account has the privilege: ResetPassword.  If the user does not have the privilege, LIMS returns the error: "No privilege for the operation."

•LIMS verifies that a user account has been specified.  If no user account has been specified, LIMS returns the error: "No user name specified."

•LIMS verifies that the user account specified exists in the system.  If the account is not found, the LIMS returns the last error:
"Invalid user specified:" followed by the input value.

•LIMS verifies that a new password has been provided.  If no password is provided, the LIMS returns the last error: "No password specified."

•LIMS resets the password and the password expiration counter.  LIMS makes an audit trail entry ChangePassword with the reason specified.

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following code resets the password for the account: DEMO_A to "Hello" and sets the audit reason to "Documentation".

UserName = " DEMO_A"
newPassword = "Hello"
auditReason = "Documentation"
status = ResetPassword(userName, newPassword, auditReason)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

RollUpRoles  

Purpose:

This function rolls the groups, optional groups and function privileges from all the user’s assigned roles up to the user, i.e., it sets the groups, optional groups and function privileges to the user from a combination of all the user’s assigned roles. In other words, this function evaluates a user’s account and updates the user’s Groups, Optional Groups and Functional Privileges based on all the roles available to that user.

Syntax:

status = RollUpRoles()

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS creates a temporary virtual user account as a copy of the logged in user’s account.

•LIMS adds all functional privileges on each of the user account’s assigned roles up to the virtual user account.

•LIMS adds all Groups on each of the user account’s assigned roles up to the virtual user account.

•LIMS adds all Optional Groups on each of the user account’s assigned roles up to the virtual user account (ie., those that have not already been added as a Group).

NOTE: The Menus and Toolbars are NOT rolled up. In other words, the Menus and Toolbars continue to be the ones as defined on the role the user logged in with and NOT as those defined on any of the user account’s assigned roles that were rolled up.


Example:

In the following example, we consider 3 roles defined in the system as follows:

"AUTHMANAGER", with access to the following functional privileges: AuthorizeBatchResults, AuthorizeProjectResults, AuthorizeResult, AuthorizeSample, AuthorizeTest;

"PROJECTMANAGER", with access to the following functional privileges: ProductSpecificationDesigner, ProjectManager;

"SYSADMIN", with access to all other functional privileges except the ones stated above.

User "JANES" is configured to use roles and has the above 3 roles assigned to it’s account. If the user logs in to LIMS with any one of the above 3 roles, then

status = RollUpRoles()

assigns all functional privileges, assigned groups & optional groups for all the above roles to the user.

Returns:

This function returns True if it is successful, else, it returns the status False.

 

SetAccessFunctionIds  

Purpose:

This function adds all the given access function ids to all the given users, except for those given users who use roles. The given access function ids may either be added to the users, or, maybe set by replacing all the existing ones.

Syntax:

status = SetAccessFunctionIds(userNameArray, functionIdArray, appendFlag)

userNameArray – input array containing user ids of users for whom the access function ids are to be added.

functionIdArray – input array containing the defined access function ids to be added to the array of users.

appendFlag – Boolean value; if set to True, the given access function ids would be added to the existing ones for each user, else, if set to False, they would replace the existing ones.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS verifies if the input user name array is an actual array; if not, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "Parameter 1 is not an array.".

•LIMS verifies if the input function id array is an actual array; if not, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "Parameter 2 is not an array.".

•LIMS verifies if an append flag Boolean has been specified; if not, then LIMS sets the append flag as "True".

•LIMS verifies if the input user name array is empty; if yes, then LIMS stops the execution and returns true.

•LIMS verifies if the input function id array is empty and if the append flag is set to True; if yes, then LIMS stops the execution and returns true.

•LIMS takes the users given in the user name input array one by one and verifies if they are valid users; if any one of them is found to be invalid, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "User <user name> does not exist", where <user name> is the given user name.

•LIMS also verifies if any of the given users uses roles; if true, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "User <user name> uses roles", where <user name> is the given user name.

•If all verifications are satisfactorily through, then LIMS checks the append flag Boolean value. If set to True, then LIMS checks each of the users defined in the user input array one by one and verifies whether each of the access function ids (defined in the input function id array) are already assigned to the user. If any such are not found to be assigned, then LIMS adds the function id to the user and saves the change to the database.

•If the append flag Boolean value is set to False, then for each user defined in the user input array, all existing access function ids are removed and all those defined in the input function id array are added to the user, the changes being saved to the database.

Example:

The following example adds the access functions ids given in the input array "functionArray" ("AuthorizeBatchResults", "AuthorizeProjectResults", "AuthorizeResult", "AuthorizeSample" and "AuthorizeTest") to each of the users "JANES" and "JOHNS" given in the input array "userArray" and saves the changes to the database.

userArray[1] = "JANES"

userArray[2] = "JOHNS"

 

functionArray[1] = "AuthorizeBatchResults"

functionArray[2] = "AuthorizeProjectResults"

functionArray[3] = "AuthorizeResult"

functionArray[4] = "AuthorizeSample"

functionArray[5] = "AuthorizeTest"

 

status = SetAccessFunctionIds(userArray, functionArray, T)

Returns:

This function returns True if it is successful, else, it returns the status False.

 

SetAuditReason    

Purpose:

The SetAuditReason function defines a default audit reason.

Syntax:

status = SetAuditReason(auditReason, tableName)

auditReason – the reason to be assigned

tableName – the name of the table with which the default reason is to be associated

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the audit reason is blank or missing, LIMS will trigger the error "No Audit Reason set".

•If a table name is specified, LIMS will make the specified reason the default reason for that table.  Otherwise, LIMS will make it the default reason for all tables.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.

 

When changes are saved to an object and no reason has already been specified for that object, LIMS will use the default reason for the corresponding table.  If no reason exists, LIMS will use the default reason for all tables.

CAUTION:  You cannot use this function with Database Events.  You must use the SetAuditReasonForObject for Database events.  Refer to the section on Event Triggers and Database events in the LIMS User Manual for more on Database Events.


Example:

The following example sets the audit reason for the sample table to "Correction".

status = SetAuditReason("Correction", "SAMPLE")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

SetAuditReasonForObject    

Purpose:

The SetAuditReasonForObject function defines the audit reason for pending changes to a particular object.

Syntax:

status = SetAuditReasonForObject(auditReason, tableName, keyFieldValue)

auditReason – the audit reason to be assigned

tableName – the name of the table in which the object is located

keyFieldValue – the primary key of the object

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the audit reason is blank or missing, LIMS will trigger the error "No Audit Reason set".

•If the key value is blank or missing, LIMS will trigger the error "No key value specified".

•LIMS associates the audit reason with the object identified by the specified table name and key value.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.

NOTE:  This function cannot be used with the SetAuditReason function in the same code. 


Example:

The following example sets the audit reason for the LOD analysis to "Correction".

status = SetAuditReasonForObject("Correction", "ANALYSIS", "LOD")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

SetGroups  

Purpose:

This function adds all the given group names to all the given users, except for those given users who use roles. The given group names may either be added to the users, or, maybe set by replacing all the existing ones.

Syntax:

status = SetGroups(userNameArray, groupNamesArray, appendFlag)

userNameArray – input array containing user ids of users for whom the group names are to be added.

groupNamesArray – input array containing the defined group names to be added to the array of users.

appendFlag – Boolean value; if set to True, the given group names would be added to the existing ones for each user, else, if set to False, they would replace the existing ones.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS verifies if the input user name array is an actual array; if not, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "Parameter 1 is not an array.".

•LIMS verifies if the input group names array is an actual array; if not, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "Parameter 2 is not an array.".

•LIMS verifies if an append flag Boolean has been specified; if not, then LIMS sets the append flag as "True".

•LIMS verifies if the input user name array is empty; if yes, then LIMS stops the execution and returns true.

•LIMS verifies if the input group names array is empty and if the append flag is set to True; if yes, then LIMS stops the execution and returns true.

•LIMS takes the users given in the user name input array one by one and verifies if they are valid user names; if any one of them is found to be invalid, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "User <user name> does not exist", where <user name> is the given user name.

•LIMS also verifies if any of the given users uses roles; if true, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "User <user name> uses roles", where <user name> is the given user name.

•If all verifications are satisfactorily through, then LIMS checks the append flag Boolean value. If set to True, then LIMS checks each of the users defined in the user input array one by one and verifies whether each of the group names (defined in the input group names array) are already assigned to the user. If any such are not found to be assigned, then LIMS adds the group name to the user and saves the change to the database.

•If the append flag Boolean value is set to False, then for each user defined in the user input array, all existing group names are removed and all those defined in the input group names array are added to the user, the changes being saved to the database.

Example:

The following example adds the defined groups given in the input array "groupsArray" ("AUTH" and "TEST") to each of the users "JANES" and "JOHNS" given in the input array "userArray" and saves the changes to the database.

userArray[1] = "JANES"

userArray[2] = "JOHNS"

 

groupsArray[1] = "AUTH"

groupsArray[2] = "TEST"

 

status = SetGroups(userArray, groupsArray, T)

Returns:

This function returns True if it is successful, else, it returns the status False.

 

SetPrimaryRole

Purpose:

The SetPrimaryRole function sets the primary role for the logged in user that is using combined roles.

Syntax:

status = SetPrimaryRole(roleName)

roleName – a string variable containing the role to set as the primary role.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, the system performs the following:

•The system verifies a user is currently logged into the system; if not, then the system generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "No User logged in".

•The system checks if the current user has combine roles = true; if not, then the system will trigger the error: "User , user, not using combine roles" (where user is the currently logged in user).

•The system checks if the role that is being set as primary is one of the roles available to the logged in user; if not, then the system will trigger the error: "Invalid role for the logged in user: roleName ".

•After the function is called the system will recognize this role system wide as the primary role, such as when opening approval manager to determine which approvals are visible to the logged in user.

Example:

The following example sets the primary role to QAMANAGER for the user that is currently logged into the system.

roleName = "QAMANAGER"

status = SetPrimaryRole(roleName)

Returns:

The function returns true if successful. If it is not successful, it will answer false and lastError will be set with an appropriate error message.

 

SetRDIAuditReason

Purpose:

The SetRDIAuditReason function provides a default audit reason for track changes that are updated using LIMS Basic functions and suppresses the normal prompting behavior.  If the LIMS Basic function used to update the object has an audit reason argument, that audit reason may be used in addition to the reason provided by this function. 

Syntax:

status = SetRDIAuditReason(auditReason, tableNamesArray)

auditReason – (required) the audit reason to be used for the object updates

tableNamesArray- (required) a 1D array of table names corresponding to the tables associated with the objects that are to be updated.  It should include all tables associated with the objects.  For example, updates relating to a result should include the SAMPLE, TEST and RESULT tables in this array.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

1.  LIMS checks the auditReason. If a value is not specified, LIMS will trigger the error “No Audit Reason set”.

2.  LIMS will set the default RDI audit reason for the specified tables.

NOTE:  This function will not work in Automation Scripts or Menu Routines.  The audit prompt will be displayed. 

This function does NOT save changes to the database.

 

CAUTION: When using this function, the audit reason is associated with objects in context.  If SetRDIAuditReason is called again, the audit reason may not be changed for the objects already in context.  It may be necessary to clear context using ClearSelects and/or ClearInterpretersObjects, however, make sure all pending changes have been committed before calling these functions. For a similar reason, this function should not be  used in template formulas because the audit reason may persist.


Example:

The following code will set the RDI audit reason for updating a project result:

auditReason = "Updated using LIMS Basic"

tableNamesArray [1] = "PROJECT_RESULT"

tableNamesArray [2] = "PROJECT "

status = SetRDIAuditReason(auditReason, tableNamesArray)

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

SetRoles  

Purpose:

This function adds all the given roles to all the given users, except for those given users who do not use roles. The given roles may either be added to the users, or, maybe set by replacing all the existing ones.

Syntax:

status = SetRoles(userNameArray, roleNamesArray, appendFlag)

userNameArray – input array containing user ids of users for whom the group names are to be added.

roleNamesArray – input array containing the defined roles to be added to the array of users.

appendFlag – Boolean value; if set to True, the given roles would be added to the existing ones for each user, else, if set to False, they would replace the existing ones.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•LIMS verifies if the input user name array is an actual array; if not, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "Parameter 1 is not an array.".

•LIMS verifies if the input role names array is an actual array; if not, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "Parameter 2 is not an array.".

•LIMS verifies if an append flag Boolean has been specified; if not, then LIMS sets the append flag as "True".

•LIMS verifies if the input user name array is empty; if yes, then LIMS stops the execution and returns true.

•LIMS verifies if the input role names array is empty and if the append flag is set to True; if yes, then LIMS stops the execution and returns true.

•LIMS takes the users given in the user name input array one by one and verifies if they are valid user names; if any one of them is found to be invalid, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "User <user name> does not exist", where <user name> is the given user name.

•LIMS also verifies if any of the given users uses roles; if NOT, then LIMS generates an error and populates the lastError variable with: "User <user name> does not use roles", where <user name> is the given user name.

•If all verifications are satisfactorily through, then LIMS checks the append flag Boolean value. If set to True, then LIMS checks each of the users defined in the user input array one by one and verifies whether each of the roles (defined in the input roles array) are already assigned to the user. If any such are not found to be assigned, then LIMS adds the role to the user and saves the change to the database.

•If the append flag Boolean value is set to False, then for each user defined in the user input array, all existing roles are removed and all those defined in the input roles array are added to the user, the changes being saved to the database.

Example:

The following example adds the defined roles given in the input array "rolesArray" ("AUTHMANAGER" and "PROJECTMANAGER") to each of the users "JANES" and "JOHNS" given in the input array "userArray" and saves the changes to the database.

userArray[1] = "JANES"

userArray[2] = "JOHNS"

rolesArray[1] = "AUTHMANAGER"

rolesArray[2] = "PROJECTMANAGER"

status = SetRoles(userArray, rolesArray, T)

Returns:

This function returns True if it is successful, else, it returns the status False.

 

SetTestAuditReason    

Purpose:

The SetTestAuditReason function defines a default audit reason for the specified test.

Syntax:

status = SetTestAuditReason(sampleNumber, testNumber, auditReason)

sampleNumber – the sample number of the parent sample of the target test.

testNumber – the test number of the target test.

auditReason – string to be set as the default reason on the audit trail.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evaluates the sample number.  If it cannot locate the sample, LIMS returns the last error: "Sample does not exist: " followed by the sample number.

•LIMS evaluates the test number.  If it cannot locate the test, LIMS returns the last error: "Test does not exist: " followed by the test number.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.

 

When changes are saved, then the provided audit reason is written to the audit trail.

CAUTION:  You cannot use this function with Database Events.  You must use the SetAuditReasonForObject for Database events.  Refer to the section on Event Triggers and Database events in the LIMS User Manual for more on Database Events.


Example:

The following example sets the audit reason for the test: 1246.

status = SetTestAuditReason(439, 1246, "Reason set by LIMS Basic")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

UpdateActiveFlagForApprovedRecords    

Purpose:

The UpdateActiveFlagForApprovedRecords function activates all records in a table that have been approved but are not yet active.  This only applies to tables that have the EFFECTIVE_DATE field present.

Syntax:

status = UpdateActiveFlagForApprovedRecords(tableName)

tableName – the name of the table in which records are to be activated

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no table with the specified tableName has been defined in the table master table, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid table name".

•If the table does not have a field named "EFFECTIVE_DATE", LIMS will trigger the error "Table does not include EFFECTIVE_DATE field".

•If the table does not have a field named "APPROVED", LIMS will trigger the error "Table does not include APPROVED field".

•If the table contains a field named "ACTIVE", LIMS will use that field as the active flag.  Otherwise, if the table contains a field named "ACTIVE_FLAG", LIMS will use that field as the active flag instead. If the table contains neither of these two fields, LIMS will trigger the error "Table does not contain an active flag".

•LIMS retrieves all records that have been approved, and that have an effective date prior to the current time, and that are not yet active.  For each record found, LIMS sets its active flag to true and saves the record to the database using audit type "AutoActivateRecord".

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example activates all approved analyses.

status = UpdateActiveFlagForApprovedRecords("ANALYSIS")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

UpdateAnalysisApprovalExpiration    

Purpose:

The UpdateAnalysisApprovalExpiration function deactivates all expired analysis approval records.

Syntax:

status = UpdateAnalysisApprovalExpiration()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves all analysis approval records that are not marked as expired, but whose expiration date is prior to the current date and time and whose approval interval is a value greater than zero.  (The value zero signifies that the approval is not subject to expiration.)

•LIMS marks each retrieved approval record as expired and saves the change in the database using audit type "AutoDeactivateRecord".  If this fails for any reason, LIMS will trigger the error "Commit of user record failed".

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example deactivates analysis approval records.

status = UpdateAnalysisApprovalExpiration()

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

UpdateInstrumentApprovalExpiration    

Purpose:

The UpdateInstrumentApprovalExpiration function deactivates all expired instrument approval records.

Syntax:

status = UpdateInstrumentApprovalExpiration()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves all instrument approval records that are not marked as expired, but whose expiration date is prior to the current date and time and whose approval interval is a value greater than zero.  (The value zero signifies that the approval is not subject to expiration.)

•LIMS marks each retrieved approval record as expired and saves the change in the database using audit type "AutoDeactivateRecord".  If this fails for any reason, LIMS will trigger the error "Commit of user record failed".

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example deactivates instrument approval records.

status = UpdateInstrumentApprovalExpiration()

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

VerifyUser    

Purpose:

The VerifyUser function returns a Boolean indicating whether a LIMS userid  and password are valid.

Syntax:

status = VerifyUser(userName, password)

Comments:

This function verifies the user programmatically.  It does not prompt the user for a password.  Refer to the ConfirmUser function

Example:

status = VerifyUser("MOVER", "MO")   

Returns:

status = true if the user name and password are valid, false otherwise.

 

WindowsUserName    

Purpose:

The WindowsUserName function returns the user name on the active Windows account.

Syntax:

windowsUser = WindowsUserName()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS returns the user name for the active Windows account.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example retrieves the user name and sets the value to the variable windowsUser.

windowsUser = WindowsUserName()

Returns:

The function returns the User Name of the active Windows account.

 

28.3.42  Serial Communication Functions
 

Function

ClearComm

CloseComm

OpenComm

ReadComm

SendComm

WaitComm

 

ClearComm    

Purpose:

The ClearComm function clears the serial port buffer.

Syntax:

status = ClearComm(port)

Comments:

Incoming data from the serial port is stored in a buffer.  The ClearComm function clears the buffer.  This command is typically used after an OpenComm function and prior to a SendComm function and after a ReadComm function.

Example:

status = ClearComm("COM1")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

CloseComm    

Purpose:

The CloseComm function closes the serial port.

Syntax:

status = CloseComm(port)

Comments:

The OpenComm function must be executed prior to a CloseComm.

Example:

status = CloseComm("COM1")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

OpenComm    

Purpose:

The OpenComm function opens a serial port for communication.

Syntax:

status = OpenComm(port, baud, parity, dataBits, stopBits)

Comments:

port and parity are strings.  Valid options for parity are: NO, ODD, or EVEN.

 

MsgBoxes and Trace statements used in conjunction with the SendComm and ReadComm functions may change the timing such that the ReadComm function fails to receive any information.

Example:

‘ Serial port connection to a modem

status = OpenComm("COM1", 9600, "NO", 8, 1)

status = ClearComm("COM1")

‘ add CR LF to terminate string

sendString = "ati3" + Chr(13) + Chr(10)

status = SendComm("COM1", sendString)

status = ClearComm("COM1")

status = WaitComm("COM1", "OK", 5)

If (status) Then

readString = ReadComm("COM1")

Endif

status = ClearComm("COM1")

status = CloseComm("COM1")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

ReadComm    

Purpose:

The ReadComm function reads the serial port buffer.

Syntax:

string = ReadComm(port)

Comments:

string contains all the characters from the serial port buffer since the last ClearComm function.  The WaitComm function is used before this function to determine when the desired information is available in the serial port buffer.

Example:

‘ Wait for OK string from modem

status = WaitComm("COM1", "OK", 30)

IF (status) THEN

string = ReadComm("COM1")

ENDIF

Returns:

string contains the data from the serial port buffer.

 

SendComm    

Purpose:

The SendComm function sends a string to the serial port.

Syntax:

status = SendComm(port, string)

Comments:

The ClearComm function must be executed prior to this function.  Different  instruments require different string termination (carriage return – Chr(13), line feed - Chr(10)).

Example:

‘ Send a message to a modem – who are you

‘ String needs to be terminated with CR and LF

status = ClearComm("COM1")

string = "ati3" + Chr(13) + Chr(10)

status = SendComm("COM1", string)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

WaitComm    

Purpose:

The WaitComm function returns a Boolean indicating whether a specified

Syntax:

status = WaitComm(port, string, timeout, queueMsgsFlag)

string is the string being looked for in the serial port buffer.

timeOut is the number of seconds to check the serial port for the string.

queueMsgsFlag – Boolean; T, true, sets a flag on the port that queues up all the messages so they may be read one at a time.  F, false, retains only the latest message. 

Comments:

The WaitComm function is executed prior to a ReadComm function.  When the ReadComm retrieves a message, it is removed from the queue.

Example: 

‘ Look for an OK from the modem for 30 seconds

status = WaitComm("COM1", "OK", 30)

IF (status) THEN

string = ReadComm("COM1")

ENDIF

Returns:

status = true if the string was found in the buffer, false otherwise.

 

 

28.3.43  Standard and Reagents Functions
 

Function

CreateStandardReagent

DeactivateExpiredStandard

ResampleStandard

UpdateStandardFld

 

CreateStandardReagent    

Purpose:

The CreateStandardReagent function creates a new standard reagent. 

Syntax:

stdName = CreateStandardReagent(templateName, commitFlag)

templateName – Name of the Standard and Reagent template used to create the object.

commitFlag- a Boolean, if T, the data is committed, otherwise the data is committed during the next LIMS commit to the sample (the next commit may happen when a later LIMS Basic function performs a commit or when a Save operation is performed).

Comments:

This function is equivalent to the New Standard button in the Standard/Reagent management interface. The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS checks the templateName.  If not found, LIMS will trigger the error "Template does not exist: templateName".

•LIMS creates a standard using the specified template; if an error occurs LIMS will trigger the error "Standard reagent could not be created".

•If the commitFlag- a Boolean if T, the standard is committed immediately to the database.  Other the commit does not happen until the next commit.

 

NOTE: A database commit is part of this function if commitFlag = T.


Example:

stdName = CreateStandardReagent("NAOH", "T")

Returns: 

stdName = "NAOH-980415-1"

 

DeactivateExpiredStandard    

Purpose:

The DeactivateExpiredStandard function deactivates all standards and reagents that have expired.

Syntax:

status = DeactivateExpiredStandard()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the STANDARD_REAGENT table does not include a field named "ACTIVE" or a field named "ACTIVE_FLAG", LIMS will trigger the error "Table does not contain an active flag".

•LIMS retrieves all records from the STANDARD_REAGENT table where the active flag is true and the expiration date is prior to the current date and time.  If the table includes a field named "APPROVED", the query is further restricted by the condition that the approval group must be blank or the approved flag true.  (In other words, if the Approval Manager is used, and the record is in the process of being approved, the record will not be retrieved for deactivation.)

•LIMS sets the active flag of each retrieved record to false and saves the record in the database using audit type "AutoDeactivateRecord".

NOTE: This function saves changes to the database.


Example:

The following example deactivates expired standards and reagents.

status = DeactivateExpiredStandard()

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

ResampleStandard    

Purpose:

The ReSampleStandard function creates a resample of a standard/reagent. 

Syntax:

status = ResampleStandard(standardName)

Comments:

This function is equivalent to the Resample button on the Standard/Reagent dialog.

NOTE: A database commit is part of this function.


Example:

status = ResampleStandard("NAOH-980408-1")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

UpdateStandardFld    

Purpose:

The UpdateStandardFld function updates a field in a standard reagent.

Syntax:

status = UpdateStandardFld(standardName, field, value)

standardName – string defining the standard to be updated.

field – string defining the field to be updated.

value – value to be posted to the specified field.

Comments:

This function should be used with caution as any field in the STANDARD_REAGENT table may be updated.

NOTE: A database commit is not part of this function.

 

TIP: When a sample is created for a standard, the standard reagent record is put into context so fields for the Standard may be obtained from context instead of querying the database.


Example:

status = UpdateStandardFld("NAOH-980408-1", "VENDOR_SERIAL_NUM", "A-23")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

 

28.3.44  Status Rules Functions
 

Function

CanAccessFunction

CanAuthorizeResult

CanAuthorizeSample

CanAuthorizeTest

CanCancelResult

CanCancelSample

CanCancelTest

CanReactivateSample

CanReceiveSample

CanRejectResult

CanRejectSample

CanRejectTest

CanReleaseSample

CanRestoreResult

CanRestoreSample

CanRestoreTest

CanUnreleaseSample

 

CanAccessFunction    

Purpose:

The CanAccessFunction function returns true if the user is authorized to perform the specified function.

Syntax:

status = CanAccessFunction(functionId)

functionId – the ID of the function the user is attempting to perform

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

If a record exists in the ACCESS_ENTRY table for the current user and for the specified function, LIMS will return the value true.

Otherwise, LIMS will return the value false.

NOTE:  Information from the ACCESS_ENTRY table is cached in memory.  Changes made by the system administrator will take effect the next login.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example returns true if the user is authorized to receive samples.

status = CanAccessFunction("ReceiveSamples")

Returns:

LIMS returns the value true if the user is authorized to perform the specified function.  It returns the value false otherwise.

 

CanAuthorizeResult    

Purpose:

The CanAuthorizeResult function determines if the current user can authorize the specified result.

Syntax:

status = CanAuthorizeResult(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber)

sampleNumber – the sample number of the result to be authorized

testNumber – the test number of the result to be authorized

resultNumber – the result number of the result to be authorized

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the sample whose sample number is equal to the value of sampleNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample does not exist".

•LIMS retrieves the test whose test number is equal to the value of testNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Test does not exist".

•LIMS retrieves the result whose result number is equal to the value of resultNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the result "Result does not exist".

•LIMS determines if the result can be authorized by the current user.  If present, the ALLOW_AUTHORIZE status rule is processed.  If the result cannot be authorized, LIMS will trigger an error using the status rule message as the error message.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example determines if the current user can authorize result 5641, which belongs to test 3382, which in turn belongs to sample 997.

status = CanAuthorizeResult(997,3382,5641)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if the user can authorize the specified result.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered or if the user cannot authorize the result.

 

CanAuthorizeSample    

Purpose:

The CanAuthorizeSample function returns a Boolean indicating whether a sample can be authorized.

Syntax:

status = CanAuthorizeSample(sample)

sample – Sample Number of the target sample.

Comments:

Sample status must be Complete or Rejected to be authorized (In-Progress samples may be authorized if business rules allow it).

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and ALLOW_AUTHORIZE status rule is checked to determine the answer to this function.


Example:

status = CanAuthorizeSample(745)

Returns:

status = true if the sample can be authoized, false otherwise.

 

CanAuthorizeTest    

Purpose:

The CanAuthorizeTest function returns a Boolean indicating whether a test can be authorized.

Syntax:

status = CanAuthorizeTest(sampleNumber, testNumber)

sampleNumber – Sample Number of the target parent sample.

testNumber – Test Number of the target test.

Comments:

Test status must be Complete or Rejected to be authorized (In-Progress tests may be authorized if business rules allow it).  Sample status mustnot be Canceled, Authorized or Rejected.

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and ALLOW_AUTHORIZE status rule is checked to determine the answer to this function


Example:

status = CanAuthorizeTest(745, 888)

Returns:

status = true if the test can be authorized, false otherwise.

 

CanCancelResult    

Purpose:

The CanCancelResult function returns a Boolean indicating whether a result can be canceled.

Syntax:

status = CanCancelResult(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber)

sampleNumber – Sample Number of the target parent sample.

testNumber – Test Number of the target parent test.

resultNumber – Result Number of the target result.

Comments:

Result status must be Not Entered, Entered, or Modified to be canceled. Test status must be incomplete or In-progress.  Sample status must be Unreceived, Incomplete ir In Progress

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and ALLOW_CANCEL status rule is checked to determine the answer to this function.


Example:

status = CanCancelResult(745, 888, 1000)

Returns:

status =  true if the result can be canceled, false otherwise.

 

CanCancelSample    

Purpose:

The CanCancelSample function returns a Boolean indicating whether a sample can be canceled.

Syntax:

status = CanCancelSample(sample)

sample – Sample Number of the target sample.

Comments:

Sample status must be Unreceived, In Progress, Incomplete or Complete to be canceled.

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and ALLOW_CANCEL status rule is checked to determine the answer to this function.


Example:

status = CanCancelSample(748)

Returns:

status =  true if the sample can be canceled, false otherwise.

 

CanCancelTest    

Purpose:

The CanCancelTest function returns a Boolean indicating whether a test can be canceled.

Syntax:

status = CanCancelTest(sampleNumber, testNumber)

sampleNumber – Sample Number of the target parent sample.

testNumber – Test Number of the target test.

Comments:

Test status must be Incomplete, In Progress, or Complete to be canceled.

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and ALLOW_ CANCEL status rule is checked to determine the answer to this function.


Example:

status = CanCancelTest(745, 888)

Returns:

status =  true if the test can be canceled, false otherwise.

 

CanReactivateSample    

Purpose:

The CanReactivateSample function returns a Boolean indicating whether a sample can be reactivated.

Syntax:

status = CanReactivateSample(sample)

sampleNumber – Sample Number of the target sample.

Comments:

Sample status must be Authorized or Rejected to be reactivated.

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and ALLOW_REACTIVATE status rule is checked to determine the answer to this function.


Example:

status = CanReactivateSample(745)

Returns:

status = true if the sample can be reactivated, false otherwise.

 

CanReceiveSample    

Purpose:

The CanReceiveSample function determines if the current user can reject the specified result.

Syntax:

status = CanReceiveSample(sample)

sampleNumber – the sample number of the target sample.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the sample whose sample number is equal to the value of sampleNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample does not exist".

•LIMS determines if the sample can be received by the current user. 

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example determines if the current user can receive sample 375.

status = CanReceiveSample(375)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if the user can reject the specified result.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered or if the user cannot reject the result.

 

CanRejectResult    

Purpose:

The CanRejectResult function determines if the current user can reject the specified result.

Syntax:

status = CanRejectResult(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber)

sampleNumber – the sample number of the result to be rejected

testNumber – the test number of the result to be rejected

resultNumber – the result number of the result to be rejected

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the sample whose sample number is equal to the value of sampleNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample does not exist".

•LIMS retrieves the test whose test number is equal to the value of testNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Test does not exist".

•LIMS retrieves the result whose result number is equal to the value of resultNumber.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the result "Result does not exist".

•LIMS determines if the result can be rejected by the current user.  If present, the ALLOW_REJECT status rule is processed.  If the result cannot be rejected, LIMS will trigger an error using the status rule message as the error message.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example determines if the current user can reject result 5641, which belongs to test 3382, which in turn belongs to sample 997.

status = CanRejectResult(997,3382,5641)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if the user can reject the specified result.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered or if the user cannot reject the result.

 

CanRejectSample    

Purpose:

The CanRejectSample function returns a Boolean indicating whether a sample can be rejected.

Syntax:

status = CanRejectSample(sample)

sample – Sample Number of the target sample.

Comments:

Sample status must be In Progress, Complete or Authorized to be rejected.

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and ALLOW_REJECT status rule is checked to determine the answer to this function.


Example:

status = CanRejectSample(745)

Returns:

status = true if the sample can be rejected, false otherwise.

 

CanRejectTest    

Purpose:

The CanRejectTest function returns a Boolean indicating whether a test can be rejected.

Syntax:

status = CanRejectTest(sampleNumber, testNumber)

sampleNumber – Sample Number of the target parent sample.

testNumber – Test Number of the target test.

Comments:

Test status must be Complete or Authorized to be rejected.

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and ALLOW_REJECT status rule is checked to determine the answer to this function.


Example:

status = CanRejectTest(745, 888)

Returns:

status = true if the test can be rejected, false otherwise.

 

CanReleaseSample    

Purpose:

The CanReleaseSample function returns a Boolean indicating whether a sample can be Released.

Syntax:

status = CanReleaseSample(sample)

sample – Sample Number of the target sample.

Comments:

Sample status must be Authorized or Rejected to be Released. If Business Rules allow un-reviewed samples to be Released, then samples in status In Progress and Complete can be Released as well.

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and ALLOW_RELEASE status rule is checked to determine the answer to this function.


Example:

status = CanReleaseSample(745)

Returns:

status = true if the sample can be Released, false otherwise.

 

CanRestoreResult    

Purpose:

The CanRestoreResult function returns a Boolean indicating whether a result can be restored.

Syntax:

status = CanRestoreResult(sampleNumber, testNumber, resultNumber)

sampleNumber – the sample number of the result to be rejected

testNumber – the test number of the result to be rejected

resultNumber – the result number of the result to be rejected

Comments:

Result status must be Canceled to be restored.

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and ALLOW_RESTORE status rule is checked to determine the answer to this function.


Example:

status = CanRestoreResult(745, 888, 1000)

Returns:

status = true if the result can be restored, false otherwise.

 

CanRestoreSample    

Purpose:

The CanRestoreSample function returns a Boolean indicating whether a sample can be restored.

Syntax:

status = CanRestoreSample(sample)

sample – Sample Number of the target sample.

Comments:

Sample status must be Canceled to be restored.

Example:

status = CanRestoreSample(745)

Returns:

status = true if the sample can be restored, false otherwise.

 

CanRestoreTest    

Purpose:

The CanRestoreTest function returns a Boolean indicating whether a test can be restored.

Syntax:

status = CanRestoreTest(sampleNumber, testNumber)

sampleNumber – Sample Number of the target parent sample.

testNumber – Test Number of the target test.

Comments:

Test status must be Canceled to be restored.  Sample status mustnot be Authorized or Rejected.

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and ALLOW_RESTORE status rule is checked to determine the answer to this function.


Example:

status = CanRestoreTest(745, 888)

Returns:

status = true if the test can be restored, false otherwise.

 

CanUnreleaseSample    

Purpose:

The CanUnreleaseSample function returns a Boolean indicating whether a sample can be Unreleased.

Syntax:

status = CanUnreleaseSample(sample)

sample – Sample Number of the target sample.

Comments:

Sample must be Released.

NOTE:  The business rules, function privileges and ALLOW_UNRELEASE status rule is checked to determine the answer to this function.


Example:

status = CanUnreleaseSample(745)

Returns:

status = true if the sample can be Unreleased, false otherwise.
