AddRowToUserGrid  

Purpose:

The AddRowToUserGrid function adds a row to the grid with the specified field values. This function does not open an existing record but can be used to assist in adding new records.

Syntax:

status = AddRowToUserGrid(fields, fieldValues)

fields – 1-D array of fields to be populated with values in grid;

fieldValues – array of values to populate cells in grid (If a 1-D array is used then only 1 row is added, if a 2-D row is used then more rows can be added).

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS checks for a user grid window.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "There is no active window".

•LIMS checks that the User Grid is currently selected. If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "The active window is not User Grid".

•LIMS checks the size of the fieldValues array. If the size is 0, LIMS will trigger the error "No data".

•LIMS adds a new row with the specified field values

This function does NOT save changes to the database.

Example:

 

 

The following example adds 2 rows with entries in the NAME field.  

 

fields[1] = "NAME"

fieldValues[1,1] = "TEST1023E"

fieldValues[2,1] = "TEST1023F"

status = AddRowToUserGrid(fields, fieldValues)

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

CloseCrystalReportWindow    

Purpose:

The CloseCrystalReportWindow function closes the specified report window.

Syntax:

status = CloseCrystalReportWindow(name)

name – the name that was assigned to the window when it was opened.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no report window exists with the specified name, LIMS will trigger the error "Report Window not found".

•LIMS closes the window.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example closes the report window name "RPT1".

status = CloseCrystalReportWindow("RPT1")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

CloseWindow         

Purpose:

The CloseWindow function closes an open window using the title.

Syntax:

status = CloseWindow(windowTitle)

windowTitle – the title of the window which is to be closed.

Comments:

Can be used for Windows outside of LIMS.  This function may not work with the web client.

Example:

The following example closes a Project Manager window that is open to project: PRJ_12345.

windowTitle = "PRJ_TMP3 : PRJ_12345"

status = CloseWindow(windowTitle)

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

CreateDialog  

Syntax:

status = CreateDialog(formName, title, fieldsArray, "valuesArrayName", validationSub, width, height)

formName – the internal name to be used for the form.  In particular, the name by which the formula subroutine and the validation subroutine will reference the data structure.

title – the text to be displayed in the title bar of the dialog.

fieldsArray – a two-dimensional array in which the first dimension identifies the field and the second dimension identifies an attribute of the field, as follows:

[#,1]fieldName – the name of the field.  In particular, the name by which the formula subroutine and the validation subroutine will reference the field. These should be Uppercase.

[#,2]fieldLabel – the prompt text to be displayed to the left of the field.

[#,3]dataType – the data type of the entry field (one of the following: Text, Number, Integer, Boolean, List, File, Date, Time, Title, DateTime, Password, Button, MultiList)

[#,4]defaultValue – the default value to be displayed when the dialog opens

NOTE:  A default value cannot be defined for a field dependant on another field.  In this case the default value must be set using a formulaSub which is triggered when data is entered in the other field.

 

[#,5]linkTable – the table that contains the entries from which the user may select

[#,6]maxSize – the maximum number of characters that the user may enter

[#,7]listName – the name of the list that contains the entries from which the user may select

[#,8]entryMode (one of the following: UserEntry, MandatoryEntry, DisplayOnly, TitleEntry)

[#,9]dependsOn – the fieldName of the field on which this field depends

[#,10]formulaSub – the name of the subroutine to be used for calculating the value of the field

[#,11]allowUserEntry – a Boolean that only applies if the dataType is a list.  If T then user entry is allowed in the field. If F users are restricted to selecting values from the specified list.

valuesArrayName – the name of the array in which the values entered by the user should be stored.  The argument valuesArrayName is a string value that contains the name of the array.  It is not the array itself.

validationSub – the name of a subroutine to be executed when the user presses the OK button in the dialog.

width – the width of the dialog

height – the height of the dialog

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS converts the specified form name to uppercase and replaces spaces with underscore characters.

•LIMS creates the dialog and applies the title, width, height, and field attributes.  LIMS opens the dialog and waits for the user to make entries.

If the user presses the Cancel button, the dialog will close immediately.  LIMS will set the temporary variable DialogCanceled equal to true, and it will trigger the error "User canceled the dialog".

The following steps apply when the user presses the OK button.

•If any mandatory fields are blank, LIMS will display an error message and keep the dialog open.

•If a validation subroutine is specified, LIMS will execute it.  If the subroutine returns false, LIMS will keep the dialog open.

•LIMS closes the dialog, sets the temporary variable DialogCanceled equal to false, and stores the values entered by the user in an array whose name is equal to the value of the valuesArrayName argument.

NOTE:  This function does not save changes to the database.

NOTE:  The values entered by the user will be placed in context prior to execution of the validation subroutine.  These values will be available to the validation subroutine in the form of a structure that can be accessed in the same way a database record is accessed in context.  Each field can be accessed via the specified field name, and the entire structure can be accessed using the converted form name as the equivalent of a table name.


Example:

The following example prompts the user for a first name and a last name, and then displays the concatenated result in a message box.

myFields[1,1] = "FIRST"
myFields[1,2] = "First Name"
myFields[1,3] = "Text"
myFields[1,6] = 20
myFields[1,8] = "MandatoryEntry"
myFields[2,1] = "LAST"
myFields[2,2] = "Last Name"
myFields[2,3] = "Text"
myFields[2,6] = 20
myFields[2,8] = "MandatoryEntry"
status = CreateDialog("YOUR_NAME", "What is your name?", myFields, "myValues", "", 400, 400)
if (status) then
    MsgBox(myValues[1] + " " + myValues[2])
end if

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

The system variable: DialogCanceled is set to true if the dialog is closed using the Cancel button; otherwise true.

 

CreateGridDialog  

Purpose:

This function creates and opens a grid dialog.  The generated values array contains the user entries.

Syntax:

status = CreateGridDialog(formName, table, title, fieldsArray, recordIds, recordsArray, "valuesArrayName", validationSub, width, height, freezeColumns, menusArray, colWidth, rowHeight, freezeRows)

formName – the internal name to be used for the form.  In particular, the name by which the formula subroutine and the validation subroutine will reference the data structure

table- This is a name used for referencing the records in the grid.  It can be the name of a table in the LIMS, if the records are associated with a LIMS table or it can be a pseudonym used for referencing records in the grid (allowing use of SELECT statements in formulas in the grid).  If not specified the formname is used (optional)

title – string displayed in the title bar of the created user grid.

fieldsArray – multi-dimensional array defining the characteristics of the grid dialog fields.

fieldName – the name of the field.  In particular, the name by which the formula subroutine and the validation subroutine will reference the field. These should be Uppercase.

fieldLabel – the prompt text to be displayed to the left of the field.

dataType – the data type of the entry field (one of the following: Text, Number, Integer, Boolean, List, File, Date, Time, Title, DateTime)

defaultValue – the default value to be displayed when the dialog opens

NOTE:  A default value cannot be defined for a field dependant on another field.  In this case the default value must be set using a formulaSub which is triggered when data is entered in the other field.

linkTable – the table that contains the entries from which the user may select

maxSize – the maximum number of characters that the user may enter

listName – the name of the list that contains the entries from which the user may select

NOTE:  A listName and a formulaSub should not both be specified to set list values..

entryMode (one of the following: UserEntry, MandatoryEntry, DisplayOnly, TitleEntry)

NOTE:  If a cell is designated as DisplayOnly the cell is locked.  Consequently function like SetCellColor will not work with these cells.

dependsOn – the fieldName of the field on which this field depends

formulaSub – the name of the subroutine to be used for calculating the value of the field

recordIds – one dimensional array of key values to open the grid on specific records.

recordsArray – two dimensional array containing the record field values to be displayed in the cells.

valuesArrayName –  string defining the name of the return array containing the values from the user grid.

validationSub – string defining the subroutine triggered when the dialog is closed.

width – integer defining the width of the dialog.

height – integer defining the height of the dialog.

freezeColumns – integer defining the number of columns to the left of the grid scroll freeze.  (Optional)

menusArray – multi-dimensional array defining the characteristics of the displayed menus.  (Optional)

Menu Label – the name of the main menu displayed on the menubar.

Menu Item – the name of the dropdown menu item.

Subroutine – string defining ths subroutine to be triggered when selected.

Bitmap – path and file name of the bitmap to be displayed on the toolbar.

colWidth – integer defining the width of the columns. (Optional)

rowHeight– integer defining the height of the row. (Optional)

freezeRows– integer defining the number of rows from the top of the grid scroll to freeze.  (Optional)

Comments:

 

Example:

fieldArray[1,1] = "GENDER"

fieldArray[1,2] = "Sex"

fieldArray[1,3] = "Text"

fieldArray[1,4] = "MALE"

fieldArray[1,5] = ""

fieldArray[1,6] = 20

fieldArray[1,7] = "GENDER_LIST"

fieldArray[1,8] = "UserEntry"

fieldArray[1,9] = "NAME"

fieldArray[1,10] = ""

 

menuArray[1,1] = "Appointments"

menuArray[1,2] = "Postpone"

menuArray[1,3] = "ADD_WEEK"

menuArray[1,4] =

 

recordIds[1] = 47

recordIds[2] = 59

 

status = CreateGridDialog("DateCheck", "SCHEDULE_ENTRY", "Appointments", fieldArray, recordIds, , "updateArray", "EMAIL_SCHED", 200,
400, , menuArray,,,)

Returns:

status = true if the function is successful, otherwise false.

 

 

CurrentActiveLIMSWindow    

Purpose:

The CurrentActiveLIMSWindow function return the name of the active window.

Syntax:

windowName = CurrentActiveLIMSWindow()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS returns the internal name of the window that currently has focus.

Example:

The following example returns the name of the active window.

windowName = CurrentActiveLIMSWindow()

Returns:

The function returns the internal name of the active window.

 

GetAllApplicationWindowNames    

Purpose:

The GetAllApplicationWindowNames function returns an array listing all window names in LIMS.

Syntax:

status = GetAllApplicationWindowNames("winArrayName")

winArrayName – name of a 1-D array that will contain the output from the function

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS checks the winArrayName.  If this is null, LIMS will trigger the error "Error: an array name must be specified". 

•If the winArrayName is provided, LIMS will output a list of all LIMS windows that support automation scripts..

 Example:

ArrayOut = "windowsNamesArray"

status = GetAllApplicationWindowNames(ArrayOut)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenAddressBook    

Purpose:

The OpenAddressBook function opens the Address Book dialog.  This is a Modal dialog so it has OK and Cancel buttons and there is no File |>Exit option.

Syntax:

status = OpenAddressBook(personNumber)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•LIMS evaluates the parameter: personNumber.

•If empty, LIMS opens the Address Book dialog without opening an entry.

•If not empty, LIMS evaluates the entry.  If it is not a valid person_number, LIMS returns the last error: Invallid person specified:" followed by the input.

•If the input parameter is value, LIMS opens the Address Book to the specified record.

Example:

The following example opens the Address Book dialog to Joe Smith, PERSON.PERSON_NUMBER = 147.

status = OpenAddressBook(147)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if successful, false if an error is triggered.

Purpose:

The OpenAlertManager function opens the Alert Manager dialog.

Syntax:

status = OpenAlertManager()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS opens the Alert Manager dialog.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens the Alert Manager dialog.

status = OpenAlertManager()

Returns:

The function returns the value true.

 

OpenAnalysisWizard    

Purpose:

The OpenAnalysisWizard function prompts the user to select an analyais using the Analysis Wizard and returns the name of the analysis that was selected by the user.

Syntax:

analysisName = OpenAnalysisWizard()

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS opens the Analysis Wizard dialog and waits for the user to make a selection.

•If the user presses the Cancel button, LIMS will return an empty string.

•If the user presses the OK button, LIMS will return the name of the selected analysis.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example displays an analysis name in a message box.

analysisName = OpenAnalysisWizard()
Msgbox(analysisName)

Returns:

The function returns a text value.

 

OpenBatchManager    

Purpose:

The OpenBatchManager function opens the Batch Manager window and displays either the specified batch or the specified template (for creating a new batch).

Syntax:

status = OpenBatchManager(templateName, batchName)

templateName – the name of the template to be opened

batchName – the name of the batch to be opened

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If both the template name and the batch name are blank or missing, LIMS will trigger the error "A template name or batch name must be specified".

•If a batch name is specified, LIMS will retrieve it.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid batch name was specified".  If the batch is found, LIMS will open the Batch Manager window and display the batch.

•If no project name is specified, LIMS will retrieve the specified template.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid batch template name specified".  If the template is found, LIMS will open the Batch Manager window and display the template.

NOTE:  If both a batch name and template are specified, the function will ignore the template and open the batch.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens the batch named "BATCH1".

status = OpenBatchManager("", "BATCH1")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenBatchSampleLogin    

Purpose:

The OpenBatchSampleLogin function opens the Batch Sample Login window using the specified batch template.

Syntax:

status = OpenBatchSampleLogin(templateName)

templateName – the name of the batch template to be used

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no template name is specified, LIMS will trigger the error "A template name must be specified."

•If LIMS is unable to locate a template in the database that has the specified template name, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid template name specified."

•LIMS opens the Batch Sample Login window with the specified template.

NOTE:  The function itself does not save changes to the database, but the user may cause changes to be written to the database in the opened window.


Example:

The following example opens the Batch Sample Login window using the template named "BULK".

status = OpenBatchSampleLogin("BULK")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenCollectData    

Purpose:

The OpenCollectData function opens the Collect Data window for RS232 result entry either by test or by sample.

Syntax:

status = OpenCollectData(tests, mode, folder, modalFlag, results)

tests – an array that contains the test numbers of the tests for which results are to be entered. This array should not contain any null values.  The order of the tests in the array does not determine the order that the tests are displayed in the Collect Data dialog.  This array is ignored if a results array is specified.

mode – the mode in which the Collect Data window is to be opened.  Specify "Test" to enter results by test.  Specify "Sample" to enter results by sample.

folder – the name of the folder that contains the tests for which results are to be entered.  This value is ignored if the tests array or results array contains at least one value.

modalFlag – a Boolean value that indicates whether the Collect Data window should be modal, i.e., if LIMS should prevent the user from switching to another LIMS window while the Collect Data window is open.  The default value is false.

results – an array that contains the result numbers for results which are to be entered. This array should not contain any null values.  The order of the results in the array does not determine the order that the results are displayed in the dialog. 

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If an invalid mode is specified, the system will trigger the error "Invalid mode".

•If the tests and results parameters are not specified, and a folder name is specified, the system will perform the following steps.

•The system attempts to retrieve the folder with the specified name.  If this fails, the system will trigger the error "Invalid folder name".

•If the user requested the "Test" mode, the system will open the Collect Data window, displaying all eligible tests in the folder.

•Otherwise, the system will open the Collect Data by Sample window, displaying all eligible tests in the folder.

•If the results array is not empty, the system retrieves all test records associated with the specified result numbers. 

•If the results array is empty and the tests array is not empty, the system retrieves all sample and test records for the specified test numbers. 

•If no tests are found, the system will trigger the error "No tests for result entry".  (Note: this error is triggered if the tests array is empty or if none of the test numbers are valid.  If some, but not all, of the test numbers exist in the database, no error will be triggered, and the invalid test numbers will be ignored.)

•If the user requested the "Test" mode, the system will open the Collect Data window and display all of the retrieved tests. Otherwise the system will open the Collect Data by Sample window and display all of the retrieved tests. If the results array is used only the specified results are displayed.

NOTE:  If the value of modalFlag is true, the LIMS Basic script that calls the OpenCollectData function will be suspended until the user closes the Collect Data window.  Otherwise, execution of the LIMS Basic script will resume as soon as the Collect Data window has opened.

NOTE:  This function does not save changes to the database while opening the Collect Data window.  However, the Collect Data window itself does save changes to the database when results are entered and saved.


Example:

The following example opens the Collect Data window on tests 14 and 15.

tests[1] = 14

tests[2] = 15

status = OpenCollectData(tests, "Test",,)

The following example opens the Collect Data by sample window on the tests in the folder named "MYFOLDER" and keeps the window modal.

status = OpenCollectData("", "Sample", "MYFOLDER", "T")

The following example opens the Collect Data window on the results in the resultsArray.

resultsArray[1] = 7456

resultsArray[2] = 7458

status = OpenCollectData(, "Test", , "T", resultsArray) 

Returns:

The function returns true if successful. If it is not successful, it will answer false and lastError will be set with an appropriate error message.

 

OpenContactManager    

Purpose:

The OpenContactManager function opens Contact Manager on specified contact.

Syntax:

OpenContactManager(customerName, whereClause, newInstanceFlag, contactNumber)

customerName – The name of the customer to be opened in Contact Manager. This argument is optional.

whereClause – text specifying a where clause such as Customer or Template.  This argument is optional.

newInstanceFlag – A Boolean which specifies if Contact Manager should open as a new instance of Contact Manager or use an existing one if already open .  This argument is optional.

contactNumber – the contact number to be opened in Contact Manager.  This argument is optional.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS checks the newInstanceFlag argument.  If not specified, LIMS will default it to true.

•LIMS checks the contact related the contactNumber argument.  If the contact is not found, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified contact is not valid: contactNumber".

•LIMS opens Contact Manager and opens existing contact if the newInstanceFlag argument is False. 

This function does NOT save changes to the database.

Example:

contactNumber = 80

newInstanceFlag = "F"

customerName = "PHARMA_Y"

'WhereClause = "TEMPLATE = 'DEFAULT'"

status = OpenContactManager(customerName, whereClause, newInstanceFlag, contactNumber)

Returns:

The function returns status true and Contact Manager opens if it completes successfully.  It returns status false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenCrystalReportWindow    

Purpose:

The OpenCrystalReportWindow function opens a report in a report window.

Syntax:

status = OpenCrystalReportWindow(name, title, args, argValues, reportName, languagePrefix)

name – a text value that identifies the report window.  This value is not displayed.

title – the title to be displayed in the title bar of the report window.

The args and argValues arrays are used to specify the filter criteria for record selection for the report.

args – one dimensional array used to specify the table_name.field_name for the filter criteria.

argValues – one dimensional array used to specify the filter values.

reportName – the file name of the Crystal Report template (.rpt) to be used to generate the report.

languagePrefix – the language prefix for the NLS translation to be displayed for Text Objects in the Crystal Report (optional).

locale – the Preferred Viewing Local to be passed from LabWare to Crystal Reports.  The Crystal Reports functions “ContentLocale” and “SelectionLocale” will be set to the specified value.

Comments:

The function performs the following:

1.  If the reportName is invalid, the lastError variable is set to “File does not exist:  <reportName>”.

2.  If the value of the reportName does not contain a directory (i.e., does not contain the “\” character, LabWare will add the path of the report files directory to the report name.

3.  If a Crystal Report Window was already opened with the same name (using a previous call to OpenCrystalReportWindow), LabWare will open the report in that window.  Otherwise, LabWare will open the report   in a new window.

4.  LabWare passes the arguments to Crystal and requests Crystal to generate the report.

5.  If the language prefix is not specified for the function, the language prefix for the logged in User will be applied.

NOTE:  To open another report in the same window, use the same value of the name argument in subsequent calls to OpenCrystalReportWindow.  In order to close he window programmatically, use the same value of the name argument in a call to CloseCrystalReportWindow.


Example:

The following example opens an analysis report for the analysis named “LOD”.  The report is run from the reports files directory.  The Crystal Reports functions “ContentLocale” and “SelectionLocale” will be set to “de_DE” respectively.

name = “WINDOW_A”

title = “Analysis”

args[1] = “ANALYSIS.NAME”

argValues[1] = “LOD”

reportName = “analysis.rpt”

languagePrefix = “DE_”

locale = “ceLocaleGerman”

status = OpenCrystalReportWindow(name, title, args, argValues, reportName, languagePrefix, locale)

Returns:

The status variable is set to true if the function completes successfully, else status is set to false.

 

OpenDataExplorer    

Purpose:

The OpenDataExplorer function opens the Data Explorer on the specified explorer name.

Syntax:

status = OpenDataExplorer(explorerName)

explorerName – the name of the explorer record to be opened

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no explorer name is specified, LIMS will trigger the error "No explorer name specified".

•If LIMS is unable to retrieve the specified explorer record, it will trigger the error "Invalid explorer name".

•LIMS opens the Data Explorer window using the specified explorer.

•The Data Explorer window is not a modal window.  Execution of the LIMS Basic script resumes as soon as the window is opened.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens the Data Explorer window using the explorer named "EXP1".

status = OpenDataExplorer("EXP1")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenFlagsDialog    

Purpose:

This function opens the Flag Dialog on the specifed object.

Syntax:

Status = OpenFlagsDialog(objectType, objectId)

objectType – string defining the table of the target object.

objectId –  string defining the key value of the object.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•If the objectType is not specified, LIMS returns the last error:  No object type specified.

•If the objectId is not specified, LIMS returns the last error: No object id specified.

•If LIMS cannot retrieve the specified object, LIMS returns the last error: The specified object does not exist.

•LIMS opens the Flags Dialog with the specified object displayed.

Example:

Status = OpenFlagsDialog("SAMPLE", 476)

Returns:

status = true if the function is successful, otherwise false.

 

OpenFolder    

Purpose:

The OpenFolder function opens the specified folder in Folder Manager.

Syntax:

status = OpenFolder(folderName)

folderName – the name of the folder to be opened

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no folder name is specified, LIMS will open the Folder Manager window without opening a folder.

•If the specified folder does not exist in the database, LIMS will trigger the error "Specified folder does not exist".

•LIMS opens the Folder Manager window and displays the specified folder.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens the folder named "FOLDER1".

status = OpenFolder("FOLDER1")

Returns:

OpenFolderGroup    

Purpose:

The OpenFolderGroup(folderGroupName) function allows to open Folder Manager for a folder group.

Syntax:

Status= OpenFolderGroup(folderGroupName)

folderGroupName – the number of folder group existing in the folder group record table.

Comments:

When this function is called within LIMS Basic, the LIMS performs the following actions:

•The LIMS evaluates the folder name.  The LIMS selects the folder group names from an array.  If the array size value returned is equal to zero then Folder Manager is opened.

•The LIMS then evaluates the folder group name from the folder group record table.  If the folderGroupName does not exist then LIMS gives a LastError: The specified folder does not exist.

•The LIMS evaluates the folderGroupName against the records in folder group record table and if it exists then the Folder Manger Menu is opened.  The submenu OpenFolderGroup is then opened and submenu foldergroup for that folderGroupName is displayed in the Window.

NOTE: A database commit is not part of this function. 

Example:

The following example opens the Folder Manager for a folder group name  .

folderGroupName = "DEMO_FOLDER"

OpenFolderGroup(folderGroupName)

Returns:

 

status = true if the function is successful, false otherwise.

 

OpenGridProductEditor    

Purpose:

The OpenGridProductEditor function opens the Grid Product Editor on one or more products, and optionally marks any of the key fields as selected when the window is first displayed.

Syntax:

status = OpenGridProductEditor(products, selectedProduct, selectedGrade, selected SamplingPoint, selectedStage, selectedSpecType, selectedComponent)

products – an array that contains the product names to be displayed.

selectedProduct – the product name to be selected.

selectedGrade – the grade to be selected.

selectedSamplingPoint – the sampling point to be selected.

selectedStage – the stage to be selected.

selectedSpecType – the spec type to be selected.

selectedComponent – the component name to be selected.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the array of product names is empty, LIMS will trigger the error "No product(s) selected."

•LIMS retrieves the requested products from the database.  If at least one of the specified product names cannot be found, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid product specified in the list."

•LIMS opens the Grid Product Editor window and shows initial default selections as follows.

•If a selectedProduct is specified, and if its value appears in the list of product names, LIMS will mark it as selected.

•If a selectedGrade is specified, and if its value appears in the list of grade names, LIMS will mark it as selected.

•If a selectedSamplingPoint is specified, and if its value appears in the list of sampling point names, LIMS will mark it as selected.

•If a selectedStage is specified, and if its value appears in the list of stage names, LIMS will mark it as selected.

•If a selectedSpecType is specified, and if its value appears in the list of spec types, LIMS will mark it as selected.

•If a selectedComponent is specified, and if its value appears in the list of component names, LIMS will mark it as selected.

•LIMS returns true as soon as the window has opened.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database. 


Example:

The following example opens the Grid Product Editor on two products and selects the grade "MEDIUM" of the first product as the initially selected grade.

myprods[1] = "PROD1"
myprods[2] = "PROD2"
status = OpenGridProductEditor(myprods, "PROD1", "MEDIUM", "", "", "", "")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenGridResultEntry    

Purpose:

The OpenGridResultEntry function opens the Grid Result Entry window for either an array of test numbers or a folder.  LIMS will display the results either by test or by sample, depending on which mode is specified.

Syntax:

status = OpenGridResultEntry(tests, mode, folder, modalFlag) 'mode = Test or Sample

tests – an array that contains the test numbers of the tests to be displayed.  This array should not contain any null values.  The order of the tests in the array does not determine the order that the tests are displayed in the Grid Result Entry dialog.

mode – the value "Test" if results should be shown by test or the value "Sample" if the results should be shown by sample

folder – the name of the folder whose results should be shown in the Grid Result Entry window..

modalFlag – an optional boolean value.  If the value "T" (true) is specified, the window will open as a modal dialog, i.e., the user will be prevented from switching to another LIMS window and the LIMS Basic script will be suspended until the result entry window is closed.  If not specified, the value "F" (false) will be used.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS takes the first letter of the specified mode and converts it to uppercase.  If this letter is not equal to "T" or "S", LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid mode".

•If no test numbers and no folder name are specified, LIMS will trigger the error "No tests for result entry".

•If at least one test number is specified, LIMS will perform the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves all requested tests and their corresponding samples from the database.  If none are found, LIMS will trigger the error "No tests for result entry".  (Note: as long as the tests array contains at least one valid test number, no error will be triggered if the array also includes invalid test numbers.)

•If the "Test" mode was specified, LIMS will open the Grid Result Entry window and display the retrieved results by test.

•If the "Sample" mode was specified, LIMS will open the Grid Result Entry window and display the retrieved results by sample.

•If a folder name (and no test number) was specified, LIMS will perform the following steps:

•LIMS retrieves the specified folder from the database.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid folder name".

•If the "Test" mode was specified, LIMS will open the Grid Result Entry window and display the folder’s results by test.

•If the "Sample" mode was specified, LIMS will open the Grid Result Entry window and display the folder’s results by sample.

•If the value of modalFlag is false, LIMS will return true as soon as the window has opened.

•Otherwise, i.e., if the value of modalFlag is true, LIMS will suspend execution of the LIMS Basic script until the user closes the Grid Result Entry window.  At that time, LIMS will return the value true.  The user will not be able to switch to other LIMS windows while the Grid Result Entry window is open.

NOTE:  This function does not save changes to the database. 


Example:

The following example opens the Grid Result Entry window by sample for test numbers 12 and 14.

mytests[1] = 12

mytests[2] = 14

status = OpenGridResultEntry(mytests, "Sample")

The following example opens the Grid Result Entry window by test for the folder named "FOLDER12".

status = OpenGridResultEntry("", "Test", "FOLDER12")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenGridReview    

Purpose:

The OpenGridReview function opens the Grid Review window on the specified collection of tests or on the tests in a specified folder.  LIMS will display the records either by test or by sample, depending on which mode is specified.

Syntax:

status = OpenGridReview(tests, mode, folder)

tests – an array that contains the test numbers to be reviewed

mode – the type of grid to be displayed.  Valid values include "TEST" and "SAMPLE".

folder – the name of the folder whose records should be shown in the Grid Review window..

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS takes the first letter of the specified mode and converts it to uppercase.  If this letter is not equal to "T" or "S", LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid mode".

•If no test numbers and no folder name are specified, LIMS will trigger the error "No tests to review".

•If at least one test number is specified, LIMS will perform the following steps.

•If none of the test numbers in the array refer to tests that exist in the database, LIMS will trigger the error "No tests to review" .  (Note: as long as the tests array contains at least one valid test number, no error will be triggered if the array also includes invalid test numbers.)

•If the value of mode begins with the letter "T", LIMS will open the Grid Review window by test and display the tests.

•Otherwise, LIMS will open the Grid Review window by sample and display the tests.

•If a folder name (and no test number) was specified, LIMS will perform the following steps:

•LIMS retrieves the specified folder from the database.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid folder name".

•If the "Test" mode was specified, LIMS will open the Grid Review window and display the folder’s results by test.

•If the "Sample" mode was specified, LIMS will open the Grid Review window and display the folder’s results by sample.

•LIMS returns true as soon as the window has opened.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens the Grid Review window by sample on the tests with test number 718 and 1403.

mytestlist[1] = 718
mytestlist[2] = 1403
status = OpenGridReview(mytestlist, "Sample")

The following example opens the Grid Review window by test for the folder named "FOLDER12".

status = OpenGridReview("", "Test", "FOLDER12")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenGridSampleLogin    

Purpose:

The OpenGridSampleLogin function opens the Grid Sample Login window using the specified template.

Syntax:

status = OpenGridSampleLogin(templateName, numSamples, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

templateName – the name of the sample login template to be opened.

numSamples – the number or sample records to be created within the Grid Sample Login dialog.

fieldsArray – a one dimensional array that contains the name of the sample fields to be populated when opening the Grid Sample Login dialog.

valuesArray – a one or two dimensional array that contains the values for the sample fields to be inserted when opening the Grid Sample Login dialog.  Two dimensional arrays are used to specify different values for the same field on different records.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the specified template name is missing or does not exist in the database, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample template does not exist".

•If the numSamples is greater than 0, the LIMS verifies that the supplied field array have only one dimension.  If the array has more than one dimension, the LastError is set to the following: .Field array has more than two dimensions’.

•If the numSamples is greater than 0, the LIMS verifies that the supplied value array have only one dimension.  If the array has more than one dimension, the LastError is set to the following: .Value array has more than two dimensions’.

•LIMS opens the Grid Sample Login window on the specified template.

•If the numSamples is greater than 0, the LIMS adds the specified number of records into the Grid Sample Login dialog.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens the Grid Sample Login window on the template named "TEMPL1" with three records displayed.  The description fields are set to: ‘function demo’ and the group_name is set to ‘SYSTEM’.

Flds[1] = "DESCRIPTION"
Flds[2] = "CUSTOMER"
Vls[1] = "function Demo"
Vls[2] = "CUST_1"

status = OpenGridSampleLogin("TEMPL1", 3, flds, vls)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenGroupSampleLogin    

Purpose:

The OpenGroupSampleLogin function opens the Group Sample Login window using a given template name.

Syntax:

status = OpenGroupSampleLogin(templateName)

templateName – the name of the sample login template to be opened

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no template name is specified, or if LIMS is unable to retrieve the template with the specified template name, LIMS will trigger the error "Sample template does not exist".

•LIMS opens the Group Sample Login window using the specified template name.

•The Group Sample Login window is not a modal window.  Execution of the LIMS Basic script resumes as soon as the window is opened.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens the Group Sample Login window using the sample login template named "MICRO".

status = OpenGroupSampleLogin("MICRO")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenInstrumentManager    

Purpose:

The OpenInstrumentManager function opens the specified instrument in the Instrument Manager window.

Syntax:

status = OpenInstrumentManager(instrumentName)

instrumentName – the name of the instrument to be opened

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the specified instrument does not exist in the database (or if no instrument is specified), LIMS will trigger the error "Instrument does not exist".

•LIMS opens the Instrument Manager window and displays the information for the specified instrument.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens the instrument named "BALANCE1".

status = OpenInstrumentManager("BALANCE1")

Returns:

OpenInventoryManager    

Purpose:

The OpenInventoryManager function opens the Inventory Manager window and displays all inventory items that match the specified filter conditions.

Syntax:

status = OpenInventoryManager(filterName, fieldsArray, valuesArray)

filtername – the name of the inventory filter to be applied.

fieldsArray – an array that contains some or all of the available fields for the specified filter

valuesArray – an array that contains the corresponding field values on which the inventory items are to be filtered.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the specified filter does not exist, LIMS will trigger the error "Filter does not exist".

•For each field in the fields array, LIMS will override or add the corresponding value to the filter.  (In other words, it is acceptable to specify fields that do not exist in the filter.)  Values are interpreted as follows:

•If the value is equal to "&USER", LIMS will substitute the name of the current user instead.

•Otherwise, if the value begins with an ampersand ("&"), LIMS will interpret it as the name of a date range.  This name must be one of the values that is permitted by a Date Range entry field, (e.g. "Last 7 Days (Inclusive)","Last Month", "This YTD", etc.) otherwise LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid Date Range".

•Otherwise, if the value contains one or more occurrences of a vertical bar ("|"), LIMS will interpret the value as a sequence of permitted values.  In other words, LIMS will display all inventory items for which the specified field contains a value that is equal to one of the values in the sequence.

NOTE: You cannot use the vertical bar ("|"),  format with Date fields.

 

Otherwise, if the value is itself an array of values, LIMS will include all inventory items for which the corresponding field value is greater than or equal to the first value in the array and less than or equal to the last value in the array.

Otherwise, if the field is a Date or DateTime field, LIMS will interpret the value as a date and ignore any time component.  LIMS will include all inventory items for which the date value of the corresponding field matches the specified date.

Otherwise, if the field is a Boolean field, LIMS will use the first character of the specified value only.  This value should be either "T" or "F" in order for records to be found.

Otherwise, the specified value is added to the filter as is.

LIMS opens the Inventory Manager window, applies the filter with the specified values, and displays all inventory items that match the filter conditions.

NOTE: This function does not make changes to the database.


Example:

The following example displays all inventory items that match the criteria of the filter named "FILTER1" and in addition have a product name that is equal to either "PROD1" or "PROD2".

myfields[1] = "PRODUCT"
myvalues[1] = "PROD1|PROD2"
status = OpenInventoryManager("FILTER1", myfields, myvalues)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenInvestigationManager    

Purpose:

The OpenInvestigationManager function opens the Investigation Manager window on an existing or a new investigation.

Syntax:

status = OpenInvestigationManager(templateName, investigationName)

templateName – the name of the template to be opened

investigationName – the name of the investigation to be opened

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If neither a template name nor an investigation name is specified, LIMS will trigger the error "A template name or investigation name must be specified".

•If an investigation name is specified, LIMS will perform the following steps.

•If no investigation with the specified name is found, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid investigation name was specified".

•LIMS opens the Investigation Manager window and displays the specified investigation.

•Otherwise (if no investigation name is specified), LIMS will perform the following steps.

•If no template with the specified template name is found, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid investigation template name specified".

•LIMS opens the Investigation Manager window and prompts the user for a new investigation using the specified template.

•LIMS returns true as soon as the window has opened.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database. 


Example:

The following example opens the investigation named "INV001".

status = OpenInvestigationManager("", "INV001")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenLotManager    

Purpose:

The OpenLotManager function opens the Lot Manager window and displays either the specified lot or the specified template (for creating a new lot).

Syntax:

status = OpenLotManager(templateName, lotNumber)

templateName – the name of the template to be opened

lotNumber – the lot number of the lot to be opened

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If both the template name and the lot number are blank or missing, LIMS will trigger the error "A template name or lot number must be specified".

•If a lot number is specified, LIMS will retrieve it.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid lot number was specified".  If the lot is found, LIMS will open the Lot Manager window and display the lot.

•If no lot number is specified, LIMS will retrieve the specified template.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid lot template name specified".  If the template is found, LIMS will open the Lot Manager window and display the template.

NOTE:  If both a lot number and template are specified, the function will ignore the template and open the lot.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens the Lot Manager to a new lot using the "ACME" lot tempolate.

status = OpenLotManager("ACME",)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenModifySamples    

Purpose:

The OpenModifySamples function can be used to open the  ‘Modify Samples Dialog’  for selected sample.

Syntax:

status =  OpenModifySamples(sampleIds, templateName)

sampleIds – the array containing Sample_Numbers

templateName – Sample Login Template to open the samples

Comments

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evaluates the input: sampleIds.  If no input is specified, the system triggers an error and the Last Error is set to: "No samples specified".

•LIMS evaluates the input: tempateName.  If no input is specified, the system triggers an error and the Last Error is set to: "A template name must be specified".

•LIMS locates the specified tempateName.  If the template could not be located, the system triggers an error and the Last Error is set to: "Invalid template name specified:" followed by the input.

•LIMS locates the specified samples.  If no samples are located, the system triggers an error and the Last Error is set to: "Invalid sample ids".

•LIMS opens the Modify Samples Dialog using the specified template name displayed any valid samples.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

var1[1] = 176
var1[2] = 177
status = OpenModifySamples(var1, "DEMO")

Returns:

status = true if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

OpenMultiSampleTestEditor   

Purpose:

The OpenMultiSampleTestEditor function opens a Multi Sample Test Edit Dialog to add Tests to multiple samples.

Syntax:

status = OpenMultiSampleTestEditor(sampleArray, sampleFieldsArray, analysesArray, testListsArray, allowAdditionalTestsFlag, variationsArray)

sampleArray – a 1 Dimensional  array containing Sample Numbers that are to be opened in the editor

sampleFieldsArray – an array containing the sample fields to be displayed in the editor

analysesArray – an array containing Analyses to be displayed as columns

testListsArray- an array containing Test Lists to be displayed as columns

allowAdditionalTestsFlag- Either True/False depending upon whether to allow Additional Tests or not.  If False, menu items on the Edit menu for adding tests are disabled.

variationsArray- an array containing the names of the variations corresponding to the analysesArray

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evaluates the size of the variationsArray.  If the size is > 0 the LIMS checks that the size is the same as the analysesArray.  If this fails, the system triggers an error and returns a Last Error: ‘The number of variations does not match the number of analyses’.

•LIMS evaluates size of the sampleArray.  If the size is not > 0, the system triggers and error and returns a Last Error: ‘No samples were specified’.

•LIMS evaluates the entries in the sampleArray.  If a sample id cannot be found, the system triggers an error and returns a Last Error: ‘Sample does not exist: id’.

•LIMS evaluates the entries in the analysesArray if the size is > 0.  If an analysis cannot be found, the system triggers an error and returns a Last Error: ‘Analysis does not exist: analysis.

•LIMS evaluates the entries in the testListsArray if the size is > 0.  If a test list cannot be found, the system triggers an error and returns a Last Error: ‘Test List does not exist: test list.

•The system opens the specified samples in the Multi Sample Test Editor.

NOTE: A database commit is not part of this function.

Example:

The following example opens the Multi Sample Test Edit Dialog for the samples ‘623’ and ‘624’.

sampleArray[1] = 623

sampleArray[2] = 624

sampleFieldsArray[1] = "CUSTOMER"

testListsArray[1] = "CHEM"

analysesArray[1] = "ICP"

analysesArray[2] = "PH"

variationsArray[1] = "PRIMARY"

variationsArray[2] = ""

allowAdditionalTestsFlag = "T"

 

status = OpenMultiSampleTestEditor(sampleArray, sampleFieldsArray, analysesArray, testListsArray, allowAdditionalTestsFlag, variationsArray)



Returns:

True if the function was successful, false otherwise.

 

OpenObjectAuditHistory

Purpose:

The OpenObjectAuditHistory function opens the Object history dialog for the specified object.  The specified object should be the parent object ID, not a child record.

Syntax:

status = OpenObjectAuditHistory(tableName, keyArray, keyValuesArray)

tableName – (required) the name of the parent table to which the object belongs

keyArray - (required) a 1D array of fields that are the key fields for the specified object table

keyValuesArray - (required) a 1D array of field values that correspond to the key fields for the specified object

Comments:

The function performs the following steps:

•LIMS checks the keyArray. If the array is not specified, LIMS will trigger the error “No keys were specified”.

•LIMS checks the sizes of the keyValuesArray. If the array is not specified, LIMS will trigger the error “No key values were specified”.

•LIMS checks the keyArray and keyValuesArray. If the array sizes do not match, LIMS will trigger the error “Key and key value arrays must be the same size”.

•LIMS will attempt to open the audit history for the specified object. If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error “The specified object does not exist”.

•LIMS opens the appropriate audit dialog for the specified object.

NOTE:  This function does NOT save changes to the database.


Example:

tableName = "SAMPLE"

keyArray[1] = "SAMPLE_NUMBER"

keyValuesArray[1] = 5737

status = OpenObjectAuditHistory(tableName, keyArray, keyValuesArray)

Returns:

 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenObjectGroupManager    

Purpose:

The OpenObjectGroupManager function opens the Object Group Manager window to a specified Object Group.

Syntax:

status = OpenObjectGroupManager(objectGroupName)

ObjectGroupName – the name of the Object Group the Object Group Manager window is to default to.  This setting is optional. 

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the specified Object Group Name is not valid, the system returns a status of false and the following last error: "Invalid object group specified: " followed by the name of the Object Group provided.

•If no Object Group Name is provided, the system opens the Object Group Manager window with no Object Group opened.  The system returns the status of true.

•If a valid Object Group Name is provided, the system opens the Object Group Manager window with the specified Object Group opened.

NOTE: This function does not save any changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens the Object Group Manager to the Object Group: Example.

status = OpenObjectGroupManager("EXAMPLE")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenOrderManager    

Purpose:

The OpenOrderManager function opens Order Manager.   

Syntax:

status = OpenOrderManager(template, orderNumber)

template – the Order template to be set as default.

orderNumber – the number of the order to be opened.

Comments:

template is ignored if orderNumber is specified. The function performs the following steps:

•The function has the following possible results if no Order Manager window is already displayed:

•If orderNumber is specified and matches an existing order, an Order Manager window is opened with the specified orderNumber.

•If orderNumber is specified and does not match an existing order, a blank Order Manager window is opened.

•If no orderNumber is specified and a valid template is specified, an Order Manager window is opened with the specified template assigned (the New Order and Open an Order functions will have the specified template preset).

•If no order Number is specified and an invalid template or no template is specified, a blank Order Manager window is opened.

•If Order Manager window is already displayed:

•If orderNumber is specified and matches an existing order, the order with the specified orderNumber is displayed in the existing Order Manager window.

•If orderNumber is specified and does not match an existing order, the existing Order Manager window is not changed.

•If no orderNumber is specified and a valid template is specified, a new Order Manager window is opened with the specified template assigned (the New Order and Open an Order functions will have the specified template preset).

•If no order Number is specified and an invalid template or no template is specified, a new blank Order Manager window is opened.

Example:

The following example opens Order Manager with order R05-00013

status = OpenOrderManager(,"R05-00013")

The following example is equivalent to the main menu Order Manager function:

status = OpenOrderManager()

Returns: 

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenParentChildrenRecordEditor   

Purpose:

This function opens the Parent Children Record Editor on the specified user added table and record.  The function also controls the displayed child tables and sets the read only status of the record.

Syntax:

status = OpenParentChildrenRecordEditor(table, key, version, childTableNames, readOnly)

table – text parameter defining the user added table to display.

key – text parameter defining the key value of the record to be displayed.  (Only single key tables supported.)

version – numeric parameter defining the version of the specified record to be displayed.

childTableNames – an array listing the child tables to be displayed and when the child table tabs appear they should be in the same order specified in the array.

readOnly – Boolean parameter defining if a user can modify the displayed records.

Comments:

When this function is triggered, LIMS performs the following:

•If no value for table is specified, LIMS will trigger the error "Parent Table not specified".

•If the specified table does not exist, LIMS will trigger the error "Table does not exist".

•If no value is specified for the key argument, LIMS will open the Parent Children Record Editor on  the specified table. The value of the readOnly argument will be ignored and the records will be Read Only.

•If no record exists for the specified key value, LIMS will trigger the error "Record does not exist".

•If a specified child table does not exist, LIMS will trigger the error "Table does not exist: table".

•LIMS opens the Parent Children Record Editor and displays the specified record as follows.

•If a version number is specified, LIMS will display that version.  Otherwise, LIMS will display the latest version.

•If the displayed version is the latest version, the value of the readOnly argument will be applied to the window.  Otherwise, the record will be read-only regardless of the value of the readOnly argument.

•If the specified table has at least one child table, and if a child table name is specified, and if this name matches the name of one of the child tables, LIMS will display the records for that child table on a tab.

•If more than one child table is specified, LIMS will displayed the child tabs in the order corresponding to the order in the array.

•If the table has no child tables, LIMS will display no child records.

 

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

table = "M0391_PARENT"

key = "RECORD1"

version = ""

childTableNames[1] = "M0391_CHILD"

childTableNames[2] = "M0391_CHILD_2"

readOnly = "F"

status = OpenParentChildrenRecordEditor(table, key, version, childTableNames, readOnly)

Returns:

If successful, this function returns status = true.  If not, returns status = false.

 

OpenProductSpecificationsDesigner  

Purpose:

The OpenProductSpecificationsDesigner function opens Product Specification Designer for a Product.

Syntax:

status = OpenProductSpecificationsDesigner(product)

product – the product to be opened in Product Specification Designer.

Comments:

The function does the following :

•If an invalid Product is entered, lasterror gets the value "Product does not exist:"  followed by the input product.  The value returned is False.

•If the function passes the above validations, the Product and it’s corresponding Grade, Specification and Specs are opened in Specification Designer.

A database commit is not a part of this function. 

NOTE:  This function does not check to see if the current user has the privilege to open the Product Specification Designer.  This check must be addressed within the configuration of this function.

Example:

product = "M0249"

status = OpenProductSpecificationsDesigner(product)

Returns:

 

True; if successful and False otherwise.

 

OpenProjectManager    

Purpose:

The OpenProjectManager function opens the Project Manager window and displays either the specified project or the specified template (for creating a new project).

Syntax:

status = OpenProjectManager(templateName, projectName)

templateName – the name of the template to be opened

projectName – the name of the project to be opened

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If both the template name and the project name are blank or missing, LIMS will trigger the error "A template name or project name must be specified".

•If a project name is specified, LIMS will retrieve it.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid project name was specified".  If the project is found, LIMS will open the Project Manager window and display the project.

•If no project name is specified, LIMS will retrieve the specified template.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid project template name specified".  If the template is found, LIMS will open the Project Manager window and display the template.

NOTE: If both a project name and template are specified, the function will ignore the template and open the project.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens the project named "PROJ1".

status = OpenProjectManager("", "PROJ1")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenRecord    

Purpose:

The OpenRecord function opens the specified record in Table Manager.  If the record being opened is in the LIMS Basic context then the information in context will be opened instead of being opened from the database.

Syntax:

status = OpenRecord(table, key, readOnly, version, allowSaveAs, ignoreGroupSecurity)

table – the name of the table that contains the record to be opened

key – the primary key value of the record to be opened

readOnly – (Boolean)the value True if the user should be prevented from making changes to the record

version – the version of the record to be opened (optional)

allowSaveAs – (Boolean)

ignoreGroupSecurity – (Boolean) if True then the current user does not need to have the group security assigned to the record to be opened to open the record. If False then the current user must have the group security assigned to the record to be opened or else they will get a message saying Invalid Entry.  If not specified, the default is False. (optional)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS interprets the readOnly flag as follows.

•If the value is blank or missing, LIMS will use the value True.

•Otherwise, if the value begins with the letter "T" or "Y", LIMS will use the value True.

•Otherwise, LIMS will use the value False.

•If no table is specified, or if no key is specified, LIMS will trigger the error "Table or Record must be specified".

•If the specified table name is not the name of one of the LIMS tables, LIMS will trigger the error "Table does not exist or cannot be opened".

•If a version greater than zero is specified, and if the table is versioned, LIMS will perform the following steps.

•LIMS retrieves the specified version of the specified record.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Record does not exist".

•LIMS opens the specified record in Table Manager.  If the value of the readOnly flag is True, or if the specified version is not the latest version of the record, the user will be prevented from making changes to the record.

•Otherwise, i.e., if no version is specified, or if the specified version is not greater than zero, or if the table is not versioned, LIMS will perform the following steps.

•If the specified table does not contain a record whose key matches the specified key value, LIMS will trigger the error "Record does not exist"

•LIMS opens the specified record in Table Manager.  If the table is versioned, the latest version will be opened.  If the value of the readOnly flag is True, the user will be prevented from making changes to the record.

•LIMS returns true as soon as the window has opened.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database. 


Example:

The following example opens the analysis named "PH" in read-only mode.

status = OpenRecord("ANALYSIS", "PH", "T")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenRecordGridEditor    

Purpose:

The OpenRecordGridEditor function opens a grid editor window.  If the record being opened is in the LIMS Basic context then the information in context will be opened instead of being opened from the database.

Syntax:

status = OpenRecordGridEditor(table, key, readOnly, childTable, version, allowSaveAs, ignoreGroupSecurity)

table – the name of the table to be opened

key – the primary key value of the record to be opened

readOnly – the value true if the user should be prevented from making changes.  The default value is true.

childTable – the name of the child table whose records should be displayed.  This argument should be omitted only if the table has exactly one child table.

version – the version of the record to be opened.  If no version is specified, the latest version will be opened.

allowSaveAs – (Boolean)

ignoreGroupSecurity – (Boolean) if True then the current user does not need to have the group security assigned to the record to be opened to open the record. If False then the current user must have the group security assigned to the record to be opened or else they will get a message saying Invalid Entry.  If not specified, the default is False. (optional)

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If no value for table is specified, LIMS will open the grid editor window without content.  The value of the readOnly argument will be ignored.  The user will not be prevented from making changes.

•If the specified table does not exist, LIMS will trigger the error "Table does not exist".

•If no value is specified for the key argument, LIMS will open the grid editor window on all records in the specified table. The value of the readOnly argument will be ignored.  The user will not be prevented from making changes.

•If no record exists for the specified key value, LIMS will trigger the error "Record does not exist".

•LIMS opens the grid editor and displays the specified record as follows.

•If a version number is specified, LIMS will display that version.  Otherwise, LIMS will display the latest version.

•If the displayed version is the latest version, the value of the readOnly argument will be applied to the window.  Otherwise, the record will be read-only regardless of the value of the readOnly argument.

•If the specified table has at least one child table, and if no child table name is specified in the argument list, LIMS will display the records from just one of the child tables.

•If the specified table has at least one child table, and if a child table name is specified, and if this name matches the name of one of the child tables, LIMS will display the records in that child table.

•If neither of the above two conditions apply, i.e., if the table has no child tables or if an invalid child table name is specified, LIMS will display no child records.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database. 

NOTE:  If a grid editor window is already open for the specified table, LIMS will use that window instead of opening a new window.

Example:

The following example displays the latest version of the "LOD" analysis with its components in read-only mode.

status = OpenRecordGridEditor("ANALYSIS", "LOD", true, "COMPONENT")

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenResultEntry    

Purpose:

The OpenResultEntry function opens the Result Entry window on an array of tests.  This window is sometimes referred to as the Classic Result Entry window (as opposed to the Grid Result Entry window).

Syntax:

status = OpenResultEntry(tests)

tests – an array that contains the test numbers to be displayed in the window

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If the tests array has no elements, LIMS will trigger the error "No tests for result entry".

•LIMS retrieves all requested tests and their corresponding samples from the database.  If none are found, LIMS will trigger the error "No tests for result entry".  (Note: as long as the tests array contains at least one valid test number, no error will be triggered if the array also includes invalid test numbers.)

•LIMS opens the Result Entry window on all retrieved tests.

•LIMS waits for the user exit Result entry window before any subsequent code is processed.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens the Result Entry window for test numbers 12 and 14.

mytests[1] = 12
mytests[2] = 14
status = OpenResultEntry(mytests)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenReviewWindow    

Purpose:

The OpenReviewWindow function opens the Review Dialog with the specified samples displayed.

Syntax:

status = OpenReviewWindow(samples)

samples – an array of integers specifying the sample numbers to be displayed on the Review Dialog window.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS verifies that at least one sample number was provided.  If no sample numbers were provided, the LIMS returns: "No samples for review."

•LIMS opens the Review Dialog with all the located samples displayed.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following code opens the Review Dialog with samples 5 and 8 displayed.

Samples[1] = 5
samples[2] = 8
status = OpenReviewWindow(samples)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenSampleInterface    

Purpose:

The OpenSampleInterface function opens the Sample Interface window and displays either the specified sample or the specified template (for logging in a new sample).

Syntax:

status = OpenSampleInterface(templateName, sampleNumber)

templateName – the name of the template to be opened

sampleNumber – the sample number of the sample to be opened

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If both the template name and the sample number are blank or missing, LIMS will trigger the error "A template name or sample number must be specified".

•If a sample number is specified, LIMS will retrieve it.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid sample number was specified".  If the sample is found, LIMS will open the Sample Interface window and display the sample.

•If no sample number is specified, LIMS will retrieve the specified template.  If this fails, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid sample template name specified".  If the template is found, LIMS will open the Sample Interface window and display the template.

NOTE:  If both a sample number and template are specified, the function will ignore the template and open the sample.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens the sample that has sample number 919.

status = OpenSampleInterface("", 919)

Returns:

The function returns the value true if it completes successfully.  It returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenScheduleWindow    

Purpose:

This function opens the Schedule Window and sets the Schedule Type.

Syntax:

status = OpenScheduleWindow(scheduleType, whereClause, baseDate)

scheduleType – string defining the setting for the Schedule Type field on  the Schedule Window. This parameter is optional.

whereClause– string containing where Clause which defines the schedule entry records which are opened in the schedule window. This parameter is optional. If the scheduleType and whereClause are specified, the scheduleType must be contained in the results returned by the whereClause.

baseDate – a date parameter defining what month/year is initially opened. This parameter is optional.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If specified, the system checks the scheduleType parameter.  If invalid, the system will trigger the error " Invalid schedule type specified: scheduleType ".

•If specified, the system checks the baseDate parameter. If not a valid date, the system will trigger the error " baseDate must be a valid date ".

•The system will open the Schedule Window, using the specified where clause and base date if specified.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database. However, changes can be saved in the Schedule Window.


Example:

whereClause = "COMPLETE = 'F'"

Status = OpenScheduleWindow("APPOINTMENTS", whereClause,)

Returns:

The function returns true if successful. If it is not successful, it will answer false and lastError will be set with an appropriate error message.

 

OpenSchedulerEntryDialog

Purpose:

This function opens the Scheduler Entry Dialog on the specified entry.

Syntax:

status = OpenSchedulerEntryDialog(entryCode)

entryCode – (Integer, Required) the entry code corresponding to the schedule entry record which is to be opened in the Scheduler Entry Dialog.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•If specified, the system checks the entryCode parameter.  If invalid, the system will trigger the error "Invalid entry code specified: entryCode ".  If not found, the system will trigger the error "No records found for entry code specified: entryCode".

•The system will open the Scheduler Entry Dialog to the specified entry. If not successful, the system will trigger the error "Error editing schedule entry ".

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database. However, changes can be saved in the Schedule Window.


Example:

entryCode = 659

status = OpenSchedulerEntryDialog(entryCode)

Returns:

The function returns true if successful. If it is not successful, it will answer false and lastError will be set with an appropriate error message.

 

OpenSearchDialog    

Purpose:

The OpenSearchDialog function launches the Search Dialog

Syntax:

status = OpenSearchDialog(searchTemplateName, multiSelectFlag, "returnArrayName", returnFieldsArray)

searchTemplateName – the name of the Search Template

multiSelectFlag – A Boolean indicating if multiple records can be selected.  If True then multiple records can be selected.  If not specified then it defaults to false.

returnArrayName – the name of the array containing the key fields of the selected records

returnFieldsArray – name of the array containing key values of qualifying records.

Comments:

The function performs the following steps.

•LIMS evaluates the specified searchTemplateName.

•If no template is specified, LIMS will trigger the error "No search template was specified".

•If an invalid template is specified, LIMS will trigger the error "Invalid search template was specified:" followed by the input searchTemplateName.

•LIMS evaluates the specified returnArrayName.  If no array name is specified, LIMS will trigger the error "No return array name specified".

•If no errors are generated, the function will open the Search Dialog using the specified searchTemplateName.

•If the multiSelectFlag the Search Dialog will allow multiple selections.

•When closed, the system places the selected object(s) into an array named from the returnArrayName input.

NOTE: This function does not save changes to the database.


Example:

The following example opens the SEARCH1 template (which is for the CUSTOMER table):

searchTemplateName = "SEARCH1"

multiSelectFlag = "F"

returnFieldsArray[1] = "COMPANY_NAME"

status = OpenSearchDialog(searchTemplateName, multiSelectFlag, "returnArrayName ", returnFieldsArray)

Returns:

 

The function returns true if it the dialog opens successfully and the user clicks OK.  Once the user selects records and clicks OK, the values are retruned in the returnArrayName .  If the user clicks Cancel no values are returned and the function returns a status of false. It also returns the value false if an error is triggered.

 

OpenStandardReagentManager    

Purpose:

The OpenStandardReagentManager function opens the specified standard/reagent template in the Standard Reagent Manager window.

Syntax:

status = OpenStandardReagentManager(templateName, inactiveName)

templateName – the name of the template to be opened

inactiveName – the name of the inactive standard that is to be opened

Comments:
